We may post public privacy policies and other statements regarding our collection, storage, sharing and other processing of personal information, and any actual or perceived failure to comply with such privacy policies and other statements, as well as the foregoing data privacy and cybersecurity laws and regulations, may subject us to enforcement actions, investigations, litigation, reputational harm or requirements to modify or cease our business practices.
其中许多法律法规处于变化中且解释不确定，其适用可能在不同司法管辖区之间显著不同。合规可能要求我们修改政策、程序和控制措施，增加合规成本和运营复杂性。我们可能就个人信息的收集、存储、共享和其他处理发布公开隐私政策和其他声明，任何实际或被视为未遵守此类隐私政策和声明、以及上述数据隐私和网络安全法律法规的情况，都可能使我们面临执法行动、调查、诉讼、声誉损害或被要求修改或停止我们的业务做法。
Our business strategy depends on successfully designing, developing, and deploying our products and services, as well as related platforms, infrastructure, and other strategic initiatives, at an unprecedented scale, which presents significant execution, cost, and timing risks.
我们的业务战略依赖于以前所未有的规模成功设计、研发和部署我们的产品和服务以及相关平台、基础设施和其他战略举措，这带来了显著的执行、成本和时间风险。
Our business plan, and ultimately, the achievement of our mission, is predicated on building, commercializing, and operating products and services, as well as related infrastructure and strategic initiatives at a scale that has not previously been achieved. This objective requires us to integrate complex technologies, develop new processes and infrastructure, and coordinate across multiple suppliers, contractors, regulators, and stakeholders. Because we are attempting to execute at a scale for which there is limited precedent, we face heightened uncertainty with respect to design, engineering, procurement, construction, commissioning, and operational performance, which is further heightened by the novel nature of the technologies underlying the products and services we intend to develop.
我们的业务计划及最终对使命的实现，建立在以从未达到过的规模建造、商业化和运营产品和服务以及相关基础设施和战略举措的基础上。该目标要求我们整合复杂技术、研发新流程和基础设施，并跨多个供应商、承包商、监管机构和利益相关方协调。由于我们试图以缺乏先例的规模执行，我们在设计、工程、采购、施工、调试和运营性能方面面临更高的不确定性，而我们打算研发的产品和服务所依赖的技术的新颖性进一步加剧了这一不确定性。
As a result, timelines for developing and deploying our products and services may be longer than we currently anticipate, and we may encounter delays due to, among other things, technical challenges, including those resulting from the nascent state of certain of our products and services, the unavailability or immaturity of key technologies, supply chain constraints, energy shocks, including related price volatility, labor availability, permitting and regulatory approvals, or the need to redesign or reengineer key components. In addition, the costs associated with developing and deploying our products and services and related platforms, infrastructure and strategic initiatives at scale may exceed our current estimates, including due to inflationary pressures, energy prices, unforeseen engineering complexities, the cost of developing or licensing technologies that are not yet commercially available, competitive dynamics, changes in scope, or the need for additional capital expenditures, contingency reserves or working capital.
因此，研发和部署我们产品和服务的时间表可能比我们当前预期的更长，我们可能因多种因素遇到延迟，包括：技术挑战（包括我们某些产品和服务处于初级阶段所导致的挑战）、关键技术不可用或不成熟、供应链限制、能源冲击（包括相关的价格波动）、劳动力可用性、许可与监管批准，或重新设计或重新工程关键部件的需要。此外，规模化研发和部署我们产品和服务及相关平台、基础设施和战略举措的成本可能超过我们当前的估计，包括因通胀压力、能源价格、未预见的工程复杂性、研发或许可尚未商业化技术的成本、竞争动态、范围变化或需要额外的资本支出、应急储备或营运资本。
If we are unable to successfully execute our growth strategy on the anticipated timeline or within our expected cost parameters, our business, financial condition and results of operations could be materially adversely affected. Delays or cost overruns could also impact our ability to achieve projected returns, meet contractual commitments, access additional financing on acceptable terms, or maintain investor confidence. Moreover, even if we successfully deploy our growth strategy, including Starship, Terafab, orbital AI, and the creation of the lunar economy, they may not perform as expected at scale, which could result in operational inefficiencies, increased costs, reduced revenues, or declines in our stock price.
如果我们无法按预期时间表或在预期成本范围内成功执行增长战略，我们的业务、财务状况和经营成果可能受到重大不利影响。延迟或成本超支也可能影响我们实现预计回报、履行合同承诺、以可接受条件获得额外融资或维持投资者信心的能力。此外，即使我们成功部署增长战略（包括 Starship、Terafab、轨道 AI 和创建月球经济），这些产品和服务在规模化时可能不符合预期，可能导致运营效率低下、成本增加、收入减少或股价下跌。
We have experienced, and will likely continue to experience, launch delays and failures that could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们过去曾经历（且可能继续经历）发射延迟和失败，这些可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
Launch vehicle underperformance, propulsion anomalies, structural failures, software errors, or other malfunctions could result in launch delays or partial or total mission failures, including the loss of satellites or payloads. The occurrence of mission failures or other significant operational disruptions could also expose us to litigation as well as increased scrutiny from regulatory authorities, lead to the imposition of additional compliance requirements, and adversely affect our brand and reputation, and our ability to obtain future licenses, permits, or government contracts.
运载火箭性能不足、推进异常、结构失效、软件错误或其他故障可能导致发射延迟或部分/全部任务失败，包括失去卫星或有效载荷。任务失败或其他重大运营中断的发生也可能使我们面临诉讼以及监管机构加大审查、施加额外合规要求，对我们的品牌和声誉、以及获得未来许可证、批准或政府合同的能力产生不利影响。
We do not typically obtain insurance coverage for our satellites, payloads, or launch vehicles, and as a result we bear the full financial cost of any such losses. Repeated anomalies or high visibility mission failures could also negatively affect our brand, reputation, ability to win new business, and our customers' ability to procure launch and in-orbit insurance at competitive rates (to the extent we decide to pursue it). Such repeated anomalies or mission failures could also result in, regulators delaying, conditioning or denying approvals, waivers or licenses required for future launches or reentries, which could reduce our launch cadence and delay the deployment of our satellites and other services. In the past, certain of our launch vehicles have experienced partial or total mission failures, including anomalies that resulted in the loss of payloads and damage to launch vehicles. In certain circumstances, such mission failures could result in, debris from our launch vehicles causing significant damage to persons or property on the ground as well as environmental damage. There can be no assurance that similar or other failures will not occur with future launches. In addition, satellites may be deployed into incorrect or suboptimal orbits due to vehicle performance issues, separation events, or guidance, navigation and control errors. Incorrect orbital placement can materially reduce a satellite's operational life, impair performance, increase fuel consumption, or render the satellite unusable.
我们通常不为卫星、有效载荷或运载火箭购买保险，因此我们承担此类损失的全部财务成本。反复的异常或高曝光度的任务失败也可能对我们的品牌、声誉、赢得新业务的能力，以及客户以有竞争力的费率购买发射和在轨保险（如我们决定追求）的能力产生负面影响。此类反复异常或任务失败还可能导致监管机构延迟、附加条件或拒绝授予未来发射或再入所需的批准、豁免或许可证，从而降低我们的发射频次并延迟卫星和其他服务的部署。过去某些运载火箭曾经历部分或全部任务失败，包括导致有效载荷损失和运载火箭损坏的异常。在某些情况下，此类任务失败可能导致我们运载火箭的碎片对地面人员或财产造成重大损害，以及环境损害。无法保证未来发射不会发生类似或其他失败。此外，由于运载火箭性能问题、分离事件或制导、导航与控制错误，卫星可能被部署到错误或次优轨道。错误的轨道入轨可能大幅缩短卫星的运营寿命、损害性能、增加燃料消耗或使卫星无法使用。
Our satellites, launch vehicles, and other space-related technologies operate, and in the case of orbital AI compute, will operate, in the harsh and unpredictable environment of space, exposing them to a wide and unique range of space-related risks that could cause them to malfunction or fail, and any such malfunction or failure could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们的卫星、运载火箭和其他空间相关技术运行（轨道 AI 算力的情况下，未来将运行）于太空恶劣且不可预测的环境中，使其面临多种独特的空间相关风险，这些风险可能导致它们故障或失效；任何此类故障或失效都可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
Operating in space subjects our satellites, launch vehicles, spacecraft, and related systems to extreme and highly variable conditions that can adversely affect performance, reduce useful life, or result in total mission failure. Space is inherently hostile. Hardware must withstand: significant vibration and acoustic loads during launch; wide-ranging thermal cycles; radiation from solar and cosmic sources; micrometeoroids and orbital debris; and other environmental hazards, each of which testing cannot fully replicate. In particular, we have not, and no one else has, previously operated or attempted to operate orbital AI compute, and the conditions of space on such AI infrastructure have not been tested. Once deployed, orbital AI compute infrastructure will not be readily accessible, and as a result, will not be easily repaired or upgraded, such that any component failures could result in permanent capacity loss, accelerated depreciation, decommissioning or need for replacement of the infrastructure.
在太空中运行使我们的卫星、运载火箭、航天器及相关系统受到极端且高度可变的条件影响，这些条件可能不利影响性能、缩短使用寿命或导致任务完全失败。太空本质上是充满敌意的。硬件必须承受：发射期间的显著振动和声学载荷；范围广泛的热循环；来自太阳和宇宙源的辐射；微流星体和轨道碎片；以及其他环境危害，每一项测试都无法完全复制。特别是，我们以及其他任何人此前从未运营或尝试运营过轨道 AI 算力，太空对此类 AI 基础设施的影响尚未经过测试。一旦部署，轨道 AI 算力基础设施将无法轻易接近，因此无法轻易维修或升级，任何部件故障都可能导致永久容量损失、加速折旧、退役或需要更换基础设施。
In addition, space weather events, such as geomagnetic storms, solar flares, and other forms of radiation activity, have in the past disrupted and could in the future disrupt satellite propulsion, power systems, and communications equipment, potentially leading to reduced performance or permanent damage. Although we incorporate certain radiation-hardened components, shielding, and redundancy into our systems, these measures may not be sufficient to prevent material adverse impacts in all scenarios. Failures or performance degradation resulting from these risks could delay deployments, reduce available capacity, increase operating costs, require significant capital expenditures to replace affected assets, or interrupt or degrade services provided to customers. Furthermore, the useful life of our satellites is inherently shorter than that of the information technology systems and infrastructure they host. As a result, we must periodically launch replacement satellites as existing satellites reach the end of their useful lives and are decommissioned, which may truncate the effective lifespan of those underlying information technology systems and infrastructure. Any such events could adversely affect our reputation, compliance with applicable laws and regulations, business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
此外，太空天气事件（如地磁风暴、太阳耀斑和其他形式的辐射活动）过去曾扰乱并可能在未来扰乱卫星推进、电力系统和通信设备，可能导致性能下降或永久损坏。尽管我们将某些抗辐射部件、屏蔽和冗余设计纳入系统，但这些措施在所有情景中可能不足以防止重大不利影响。这些风险造成的故障或性能下降可能延迟部署、减少可用容量、增加运营成本、要求大量资本支出更换受影响资产，或中断/降级为客户提供的服务。此外，我们卫星的使用寿命本质上短于它们所承载的信息技术系统和基础设施。因此，随着现有卫星达到使用寿命终结并退役，我们必须定期发射替换卫星，这可能缩短那些底层信息技术系统和基础设施的有效寿命。任何此类事件都可能对我们的声誉、对适用法律法规的遵守、业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
The continued proliferation of satellite constellations in Low-Earth Orbit, as well as the risk of collisions with space debris or other spacecraft, could limit or impair our launch flexibility and satellite deployment, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
低地球轨道 (Low-Earth Orbit) 卫星星座的持续扩散，以及与空间碎片或其他航天器碰撞的风险，可能限制或损害我们的发射灵活性和卫星部署，从而对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
The continued proliferation of Low-Earth Orbit constellations can increase the risk of collisions with space debris or other spacecraft if operators fail to adhere to responsible space safety, debris mitigation, or coordination practices.
如果运营方未能遵守负责任的空间安全、碎片缓解或协调做法，低地球轨道星座的持续扩散可能增加与空间碎片或其他航天器碰撞的风险。
Our growth strategy depends, in part, on continuing to launch additional satellites into Low-Earth Orbit. As the number of satellites and other objects in Low-Earth Orbit continues to grow, the probability of accidental collisions, fragmentation events, or other in-orbit incidents increases, which could result in the loss or degradation of our satellites, increased costs for collision avoidance maneuvers, or the need to replace or reposition assets on an accelerated schedule. Not all satellite operators or other space actors adhere to the same rigorous space safety, debris mitigation, or coordination practices that we adhere to, which may increase the likelihood of congestion, conjunctions, or other operational risks outside of our control and, in extreme cases, could contribute to fragmentation events or cascading debris effects that further increase collision risks in Low-Earth Orbit.
我们的增长战略部分依赖于继续向低地球轨道发射额外卫星。随着低地球轨道中卫星和其他物体数量持续增长，意外碰撞、碎片化事件或其他在轨事件的概率增加，这可能导致我们的卫星损失或退化、避撞机动成本增加，或需要按加速时间表更换或重新部署资产。并非所有卫星运营方或其他太空参与者都遵守与我们相同的严格空间安全、碎片缓解或协调做法，这可能增加超出我们控制的拥堵、近距离接触或其他运营风险的可能性，极端情况下可能造成碎片化事件或级联碎片效应，进一步增加低地球轨道的碰撞风险。
In addition, some domestic and international authorities have applied heightened regulatory scrutiny as interest in utilizing Low-Earth Orbit for satellite operations has increased. Debris mitigation regulations may emerge if congestion increases. Failure to meet debris requirements could result in monetary penalties or loss of licensing authority, which would adversely affect our satellite constellation deployment and expansion plans, and future regulatory actions could impose more restrictive operational, deployment, or debris mitigation requirements that could limit our ability to launch or operate satellites in Low-Earth Orbit. In addition, there is a burgeoning effort to further regulate Low-Earth Orbit, MEO, and GSO and establish liability regimes for operators, including regimes similar to those under the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act, which imposes strict liability for environmental contamination or remediation costs, as well as growing concern over the potential environmental effects of emissions and other byproducts from rocket launches in Earth's upper atmosphere.
此外，随着利用低地球轨道开展卫星运营的兴趣增加，部分国内和国际主管机构已加强监管审查。如果拥堵增加，可能出现碎片缓解法规。未能满足碎片要求可能导致货币处罚或失去许可权，这将对我们的卫星星座部署和扩张计划产生不利影响；未来的监管行动可能施加更严格的运营、部署或碎片缓解要求，从而限制我们在低地球轨道发射或运营卫星的能力。此外，对低地球轨道、中地球轨道 (MEO) 和地球静止轨道 (GSO) 的进一步监管以及为运营方建立责任制度的努力正在兴起，包括类似于《综合环境响应、补偿与责任法》(Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation and Liability Act) 的制度（该法对环境污染或修复费用施加严格责任），以及对火箭发射在地球高层大气中产生的排放物和其他副产品潜在环境影响的日益关注。
Additional regulation in this area could adversely impact our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
该领域的额外监管可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
Furthermore, any damage to our satellites or impairment of their functionality resulting from collisions with space debris or other spacecraft could materially and adversely affect our ability to deliver reliable services to our customers, harm our reputation, and expose us to potential contractual liabilities or insurance claims. The growing challenges associated with space debris management may require us to invest in additional technologies or processes to safeguard our assets and maintain compliance with evolving regulatory frameworks, which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
此外，因与空间碎片或其他航天器碰撞而造成的对我们卫星的任何损害或功能损害，都可能严重不利影响我们向客户提供可靠服务的能力，损害我们的声誉，并使我们面临潜在的合同责任或保险索赔。与空间碎片管理相关的日益增长的挑战可能要求我们投资额外技术或流程以保护资产并维持对不断演变的监管框架的合规，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
Interruptions in the operation of critical satellite network, ground station, launch, manufacturing, or spacecraft or data center infrastructure could result in significant downtime, operational delays or loss of service, each of which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
关键卫星网络、地面站、发射、制造、航天器或数据中心基础设施运营中断可能导致重大停机、运营延迟或服务损失，每一种都可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
Our ability to provide reliable services across our Space, Connectivity, and AI business segments depends on the uninterrupted operation of our critical infrastructure, including but not limited to satellite and communications networks, ground stations, launch facilities, and data centers. An interruption or failure affecting any aspect of this infrastructure, whether due to equipment malfunctions, power outages, disruptions in, or unauthorized access to, our computer systems (such as software or hardware failures, or cyberattacks), natural disasters (such as earthquakes, floods, fires, or severe weather events), terrorism, war, sabotage, pandemics, epidemics, or other unforeseen circumstances, could result in significant downtime, operational delays, or complete loss of service. Any such attack could destroy or disable a significant number of our satellites and, depending on its scale, could trigger a cascading collision event that renders our licensed orbits, and potentially other orbits, unusable for an extended period.
我们在 Space、Connectivity 和 AI 业务部门提供可靠服务的能力依赖于关键基础设施的不间断运行，包括但不限于卫星和通信网络、地面站、发射设施和数据中心。此基础设施任何方面的中断或故障——无论是由于设备故障、电力中断、计算机系统中断或未经授权的访问（如软件或硬件故障，或网络攻击）、自然灾害（如地震、洪水、火灾或恶劣天气事件）、恐怖主义、战争、破坏、流行病、传染病或其他不可预见的情况——都可能导致重大停机、运营延迟或服务完全损失。任何此类攻击可能摧毁或停用大量我们的卫星，根据其规模，可能引发级联碰撞事件，使我们获得许可的轨道（以及可能其他轨道）长期无法使用。
Similarly, the use of our satellites to enable communications access in conflict zones may expose us to retaliation from foreign governments and non-state actors. Such an event could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. These events may disrupt power, damage facilities, interrupt service despite contingency plans or compromise our ability to deliver services to customers as promised, hinder our ability to meet regulatory or contractual requirements, and erode trust among our customers, partners, regulators and stakeholders. In particular, an interruption or failure affecting our critical infrastructure could result in outages of service to our Starlink Subscribers. Any such outage could erode the trust of existing and potential Starlink Subscribers in our service, which could result in the loss of existing or potential subscribers. In addition, the complexity and interdependence of our engineering, manufacturing, assembly and terrestrial, space transportation, and infrastructure systems mean that a disruption in one component can have cascading effects throughout our operations. For example, an outage at a data center or ground station could impact command and control functions, mission planning, or real-time telemetry, while interruptions at launch facilities could cause postponements or cancellations of scheduled launches.
类似地，使用我们的卫星在冲突地区提供通信接入可能使我们面临外国政府和非国家行为者的报复。此类事件可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。这些事件可能扰乱电力、损坏设施、尽管有应急计划仍中断服务，或损害我们按承诺向客户交付服务的能力，妨碍我们满足监管或合同要求，侵蚀客户、合作伙伴、监管机构和利益相关方对我们的信任。特别是，影响关键基础设施的中断或故障可能导致 Starlink 订户服务中断。任何此类中断可能侵蚀现有和潜在 Starlink 订户对我们服务的信任，从而导致现有或潜在订户流失。此外，我们工程、制造、装配和地面、太空运输与基础设施系统的复杂性与相互依赖性意味着一个组件的中断可能在我们的运营中产生级联效应。例如，数据中心或地面站的中断可能影响指挥控制功能、任务规划或实时遥测，而发射设施的中断可能导致计划发射推迟或取消。
Adverse global macroeconomic and geopolitical conditions may negatively affect our business, financial condition, results of operations and future prospects.
全球宏观经济和地缘政治状况的不利变化可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生负面影响。
Adverse global or regional economic and geopolitical conditions could reduce demand for certain of our products and services, which may negatively affect our business, financial condition, results of operations and future prospects. Economic downturns, inflation, higher interest rates, tighter credit conditions, reduced consumer spending, lower business or government investment, or geopolitical developments may negatively affect demand for our offerings. Reduced consumer or enterprise spending for each of our Starlink connectivity services or our AI- related offerings would limit our ability to grow our business, which may slow the pace at which we deploy satellites and expand our constellation or adversely affect the utilization of our launch capabilities.
全球或区域经济和地缘政治状况的不利变化可能减少对我们某些产品和服务的需求，从而对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生负面影响。经济下行、通货膨胀、更高利率、更紧信贷条件、消费者支出减少、企业或政府投资减少或地缘政治发展，可能对我们产品的需求产生负面影响。消费者或企业对我们的 Starlink 连接服务或 AI 相关产品的支出减少将限制我们扩张业务的能力，可能减缓我们部署卫星和扩展星座的步伐，或对我们发射能力的利用产生不利影响。
Manufacturing, testing and launching rockets, satellites, and spacecraft, including our efforts to reuse rockets and spacecraft, involve inherent risks that could result in human injury or death, property damage and environmental damage or other adverse environmental impacts due to accidents or equipment failures. Any such events could result in substantial losses, including reputational harm and legal liability, which could have a material adverse effect on our business.
制造、测试和发射火箭、卫星和航天器（包括我们重复使用火箭和航天器的努力）涉及固有风险，可能因事故或设备故障导致人身伤害或死亡、财产损失和环境损害或其他不利环境影响。任何此类事件都可能导致重大损失（包括声誉损害和法律责任），并可能对我们的业务产生重大不利影响。
The manufacturing, testing, launching, and recovery of our rockets, satellites, and spacecraft are complex activities that are conducted under challenging conditions and involve a high degree of risk. Our reusable vehicles will reenter Earth's atmosphere and fly over populated land for extended periods, which carries inherent risks to populations in the event of failure, such as structural breakup, loss of control, or debris dispersal. Although we implement extensive safety protocols and operational safeguards designed to protect personnel and the public, these protocols and safeguards may not in all circumstances prevent exposure of our personnel and potentially members of the public to hazards such as explosions, structural failures or debris dispersal. A manufacturing defect, testing anomaly, launch failure, recovery incident, or similar event involving injury to humans, any human fatalities, property damage, or environmental damage or other adverse environmental impacts could result in substantial losses, including reputational harm and legal liability, which could have a material adverse effect on our business.
我们火箭、卫星和航天器的制造、测试、发射和回收是在具有挑战性的条件下进行的复杂活动，涉及高度风险。我们的可回收飞行器将再入地球大气层并长时间飞越居住陆地，在发生失败（如结构性解体、失控或碎片散布）时对人群构成固有风险。尽管我们实施旨在保护人员和公众的广泛安全协议和运营防护措施，但这些协议和防护措施在所有情况下可能无法防止人员和潜在公众成员受到爆炸、结构失效或碎片散布等危害。涉及人身伤害、人员死亡、财产损失或环境损害或其他不利环境影响的制造缺陷、测试异常、发射失败、回收事故或类似事件可能导致重大损失（包括声誉损害和法律责任），并可能对我们的业务产生重大不利影响。
Although we are focused on the vertical integration of our businesses, we depend on third parties to manufacture and supply certain key components necessary for the provision of our launch, connectivity, and AI services, and any supply shortages or disruptions or failures in their performance could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
尽管我们专注于业务的垂直整合，但我们依赖第三方制造和供应提供发射、连接和 AI 服务所需的某些关键部件，任何供应短缺或中断或其性能失败都可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
Disruptions in the supply chain for essential raw materials or components, challenges in the supplier qualification process, or increases in the prices of inputs could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. Despite our supply chain being largely vertically integrated, our reliance on third-party manufacturers and suppliers for key components introduces risks related to supply chain continuity, quality assurance, and vendor performance. We depend on both domestic and international suppliers for certain specialized materials, components, and services that are essential to the production and operation of our launch vehicles, spacecraft, satellites, user terminals (including Starlink consumer terminals), AI segment and related infrastructure. Any failure or delay by these partners to deliver components in the required quantities, within specifications, or on schedule has in the past and may in the future adversely affect our production schedules, operational reliability, and our ability to meet contractual obligations. In addition, disruptions in the supply chain due to shortages, quality issues, natural disasters, geopolitical events, labor disputes, pandemics, epidemics, tariffs or trade restrictions, criminal activity (including terrorism, sabotage or cyberattacks) or other factors outside our control could result in significant delays, increased costs, or an inability to deliver products and services to customers in a timely and cost-effective manner. The process of qualifying new suppliers or transitioning to alternative vendors can be time-consuming and may not be successful, further increasing our exposure to supply chain interruptions. Furthermore, our limited pool of qualified vendors for certain critical products or services exposes us to increased pricing pressures and quality risks. In particular, certain materials and products that are key inputs in our Space, Connectivity, and AI segments are available from a limited number of suppliers, including sole or limited-source suppliers, and our direct chip suppliers are dependent on a concentrated group of advanced semiconductor fabrication facilities. For additional information regarding supply chain risk relating to our AI processors, please see "Our ability to scale our AI products relies on our terrestrial and orbital AI compute infrastructure, which depends on the availability of power, AI processors, and other critical components, telecommunications services, and any shortages or disruptions thereof would materially adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects." The inability of these suppliers to deliver necessary components of the products in a timely manner and at prices, quality levels, and volumes acceptable to us, or interruptions in supply of materials or products on which these suppliers rely, could have an adverse effect on our ability to meet customer demands and contractual obligations, execute on our growth strategy, or manage our expenses or timelines as expected, which could adversely impact our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
基本原材料或部件供应链的中断、供应商资格审查过程中的挑战或投入品价格上涨可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。尽管我们的供应链在很大程度上垂直整合，但我们对关键部件依赖第三方制造商和供应商引入了与供应链连续性、质量保证和供应商绩效相关的风险。我们依赖国内外供应商提供对运载火箭、航天器、卫星、用户终端（包括 Starlink 消费者终端）、AI 业务部门及相关基础设施生产和运营至关重要的某些专用材料、组件和服务。这些合作伙伴未能或延迟按规格、按计划交付所需数量的部件，过去曾经（未来可能）对我们的生产计划、运营可靠性和履行合同义务的能力产生不利影响。此外，由于短缺、质量问题、自然灾害、地缘政治事件、劳动争议、流行病、传染病、关税或贸易限制、犯罪活动（包括恐怖主义、破坏或网络攻击）或我们控制之外的其他因素导致的供应链中断，可能导致重大延迟、成本增加，或无法及时和经济地向客户交付产品和服务。对新供应商进行资格审查或转向替代供应商的过程可能耗时，且可能不成功，进一步增加我们对供应链中断的暴露。此外，我们某些关键产品或服务合格供应商有限，使我们面临更大的定价压力和质量风险。特别是，我们 Space、Connectivity 和 AI 业务部门关键投入的某些材料和产品由有限的供应商提供，包括独家或有限来源供应商，我们的直接芯片供应商依赖于一组集中的先进半导体制造设施。关于 AI 处理器的供应链风险的额外信息，请参阅"我们扩展 AI 产品的能力依赖于陆地和轨道 AI 算力基础设施，这取决于电力、AI 处理器和其他关键部件、电信服务的可用性，任何此类短缺或中断都将对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。"这些供应商如不能及时、以可接受的价格、质量水平和数量交付产品的必要部件，或这些供应商所依赖的材料或产品供应中断，可能对我们满足客户需求和合同义务、执行增长战略或按预期管理费用或时间的能力产生不利影响，从而对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
Our ability to scale our AI products relies on our terrestrial and orbital AI compute infrastructure, which depends on the availability of power, AI processors, and other critical components, telecommunications services, and any shortages or disruptions thereof would materially adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们扩展 AI 产品的能力依赖于陆地和轨道 AI 算力基础设施，这取决于电力、AI 处理器和其他关键部件、电信服务的可用性，任何此类短缺或中断都将对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
Our ability to scale our data center infrastructure, which supports our AI segment, is increasingly constrained by the availability of power at economically feasible prices, long lead times, availability of materials, and changing regulatory requirements. For example, energy supply is constrained globally due to the significant increase in demand for, and limited availability of, energy to power AI compute. Securing this capacity can involve entering into complex, long-lead-time arrangements or proceeding with alternative sources of power generation. We currently rely significantly on natural gas and gas turbine technology to power our data center operations. As such, our ability to scale our infrastructure depends in part on our continued access to natural gas supply at economically feasible prices, the availability of gas turbines and related equipment, and the maintenance of a regulatory environment that permits and supports the use of natural gas for large-scale power generation. Our AI products also rely on GPUs and other processors, servers, network equipment and other critical components sourced from third- party suppliers for use in our data centers. Manufacturing and supply of servers and network equipment for our technical infrastructure, particularly for GPUs and other specialized components, is limited to a small number of qualified suppliers. We do not have any long-term or other material contractual arrangements with our direct chip suppliers, instead procuring all of our GPUs on a purchase-order basis. Our direct chip suppliers are dependent on a concentrated group of advanced semiconductor fabrication facilities, or "fabs." Any disruption to our upstream supply chain, including fab capacity constraints, manufacturing issues, shortages of raw materials such as silicon wafers or rare earth elements, geopolitical tensions affecting fab operations, or natural disasters impacting key fabrication regions, could limit our chip suppliers' ability to fulfill our orders, which could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, and results of operations. Our ability to achieve orbital AI at scale depends on our ability to access a sufficient number of AI chips, significantly more than are currently available to us. While we expect to construct Terafab to address such supply constraints, Terafab may not be successful, in which case we may not have other sources of sufficient AI chips to meet our orbital AI compute demands. While Terafab is intended to expand our internal chip manufacturing capabilities and alleviate potential future AI chip shortages at SpaceX, particularly as we pursue orbital AI at scale, we expect to continue sourcing a significant portion of our compute hardware from third-party suppliers, and there can be no assurance that we will be able to achieve our objectives with respect to Terafab within the expected timeframes, or at all. While we have a framework agreement with Tesla, neither Tesla nor Intel are obligated to remain a part of the project, and we may not enter into any such definitive agreements. Our AI segment also relies on services from third-party telecommunications providers, including connectivity to the cloud, and internet bandwidth suppliers to provide uninterrupted and error- free services through their networks. We may be unable to obtain AI processors or other necessary components or telecommunications services at prices or volumes that are acceptable to us or in a timely manner. Our suppliers and telecommunications and internet service providers also serve other customers, including certain of our competitors, and such suppliers or providers may prioritize capacity for such other customers, increase prices on short notice, require onerous prepayments, or reduce or delay deliveries to us. Any failure by our suppliers and service providers to meet our cost, quality, volume, or delivery requirements, or any shortage or disruption in the supply of chips, telecommunications services or other components required for our AI segment, could result in service disruption or outages, delay critical data center or network infrastructure upgrades or expansions, impair our ability to train our AI models and meet customer demand for our AI segment products and materially adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations and future prospects.
我们扩展支持 AI 业务部门的数据中心基础设施的能力，日益受到经济上可行价格的电力可用性、长交货期、材料可用性以及不断变化的监管要求的制约。例如，由于 AI 算力对能源的需求大幅增加而能源可用性有限，全球能源供应受到制约。确保这一容量可能涉及签订复杂的、长交货期的安排或推进替代电源。我们目前在很大程度上依赖天然气和燃气轮机技术为数据中心运营供电。因此，我们扩展基础设施的能力部分取决于以经济上可行的价格持续获取天然气供应、燃气轮机及相关设备的可用性，以及维持允许并支持使用天然气进行大规模发电的监管环境。我们的 AI 产品也依赖于从第三方供应商采购的 GPU 和其他处理器、服务器、网络设备和其他关键部件供数据中心使用。我们技术基础设施所需的服务器和网络设备的制造和供应，特别是 GPU 和其他专用部件，仅限于少数合格供应商。我们与直接芯片供应商没有任何长期或其他重要的合同安排，而是基于采购订单基础采购所有 GPU。我们的直接芯片供应商依赖于一组集中的先进半导体制造设施，即 'fabs'。我们上游供应链的任何中断（包括 fab 产能限制、制造问题、硅晶圆或稀土元素等原材料短缺、影响 fab 运营的地缘政治紧张局势或影响关键制造区域的自然灾害）都可能限制我们的芯片供应商履行订单的能力，从而对我们的业务、财务状况和经营成果产生重大不利影响。我们实现规模化轨道 AI 的能力依赖于获取足够数量的 AI 芯片，远多于我们目前可用的数量。虽然我们预计通过建设 Terafab 解决此类供应限制，但 Terafab 可能不成功，在这种情况下我们可能没有其他足够的 AI 芯片来源来满足我们的轨道 AI 算力需求。虽然 Terafab 旨在扩大我们的内部芯片制造能力并缓解 SpaceX 未来潜在的 AI 芯片短缺（特别是在我们追求规模化轨道 AI 时），我们预计将继续从第三方供应商采购大量算力硬件，且无法保证我们能在预期时间内（甚至完全）实现 Terafab 的目标。虽然我们与 Tesla 签订了框架协议，但 Tesla 和 Intel 都没有义务继续参与该项目，我们也可能不会签订任何此类最终协议。我们的 AI 业务部门还依赖第三方电信提供商的服务，包括与云的连接和互联网带宽供应商，以通过其网络提供不间断和无错误的服务。我们可能无法以可接受的价格或数量、或以及时方式获得 AI 处理器或其他必要部件或电信服务。我们的供应商和电信及互联网服务提供商也为其他客户（包括我们的某些竞争对手）提供服务，此类供应商或提供商可能优先考虑为其他客户预留容量、在短时间内提高价格、要求繁重的预付款，或减少或延迟向我们的交付。我们的供应商和服务提供商未能满足我们的成本、质量、数量或交付要求，或我们的 AI 业务部门所需的芯片、电信服务或其他部件的任何短缺或供应中断，都可能导致服务中断或宕机，延迟关键数据中心或网络基础设施的升级或扩展，损害我们训练 AI 模型和满足客户对 AI 业务部门产品需求的能力，并对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
We also rely on third-party cloud compute providers for a portion of the compute used for the X platform and may from time to time rely on third-party data center providers, which exposes us to several risks that are beyond our direct control, including vulnerability to outages, performance issues, and cyberattacks. We have non-cancellable, multi-year capacity commitments to cloud compute providers, requiring payment regardless of usage. A termination or lapse in service from third-party cloud compute and data center providers could expose us to service interruptions, significant delays, and additional expenses to re-architect products for a different provider. Additionally, in the event of nonperformance by us or our providers, or an industry downturn, we may incur liabilities, have excess capacity that we cannot easily redeploy, and fail to receive payments from our counterparties or customers.
我们还依赖第三方云算力提供商提供 X 平台所用的部分算力，并可能不时依赖第三方数据中心提供商，这使我们面临若干超出我们直接控制的风险，包括对中断、性能问题和网络攻击的脆弱性。我们对云算力提供商有不可取消的多年容量承诺，无论使用与否都需付款。第三方云算力和数据中心提供商的服务终止或中断可能使我们面临服务中断、重大延迟以及为不同提供商重新架构产品的额外开支。此外，如果我们或我们的提供商不履约或行业下行，我们可能产生负债，拥有无法轻易重新部署的过剩容量，并未能从交易对手或客户处收到付款。
We face intense competition in the markets in which we operate, and while we have historically outperformed certain competitors in our Space and Connectivity segments, we may not continue to do so, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们在运营市场面临激烈竞争，虽然我们在 Space 和 Connectivity 业务部门历史上超越某些竞争对手，但未来可能无法继续如此，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
The markets in which we operate are rapidly evolving and intensely competitive, and we face competition from a range of established and emerging companies, including large, well-capitalized technology companies and aerospace firms, including foreign competitors. Some competitors are investing significant capital to develop and deploy satellite constellations and related infrastructure that compete directly with our offerings, and companies based in China and other jurisdictions may benefit from government support, favorable regulatory environments, or strategic national prioritization.
我们运营的市场快速演变且竞争激烈，我们面临来自一系列成熟和新兴公司的竞争，包括资本充足的大型科技公司和航空航天公司，其中包括外国竞争对手。一些竞争对手正在投资大量资本来开发和部署与我们产品直接竞争的卫星星座和相关基础设施，总部位于中国和其他司法管辖区的公司可能受益于政府支持、有利的监管环境或战略国家优先权。
Some of our current and potential competitors, particularly in our AI segment, have greater financial, technical, manufacturing, or other resources than we do, and may devote more resources to the development and commercialization of competing products and services. Competitors may adopt more aggressive pricing, secure more favorable supplier or distribution arrangements, bundle services, form strategic alliances or otherwise take actions that enhance their competitive position in ways that could adversely affect our business. In certain markets, regulatory or geopolitical factors may result in preferential treatment for domestic competitors or otherwise limit our ability to compete effectively.
我们当前和潜在的某些竞争对手，特别是在 AI 业务部门，拥有比我们更多的财务、技术、制造或其他资源，可能将更多资源投入到竞争产品和服务的开发与商业化上。竞争对手可能采取更激进的定价、获得更有利的供应商或分销安排、捆绑服务、组建战略联盟或采取其他可能不利影响我们业务的方式来增强其竞争地位的行动。在某些市场，监管或地缘政治因素可能导致国内竞争对手获得优惠待遇，或以其他方式限制我们有效竞争的能力。
Competition continues to intensify as new technologies are developed and new entrants emerge. While we have historically outperformed certain competitors in aspects of our business, such as our Space and Connectivity segments, there can be no assurance that we will maintain this position.
随着新技术的发展和新进入者的出现，竞争持续加剧。虽然我们在业务的某些方面（如 Space 和 Connectivity 业务部门）历史上超越了某些竞争对手，但无法保证我们将维持这一地位。
We depend on our ability to recruit and retain employees who have advanced engineering and technical skills, and intense competition for such employees may increase costs and affect our ability to meet development and production timelines.
我们依赖招募和留住具备先进工程和技术技能的员工的能力，对此类员工的激烈竞争可能增加成本并影响我们达到研发和生产时间表的能力。
We depend on our ability to recruit and retain employees who have advanced engineering and technical skills and, in some cases, employees with the necessary national security clearances to perform under our government contracts or win new business. These employees are in great demand and are likely to remain a limited resource in the foreseeable future. The current tight labor market has adversely impacted our ability to recruit qualified personnel, including engineers, particularly with respect to our AI segment. Increased restrictions on the import or retention of foreign labor may also increase demand for engineering personnel and adversely impact our ability to hire and retain qualified personnel. Continued turnover may impact employee morale and create other challenges as we attempt to scale our AI business. In addition, significant amounts of time and resources are required to train technical and other personnel, and we have in the past lost and may in the future lose new employees to our competitors or other companies before we realize the benefit of our investment in recruiting and training them. Our ability to recruit and retain qualified employees depends on a number of things, including our ability to pay market compensation, provide opportunities for advancement, and secure visa sponsorships and work permits for qualified international candidates. If we are unable to recruit and retain a sufficient number of these employees, then our ability to maintain our competitiveness and grow our business could be negatively affected. In addition, because of the highly technical nature of our products and services, the loss of any significant number of our existing engineering personnel could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. A significant portion of the talent pool for advanced engineering and technical roles is international, and changes in immigration laws or policies in the jurisdictions in which we operate could limit our ability to hire and retain such candidates and intensify competition for talent.
我们依赖招募和留住具备先进工程和技术技能的员工的能力，在某些情况下，员工还须持有必要的国家安全许可，以履行政府合同或赢得新业务。这些员工需求很大，在可预见的未来可能仍是有限资源。当前紧张的劳动力市场已经对我们招募合格人员（包括工程师，特别是在 AI 业务部门）的能力产生不利影响。对外国劳动力进口或留用的限制增加也可能增加对工程人员的需求，并对我们雇用和留住合格人员的能力产生不利影响。持续的人员流动可能影响员工士气，并在我们尝试扩展 AI 业务时造成其他挑战。此外，培训技术和其他人员需要大量时间和资源，过去我们曾在实现招募和培训投资收益之前就失去新员工到竞争对手或其他公司，未来也可能如此。我们招募和留住合格员工的能力取决于多个因素，包括我们支付市场薪酬、提供晋升机会、为合格国际候选人获得签证赞助和工作许可的能力。如果我们无法招募和留住足够数量的此类员工，我们维持竞争力和发展业务的能力可能受到负面影响。此外，由于我们产品和服务的高技术性，失去任何大量现有工程人员都可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。先进工程和技术职位的人才库中很大部分是国际人才，我们运营所在司法管辖区的移民法律或政策的变化可能限制我们雇用和留住此类候选人的能力，并加剧人才竞争。
From time to time, we are involved in litigation, investigations, and other regulatory proceedings which could be costly, time-consuming, and divert management attention, materially adversely affecting our business.
我们不时卷入诉讼、调查和其他监管程序，这些可能成本高昂、耗时并分散管理层精力，对我们的业务产生重大不利影响。
From time to time, we have been and may in the future become involved in various legal proceedings relating to a variety of matters, including intellectual property, commercial, regulatory, product liability, employment, personal injury, class action, employee or contractor health and safety, environmental, whistleblower, securities and other litigation and claims, and governmental and other regulatory investigations and proceedings, including tax examinations. Additionally, our share price may be volatile and, in the past, companies that have experienced volatility in the market price of their stock have been subject to securities litigation, including class action litigation.
我们不时曾经并可能在未来卷入与各种事项相关的法律程序，包括知识产权、商业、监管、产品责任、雇佣、人身伤害、集体诉讼、员工或承包商健康与安全、环境、举报人、证券和其他诉讼与索赔，以及政府和其他监管调查与程序（包括税务审查）。此外，我们的股价可能波动，过去经历过股票市场价格波动的公司曾受到证券诉讼（包括集体诉讼）影响。
Such matters could be costly, time-consuming, and divert management's attention from executing our strategic initiatives and operating our business. The industries in which we operate have historically experienced significant litigation and regulatory scrutiny, and with our public profile, expanding operations and the novel nature of some of our offerings, including our AI solutions, we may face an increased risk of such actions. Litigation and regulatory proceedings are inherently unpredictable. Any adverse judgments, settlements, or regulatory penalties could result in substantial financial costs, reputational harm, and operational disruptions. Certain of our hardware products are new and relatively unproven. If a product defect were to arise, especially one leading to product liability claims, the resulting warranty and damage claims, together with any associated harm to our reputation, could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. Even if we prevail in these matters, the defense and resolution of litigation and regulatory proceedings may require significant resources and management attention, which could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. Additionally, the mere initiation of litigation or government inquiries, regardless of the outcome, could negatively impact investor confidence and our stock price. As we continue to innovate and pursue new commercial and government contracts, expand our product offerings, and enter new markets, the likelihood of facing legal and regulatory challenges may increase, further exposing us to these risks. Please refer to "Business--Legal Proceedings" and Note 17, Commitments and Contingencies, in our audited consolidated financial statements and Note 16, Commitments and Contingencies in our unaudited consolidated financial statements include elsewhere in this prospectus.
此类事项可能成本高昂、耗时，并将管理层精力从执行战略举措和经营业务转移。我们运营的行业历史上经历了大量诉讼和监管审查，鉴于我们的公众形象、不断扩展的运营和某些产品（包括 AI 解决方案）的新颖性，我们可能面临更高的此类诉讼风险。诉讼和监管程序本质上不可预测。任何不利判决、和解或监管处罚可能导致重大财务成本、声誉损害和运营中断。我们的某些硬件产品是新的且相对未经验证。如果出现产品缺陷（尤其是导致产品责任索赔的缺陷），由此产生的保修和损害索赔以及对我们声誉的相关损害，可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。即使我们在这些事项中胜诉，诉讼和监管程序的辩护和解决可能需要大量资源和管理层精力，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。此外，无论结果如何，仅诉讼或政府询问的启动都可能对投资者信心和我们的股价产生负面影响。随着我们继续创新并追求新的商业和政府合同、扩展产品供应并进入新市场，面临法律和监管挑战的可能性可能增加，进一步使我们暴露于这些风险。请参阅本招股说明书其他部分包含的"业务——法律程序"和经审计合并财务报表中的附注 17"承诺和或有事项"以及未经审计合并财务报表中的附注 16"承诺和或有事项"。
Any significant disruption in, or unauthorized access to, our computer and data systems or those of third parties that we utilize in our operations could result in a loss or degradation of service, loss of trust in us and harm to our business.
我们计算机和数据系统或我们运营中使用的第三方系统的任何重大中断或未经授权的访问，都可能导致服务损失或退化、对我们信任的损失以及对我们业务的损害。
An operational disruption in, or unauthorized access to, our computer and data systems or those of third parties that we utilize in our operations could compromise sensitive (including classified or otherwise government-controlled), proprietary, confidential, or personal information, impede operations, and result in financial losses, legal liabilities, reputational harm, and erosion of our competitive position in launch services, space-based internet, and mobile phone services. Our business depends on the continuous and secure operation of our information technology systems and infrastructure, including those that support our launch operations, manufacturing facilities, Starlink services, government services, employee databases, and mission-critical communications. Our systems and infrastructure may also be subject to cyberattacks, including sophisticated hacking attempts by nation-states, state-sponsored actors, cybercriminals, or other malicious third parties, which could result in unauthorized access to, disruption of, or degradation of our satellite systems, ground infrastructure, or data networks. Such disruptions or unauthorized access, which may result from a wide variety of incidents or activities, including inadvertent compromises arising from process, coding or human errors, cyberattacks, data breaches, exploitation of known or unknown software or hardware vulnerabilities, malware, ransomware, credential harvesting, computer viruses, social engineering (such as phishing), denial of service attacks, software or hardware failure, or other malicious or disruptive incidents or activities--whether perpetrated by external actors, including nation-states, state-sponsored organizations, or cybercriminal groups, insiders, or other threat actors, any of whom may see their efforts enhanced by the use of AI --could lead to the theft, destruction, or unauthorized disclosure of sensitive (including classified or otherwise government-controlled), proprietary, confidential or personal information, including technical data, customer or partner information, and intellectual property, particularly because some of our products and services involve the collection, storage, and processing of such data and information. Our development and deployment of AI models, internal and third-party AI tools, and other AI applications expose us to increased and novel risks and vulnerabilities, including prompt injection, hallucinations, errors, and other issues related to AI agents, as well as the risk of compromise of valuable intellectual property including source code, model weights, and other assets. Certain internal and external threat actors, such as nation-states, state-sponsored organizations, organized threat networks and corporate espionage actors, among others have and will continue to sustain malicious activities for extended periods and deploy significant resources to attempt, and in some cases succeed, at causing significant disruptions in, or unauthorized access to, our computer systems or those of third parties that we utilize in our operations. Such incidents have in the past and may in the future also disrupt or degrade our ability to design, produce, launch, or manage our products and services, resulting in operational delays, violations of applicable data privacy and cybersecurity laws and regulations, disruptions in, or unauthorized access to, our customers' computer systems, increased costs, loss of revenue, loss of trust, litigation or regulatory penalties.
我们计算机和数据系统或我们运营中使用的第三方系统的运营中断或未经授权的访问可能危及敏感（包括机密或其他政府控制的）、专有、保密或个人信息，妨碍运营，并导致财务损失、法律责任、声誉损害，以及侵蚀我们在发射服务、太空互联网和移动电话服务方面的竞争地位。我们的业务依赖于我们信息技术系统和基础设施的持续安全运行，包括支持发射运营、制造设施、Starlink 服务、政府服务、员工数据库和关键任务通信的系统和基础设施。我们的系统和基础设施也可能受到网络攻击，包括来自国家、国家支持行为者、网络犯罪分子或其他恶意第三方的复杂黑客攻击，这可能导致对我们卫星系统、地面基础设施或数据网络的未经授权访问、中断或退化。此类中断或未经授权的访问可能由各种事件或活动造成，包括由流程、编码或人为错误、网络攻击、数据泄露、已知或未知软件或硬件漏洞利用、恶意软件、勒索软件、凭证收集、计算机病毒、社会工程（如钓鱼）、拒绝服务攻击、软件或硬件故障或其他恶意或破坏性事件或活动造成的无意泄露——无论是由外部行为者（包括国家、国家支持组织或网络犯罪团伙）、内部人员或其他威胁行为者实施，所有这些都可能利用 AI 增强其效果——可能导致敏感（包括机密或其他政府控制的）、专有、保密或个人信息（包括技术数据、客户或合作伙伴信息和知识产权）被盗、销毁或未经授权披露，特别是因为我们的某些产品和服务涉及收集、存储和处理此类数据和信息。我们对 AI 模型、内部和第三方 AI 工具及其他 AI 应用的研发和部署使我们面临增加的新型风险和漏洞，包括 prompt 注入、幻觉、错误和与 AI agent 相关的其他问题，以及包括源代码、模型权重和其他资产在内的有价值知识产权被泄露的风险。某些内部和外部威胁行为者，如国家、国家支持组织、有组织的威胁网络和企业间谍行为者等，过去曾持续并将继续长期开展恶意活动并部署大量资源尝试（在某些情况下成功）对我们的计算机系统或我们运营中使用的第三方系统造成重大中断或未经授权的访问。此类事件过去曾经（未来可能）扰乱或降低我们设计、生产、发射或管理产品和服务的能力，导致运营延迟、违反适用的数据隐私和网络安全法律法规、客户计算机系统的中断或未经授权访问、成本增加、收入损失、信任损失、诉讼或监管处罚。
As the scale, frequency, sophistication, or intensity of cyber and data privacy threats continue to evolve, and as our reliance on interconnected systems and third-party vendors grows, we remain exposed to vulnerabilities despite our efforts to implement security measures, monitoring, and incident response protocols. There can be no assurance that our cybersecurity risk management processes, including our policies, procedures, and controls, will be effective in promptly or effectively detecting, containing, or remediating cybersecurity attacks. Any significant security and data breach or system failure could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects, and could result in loss of trust among customers, regulators, government agencies, and partners. Furthermore, our efforts to investigate, mitigate, contain, and remediate the harm caused by a significant disruption in, or unauthorized access to, our computer and data systems or those of third parties that we utilize in our operations may be costly and time-consuming and may not be successful, and we may make errors or fail to take necessary actions. Remediation efforts, litigation, regulatory investigations, and compliance obligations (including obligations to notify appropriate regulators and affected parties) arising from such incidents could require substantial management attention and resources, and we rely on our own funds to cover such losses or liabilities. In addition, rapid changes to U.S. and international cybersecurity and privacy laws and regulations have expanded regulatory regimes and compliance requirements, and regulators continue to undertake enforcement actions in these areas. We expect the regulatory environment to grow more complicated, which may increase our operational and compliance expenditures, as well as those of our suppliers. Moreover, some third parties we utilize in our operations may receive or store information provided by us or by our customers. If these third parties fail to adopt or adhere to adequate data privacy and security practices, or their systems or networks are breached in the manner described above, our data or our customers' data may be improperly accessed, used, or disclosed to unauthorized recipients, which could result in financial losses, legal liabilities, reputational harm, and additional compliance obligations. We do not control the privacy and cybersecurity measures put in place by such third parties, and any contractual protections with such third parties, such as obligations to indemnify us, if any, may be ineffective or otherwise inadequate.
随着网络和数据隐私威胁的规模、频率、复杂性或强度持续演变，以及我们对互联系统和第三方供应商的依赖增加，尽管我们努力实施安全措施、监控和事件响应协议，但我们仍面临漏洞。无法保证我们的网络安全风险管理流程（包括我们的政策、程序和控制措施）将有效及时或有效地检测、遏制或修复网络安全攻击。任何重大安全和数据泄露或系统故障都可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响，并可能导致客户、监管机构、政府机构和合作伙伴对我们的信任丧失。此外，我们调查、缓解、遏制和修复我们计算机和数据系统或我们运营中使用的第三方系统中重大中断或未经授权访问所造成损害的努力可能成本高昂、耗时并可能不成功，我们可能犯错或未能采取必要行动。由此类事件引起的补救工作、诉讼、监管调查和合规义务（包括通知适当监管机构和受影响方的义务）可能需要大量管理层精力和资源，我们依靠自有资金弥补此类损失或负债。此外，美国和国际网络安全和隐私法律法规的快速变化扩大了监管制度和合规要求，监管机构在这些领域继续采取执法行动。我们预计监管环境将变得更加复杂，这可能增加我们和供应商的运营和合规支出。此外，我们运营中使用的某些第三方可能接收或存储我们或客户提供的信息。如果这些第三方未能采用或遵守充分的数据隐私和安全实践，或其系统或网络以上述方式被入侵，我们的数据或客户数据可能被不当访问、使用或披露给未授权接收者，这可能导致财务损失、法律责任、声誉损害和额外的合规义务。我们不控制此类第三方实施的隐私和网络安全措施，与此类第三方的任何合同保护（如对我们的赔偿义务）（如有）可能无效或不充分。
The development and maintenance of the technologies and infrastructure necessary to support our current and future operations will require significant capital expenditures, and if we are unable to generate sufficient cash flow from operations or obtain additional financing on acceptable terms, our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects could be materially and adversely affected.
支持我们当前和未来运营所需技术和基础设施的研发与维护将需要大量资本支出，如果我们无法从运营中产生足够现金流或以可接受的条件获得额外融资，我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果和未来前景可能受到重大不利影响。
Our business requires substantial capital expenditures to design, develop, expand, and maintain our technologies and infrastructure to support our operations. For example, we have incurred significant capital expenditures and expect to increase our capital expenditures substantially in the future in connection with the design, development, and deployment of our satellite constellations, launch vehicles, ground stations, manufacturing facilities, and programs, including Terafab, AI compute infrastructure, data centers, and other supporting infrastructure. These expenditures include, but are not limited to, costs associated with research and development, construction and expansion of production capabilities, acquisition of property and equipment, and ongoing maintenance and upgrades to ensure reliability and competitiveness. In particular, the development, testing, and deployment of Starship in accordance with our anticipated schedule, as well as our pursuit of orbital AI, other space-related services, and lunar and interplanetary missions, will require the investment of significant additional capital resources. In addition, we have made and intend to continue to make substantial capital expenditures to support the growth of our AI products, including costs related to obtaining third-party GPUs, manufacturing our own GPUs, and constructing, leasing, maintaining, enhancing, and expanding our data centers. We may choose to increase or accelerate the pace of any of these investments at any time, which could result in periods of reduced profitability or increased losses as we prioritize long-term growth over near-term financial performance. Many of the products and services that are important for our growth prospects are novel and untested, and therefore our estimates of capital expenditures may prove to be inaccurate.
我们的业务需要大量资本支出来设计、研发、扩展和维护支持运营的技术和基础设施。例如，我们已经产生大量资本支出，并预计未来将大幅增加资本支出，用于设计、研发和部署我们的卫星星座、运载火箭、地面站、制造设施和项目，包括 Terafab、AI 算力基础设施、数据中心和其他支持性基础设施。这些支出包括但不限于：研发成本、生产能力的建设与扩展、财产和设备的购置以及为确保可靠性和竞争力的持续维护和升级。特别是，按照预期时间表对 Starship 的研发、测试和部署，以及对轨道 AI、其他太空相关服务和月球与星际任务的追求，将需要投入大量额外资本资源。此外，我们已经并打算继续进行大量资本支出来支持 AI 产品的增长，包括与获取第三方 GPU、自制 GPU 以及建设、租赁、维护、增强和扩展数据中心相关的成本。我们可能选择随时增加或加速任何这些投资，这可能导致由于我们优先考虑长期增长而非近期财务表现而出现盈利能力下降或亏损增加的时期。许多对我们增长前景重要的产品和服务是新颖且未经验证的，因此我们的资本支出估算可能不准确。
If we raise additional capital through further issuances of equity or convertible debt securities, our shareholders could suffer significant dilution and any new equity securities we issue could have rights, preferences, and privileges superior to those of holders of our Class A common stock. The agreements governing our indebtedness contain various restrictive covenants and any additional debt financing secured by us in the future could involve restrictive covenants relating to our capital-raising activities and other financial and operational matters, which could limit our operational flexibility and make it more difficult for us to obtain additional capital and to pursue business opportunities. Our ability to access the capital markets or secure other sources of financing may be adversely affected by factors beyond our control, including fluctuations in market conditions, changes in investor sentiment, increases in interest rates, or adverse events affecting the broader industry or economy.
如果我们通过进一步发行股权或可转换债务证券筹集额外资本，我们的股东可能遭受重大稀释，我们发行的任何新股权证券可能具有优于 A 类普通股持有人的权利、优先权和特权。约束我们债务的协议包含各种限制性契约，未来我们获得的任何额外债务融资可能涉及与筹资活动和其他财务及运营事项相关的限制性契约，这可能限制我们的运营灵活性，并使我们更难获得额外资本和追求商业机会。我们进入资本市场或获得其他融资来源的能力可能受到超出我们控制的因素（包括市场状况波动、投资者情绪变化、利率上升或影响更广泛行业或经济的不利事件）的不利影响。
Our substantial level of indebtedness could materially adversely affect our financial condition.
我们大量的债务水平可能对我们的财务状况产生重大不利影响。
We have significant indebtedness that could materially adversely affect our business by increasing our vulnerability to general adverse economic and industry conditions; requiring us to dedicate a substantial portion of our cash flow from operations to payments on our indebtedness, thereby reducing the availability of our cash flow to fund operations, our growth strategy, product development and strategic initiatives; limiting our flexibility in planning for, or reacting to, changes in our business and the industry in which we operate; and exposing us to the risk of increased interest rates as our borrowings are, and may in the future be, at variable interest rates. As of March 31, 2026, we had total principal indebtedness outstanding of $29,132 million. Our substantial indebtedness may also adversely affect our credit ratings or outlook, which may increase our cost of capital, limit our access to financing, and impair our ability to obtain additional financing on acceptable terms, or at all. The occurrence of any one of these events could have a material adverse effect on our business, results of operations, and financial condition, and ability to satisfy our obligations under the agreements governing our indebtedness. If we fail to comply with the terms of our debt agreements, our lenders could declare a default and accelerate our repayment obligations, which could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们拥有大量债务，可能通过以下方式对我们的业务产生重大不利影响：增加我们对一般不利经济和行业状况的脆弱性；要求我们将大量运营现金流用于偿还债务，从而减少为运营、增长战略、产品研发和战略举措提供资金的现金流可用性；限制我们规划或应对业务和我们运营行业变化的灵活性；以及使我们面临利率上升的风险（因为我们的借款是、且未来可能是浮动利率）。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们未偿还本金债务总额为 291.32 亿美元 ($29,132 million)。我们大量的债务也可能对我们的信用评级或展望产生不利影响，这可能增加我们的资本成本、限制我们获得融资的渠道，并损害我们以可接受条件（甚至完全无法）获得额外融资的能力。任何这些事件的发生都可能对我们的业务、经营成果、财务状况以及履行约束我们债务的协议项下义务的能力产生重大不利影响。如果我们未能遵守债务协议的条款，我们的贷款人可能宣布违约并加速偿还义务，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
Our future revenue and operating results depend upon our ability to develop new technologies and respond to changes in customer demands and industry standards in highly competitive markets, and if we are unable to do so, our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects may be materially and adversely affected.
我们未来的收入和经营成果取决于我们在高度竞争市场中研发新技术并响应客户需求和行业标准变化的能力，如果我们无法做到这一点，我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景可能受到重大不利影响。
Our future revenue growth and operating results are highly dependent on our ability to design, develop and successfully commercialize new and innovative technologies, products, and services on a timely and cost-effective basis. The markets in which we operate are characterized by rapid and disruptive technological change, evolving industry standards, the emergence of new and well-funded competitors, frequent new product and service introductions, changing customer demands and regulatory changes. In addition, we may expand into new markets, which may lead to similar or additional challenges that we cannot foresee and may require novel innovations to navigate or overcome. As a result, we may from time to time rapidly adjust, modify or change our strategic priorities, capital allocation, product or service focus or operational initiatives across our business in response to these other changes or new markets. In particular, the AI industry is nascent, highly competitive, capital intensive and rapidly changing. There are a number of companies today that develop or may develop products or services that compete with our AI segment, and new competitors may emerge over time. Some of our current or potential competitors in the AI market are large technology companies that have significant financial, technical and marketing resources, and in some cases greater access to data, and others are smaller specialized companies that possess specialized expertise and may have greater flexibility than we do. We also have a limited number of customers for our AI products when compared to certain of our competitors. Current and potential competitors have established, or may in the future establish, cooperative relationships among themselves or with third parties to increase the ability of their AI technologies to address the needs of current and prospective users of our AI products. Furthermore, current or prospective users may decide to develop competing products for particular use cases or to establish strategic relationships with our competitors for such use cases. Current and potential competitors and bad actors, may also attempt to reverse engineer or otherwise replicate our AI technology, including through model extraction or distillation techniques. Increased competition with our AI products could result in price reductions, revenue shortfalls, loss of customers and loss of market share, which may harm our business, financial condition results of operations and future prospects.
我们未来的收入增长和经营成果高度依赖于及时、经济地设计、研发和成功商业化新颖创新技术、产品和服务的能力。我们运营的市场以快速颠覆性的技术变革、不断演变的行业标准、新进入者出现（资金充足）、频繁推出新产品和服务、不断变化的客户需求和监管变化为特征。此外，我们可能扩展到新市场，这可能带来我们无法预见的类似或额外挑战，可能需要新颖的创新来应对或克服。因此，我们可能不时迅速调整、修改或改变战略优先事项、资本配置、产品或服务重点或业务部门的运营举措，以响应这些其他变化或新市场。特别是，AI 行业处于初期阶段、竞争激烈、资本密集且快速变化。当今有许多公司开发或可能开发与我们 AI 业务部门竞争的产品或服务，新竞争对手可能随着时间出现。AI 市场中我们当前或潜在的某些竞争对手是大型科技公司，拥有显著的财务、技术和营销资源，在某些情况下拥有更大的数据访问权，其他是较小的专业公司，拥有专业知识，可能比我们更具灵活性。与某些竞争对手相比，我们 AI 产品的客户数量也有限。当前和潜在竞争对手已经或将来可能建立彼此之间或与第三方的合作关系，以增强其 AI 技术满足我们 AI 产品当前和潜在用户需求的能力。此外，当前或潜在用户可能决定为特定用例开发竞争产品，或为此类用例与我们的竞争对手建立战略关系。当前和潜在竞争对手以及不良行为者也可能尝试逆向工程或以其他方式复制我们的 AI 技术，包括通过模型提取或蒸馏 (distillation) 技术。我们 AI 产品竞争加剧可能导致价格下降、收入短缺、客户流失和市场份额损失，可能损害我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景。
In our Connectivity segment, including Starlink broadband and Starlink Mobile, we face competition from terrestrial fixed network providers, mobile network operators, and other satellite providers, and our services may be less competitive in certain markets, including dense urban areas where terrestrial fiber and wireless networks may offer higher capacity, lower cost, or more consistent performance. In addition, our Starlink Mobile offering operates in a highly competitive and evolving market, and may be affected by the pace of technological development, spectrum availability, and the success of our partnerships with mobile carriers. In addition, the X platform faces intense competition from social media, messaging and media companies and traditional media outlets, such as television, radio and print, for advertising budgets. Advertisers generally do not have long-term commitments to the X platform and may reduce or discontinue their advertising spending for a variety of reasons outside our control. We are expending resources to improve the X platform and improve its attractiveness to users and advertisers. While we have introduced new user interface enhancements, algorithm updates, and other product features, improvements to the X platform, introducing new products and services on the X platform and other initiatives may be costly and difficult to implement, and we cannot be sure that they will be positively received by users, content creators, or advertisers, or provide positive returns on our investment. Losing users who migrate to other platforms may negatively impact our potential subscription or advertising revenue. Additionally, if users do not continue to contribute content and otherwise engage with the X platform, we are unable to provide users with valuable and timely content, or if content that is considered to be problematic or offensive is made available on the X platform, the size of the X platform's user base and their engagement may decline, leading to a decline in monetizable usage and the loss of potential subscription revenue from such users, and the X platform may experience brand or reputational harm. A decline in users on the X platform, or the volume or quality of their content on the X platform, could also impact the ongoing development of our AI product, which in part utilizes data and user-generated content from the X platform. We plan to publicly launch the Money product on the X platform (the "Money Product");
在我们的 Connectivity 业务部门，包括 Starlink 宽带和 Starlink Mobile，我们面临来自地面固定网络提供商、移动网络运营商和其他卫星提供商的竞争，我们的服务在某些市场（包括地面光纤和无线网络可能提供更高容量、更低成本或更稳定性能的密集城市地区）可能竞争力较弱。此外，我们的 Starlink Mobile 产品在高度竞争和不断演变的市场中运营，可能受技术发展节奏、频谱可用性和我们与移动运营商合作伙伴关系成功的影响。此外，X 平台面临来自社交媒体、消息和媒体公司以及传统媒体（如电视、广播和印刷）的激烈竞争，争夺广告预算。广告主通常对 X 平台没有长期承诺，可能因我们控制之外的各种原因减少或停止广告支出。我们正在投入资源改进 X 平台并提高其对用户和广告主的吸引力。虽然我们已经引入了新的用户界面增强、算法更新和其他产品功能，但对 X 平台的改进、在 X 平台上引入新产品和服务以及其他举措可能成本高昂且难以实施，我们不能确定它们将受到用户、内容创作者或广告主的积极接受，或提供积极的投资回报。失去迁移到其他平台的用户可能对我们的潜在订阅或广告收入产生负面影响。此外，如果用户不继续向 X 平台贡献内容并参与其他互动，我们无法为用户提供有价值和及时的内容，或 X 平台上提供被视为有问题或冒犯性的内容，X 平台用户群体的规模及其参与度可能下降，导致可货币化使用的下降以及来自此类用户的潜在订阅收入损失，X 平台可能遭受品牌或声誉损害。X 平台用户数量减少或其在 X 平台上的内容数量或质量下降，也可能影响我们 AI 产品的持续研发，因为该产品部分利用来自 X 平台的数据和用户生成内容。我们计划在 X 平台上公开推出 Money 产品（'Money Product'）；
however, we are competing against large, established companies with significantly greater resources and market presence than us. If we are unable to anticipate technological trends, respond to technological advancements or changing customer demands, or successfully develop and commercialize new or enhanced offerings, we may be unable to establish or maintain a meaningful market position and our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects could be materially and adversely affected.
然而，我们正在与比我们拥有显著更多资源和市场存在的大型成熟公司竞争。如果我们无法预测技术趋势、响应技术进步或不断变化的客户需求，或成功研发和商业化新的或增强的产品，我们可能无法建立或维持有意义的市场地位，我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景可能受到重大不利影响。
The estimates of future market opportunity and forecasts of market growth, and our ability to capture such markets, included in this prospectus may prove to be inaccurate.
本招股说明书中包含的未来市场机会估算和市场增长预测以及我们获取此类市场的能力可能被证明是不准确的。
Our estimates for the total addressable market for our Space, Connectivity and AI businesses, as well as estimates regarding the growth of AI and its impacts, contained elsewhere in this prospectus are based on a number of internal and third-party estimates. For example, our estimates of market opportunity for our Space, Connectivity and AI businesses rely in part on third-party data and a number of internal assumptions. With respect to our Space segment, these estimates rely in part on estimates published by Novaspace regarding the size of the global market for space- enabled solutions, including spacecraft manufacturing, launch services and related activities. Our connectivity market estimates are based in part on estimates of the number of households, businesses, aircraft and maritime vessels globally derived from third-party sources, together with assumptions regarding ARPU and monthly service revenue derived from third-party industry data and our internal expectations regarding pricing, adoption rates and service penetration across different geographic regions and economic environments. Our AI market estimates are based in part on projections of global data center compute demand from third-party sources, including estimates published by RAND Corporation, together with internal assumptions regarding the portion of global compute capacity that may be utilized for AI workloads and other operational assumptions such as power usage, utilization rates and pricing.
我们对 Space、Connectivity 和 AI 业务总可寻址市场 (total addressable market) 的估算以及关于 AI 增长及其影响的估算（包含在本招股说明书的其他位置）基于若干内部和第三方估算。例如，我们对 Space、Connectivity 和 AI 业务市场机会的估算部分依赖于第三方数据和若干内部假设。关于 Space 业务部门，这些估算部分依赖于 Novaspace 关于全球太空赋能解决方案市场规模的估算，包括航天器制造、发射服务和相关活动。我们对连接市场的估算部分基于来自第三方来源的全球家庭、企业、飞机和海上船舶数量的估算，以及关于 ARPU 和月服务收入的假设（来自第三方行业数据）和我们关于不同地理区域和经济环境中定价、采用率和服务渗透率的内部预期。我们对 AI 市场的估算部分基于来自第三方来源（包括 RAND Corporation 发布的估算）的全球数据中心算力需求预测，以及关于可能用于 AI 工作负载的全球算力容量比例的内部假设以及电力使用、利用率和定价等其他运营假设。
These estimates require us to make numerous assumptions and judgments regarding factors that are inherently uncertain and subject to change, including the pace of technological development, future demand for launch, connectivity and AI services, the rate of adoption of satellite connectivity and AI technologies, the availability and cost of power and computing hardware, the evolution of regulatory frameworks, and broader macroeconomic conditions.
这些估算要求我们对本质上不确定且可能发生变化的因素做出大量假设和判断，包括技术发展速度、未来对发射、连接和 AI 服务的需求、卫星连接和 AI 技术的采用率、电力和计算硬件的可用性和成本、监管框架的演变以及更广泛的宏观经济状况。
While we believe our assumptions and the data underlying our estimates are reasonable, these assumptions and estimates may not be correct and the conditions supporting our assumptions or estimates may change at any time, thereby reducing the predictive accuracy of these underlying factors. As a result, our estimates of the total addressable market for our services, as well as the expected growth rate for the total addressable market for our services, may prove to be inaccurate.
虽然我们认为我们的假设和估算所基于的数据是合理的，但这些假设和估算可能不正确，支持我们假设或估算的条件可能随时发生变化，从而降低这些基础因素的预测准确性。因此，我们对服务总可寻址市场的估算以及对我们服务总可寻址市场预期增长率的估算可能被证明是不准确的。
Many of our initiatives, including those to develop orbital AI compute at scale, manufacture AI chips at scale, establish a lunar economy, develop human augmentation systems, and transport humans and cargo to the Moon and Mars, involve significant technical complexity, unproven technologies, or technologies that do not exist or may require significant advancement, and such initiatives may not achieve commercial viability.
我们的许多举措，包括大规模研发轨道 AI 算力、大规模制造 AI 芯片、建立月球经济、研发人体增强系统以及向月球和火星运输人员和货物，涉及重大技术复杂性、未经验证的技术或不存在或可能需要重大进步的技术，此类举措可能无法实现商业可行性。
Our initiatives to develop orbital AI compute at scale, establish a lunar economy, develop human augmentation systems, and transport humans and cargo to the Moon and Mars are in early stages of conception, design and development and have not yet been proven at commercial scale, or at all, and may ultimately be unsuccessful. In particular, the timeline for these initiatives, and the launch cadence required to achieve them may be difficult or impossible to determine. These efforts require substantial and ongoing investments of financial, technical, and human resources over extended time horizons, including, but not limited to, research and development, testing, infrastructure, regulatory approvals, and mission execution. The technologies, systems, and operational capabilities required for each of these initiatives involve significant technical complexity and are subject to design, engineering, and performance risks, many of which may only become apparent as development and testing progress. Many of these technologies, systems and operational capabilities are novel and untested, and we expect to incur significant capital expenditures over a period of years before our AI products and services and other strategic initiatives, including AI compute infrastructure and in-orbit, lunar, and interplanetary industrialization efforts, become profitable, which may never occur. In addition, in-orbit refueling of Starship is essential to our lunar, Mars, asteroid mining, and other deep space ambitions beyond geostationary Earth orbit. In-orbit refueling is complex, and we have not yet demonstrated or attempted it. We may not be able to develop, commercialize, scale, or successfully implement these or other strategic initiatives on the timelines we currently anticipate, or at all. Furthermore, the viability of orbital AI compute depends in part on the cost advantages of solar energy relative to existing terrestrial energy sources. To the extent that breakthrough developments in terrestrial energy access, such as advances in nuclear energy, significantly reduce energy costs or alleviate infrastructure constraints, the viability of our orbital AI compute infrastructure may be materially diminished. Even if our orbital AI compute infrastructure proves to be commercially viable, a material slowdown in the growth of AI applications and related compute demand could result in existing terrestrial data centers sufficiently meeting such demand, thereby reducing the need for our orbital AI compute infrastructure. As a result, we may be required to devote financial, technical, human or other resources in excess of our current expectations, and there can be no assurance that these investments will generate adequate revenue, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们大规模研发轨道 AI 算力、建立月球经济、研发人体增强系统以及向月球和火星运输人员和货物的举措处于构思、设计和研发的早期阶段，尚未在商业规模上得到验证（甚至完全没有），最终可能不成功。特别是，这些举措的时间表以及实现这些目标所需的发射频次可能难以或不可能确定。这些努力需要在长时间内对财务、技术和人力资源进行大量持续投资，包括但不限于研发、测试、基础设施、监管批准和任务执行。每一项举措所需的技术、系统和运营能力涉及重大技术复杂性，受设计、工程和性能风险的影响，其中许多可能只有在研发和测试进展中才会显现。许多此类技术、系统和运营能力是新颖且未经验证的，我们预计在 AI 产品和服务及其他战略举措（包括 AI 算力基础设施和在轨、月球和星际工业化努力）实现盈利之前，将在多年时间内产生大量资本支出，而这可能永远不会实现。此外，Starship 的在轨加注对我们月球、火星、小行星采矿和地球静止轨道以外的其他深空雄心至关重要。在轨加注很复杂，我们尚未演示或尝试过。我们可能无法按目前预期的时间表（甚至完全无法）研发、商业化、扩展或成功实施这些或其他战略举措。此外，轨道 AI 算力的可行性部分取决于太阳能相对于现有陆地能源的成本优势。如果陆地能源获取出现突破性发展（如核能进展）显著降低能源成本或缓解基础设施限制，我们的轨道 AI 算力基础设施的可行性可能大幅减弱。即使我们的轨道 AI 算力基础设施被证明商业可行，AI 应用和相关算力需求增长的重大放缓可能导致现有陆地数据中心充分满足此类需求，从而减少对我们轨道 AI 算力基础设施的需求。因此，我们可能需要投入超过当前预期的财务、技术、人力或其他资源，无法保证这些投资将产生足够的收入，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生不利影响。
Several of our anticipated market opportunities, including certain AI, orbital, lunar, and interplanetary transportation and industrial activities, are still emerging and evolving or do not currently exist, and such markets may not develop as we expect, or at all.
我们若干预期的市场机会，包括某些 AI、轨道、月球和星际运输和工业活动，仍处于新兴和演变阶段或目前不存在，此类市场可能无法按预期发展（甚至完全不发展）。
A portion of our anticipated market opportunities is associated with industries described in the section entitled "Business--Future Markets." Certain of these industries, such as space tourism, human augmentation, and cargo transport to the Moon, are still emerging. Others, including in-orbit manufacturing, passenger transport to the Moon, an established human presence or gateway hub on the Moon, passenger and cargo transport to Mars, energy production on the Moon or Mars, manufacturing capabilities on the Moon or Mars, and asteroid mining do not exist today. Any estimate we make regarding the size or timing of our anticipated market opportunities is inherently uncertain and necessarily involves significant assumptions about future customer demand, adoption, technological development, regulatory conditions and the emergence of a broader commercial market that does not currently exist.
我们预期市场机会的一部分与"业务——未来市场"一节中描述的行业相关。其中某些行业（如太空旅游、人体增强和向月球运输货物）仍处于新兴阶段。其他行业（包括在轨制造、向月球运送旅客、月球上建立人类存在或门户枢纽、向火星运送旅客和货物、月球或火星上的能源生产、月球或火星上的制造能力以及小行星采矿）今天不存在。我们对预期市场机会规模或时间的任何估算本质上是不确定的，必然涉及对未来客户需求、采用、技术发展、监管条件以及目前不存在的更广泛商业市场的出现的重大假设。
While we believe these industries will develop over time, the manner in which they emerge, including the timing of commercialization, the scale and pace of adoption, and the applicable technical, regulatory, geopolitical and economic frameworks may differ materially from our current expectations. If these industries do not develop, develop on slower timelines, at smaller scales, or under different economic or regulatory conditions than we anticipate, this could require us to modify, delay, or abandon certain of our business plans, or cause such plans not to develop at all, which could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
虽然我们相信这些行业将随时间发展，但它们出现的方式（包括商业化时间、采用规模和速度以及适用的技术、监管、地缘政治和经济框架）可能与我们当前预期存在重大差异。如果这些行业未发展、以比我们预期更慢的时间表、更小的规模或在不同的经济或监管条件下发展，可能要求我们修改、延迟或放弃某些业务计划，或使此类计划完全无法发展，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
The global nature of our business poses risks with respect to unstable, malicious or arbitrary legal regimes and authorities.
我们业务的全球性带来了与不稳定、恶意或任意法律制度和当局相关的风险。
We, particularly through Starlink, maintain global operations. As a result, we may face risks that our operations will be subject to unstable, capricious, or malicious legal regimes and authorities. The increasing militarization of space and the potential development of space-based warfare capabilities may expose our assets and operations to heightened geopolitical and security risks, including the risk that foreign governments or other actors could target our satellites or related infrastructure. Certain foreign governments have publicly discussed the potential use of anti- satellite weapons against the Starlink constellation. These and other actions by foreign governments, whether through military, regulatory or other means, may adversely affect our operations and assets. Even if we attempt to comply with known local laws, our assets (both physical, intangible and financial) may be subject to seizure or other expropriation. There is no guarantee that we will be able to maintain operations in any jurisdiction, and, if our assets or properties are subject to seizure or other expropriation, there can be no assurances that we will be able to recover our assets or properties. Any such legal or other governmental action could have an adverse effect on us. For example, in August 2024, Starlink received an order from Brazil's Supreme Court that froze Starlink's Brazilian financial assets and prevented Starlink from conducting financial transactions in Brazil (the "Brazil Asset Seizure").
我们（特别是通过 Starlink）维持全球运营。因此，我们可能面临运营受不稳定、反复无常或恶意法律制度和当局约束的风险。太空军事化的加剧和潜在的太空战能力发展可能使我们的资产和运营面临更高的地缘政治和安全风险，包括外国政府或其他行为者可能针对我们的卫星或相关基础设施的风险。某些外国政府已公开讨论可能对 Starlink 星座使用反卫星武器。外国政府的这些和其他行动，无论是通过军事、监管还是其他手段，都可能对我们的运营和资产产生不利影响。即使我们试图遵守已知的当地法律，我们的资产（包括有形、无形和财务资产）也可能被没收或以其他方式征收。无法保证我们能在任何司法管辖区维持运营，如果我们的资产或财产被没收或以其他方式征收，无法保证我们能够收回资产或财产。任何此类法律或其他政府行动都可能对我们产生不利影响。例如，2024 年 8 月，Starlink 收到巴西最高法院的命令，冻结了 Starlink 在巴西的金融资产，并阻止 Starlink 在巴西进行金融交易（'巴西资产没收'）。
The action taken by the Brazilian Supreme Court arose out of purported violations of Brazilian law by X, which at the time was not owned by us and was only affiliated with Mr. Musk. It is possible that we may be subject to actions like the Brazil Asset Seizure in the future (whether in Brazil or another country) and, regardless of whether any such action is consistent with local and international law, we may never recover assets seized in any similar action.
巴西最高法院采取的行动源于 X 涉嫌违反巴西法律，而 X 当时不为我们所拥有，只与 Musk 先生相关。我们未来可能在巴西或其他国家受到类似巴西资产没收的行动影响，无论此类行动是否符合当地和国际法律，我们都可能永远无法收回任何类似行动中被没收的资产。
Additionally, actions that we take to minimize the impact of actions such as the Brazil Asset Seizure to our customers, for example, by continuing to provide service without charge or otherwise altering payment processes and methods to permit customers to maintain service, may have a material impact on our financial performance. As evidenced by the Brazil Asset Seizure, we may be subject to adverse actions from governmental actors on the basis of assumptions, facts or events that are not directly related to our operations and instead relate to the actions of our directors, officers, or shareholders or operations of businesses that are affiliated with them.
此外，我们为减轻巴西资产没收等行动对客户影响而采取的行动（例如继续免费提供服务或以其他方式更改付款流程和方法以允许客户维持服务）可能对我们的财务表现产生重大影响。如巴西资产没收所示，我们可能因与我们运营无直接关系而是与我们的董事、高管或股东行动或与他们相关的业务运营相关的假设、事实或事件而受到政府行为者的不利行动。
Our services are subject to risks related to supplying services to the U.S. government.
我们的服务受到向美国政府提供服务相关风险的约束。
Supplying services to the U.S. government subjects us to unique risks, including compliance with complex regulations, vulnerability to changes in government priorities or funding levels, and exposure to contractual disputes or audits. In 2025, approximately one-fifth of our revenue was attributable to agencies within the U.S. federal government. As a contractor to various U.S. government agencies, we are subject to extensive federal procurement regulations, including the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) and Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS), as well as other rules governing cost accounting, cybersecurity, ethics, and national security.
向美国政府提供服务使我们面临独特风险，包括遵守复杂法规、对政府优先事项或资金水平变化的脆弱性以及合同纠纷或审计的暴露。2025 年，我们约五分之一的收入来自美国联邦政府机构。作为美国各政府机构的承包商，我们受广泛的联邦采购法规约束，包括《联邦采购条例》(Federal Acquisition Regulation, FAR) 和《国防联邦采购条例补编》(Defense Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement, DFARS)，以及管辖成本会计、网络安全、道德和国家安全的其他规则。
These regulations impose stringent requirements on our operations, business practices, and reporting, and noncompliance could result in civil or criminal penalties, suspension or debarment from government contracting, or loss of existing or future business. These requirements, although customary in U.S. government contracts, increase our performance and compliance costs. These costs might increase in the future. For those reasons and in order to achieve our orbital compute goals, we may prioritize our own launch payloads over additional U.S. government contracts or third-party customers. This prioritization of launch capacity may limit revenue growth in our Space segment, and impact our relationship with regulators, and could invite litigation from customers or competitors.
