未能遵守设计、实施和维持有效内部控制的要求可能对我们的业务和股价产生重大不利影响。
As a privately held company, we were not required to evaluate our internal control over financial reporting in a manner that meets the standards of publicly traded companies required by Section 404(a) of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act ("Section 404").
作为一家私营公司，我们不需要按符合《萨班斯-奥克斯利法案》(Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Section 404(a)（'Section 404'）所要求的上市公司标准评估财务报告内部控制。
As a public company, we will have significant requirements for enhanced financial reporting and internal controls.
作为上市公司，我们将对增强财务报告和内部控制有重大要求。
The process of designing and implementing effective internal controls is a continuous effort that requires us to anticipate and react to changes in our business and the economic and regulatory environments and to expend significant resources to maintain a system of internal controls that is adequate to satisfy our reporting obligations as a public company. If we are unable to establish or maintain appropriate internal financial reporting controls and procedures, it could cause us to fail to meet our reporting obligations on a timely basis, result in material misstatements in our consolidated financial statements, and harm our results of operations. In addition, we will be required, pursuant to Section 404, to furnish a report by management on, among other things, the effectiveness of our internal control over financial reporting in the second annual report following the completion of this offering.
设计和实施有效内部控制的过程是一项持续努力，要求我们预测并应对业务以及经济和监管环境的变化，并投入大量资源维持足以满足作为上市公司报告义务的内部控制系统。如果我们无法建立或维持适当的内部财务报告控制和程序，可能导致我们未能及时履行报告义务、合并财务报表出现重大错报，并损害我们的经营成果。此外，根据 Section 404，我们将被要求在本次发行完成后的第二份年度报告中提供管理层关于（除其他外）我们财务报告内部控制有效性的报告。
This assessment will need to include disclosure of any material weaknesses identified by our management in our internal control over financial reporting. The rules governing the standards that must be met for our management to assess our internal control over financial reporting are complex and require significant documentation, testing, and possible remediation. Testing and maintaining internal controls may divert our management's attention from other matters that are important to our business. Additionally, our independent registered public accounting firm will be required to attest to the effectiveness of our internal control over financial reporting on an annual basis, beginning with our second annual report.
此评估将需要披露我们管理层在我们财务报告内部控制中识别的任何重大缺陷。管理层评估我们财务报告内部控制必须达到的标准的规则很复杂，需要重大文档、测试和可能的补救。测试和维持内部控制可能将管理层的精力从对业务重要的其他事项中转移。此外，从我们的第二份年度报告开始，我们的独立注册会计师事务所将被要求每年对我们财务报告内部控制的有效性进行鉴证。
We are currently in the process of updating our control processes and automating certain of our procedures and systems in anticipation of becoming a public company, but our internal controls over financial reporting currently do not meet all of the standards contemplated by Section 404 that we will eventually be required to meet. Because we currently do not have comprehensive documentation of our internal controls and have not yet tested our internal controls in accordance with Section 404, we cannot conclude in accordance with Section 404 that we do not have a material weakness in our internal controls or a combination of significant deficiencies that could result in the conclusion that we have a material weakness in our internal controls. In connection with updating our control processes and the implementation of the necessary procedures and practices related to internal control over financial reporting, we have identified deficiencies and may identify deficiencies in the future that we may not be able to remediate in time to meet the deadline imposed by the Sarbanes-Oxley Act for compliance with the requirements of Section 404. In addition, we may encounter problems or delays in completing the remediation of any deficiencies identified by our independent registered public accounting firm in connection with the issuance of their attestation report. Our testing, or the subsequent testing (if required) by our independent registered public accounting firm, may reveal deficiencies in our internal controls over financial reporting that are deemed to be material weaknesses. Any material weaknesses could result in a material misstatement of our annual or quarterly consolidated financial statements or disclosures that may not be prevented or detected.
我们目前正在更新控制流程并自动化某些程序和系统，以期成为上市公司，但我们的财务报告内部控制目前不符合 Section 404 设想的我们最终将被要求达到的所有标准。由于我们目前没有内部控制的全面文档，也尚未根据 Section 404 测试内部控制，我们无法根据 Section 404 得出我们没有内部控制重大缺陷或可能导致结论认为我们有内部控制重大缺陷的重大缺陷组合的结论。在更新控制流程并实施与财务报告内部控制相关的必要程序和实践时，我们已识别了缺陷，未来可能识别我们无法及时补救以满足《萨班斯-奥克斯利法案》关于遵守 Section 404 要求的截止日期的缺陷。此外，我们可能在完成补救我们独立注册会计师事务所就发布鉴证报告而识别的任何缺陷方面遇到问题或延迟。我们的测试或我们独立注册会计师事务所的后续测试（如有要求）可能揭示我们财务报告内部控制中被视为重大缺陷的缺陷。任何重大缺陷可能导致年度或季度合并财务报表或披露的重大错报，可能无法防止或检测。
Our insurance coverage strategy may not be adequate to protect us from all business risks.
我们的保险覆盖策略可能不足以保护我们免受所有业务风险。
We may be subject, in the ordinary course of business, to losses resulting from accidents, acts of God and other claims against us, for which we may have no insurance coverage. As a general matter, we do not maintain as much insurance coverage as many other companies do, and in some cases, we do not maintain any at all, including with respect to our in-orbit satellites, which we currently do not insure and do not expect to insure in the future.
在正常业务过程中，我们可能受到事故、不可抗力 (acts of God) 和其他针对我们的索赔造成的损失影响，而我们可能没有保险覆盖。一般而言，我们维持的保险覆盖不如许多其他公司多，在某些情况下我们根本不维持任何保险，包括我们目前不为在轨卫星投保且预计未来也不投保。
Additionally, the policies that we do have may include significant deductibles or self-insured retentions, policy limitations and exclusions, and we cannot be certain that our insurance coverage will be sufficient to cover all future losses or claims against us. A loss that is uninsured or which exceeds policy limits may require us to pay substantial amounts, which may harm our financial condition and operating results.
此外，我们确实拥有的保单可能包括重大免赔额或自留额、保单限制和除外条款，我们无法确定我们的保险覆盖足以覆盖未来所有损失或针对我们的索赔。未投保或超过保单限额的损失可能要求我们支付大量金额，这可能损害我们的财务状况和经营业绩。
Risks Related to Our Corporate Structure, Ownership of our Class A Common Stock and This Offering Conflicts of interest could arise in the future between us, on the one hand, and Mr. Musk and entities owned by or affiliated with him, on the other hand, concerning among other things, business transactions, potential competitive activities or other business opportunities.
与我们的公司结构、A 类普通股所有权和本次发行相关的风险 — 我们与 Musk 先生及由其拥有或与其相关的实体之间未来可能在商业交易、潜在竞争活动或其他商业机会等方面出现利益冲突。
Conflicts of interest could arise in the future between us, on the one hand, and Mr. Musk and entities owned by or affiliated with him, on the other hand, concerning among other things, business transactions, potential competitive business activities or other opportunities. In the normal course of business, we have engaged in a variety of transactions with some of these companies. Please refer to "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions."
我们与 Musk 先生及由其拥有或与其相关的实体之间未来可能在商业交易、潜在竞争商业活动或其他机会等方面出现利益冲突。在正常业务过程中，我们已与其中一些公司进行了各种交易。请参阅"某些关系和相关人交易"。
In addition, we have previously engaged, are currently engaged, and expect to continue to engage in the future in a number of strategic collaborations with Tesla, including with respect to Macrohard and Terafab. Certain of these projects, including Macrohard and Terafab, are in the very early stages, as a result of which we and Tesla have not finalized a variety of details relating to our collaboration, including, but not limited to, financial terms, intellectual property rights, and the ultimate term of our collaboration. Furthermore, Mr. Musk and other businesses owned by or affiliated with him may now, or in the future, directly or indirectly, compete with us for investment or business opportunities.
此外，我们之前曾经、目前正在、并预计未来将继续与 Tesla 进行多项战略合作，包括关于 Macrohard 和 Terafab 的合作。这些项目中的某些（包括 Macrohard 和 Terafab）处于非常早期阶段，因此我们和 Tesla 尚未最终确定与合作相关的各种细节，包括但不限于财务条款、知识产权和我们合作的最终期限。此外，Musk 先生和由其拥有或与其相关的其他业务现在或未来可能直接或间接地与我们竞争投资或业务机会。
Mr. Musk or his affiliates may become aware, from time to time, of certain business opportunities (such as acquisition opportunities or technological developments) and may direct such opportunities to other businesses in which they have invested, in which case we may not become aware of or otherwise have the ability to pursue such opportunity. In addition, Mr. Musk and his affiliates may dispose of their interests in other companies or other assets in the future, without any obligation to offer us the opportunity to purchase any of those interests or assets.
Musk 先生或其关联方可能不时知道某些商业机会（如收购机会或技术发展），并可能将此类机会引导到他们投资的其他业务中，在这种情况下我们可能不会知道或无法以其他方式追求此类机会。此外，Musk 先生及其关联方可能未来处置其在其他公司的权益或其他资产，没有义务向我们提供购买任何这些权益或资产的机会。
Under our charter, Mr. Musk and his affiliates are not restricted from owning assets or engaging in businesses that compete directly or indirectly with us and will not have any duty to refrain from engaging, directly or indirectly, in the same or similar business activities or lines of business as us, including those business activities or lines of business deemed to be competing with us, or doing business with any of our customers or vendors. Moreover, we have in the past entered into, and may in the future enter into, transactions with entities affiliated with Mr. Musk. We may enter into such transactions in lieu of pursuing other opportunities that some other shareholders may prefer or that may prove to be more accretive than the opportunities we elect to pursue. In any of these matters, the interests of Mr. Musk and entities owned by or affiliated with him may differ or conflict with the interests of our other shareholders. Any actual or perceived conflicts of interest with respect to the foregoing could have an adverse impact on the trading price of our Class A common stock.
根据我们的章程，Musk 先生及其关联方不受限制地拥有资产或从事与我们直接或间接竞争的业务，并且没有义务避免直接或间接从事与我们相同或类似的业务活动或业务线（包括被视为与我们竞争的业务活动或业务线），或与我们的任何客户或供应商开展业务。此外，我们过去曾与 Musk 先生关联的实体进行过交易，未来可能进行此类交易。我们可能进行此类交易而不是追求其他股东可能更喜欢的其他机会，或者可能比我们选择追求的机会更具增值性的机会。在任何这些事项中，Musk 先生及由其拥有或与其相关的实体的利益可能与我们其他股东的利益不同或冲突。关于上述事项的任何实际或察觉到的利益冲突可能对我们 A 类普通股的交易价格产生不利影响。
Certain of our directors and key employees may have conflicts of interest because they are also employees or directors of affiliates of Mr. Musk or other large shareholders. The resolution of these conflicts of interest may not be in our or your best interests.
我们的某些董事和关键员工可能存在利益冲突，因为他们同时是 Musk 先生或其他大股东关联方的员工或董事。这些利益冲突的解决可能不符合我们或您的最佳利益。
Certain of our directors and key employees may have conflicts of interest because they are also employees or directors of affiliates of Mr. Musk or other large shareholders. Such directors may have interests in, serve on the boards of, or have financial or other relationships with other companies, ventures, or initiatives that are related to or competitive with our business, including but not limited to other space or AI companies, technology ventures, satellite communications businesses, and government or commercial space contracts. Please refer to "Management."
我们的某些董事和关键员工可能存在利益冲突，因为他们同时是 Musk 先生或其他大股东关联方的员工或董事。此类董事可能在与我们业务相关或竞争的其他公司、企业或举措中拥有利益、担任董事会成员或拥有财务或其他关系，包括但不限于其他太空或 AI 公司、技术企业、卫星通信业务以及政府或商业太空合同。请参阅"管理"。
These relationships and interests could create actual or perceived conflicts of interest, particularly with respect to the allocation of time, resources, business opportunities, or strategic decisions. In addition, our charter provides that, to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, we renounce certain corporate opportunities that may be presented to Mr. Musk and certain of our directors and their respective affiliates, and such persons may have no duty to present such opportunities to us. Please refer to "Description of Capital Stock--Corporate Opportunities." Any actual or perceived conflicts of interest could harm our reputation, lead to disputes, divert management attention, or result in decisions that are not in the best interests of us or our shareholders, which could materially and adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
这些关系和利益可能产生实际或察觉到的利益冲突，特别是在时间、资源、商业机会或战略决策的分配方面。此外，我们的章程规定，在适用法律允许的最大范围内，我们放弃可能提交给 Musk 先生及我们某些董事及其各自关联方的某些公司机会，且这些人可能没有义务向我们呈现此类机会。请参阅"资本股票描述——公司机会"。任何实际或察觉到的利益冲突可能损害我们的声誉、引发争议、分散管理层精力，或导致不符合我们或我们股东最佳利益的决定，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
We are highly dependent on the continued services of Mr. Musk, our Chief Executive Officer and Chief Technical Officer, and other key personnel, and the loss or reduced involvement of one or more of these individuals could adversely affect our ability to execute our business strategy.
我们高度依赖 Musk 先生（我们的首席执行官和首席技术官）和其他关键人员的持续服务，失去或减少其中一人或多人的参与可能对我们执行业务战略的能力产生不利影响。
We are highly dependent on the continued service and performance of Mr. Musk, whose leadership, vision, and expertise are critical to the development of our technologies and the execution of our business strategy. Mr. Musk has been, and continues to be, a driving force behind our growth, innovation, and operational success. The loss of Mr. Musk, whether due to death, disability, or otherwise, or his inability or unwillingness to continue in his current roles, could significantly disrupt our management structure, adversely affect our ability to execute our strategic plans, and negatively impact our reputation and relationships with customers, partners, and other stakeholders. Our intense, mission-driven, engineering-first culture has been a key driver of our growth and execution, and any erosion of this culture, including as a result of the loss or reduced involvement of Mr. Musk, could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. We do not maintain key- person life insurance on Mr. Musk. Further, although Mr. Musk devotes significant time to our businesses and is highly active in our management, he does not devote his full time and attention to our businesses and devotes time and attention to other significant roles (and may in the future serve in additional roles). For instance, Mr. Musk currently serves as Technoking and Chief Executive Officer of Tesla and is involved in other emerging technology ventures, including Neuralink and The Boring Company. Mr. Musk has also previously served as Senior Advisor to the President of the United States. Any such loss or reduced involvement in our business could result in a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects. The process of identifying and recruiting a successor with the combination of skills and experience possessed by Mr. Musk, as well as the ability to maintain the confidence of the market, could be lengthy and uncertain, and there can be no assurance that we would be able to attract or retain a suitable replacement in a timely manner or at all.
我们高度依赖 Musk 先生的持续服务和业绩，其领导力、远见和专业知识对我们技术研发和业务战略执行至关重要。Musk 先生一直是我们增长、创新和运营成功背后的推动力。失去 Musk 先生（无论是由于死亡、残疾还是其他原因）或其无法或不愿继续担任当前角色，可能严重扰乱我们的管理结构、对我们执行战略计划的能力产生不利影响，并对我们的声誉以及与客户、合作伙伴和其他利益相关方的关系产生负面影响。我们紧密的、使命驱动的、工程优先的文化是我们增长和执行的关键驱动力，任何此文化的侵蚀（包括因失去或减少 Musk 先生的参与）可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。我们没有为 Musk 先生维持关键人物人寿保险。此外，尽管 Musk 先生为我们的业务投入大量时间并高度积极参与我们的管理，但他并未将全部时间和精力投入到我们的业务中，并将时间和精力投入其他重要角色（未来可能担任其他角色）。例如，Musk 先生目前担任 Tesla 的 Technoking 和首席执行官，并参与其他新兴技术企业，包括 Neuralink 和 The Boring Company。Musk 先生还曾担任美国总统高级顾问。任何此类损失或减少对我们业务的参与可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。识别并招募具有 Musk 先生所拥有的技能和经验组合以及维持市场信心能力的继任者的过程可能漫长且不确定，无法保证我们能及时（甚至完全）吸引或留住合适的替代者。
We, Mr. Musk, and other companies Mr. Musk is affiliated with frequently receive an immense amount of media attention. The actions and statements of Mr. Musk and his affiliated ventures, whether or not directly relating to us, may draw significant public attention and scrutiny to us and could potentially have a positive or negative impact on our business, relationships with customers and regulators, or stock price.
我们、Musk 先生以及与 Musk 先生相关的其他公司经常受到大量媒体关注。Musk 先生及其关联企业的行为和声明，无论是否直接涉及我们，都可能引起对我们的重大公众关注和审查，并可能对我们的业务、与客户和监管机构的关系或股价产生积极或消极影响。
In addition to Mr. Musk, we have key personnel who are invaluable to our businesses. We rely upon their knowledge, expertise, and leadership to develop, manufacture, launch, sell, and support our products and services.
除 Musk 先生外，我们还有对我们业务非常宝贵的关键人员。我们依靠他们的知识、专业知识和领导力来研发、制造、发射、销售和支持我们的产品和服务。
None of our key employees are bound by an employment agreement for any specific term and we may not be able to successfully attract and retain the senior leadership necessary to continue to grow our business. Our compensation arrangements, such as our equity award programs, may not always be successful in attracting new employees and retaining and motivating existing key personnel. Our success depends upon our ability to attract and retain key personnel and any failure to do so could have a material adverse effect on our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects.
我们的关键员工没有任何特定期限的雇佣协议约束，我们可能无法成功吸引和留住继续发展业务所需的高级领导层。我们的薪酬安排（如股权奖励计划）可能并非总是成功吸引新员工和留住并激励现有关键人员。我们的成功取决于我们吸引和留住关键人员的能力，未能做到这一点可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果及未来前景产生重大不利影响。
A significant reduction by Mr. Musk or other existing shareholders of their ownership interest in us could adversely affect us.
Musk 先生或其他现有股东大幅减少其在我们公司的所有权权益可能对我们产生不利影响。
We believe that Mr. Musk's substantial ownership interest in us provides him with an economic incentive to assist us to be successful. Upon the expiration or earlier waiver of the lock-up restrictions on transfers or sales of our securities following the completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will not be subject to any obligation to maintain his ownership interest in us and may elect at any time thereafter to sell all or a substantial portion of or otherwise reduce his ownership interest in us. If Mr. Musk sells all or a substantial portion of his ownership interest in us, he may have less incentive to assist in our success, which could adversely affect our future prospects. Additionally, future resales of our Class A common stock by Mr. Musk or other existing shareholders, or the perception that such sales may occur, could cause the market price of our Class A common stock to decline significantly, regardless of our actual business performance. In particular, subject to the expiration or waiver of any applicable lock-up period, parties to the Investors' Rights Agreement described in "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions-- Investors' Rights Agreement" will have the right, subject to certain exceptions and conditions, to require us to register approximately shares of Class A common stock under the Securities Act, and they will have the right to participate in certain future registrations of securities by us. Registration of any of such shares would result in such shares becoming freely tradable without compliance with Rule 144 limitations upon effectiveness of the registration statement. In addition, approximately shares of Class A common stock will generally be available for resale under Rule 144 starting 90 days after this offering, subject to lock-up restrictions described elsewhere in this prospectus. See "Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions--Investors' Rights Agreement" and "Shares Eligible for Future Sale--Registration Rights."
我们相信，Musk 先生在我们公司的大量所有权权益为他提供了协助我们取得成功的经济激励。本次发行完成后转让或出售我们证券的锁定期限制到期或提前豁免后，Musk 先生不受任何义务约束维持其在我们公司的所有权权益，此后可能随时选择出售其在我们公司全部或大部分所有权权益或以其他方式减少其权益。如果 Musk 先生出售其在我们公司全部或大部分所有权权益，他可能减少协助我们成功的激励，这可能对我们的未来前景产生不利影响。此外，Musk 先生或其他现有股东未来转售我们的 A 类普通股，或此类销售可能发生的看法，可能导致我们 A 类普通股的市场价格大幅下跌，无论我们的实际业务表现如何。特别是，在适用锁定期到期或豁免的前提下，"某些关系和相关人交易——投资者权利协议"中描述的投资者权利协议的各方将有权（在某些例外和条件下）要求我们根据《证券法》注册约 股 A 类普通股，并将有权参与我们未来的某些证券注册。任何此类股份的注册将导致此类股份在注册声明生效后无需遵守 Rule 144 限制即可自由交易。此外，约 股 A 类普通股将通常在本次发行后 90 天起根据 Rule 144 可供转售，但须遵守本招股说明书其他部分描述的锁定限制。请参阅"某些关系和相关人交易——投资者权利协议"和"未来出售合格股份——注册权"。
Following the consummation of this offering, we will be a "controlled company" within the meaning of the Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas listing rules and, as a result, will qualify for and rely on exemptions from certain corporate governance requirements.
本次发行完成后，根据 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 上市规则的含义，我们将成为'受控公司'(controlled company)，因此将符合并依赖某些公司治理要求的豁免。
Because Mr. Musk will beneficially own shares of Class A common stock and shares of Class B common stock, which represents greater than 50% of the voting power of our common stock with respect to director elections and moreover, holders of our Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, will be entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors constituting our board (rounded up to the nearest whole number), following the completion of this offering, we will be a controlled company under the listing rules of Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas.
由于 Musk 先生将实益拥有 股 A 类普通股和 股 B 类普通股（这代表我们普通股关于董事选举的投票权超过 50%），此外，我们 B 类普通股持有人作为一个独立类别投票，将有权选举构成我们董事会授权董事总数的 51%（向上四舍五入到最接近的整数），本次发行完成后，我们将成为 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 上市规则下的受控公司。
Under the listing rules of Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas, a company of which more than 50% of the voting power with respect to director elections is held by another person or group of persons acting together is a "controlled company"
根据 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 上市规则，关于董事选举的投票权超过 50% 由另一人或一致行动的一组人持有的公司是'受控公司'(controlled company)
and may elect not to comply with certain Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas corporate governance requirements, including the requirements that:
可以选择不遵守某些 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 公司治理要求，包括以下要求：
•a majority of such company's board of directors consist of independent directors as defined under the listing rules of Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas;
• 此类公司董事会的多数成员是 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 上市规则定义的独立董事；
•director nominees be selected or recommended for board of directors' selection by a nominating committee composed entirely of independent directors, with a written charter addressing the nominations process as required under the listing rules of Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas;
• 由完全由独立董事组成的提名委员会选择或推荐董事候选人供董事会选择，并具有处理 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 上市规则要求的提名流程的书面章程；
•the compensation committee be composed entirely of independent directors with a written charter addressing the committee's purpose and responsibilities; and •annual performance evaluations of the compensation and nominating committees be conducted.
• 薪酬委员会完全由独立董事组成，并具有处理委员会宗旨和职责的书面章程；以及 • 对薪酬和提名委员会进行年度绩效评估。
Following the completion of this offering, we intend to utilize certain of these exemptions. As a result, we do not expect to have a compensation and nominating committee that is composed entirely of independent directors or that has a committee charter that addresses all Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas requirements applicable to companies that are not controlled companies. Additionally, we may elect to take advantage of certain other exemptions in the future for as long as we remain a "controlled company." Accordingly, our Class A shareholders will not have the same protections afforded to shareholders of companies that are subject to all of the corporate governance requirements of Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas. In the event that we cease to be a "controlled company" and our shares continue to be listed on Nasdaq and Nasdaq Texas, we will be required to comply with all of the applicable governance requirements within the applicable transition periods. Please refer to "Management."
本次发行完成后，我们打算使用其中某些豁免。因此，我们预计不会有完全由独立董事组成的薪酬和提名委员会，也不会有处理所有适用于非受控公司的 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 要求的委员会章程。此外，只要我们仍然是'受控公司'，我们未来可能选择利用某些其他豁免。因此，我们的 A 类股东将不会获得受 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 所有公司治理要求约束的公司股东所获得的相同保护。如果我们不再是'受控公司'且我们的股票继续在 Nasdaq 和 Nasdaq Texas 上市，我们将被要求在适用的过渡期内遵守所有适用的治理要求。请参阅"管理"。
Our ability to provide returns to shareholders will depend on appreciation in our share price, as we do not plan to pay dividends for the foreseeable future.
我们向股东提供回报的能力将取决于股价升值，因为我们在可预见的未来不打算支付股息。
The ability of investors to realize a return on their investment will depend largely on the appreciation of the price of our Class A common stock, as we do not anticipate paying dividends in the foreseeable future. We have never declared or paid any cash dividends on our common stock, and we currently intend to retain all available funds and any future earnings to support the growth and operation of our business, including investment in new technologies and commercial opportunities. As a result, investors seeking cash returns from their investment will not receive any dividend income, and the only way to realize a return may be through an increase in the market price of our Class A common stock, which may not occur. The trading price of our Class A common stock may be volatile and subject to wide fluctuations in response to various factors, including our financial condition and operating results, changes in our business or future prospects, technological innovations, announcements by us or our competitors, changes in the regulatory environment, harm to our brand and reputation, broader market or economic conditions, and the fact that a number of shares of our Class A common stock are expected to be allocated to retail investors in this offering.
投资者实现投资回报的能力将主要取决于我们 A 类普通股价格的升值，因为我们在可预见的未来不预计支付股息。我们从未对普通股宣布或支付任何现金股息，目前打算保留所有可用资金和任何未来收益以支持业务的增长和运营，包括对新技术和商业机会的投资。因此，寻求从投资中获得现金回报的投资者将不会收到任何股息收入，唯一实现回报的方式可能是通过我们 A 类普通股市场价格的上涨，这可能不会发生。我们 A 类普通股的交易价格可能波动并对各种因素（包括我们的财务状况和经营成果、业务或未来前景的变化、技术创新、我们或竞争对手的公告、监管环境的变化、对我们品牌和声誉的损害、更广泛的市场或经济状况，以及预计将分配给本次发行中散户投资者的若干 A 类普通股股份的事实）作出广泛波动。
Additionally, high retail investor interest in our Class A common stock may occur following this offering, which may lead to increased volatility of the trading price. Some of these factors are outside of our control, and the trading price of our Class A common stock may not reflect our actual operating performance. Accordingly, investors may not be able to realize a gain on their investment and could lose all or part of their investment in our Class A common stock.
此外，本次发行后我们 A 类普通股可能引起散户投资者的高度兴趣，这可能导致交易价格波动加剧。其中一些因素超出我们的控制范围，我们 A 类普通股的交易价格可能不反映我们的实际经营业绩。因此，投资者可能无法在投资中实现收益，并可能损失对我们 A 类普通股的全部或部分投资。
Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will serve as our Chief Executive Officer, Chief Technical Officer, and Chairman of our board and control the election of our directors, and our dual class structure concentrates voting control with Mr. Musk and other holders of our Class B common stock. This will limit or preclude your ability to influence corporate matters and the election of our directors.
本次发行完成后，Musk 先生将担任我们的首席执行官、首席技术官和董事会主席，并控制我们董事的选举，我们的双类股结构将投票控制权集中于 Musk 先生和其他 B 类普通股持有人。这将限制或排除您影响公司事务和我们董事选举的能力。
Our Class B common stock will have ten votes per share; our Class A common stock will have one vote per share;
我们的 B 类普通股每股将有十票投票权；我们的 A 类普通股每股将有一票投票权；
and, except as summarized here, our Class A common stock will vote together with our Class B common stock on any matter submitted to the shareholders for a vote. Under our charter, holders of our Class B common stock, voting separately as a class, will be entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors constituting our board (rounded up to the nearest whole number) and will have the ability to remove those directors for as long as there is at least one share of Class B common stock outstanding. As a result, holders of our Class B common stock will have control over the composition of our board and significant influence over the outcome of matters requiring shareholder approval. Please refer to "Description of Capital Stock" for certain other actions that will require approval of a majority of the voting power of the outstanding shares of Class B common stock voting separately as a class. This concentration of voting power will limit or preclude the ability of holders of our Class A common stock, including purchasers of Class A common stock in this offering, to influence corporate matters and the election of our directors.
除此处总结的以外，我们的 A 类普通股将与我们的 B 类普通股一起对提交给股东投票的任何事项进行投票。根据我们的章程，我们 B 类普通股持有人作为一个独立类别投票，将有权选举构成我们董事会授权董事总数的 51%（向上四舍五入到最接近的整数），并将有能力罢免那些董事，只要至少有一股 B 类普通股流通在外。因此，我们 B 类普通股持有人将控制我们董事会的组成，并对需要股东批准的事项的结果产生重大影响。请参阅"资本股票描述"中关于将需要 B 类普通股流通在外股份多数投票权作为一个独立类别投票批准的某些其他行动。这种投票权的集中将限制或排除我们 A 类普通股持有人（包括本次发行中 A 类普通股的购买者）影响公司事务和我们董事选举的能力。
Upon completion of this offering, Mr. Musk will beneficially own a majority of the outstanding shares of our Class B common stock and a majority of the voting power of the common stock (the Class A common stock and the Class B common stock voting together) and therefore will be able to elect all the members of our board. Mr. Musk, who will serve as our Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of our board under our charter and can only be removed from our board or these positions by the vote of Class B holders, as set forth in our charter, will exert significant influence over our business and affairs.
本次发行完成后，Musk 先生将实益拥有我们 B 类普通股流通在外股份的多数，以及普通股投票权的多数（A 类普通股和 B 类普通股一起投票），因此将能够选举我们董事会的所有成员。Musk 先生（根据我们的章程将担任我们的首席执行官和董事会主席，且只能根据我们章程规定的 B 类持有人投票被从董事会或这些职位罢免）将对我们的业务和事务行使重大影响力。
Class B common stock will continue to have ten votes per share, except that, subject to exceptions for certain inter- family transfers and transfers to certain entities that qualify as "permitted transferees" (as described elsewhere in this prospectus), transfers by holders of our Class B common stock will generally result in those shares converting to Class A common stock. The conversion of Class B common stock to Class A common stock will have the effect, over time, of increasing the relative voting power of those holders of Class B common stock who retain their shares.
B 类普通股将继续每股有十票投票权，但某些家族内部转让和向某些符合资格的'允许受让人'(permitted transferees)（如本招股说明书其他部分所述）的转让的例外情况除外，我们 B 类普通股持有人的转让一般将导致这些股份转换为 A 类普通股。B 类普通股转换为 A 类普通股将随时间增加保留股份的 B 类普通股持有人的相对投票权。
If Mr. Musk retains a significant portion of his holdings of Class B common stock for an extended period of time, he could continue to control the election and removal of a majority of our board.
如果 Musk 先生长时间保留其 B 类普通股持股的很大部分，他可能继续控制我们董事会多数的选举和罢免。
However, other persons will also hold shares of Class B common stock. If Mr. Musk were to sell, transfer or otherwise dispose of a sufficient number of his shares of Class B common stock such that he no longer holds a majority of the outstanding shares of Class B common stock, another holder or group of holders of Class B common stock could obtain the ability to elect and remove a majority of our board and thereby effectively control the Company. Any such change in control could result in changes to our strategic direction, management, business plans or policies that may not be aligned with the interests of holders of our Class A common stock.
然而，其他人也将持有 B 类普通股股份。如果 Musk 先生出售、转让或以其他方式处置足够数量的 B 类普通股股份，使他不再持有 B 类普通股流通在外股份的多数，B 类普通股的另一名持有人或一组持有人可能获得选举和罢免我们董事会多数的能力，从而有效控制公司。任何此类控制权变更可能导致我们的战略方向、管理、业务计划或政策发生变化，可能不符合我们 A 类普通股持有人的利益。
In addition, our charter will provide that other than for specified class votes by the Class B common stock or any rights granted to other classes in the future, classes of stock will not be entitled to any separate class votes provided for under the Texas Business Organizations Code (the "TBOC"), including among others (i) the increase or decrease of the aggregate number of authorized shares of a class outstanding, (ii) the exchange, reclassification, or cancellation of all or part of the shares of a class, (iii) a change of shares of a class, with or without par value, into the same or a different number of shares of the same or another class, with or without par value, (iv) the creation of a new class of shares with rights and preferences equal, prior, or superior to the shares of the class and (v) cancellation or other effectuation of the dividends on the shares of the class or series that have accrued but have not been declared.
此外，我们的章程将规定，除了 B 类普通股的指定类别投票或未来授予其他类别的任何权利外，股票类别将无权获得《德克萨斯州商业组织法》（'TBOC'）规定的任何独立类别投票，包括但不限于（i）增加或减少类别流通在外的授权股份总数、（ii）交换、重新分类或注销一个类别全部或部分股份、（iii）将一个类别（有或无面值）的股份变更为相同或不同数量的相同或另一类别（有或无面值）股份、（iv）创建权利和优先权等同于、优先于或优于该类别股份的新股份类别，以及（v）注销或以其他方式实施已应计但未宣布的该类别或系列股份股息。
The TBOC and our charter include provisions that may limit shareholders' ability to bring a cause of action against our directors or officers for certain acts or omissions in their capacity as directors or officers of the Company, including minimum share ownership for derivative proceedings and the presumption of the business judgment rule.
TBOC 和我们的章程包括可能限制股东就董事或高管以其作为公司董事或高管身份的某些作为或不作为对其提起诉因的能力的条款，包括衍生诉讼的最低股份所有权和商业判断规则的推定。
The TBOC and our governing documents include certain provisions that may limit our shareholders' ability to bring certain derivative claims against our officers and directors. For example, the TBOC provides that, if a corporation has a class of stock listed on a national securities exchange, the governing documents may provide that the minimum ownership threshold for a shareholder or group of shareholders to institute or maintain such derivative proceeding is 3% of shares outstanding. A similar ownership threshold provision based on this 2025 TBOC provision has already been challenged in court proceedings involving another Texas corporation and, although the federal district court found the provision enforceable in that case, its enforceability or governing documents containing its provisions could be subject to further challenges or interpretation. The TBOC also permits corporations to request a court, at the start of a transaction (including a related party transaction) or inquiry into a derivative claim, to determine the independence and disinterestedness of directors serving on a special committee reviewing the transaction or directors or other individuals on panels reviewing derivative claims. Subsequent challenges to independence or disinterestedness would require new facts. Our bylaws will provide that these TBOC provisions will apply to us.
TBOC 和我们的管理文件包括某些可能限制股东对我们高管和董事提起某些衍生索赔能力的条款。例如，TBOC 规定，如果公司在国家证券交易所上市的股票类别，管理文件可以规定，股东或股东群体提起或维持此类衍生诉讼的最低所有权门槛为流通在外股份的 3%。已经有基于此 2025 年 TBOC 条款的类似所有权门槛条款在涉及另一家德克萨斯州公司的法院程序中受到挑战，尽管联邦地区法院在该案中发现该条款可执行，但其可执行性或包含其条款的管理文件可能受到进一步挑战或解释。TBOC 还允许公司请求法院在交易（包括关联方交易）或对衍生索赔的询问开始时确定审查交易的特别委员会董事或审查衍生索赔的小组董事或其他个人的独立性和无利害关系。对独立性或无利害关系的后续挑战将需要新的事实。我们的章程将规定这些 TBOC 条款将适用于我们。
In addition, Section 21.419 of the TBOC sets forth certain presumptions concerning compliance by directors and officers with respect to their duties to a corporation, including the duty of care and duty of loyalty. Specifically, in taking or declining to take any action on any matters of a corporation's business, Section 21.419, which applies to us, provides that a director or officer is presumed to have acted (i) in good faith, (ii) on an informed basis, (iii) in furtherance of the interests of the corporation and (iv) in obedience to the law and the corporation's governing documents. These provisions are described as codifying the "business judgment rule." In order to succeed in a cause of action against a director or officer, the Company or a shareholder pursuing such an action must rebut one or more of the foregoing presumptions and prove with particularity the director or officer's act or omission constituted a breach of duty as a director or officer and that such breach involved fraud, intentional misconduct, an ultra vires act or a knowing violation of law.
此外，TBOC Section 21.419 列出了关于董事和高管遵守其对公司义务（包括注意义务和忠实义务）的某些推定。具体而言，在采取或拒绝采取关于公司业务任何事项的任何行动时，适用于我们的 Section 21.419 规定，董事或高管被推定（i）善意地、（ii）在知情的基础上、（iii）为公司利益、和（iv）服从法律和公司管理文件而行事。这些条款被描述为编纂'商业判断规则'(business judgment rule)。为了在对董事或高管的诉讼中获胜，公司或追求此类诉讼的股东必须反驳上述一项或多项推定，并具体证明董事或高管的作为或不作为构成对作为董事或高管义务的违反，并且此类违反涉及欺诈、故意不当行为、越权行为或明知违反法律。
Our bylaws will impose minimum stock ownership and solicitation requirements on shareholders seeking to submit proposals for shareholder approval, which could limit the ability of our shareholders to bring matters before a meeting of shareholders.
我们的章程将对寻求提交股东批准提案的股东施加最低股份所有权和征集要求，这可能限制我们股东将事项提交股东大会的能力。
Upon the completion of this offering, we will qualify as a "nationally listed corporation" under Section 21.373 of the TBOC, and our bylaws will provide that the shareholder proposal requirements permitted by that section will apply immediately upon qualifying as a "nationally listed corporation." As a result, except with respect to director nominations and procedural resolutions ancillary to the conduct of a shareholders' meeting, a shareholder or group of shareholders seeking to submit a proposal for approval at a meeting of shareholders will be required to satisfy specified ownership, holding-period and solicitation requirements. Under these provisions, the proposing shareholder or shareholder group must hold an amount of voting shares (determined as of the date of submission of the proposal) equal to at least 3% of our voting shares, must have held that amount continuously for at least six months before the date of the meeting and throughout the entire duration of the meeting, and must solicit holders of shares representing at least 67% of the voting power of shares entitled to vote on the proposal at the shareholder meeting. For the purpose of this paragraph, "voting shares" means shares that entitle the holder of the shares to vote on the proposal. These requirements are more restrictive than the requirements that would otherwise apply absent such a bylaw provision and may make it more difficult, or in some cases impracticable, for shareholders to submit proposals for consideration at a shareholders' meeting. As a result, our shareholders may have fewer opportunities to present proposals for shareholder approval, even on matters they believe are important, which could limit shareholder influence over corporate governance and other matters. Section 21.373 of the TBOC was enacted in 2025 and, while its enforceability has not yet been challenged in court and we do not have any material concerns related to enforceability of Section 21.373 or the related bylaws provision, like many new laws, we expect the enforceability of TBOC Section 21.373 will eventually be challenged.
本次发行完成后，我们将根据 TBOC Section 21.373 符合"全国上市公司"(nationally listed corporation) 资格，我们的章程将规定，该条款允许的股东提案要求将在符合"全国上市公司"资格后立即适用。因此，除关于董事提名和股东大会进行附属的程序性决议外，寻求提交股东大会批准提案的股东或股东群体将被要求满足指定的所有权、持有期和征集要求。根据这些条款，提议股东或股东群体必须持有相当于我们投票股份至少 3% 的投票股份数量（按提交提案之日确定），必须在大会日期之前至少连续持有该数量六个月，并在整个大会期间持有，并且必须征集代表大会上有权对提案投票股份至少 67% 投票权的股东。本段中的'投票股份'指赋予股份持有人对提案投票的股份。这些要求比缺少此类章程条款时通常适用的要求更严格，可能使股东在股东大会上提交考虑的提案更困难，在某些情况下不切实际。因此，我们的股东可能有较少的机会提交股东批准的提案，即使在他们认为重要的事项上，这可能限制股东对公司治理和其他事项的影响。TBOC Section 21.373 于 2025 年颁布，虽然其可执行性尚未在法庭上受到挑战，我们对 Section 21.373 或相关章程条款的可执行性没有重大担忧，但与许多新法律一样，我们预计 TBOC Section 21.373 的可执行性最终将受到挑战。
Our bylaws place restrictions on the forum, venue and procedures for legal actions or proceedings initiated by our shareholders, including certain requirements for mandatory arbitration. These provisions could limit our shareholders' ability to pursue certain claims and/or increase the cost of doing so and could also affect the procedures, rights, and remedies available to our shareholders in such legal actions or proceedings.
我们的章程对股东发起的法律诉讼或程序的法院、地点和程序施加限制，包括强制仲裁的某些要求。这些条款可能限制我们股东追求某些索赔的能力和/或增加这样做的成本，并可能影响我们股东在此类法律诉讼或程序中可用的程序、权利和补救。
Our bylaws will contain a section (the "Forum Section Bylaw") that will provide that, unless the Company consents in writing to the selection of an alternative forum, the sole and exclusive forum for the filing, adjudication, and trial of all disputes between (i) one or more shareholders and (ii) the Company or its directors, officers, or controlling persons, or any underwriter of securities issued by the Company (or controlling person thereof) relating to any of the following: (1) any derivative proceeding, meaning a civil dispute brought in the right of the Company; (2) any action based on the governance, governing documents, or internal affairs of the Company; (3) any action based on state or federal securities or trade regulation laws; (4) any action based on the alleged act(s) or omission(s) by a person in its capacity as a shareholder, controlling person, director, officer or other managerial official of the Company; (5) any action based on the alleged breach(es) by one or more shareholders, controlling persons, directors, officers, or other managerial officials of a duty owed, in his or her capacity as such, to the Company or to any shareholder thereof; (6)
我们的章程将包含一节（'法院选择章程'），规定除非公司书面同意选择替代法院，否则以下事项的存档、裁决和审理的唯一专属法院为：在（i）一个或多个股东和（ii）公司或其董事、高管或控制人或公司发行证券的任何承销商（或其控制人）之间，关于以下任何事项的所有争议：(1) 任何衍生诉讼，即代表公司提起的民事争议；(2) 任何基于公司治理、管理文件或内部事务的诉讼；(3) 任何基于州或联邦证券或贸易监管法律的诉讼；(4) 任何基于以其作为公司股东、控制人、董事、高管或其他管理官员身份的人的所谓作为或不作为的诉讼；(5) 任何基于一名或多名股东、控制人、董事、高管或其他管理官员对其在该身份下应对公司或其任何股东履行义务的所谓违反的诉讼；(6)
an action seeking to hold a shareholder, controlling person, director, officer, or other managerial official of the Company liable for an obligation of the Company, other than on account of a written contract signed by the person to be held liable in a capacity other than as a shareholder or managerial official; and (7) any action arising out of the TBOC, will be the Texas Business Court, Eleventh Division (the "Business Court") (for purposes of this summary, each, an "Internal Dispute").
寻求让公司股东、控制人、董事、高管或其他管理官员对公司义务承担责任的诉讼（除非基于由要被追究责任的人以非股东或管理官员身份签署的书面合同）；以及 (7) 任何因 TBOC 引起的诉讼，将是德克萨斯州商业法院第十一分部（'商业法院'）（为本摘要目的，每一项称为'内部争议'）。
The selection of the Business Court as the exclusive forum for Internal Disputes may limit a shareholder's ability to bring a claim in a judicial forum that it finds favorable for disputes with us or our directors, officers, other managerial officials, or other employees, which may discourage lawsuits against us and our directors, officers, other managerial officials, and other employees. Except to the extent that the Company consents in writing, or a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final and unappealable judgment, that an Internal Dispute is not subject to the sole and exclusive venue and forum or jurisdiction of the Business Court or arbitration (as described further below), a shareholder will not be permitted to litigate an Internal Dispute in federal court or in any state court other than the Business Court, and will not be able to avail itself of any potential advantages or procedural protections of such other forums. Any person or entity purchasing or otherwise acquiring any interest in our shares of capital stock will be deemed to have notice of and have consented to these provisions. For more information, please refer to "Description of Capital Stock--Anti-Takeover Effects of Provisions of Our Charter, our Bylaws and Texas Law."
选择商业法院作为内部争议的专属法院可能限制股东在其认为对我们、董事、高管、其他管理官员或其他员工争议有利的司法法院提起索赔的能力，这可能阻止对我们、董事、高管、其他管理官员和其他员工的诉讼。除非公司书面同意，或主管司法管辖区的法院在最终且不可上诉的判决中确定内部争议不受商业法院或仲裁（如下文进一步描述）的唯一专属地点和法院或司法管辖权约束，股东将不被允许在联邦法院或商业法院以外的任何州法院诉讼内部争议，也无法利用此类其他法院的任何潜在优势或程序保护。任何购买或以其他方式获得我们资本股票权益的人或实体将被视为已知悉并同意这些条款。有关更多信息，请参阅"资本股票描述——我们章程、章程细则和德克萨斯州法律条款的反收购效应"。
SpaceX maintains that the Forum Selection Bylaw, including without limitation the selection of the Business Court as the sole and exclusive forum for all actions brought under federal securities laws, accords with the law and is enforceable. However, the law governing the selection of a forum other than a federal court for certain actions brought under the federal securities laws is unsettled, and there is some risk that, if an Internal Dispute were filed under the Exchange Act (or the rules and regulations thereunder) in a court other than the Business Court, that court could deny a motion to transfer the action to the Business Court pursuant to the Forum Selection Bylaw.
SpaceX 主张法院选择章程（包括但不限于选择商业法院作为根据联邦证券法提起的所有诉讼的唯一专属法院）符合法律并可执行。然而，关于选择联邦法院以外法院处理某些根据联邦证券法提起的诉讼的法律尚未确定，存在一定风险，如果根据《交易法》（或其规则和法规）提起的内部争议在商业法院以外的法院提起，该法院可能根据法院选择章程拒绝将诉讼转移到商业法院的动议。
Accordingly, the bylaws provide that to the extent that a court of competent jurisdiction were to determine in a final and unappealable judgment that an Internal Dispute is not subject to the sole and exclusive venue and forum or jurisdiction of the Business Court (such Internal Dispute, an "Other Dispute"), such Other Dispute would be exclusively and finally settled by arbitration, pursuant to the Texas Arbitration Act, under the Expedited Procedure Provisions of the Rules of the International Chamber of Commerce, pursuant to Article 30 thereof. To be clear, absent Company consent, a shareholder would not be able to file an arbitration demand pursuant to the Dispute Resolution Clause without first obtaining a final and unappealable judgment that the shareholder's Internal Dispute is not subject to the sole and exclusive venue and forum or jurisdiction of the Business Court. The governing law of such Other Dispute would be the federal law of the United States or the law of the State of Texas, as applicable to the issues raised in the Other Dispute, including without limitation the pleading and discovery limitations of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act.
因此，章程细则规定，如果主管司法管辖区的法院在最终且不可上诉的判决中确定内部争议不受商业法院的唯一专属地点和法院或司法管辖权约束（此类内部争议，称为'其他争议'），此类其他争议将根据《德克萨斯州仲裁法》、根据国际商会规则的快速程序条款（根据其第 30 条）专属和最终通过仲裁解决。需要明确的是，在没有公司同意的情况下，股东在未首先获得最终且不可上诉的判决（确定股东的内部争议不受商业法院的唯一专属地点和法院或司法管辖权约束）之前，将无法根据争议解决条款提交仲裁请求。此类其他争议的管辖法律将是美国联邦法律或德克萨斯州法律，视其他争议中提出的问题而定，包括但不限于《私人证券诉讼改革法》的诉状和发现限制。
Given the unsettled nature of the law in this area, there is also some risk that a court that has denied a motion to transfer an Internal Dispute to the Business Court pursuant to the Forum Selection Bylaw would also deny a motion to compel arbitration of such Other Dispute pursuant to the Forum Selection Bylaw. Accordingly, the Forum Selection Bylaw further provides that to the extent that a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final and unappealable judgment that such Other Dispute cannot be compelled to arbitration pursuant to the Forum Selection Bylaw, the sole and exclusive forum for the adjudication and trial of such Other Dispute will be the United States District Court for the Southern District of Texas, Houston Division (the "Federal Court").
鉴于该领域法律的不确定性质，还存在一些风险，即拒绝根据法院选择章程将内部争议转移到商业法院的动议的法院也可能根据法院选择章程拒绝强制仲裁此类其他争议的动议。因此，法院选择章程进一步规定，如果主管司法管辖区的法院在最终且不可上诉的判决中确定此类其他争议不能根据法院选择章程被强制仲裁，则此类其他争议的裁决和审理的唯一专属法院将是德克萨斯州南区联邦地区法院休斯顿分院（'联邦法院'）。
Finally, the Forum Selection Bylaw provides that to the extent that a court of competent jurisdiction determines in a final and unappealable judgment that the Federal Court lacks jurisdiction over any such Other Dispute, the sole and exclusive forum and venue for such Other Dispute will be the state district courts of Harris County, Texas.
最后，法院选择章程规定，如果主管司法管辖区的法院在最终且不可上诉的判决中确定联邦法院对任何此类其他争议缺乏管辖权，此类其他争议的唯一专属法院和地点将是德克萨斯州 Harris 县的州地区法院。
Regardless of the forum, venue, or procedures selected for an Internal Dispute or Other Dispute, our bylaws shall require that any Internal Dispute or Other Disputes be brought only as an individual action or derivative proceeding, and, to the fullest extent permitted by law, shall prohibit shareholders from bringing such an Internal Dispute or Other Dispute as a class action, mass action, or other form of collective action or from being consolidated or joined, in whole or in part, consistent with the Arbitration Rules. However, the Company, at its sole option, may elect to seek consolidation or joinder of matters as consistent with the Arbitration Rules.
无论为内部争议或其他争议选择何种法院、地点或程序，我们的章程细则将要求任何内部争议或其他争议仅作为单独诉讼或衍生诉讼提起，并在法律允许的最大范围内禁止股东将此类内部争议或其他争议作为集体诉讼、大众诉讼或其他形式的集体诉讼提起，或与仲裁规则一致地完全或部分被合并或加入。然而，公司可自行选择寻求按照仲裁规则进行事项合并或加入。
In addition, our bylaws will provide that any person or entity purchasing or otherwise acquiring or holding any interest in shares of stock of the Company shall be deemed to have irrevocably and unconditionally waived any right it may have to a trial by jury in any Internal Dispute. This will prevent a shareholder from requesting that a jury decide disputed issues of fact and may discourage lawsuits against us and our directors, officers, other managerial officials, and other employees.
此外，我们的章程细则将规定，任何购买或以其他方式获取或持有公司股票任何权益的人或实体将被视为不可撤销且无条件地放弃其在任何内部争议中可能拥有的接受陪审团审判的任何权利。这将阻止股东请求陪审团决定有争议的事实问题，并可能阻止对我们、董事、高管、其他管理官员和其他员工的诉讼。
These dispute resolution rules that our bylaws will establish for Internal Disputes, as well as the Arbitration Rules to the extent they will apply, are different from the procedural rules that would normally apply to the litigation of Internal Disputes in state or federal court. They may prevent a shareholder from availing itself of procedural protections that would be available under litigation in state or federal court and may render available or affect adversely the rights and remedies available to shareholders in such proceedings. Particularly in the case of arbitration, including its prohibition on class or collective actions, these dispute resolution rules may also result in greater costs being imposed on shareholders to litigate Internal Disputes, and in some cases involving lower amounts in controversy, the additional costs that may be imposed on shareholders to litigate Internal Disputes could exceed the potential recovery from such litigation.
我们章程细则为内部争议建立的这些争议解决规则，以及在适用范围内的仲裁规则，与通常适用于在州或联邦法院诉讼内部争议的程序规则不同。它们可能阻止股东利用在州或联邦法院诉讼中可用的程序保护，并可能使此类程序中可用的股东权利和补救变得不可用或受到不利影响。特别是在仲裁的情况下，包括其禁止集体或集体诉讼，这些争议解决规则也可能导致股东诉讼内部争议时承担更大的成本，在某些涉及金额较低的争议中，可能对股东施加的额外成本可能超过此类诉讼的潜在追回。
It is possible that one or more provisions of our bylaws, including those regarding the exclusive forum for Internal Disputes, mandatory arbitration for Other Disputes, or waiver of the right to proceed on a class, mass, or collective basis, may be found by a court to be inapplicable or unenforceable. In addition, the mandatory arbitration provision in our bylaws could be subject to litigation or regulatory scrutiny, which could result in the provision being enjoined or in additional costs or uncertainty. In such case, we may incur additional costs or delays associated with resolving such actions, including in other jurisdictions, which could adversely affect our business, financial condition, or results of operations.
我们章程细则中的一项或多项条款（包括关于内部争议专属法院、对其他争议的强制仲裁或放弃集体、大众或集体程序的权利）可能被法院发现不适用或不可执行。此外，我们章程细则中的强制仲裁条款可能受到诉讼或监管审查，这可能导致该条款被禁止或产生额外成本或不确定性。在这种情况下，我们可能产生与解决此类诉讼相关的额外成本或延迟（包括在其他司法管辖区），这可能对我们的业务、财务状况或经营成果产生不利影响。
CAUTIONARY STATEMENT REGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS
关于前瞻性陈述的警示性声明
This prospectus contains forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements include those that express a belief, expectation, or intention, as well as those that are not statements of historical fact. Forward-looking statements contained in this prospectus include information regarding our future operating results and financial position, our business strategy and plans and our objectives for future operations. Forward-looking statements contained in this prospectus also include, but are not limited to, statements about:
本招股说明书包含前瞻性陈述。前瞻性陈述包括表达信念、预期或意图的陈述，以及非历史事实陈述。本招股说明书中包含的前瞻性陈述包括关于我们未来经营成果和财务状况、业务战略和计划以及未来运营目标的信息。本招股说明书中包含的前瞻性陈述还包括但不限于关于以下方面的陈述：
•the development and deployment of Starship in accordance with our anticipated schedule (including commencement of payload delivery to orbit in 2026) and launch cadence and our ability to achieve expected performance, reusability, and cost efficiencies;
• Starship 按照我们预期时间表的研发和部署（包括 2026 年开始向轨道交付有效载荷）和发射频次，以及我们实现预期性能、可回收性和成本效率的能力；
•the size and growth of our various existing and future markets, including the markets for commercial launch services, satellite connectivity services, our AI platforms, AI compute infrastructure (terrestrial and orbital), lunar-related activities and interplanetary activities, including the extent to which such markets develop, particularly emerging or unproven markets that may not materialize as expected or on anticipated timelines;
• 我们各种现有和未来市场的规模和增长，包括商业发射服务、卫星连接服务、我们的 AI 平台、AI 算力基础设施（陆地和轨道）、月球相关活动和星际活动市场，包括此类市场发展的程度，特别是新兴或未经验证的市场可能未按预期或预期时间表实现；
•demand for our products and services, including our launch, connectivity, and AI offerings, and our ability to grow our customer base and generate revenue;
• 对我们产品和服务的需求，包括我们的发射、连接和 AI 产品，以及我们扩大客户群和产生收入的能力；
•the deployment of our next-generation Starlink satellites, satellite-to-mobile connectivity, and orbital AI compute infrastructure (including potential deployment of our orbital AI compute satellites as early as 2028), including our ability to successfully develop, scale, and commercialize such technologies, which are subject to significant technical complexity, capital requirements, new innovations and regulatory approvals;
• 我们下一代 Starlink 卫星、星地手机直连和轨道 AI 算力基础设施的部署（包括最早 2028 年部署轨道 AI 算力卫星），包括我们成功研发、扩展和商业化此类技术的能力，这些技术受重大技术复杂性、资本要求、新创新和监管批准的约束；
•our target launch cadence and expansion of our manufacturing and operational capacity necessary to support our strategies, including our ability to scale production, supply chain, infrastructure, and workforce efficiently;
• 我们的目标发射频次以及支持我们战略所需的制造和运营能力的扩展，包括我们高效扩展生产、供应链、基础设施和员工队伍的能力；
•our ability to execute our growth strategy and scale our operations efficiently, including managing costs, timelines, and operational complexity;
• 我们执行增长战略和高效扩展运营的能力，包括管理成本、时间表和运营复杂性；
•our ability to solve novel issues and navigate and monetize technologies and environments that have never been accessed or economized before;
• 我们解决新颖问题以及驾驭和将以前从未访问或经济化的技术和环境货币化的能力；
•our ability to design, develop and successfully commercialize new and innovative technologies, products, and services, including our AI platforms and Terafab, and our ability to achieve and maintain a low cost per token, in each case in rapidly evolving and competitive markets;
• 我们设计、研发和成功商业化新颖创新技术、产品和服务的能力，包括我们的 AI 平台和 Terafab，以及在快速演变和竞争激烈的市场中实现和维持低单 token 成本的能力；
•our ability to scale and monetize our AI products and services, including the development, performance, and adoption of our frontier models and related applications, and to realize benefits from related acquisitions and initiatives, such as our arrangement with Cursor;
• 我们扩展和货币化 AI 产品和服务的能力，包括前沿模型和相关应用的研发、性能和采用，以及实现相关收购和举措（如我们与 Cursor 的安排）的收益；
•the amount, nature and timing of our capital expenditures and the impact of such capital expenditures on our growth and performance, including our ability to fund such expenditures, manage costs, strategically reduce costs and achieve expected returns on investment;
• 我们资本支出的金额、性质和时间以及此类资本支出对我们增长和业绩的影响，包括我们为此类支出提供资金、管理成本、战略性降低成本和实现预期投资回报的能力；
•our ability to obtain sufficient power, GPUs, and other critical components and manage our supply chain to support our operations and growth;
• 我们获得足够电力、GPU 和其他关键部件以及管理供应链以支持运营和增长的能力；
•our ability to obtain and maintain required regulatory approvals, licenses and spectrum authorizations in the United States and internationally, and the timing, scope, and conditions of such approvals;
• 我们在美国和国际上获得和维持所需监管批准、许可证和频谱授权的能力，以及此类批准的时间、范围和条件；
•the competitive landscape in the industries in which we operate and our ability to compete effectively;
• 我们运营行业的竞争格局以及我们有效竞争的能力；
•the implementation, interpretation, and impact of current or future regulations including laws and regulations relating to space operations, communications, AI, data privacy, and other areas;
• 现行或未来法规的实施、解释和影响，包括与太空运营、通信、AI、数据隐私和其他领域相关的法律法规；
•our ability to realize benefits and manage risks of being a public company; and •general economic conditions.
• 我们实现作为上市公司收益和管理风险的能力；以及 • 一般经济状况。
These forward-looking statements may be accompanied by words such as "anticipate," "believe," "estimate,"
这些前瞻性陈述可能伴随诸如"anticipate"、"believe"、"estimate"、
"expect," "intend," "may," "outlook," "plan," "potential," "predict," "project," "will," "should," "could," "would,"
"expect"、"intend"、"may"、"outlook"、"plan"、"potential"、"predict"、"project"、"will"、"should"、"could"、"would"、
"likely," "future," "budget," "goal," "commit," "pursue," "target," "seek," "objective" or the negative of these words, or similar expressions that are predictions of or indicate future events or trends that do not relate to historical matters. We caution you that the foregoing list may not contain all of the forward-looking statements made in this prospectus.
"likely"、"future"、"budget"、"goal"、"commit"、"pursue"、"target"、"seek"、"objective" 等词或这些词的否定形式或类似表达，这些是对不涉及历史事项的未来事件或趋势的预测或表示。我们警告您，上述列表可能不包含本招股说明书中所做的所有前瞻性陈述。
The forward-looking statements in this prospectus speak only as of the date of this prospectus, or such other date as specified herein. We undertake no obligation to update these statements unless required by law, and we caution you not to place undue reliance on them. Forward-looking statements are not assurances of future performance and involve risks and uncertainties. We have based these forward-looking statements on our current expectations and assumptions about future events. Forecasts, goals, milestones, and expectations that cover multi-year time horizons, or unknown timelines, inherently involve increased risks with respect to predictability and actual results may differ materially from current expectations. While our management considers these expectations and assumptions to be reasonable, they are inherently subject to significant business, economic, competitive, regulatory, technological, environmental, political, and other risks, contingencies and uncertainties, which are difficult to predict and many of which are beyond our control. These risks, contingencies, and uncertainties and other important factors are described in the "Risk Factors" and "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations" sections of this prospectus. Should one or more of such risks or uncertainties occur, or should underlying assumptions prove incorrect, our actual results, performance, achievements or plans could differ materially from those expressed or implied in any forward-looking statements. In addition, because we operate in rapidly evolving and certain highly competitive markets, we may from time to time rapidly adjust, modify or change our strategic priorities, capital allocation, product or service focus or operational initiatives in response to technological developments, competitive dynamics, regulatory changes or other factors, which could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied by the forward-looking statements contained herein. New risks emerge from time to time, some risks are inherently unknown to us, and it is not possible for our management to predict all such risks. Many of the risks and uncertainties that could materially adversely affect us or our prospects are beyond our control or relate to portions of our business strategy that have a lengthy time horizon or involve unprecedented ventures. This can make assessment of certain risks more difficult and you should factor these uncertainties into your assessment of an investment in our Class A common stock. All forward-looking statements in this prospectus are expressly qualified in their entirety by the cautionary statements in this section.
本招股说明书中的前瞻性陈述仅在本招股说明书日期或本文指定的其他日期时有效。除法律要求外，我们不承担更新这些陈述的义务，我们警告您不要过分依赖它们。前瞻性陈述不是未来业绩的保证，涉及风险和不确定性。我们基于当前对未来事件的预期和假设制定这些前瞻性陈述。涵盖多年时间范围或未知时间表的预测、目标、里程碑和预期本质上涉及关于可预测性的更高风险，实际结果可能与当前预期存在重大差异。虽然我们的管理层认为这些预期和假设是合理的，但它们本质上受到难以预测且许多超出我们控制的重大业务、经济、竞争、监管、技术、环境、政治和其他风险、突发事件和不确定性的约束。这些风险、突发事件和不确定性以及其他重要因素在本招股说明书的"风险因素"和"管理层关于财务状况和经营成果的讨论与分析"部分中描述。如果发生这些风险或不确定性中的一项或多项，或如果基础假设被证明不正确，我们的实际结果、业绩、成就或计划可能与任何前瞻性陈述中表达或暗示的内容存在重大差异。此外，由于我们在快速演变且某些高度竞争的市场中运营，我们可能不时迅速调整、修改或改变战略优先事项、资本配置、产品或服务重点或运营举措，以应对技术发展、竞争动态、监管变化或其他因素，这可能导致实际结果与本文包含的前瞻性陈述所表达或暗示的结果存在重大差异。新的风险不时出现，某些风险对我们本质上是未知的，我们的管理层无法预测所有此类风险。许多可能对我们或我们前景产生重大不利影响的风险和不确定性超出我们的控制范围或涉及时间跨度较长或涉及前所未有尝试的业务战略部分。这可能使对某些风险的评估更具难度，您在评估对我们 A 类普通股的投资时应将这些不确定性纳入考虑。本招股说明书中的所有前瞻性陈述均在本节警示性声明中全部明确受到限定。
USE OF PROCEEDS
募集资金用途
We expect to receive approximately $ of net proceeds from this offering (or $ if the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares of Class A common stock in full), based upon the assumed initial public offering price of $ per share (which is the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover page of this prospectus) after deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by us.
我们预计从本次发行中收到约 $ 美元的净收益（如果承销商完全行使其购买额外 A 类普通股股份的选择权，则为 $ 美元），基于假设的首次公开发行价格 $ 每股（即本招股说明书封面上规定的价格范围的中点），扣除承销折扣和佣金以及我们应付的估计发行费用后。
We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering to fund our growth strategy, including the expansion of our AI compute infrastructure, enhancements to our launch infrastructure and launch vehicles, increases in the scale and capacity of our satellite constellations, and any remaining amounts for general corporate purposes.
我们打算将本次发行的净收益用于为增长战略提供资金，包括扩展 AI 算力基础设施、增强发射基础设施和运载火箭、增加卫星星座的规模和容量，以及任何剩余金额用于一般公司用途。
Assuming no exercise of the underwriters' option to purchase additional shares, each $1.00 change in the assumed initial public offering price of $ per share (which is the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover page of this prospectus) would cause the net proceeds from this offering, after deducting the underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by us, to change by approximately $ million, assuming no change to the number of shares of our Class A common stock offered by us, as set forth on the cover page of this prospectus. Similarly, an increase (decrease) of one million shares of Class A common stock sold in this offering by us would increase (decrease) our net proceeds by $ million, assuming the initial public offering price of $ per share (which is the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover page of this prospectus) remains the same, and after deducting the underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by us. If the net proceeds increase for any reason, we would use the additional net proceeds for the purposes set forth above. If the net proceeds decrease for any reason, then we expect that we would use the lower amount of net proceeds for the purposes set forth above.
假设承销商不行使其购买额外股份的选择权，假设的首次公开发行价格 $ 每股（即本招股说明书封面上规定的价格范围的中点）每变动 $1.00 美元将使本次发行扣除承销折扣和佣金以及我们应付的估计发行费用后的净收益变动约 $ 百万美元，假设我们发行的 A 类普通股股份数量没有变化（如本招股说明书封面上所述）。同样，本次发行中我们出售的 A 类普通股增加（减少）一百万股将使我们的净收益增加（减少）$ 百万美元，假设首次公开发行价格 $ 每股（即本招股说明书封面上规定的价格范围的中点）保持不变，并扣除承销折扣和佣金以及我们应付的估计发行费用后。如果净收益因任何原因增加，我们将使用额外的净收益用于上述目的。如果净收益因任何原因减少，则我们预计将使用较低的净收益用于上述目的。
The expected use of net proceeds from this offering represents our intentions based upon our present plans and business conditions. We cannot predict with certainty all of the particular uses for the net proceeds from this offering or the amounts that we will actually spend on each of the uses set forth above. Accordingly, our management will have significant flexibility in applying the net proceeds from this offering. The timing and amount of our actual expenditures will be based on many factors, including cash flows and the anticipated growth of our business.
本次发行净收益的预期用途代表了我们基于当前计划和业务状况的意图。我们无法确定地预测本次发行净收益的所有特定用途或我们实际将在上述每项用途上花费的金额。因此，我们的管理层在使用本次发行净收益方面将有重大灵活性。我们实际支出的时间和金额将基于许多因素，包括现金流和业务的预期增长。
DIVIDEND POLICY
股息政策
We do not anticipate declaring or paying any cash dividends to holders of our common stock in the foreseeable future. We currently intend to retain future earnings, if any, to finance the growth of our business. Our future dividend policy is within the discretion of our board and will depend upon then-existing conditions, including our results of operations, financial condition, capital requirements, investment opportunities, statutory restrictions on our ability to pay dividends, restrictions in our existing and any future debt agreements and other factors our board may deem relevant. Covenants under our Credit Agreements also restrict our ability to pay dividends, and we may enter into credit agreements or other borrowing arrangements in the future that restrict our ability to declare or pay cash dividends or make distributions in the future. Please refer to "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations--Liquidity and Capital Resources" for a description of the restrictions on our ability to pay dividends.
我们不预期在可预见的未来对我们普通股持有人宣布或支付任何现金股息。我们目前打算保留未来收益（如有）以为业务增长提供资金。我们未来的股息政策由我们的董事会自行决定，并将取决于届时存在的条件，包括我们的经营成果、财务状况、资本要求、投资机会、对我们支付股息能力的法定限制、我们现有和任何未来债务协议中的限制以及我们董事会可能认为相关的其他因素。我们信贷协议下的契约也限制我们支付股息的能力，未来我们可能签订信贷协议或其他借款安排，限制我们宣布或支付现金股息或在未来进行分配的能力。请参阅"管理层关于财务状况和经营成果的讨论与分析——流动性和资本资源"中关于我们支付股息能力限制的描述。
Please refer to "Risk Factors--Risks Related to Our Corporate Structure, Ownership of our Class A Common Stock and This Offering--Our ability to provide returns to shareholders will depend on appreciation in our share price, as we do not plan to pay dividends for the foreseeable future."
请参阅"风险因素——与我们的公司结构、A 类普通股所有权和本次发行相关的风险——我们向股东提供回报的能力将取决于股价升值，因为我们在可预见的未来不打算支付股息。"
CAPITALIZATION
资本结构

下表列出了截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的现金及现金等价物和资本结构：
•on an actual basis;
• 在实际基础上；
•on a pro forma basis, giving effect to (i) the Preferred Conversion as if such conversion had occurred on March 31, 2026, (ii) the Class C Reclassification as if such reclassification had occurred on March 31, 2026, and (iii)
• 在备考 (pro forma) 基础上，反映（i）优先股转换 (Preferred Conversion)（假设此类转换发生在 2026 年 3 月 31 日）、（ii）C 类重新分类 (Class C Reclassification)（假设此类重新分类发生在 2026 年 3 月 31 日）和（iii）
the effectiveness of our charter, which will become effective upon the completion of this offering; and •on a pro forma as adjusted basis, giving effect to (i) the pro forma adjustments set forth above, (ii) the sale of shares of our Class A common stock in this offering at an assumed initial offering price of $ per share, which is the midpoint of the range set forth on the cover page of this prospectus, and (iii) the application of the net proceeds from this offering as described under "Use of Proceeds."
我们章程的生效（将在本次发行完成时生效）；以及 • 在备考调整后基础上，反映（i）上述备考调整、（ii）以假设的首次公开发行价格 $ 每股（即本招股说明书封面上规定范围的中点）在本次发行中出售我们的 A 类普通股股份，以及（iii）按"募集资金用途"中描述使用本次发行的净收益。

.............................| | 1,755| | 1,755| | 股东权益合计 ...................................................| | $34,533| | $41,582| | 资本结构合计 ........................................................................| | $70,693| | $70,693| | ________________ (1) 截至 2026 年 4 月 30 日，我们在 SpaceX 信贷融资下没有未偿还借款。2026 年 5 月，SpaceX 信贷融资经修订将借款能力增加至 $5,000 百万美元（'修订后 SpaceX 信贷融资'）。修订后 SpaceX 信贷融资于 2031 年 5 月 19 日终止，所有未偿贷款到期应付，除非各方根据修订后 SpaceX 信贷融资条款同意延期。有关 SpaceX 信贷融资和修订后 SpaceX 信贷融资的更多信息，请参阅"管理层关于财务状况和经营成果的讨论与分析——流动性和资本资源——债务协议"。
(2)As of April 30, 2026, we had $20,000 million of borrowings outstanding under the SpaceX Bridge Loan. The SpaceX Bridge Loan matures on September 2, 2027, subject to extension in accordance with the terms of the agreement. For more information on the SpaceX Bridge Loan, please see "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations--Liquidity and Capital Resources--Debt Agreements."
(2) 截至 2026 年 4 月 30 日，我们在 SpaceX 过桥贷款下有 $20,000 百万美元的未偿还借款。SpaceX 过桥贷款于 2027 年 9 月 2 日到期，可根据协议条款延期。有关 SpaceX 过桥贷款的更多信息，请参阅"管理层关于财务状况和经营成果的讨论与分析——流动性和资本资源——债务协议"。
(3)Includes obligations related to certain AI infrastructure assets recorded as failed sale-leaseback transactions.
(3) 包括与某些被记录为失败的售后回租交易 (failed sale-leaseback transactions) 的 AI 基础设施资产相关的义务。
DILUTION
股权稀释
Purchasers of the Class A common stock in this offering will experience immediate and substantial dilution in the net tangible book value per share of the Class A common stock for accounting purposes. Our net tangible book value as of March 31, 2026 was approximately $ , or $ per share of Class A common stock. Net tangible book value per share is determined by dividing our tangible net worth (tangible assets less total liabilities) by the total number of outstanding shares of all classes of common stock outstanding immediately prior to the completion of this offering. After giving effect to the sale of shares of Class A common stock in this offering, the payment of underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses by us, the Class C Reclassification and the Preferred Conversion as if such reclassification and conversion occurred on March 31, 2026, our adjusted pro forma net tangible book value as of March 31, 2026 would have been approximately $ , or $ per share of Class A common stock. This represents an immediate decrease in the net tangible book value of $ per share of Class A common stock to Mr. Musk and other existing investors and an immediate dilution (i.e., the difference between the offering price and the adjusted pro forma net tangible book value immediately after this offering) to new investors purchasing shares of Class A common stock in this offering of $ per share. The following table illustrates the per share dilution to new investors purchasing shares of Class A common stock in this offering:
本次发行中 A 类普通股的购买者将立即且大幅经历每股 A 类普通股有形账面净值的稀释（用于会计目的）。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们的有形账面净值约为 $ 美元，或每股 A 类普通股 $ 美元。每股有形账面净值通过将我们的有形净资产（有形资产减总负债）除以本次发行完成前所有类别流通在外普通股总股数确定。在反映本次发行中销售 A 类普通股股份、我们支付承销折扣和佣金以及估计发行费用、C 类重新分类和优先股转换（假设此类重新分类和转换发生在 2026 年 3 月 31 日）的影响后，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们调整后备考有形账面净值约为 $ 美元，或每股 A 类普通股 $ 美元。这代表 Musk 先生和其他现有投资者 A 类普通股每股有形账面净值立即减少 $ 美元，并对本次发行中购买 A 类普通股股份的新投资者立即稀释（即发行价与本次发行后立即调整后备考有形账面净值之间的差额）每股 $ 美元。下表说明了本次发行中购买 A 类普通股股份的新投资者每股稀释：

Each $1.00 increase or decrease in the assumed initial public offering price would increase or decrease, as applicable, the total consideration paid by new investors and the total consideration paid by all shareholders by $ million, assuming that the number of shares of Class A common stock offered by us remains the same and after deducting estimated underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by us.
假设首次公开发行价格每股增加或减少 $1.00 美元，假设我们发行的 A 类普通股股份数量保持不变并扣除我们应付的估计承销折扣和佣金以及估计发行费用后，将分别增加或减少新投资者支付的总对价以及所有股东支付的总对价 $ 百万美元。
Similarly, an increase or decrease of shares in the number of shares of Class A common stock offered by us would increase or decrease, as applicable, the total consideration paid by new investors and the total consideration paid by all shareholders by $ million, assuming that the assumed initial public offering price remains the same and after deducting estimated underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by us.
同样，假设我们发行的 A 类普通股股份数量增加或减少 股，假设首次公开发行价格保持不变并扣除我们应付的估计承销折扣和佣金以及估计发行费用后，将分别增加或减少新投资者支付的总对价以及所有股东支付的总对价 $ 百万美元。

管理层讨论与分析 / MD&A

331 块 · 2 标题 · 329 段落 · 英文 28,361 词 · 中文 72,711 字
English (Original)
中文译文
MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF
管理层对财务状况和经营成果的讨论与分析
OPERATIONS
（续）
The following discussion and analysis of our financial condition and results of operations should be read in conjunction with our audited consolidated financial statements and the related notes and other financial information included elsewhere in this prospectus. In addition to historical consolidated financial information, the following discussion contains forward-looking statements that reflect our plans, estimates, and beliefs. Our actual results could differ materially from those discussed in the forward-looking statements. You should review the sections titled "Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements" for a discussion of forward-looking statements and "Risk Factors" for a discussion of factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from the results described in or implied by the forward-looking statements contained in the following discussion and analysis and elsewhere in this prospectus. Our audited consolidated financial statements and related notes have been prepared to reflect the retrospective combination of the companies for all periods presented as the acquisitions of xAI and X Holdings were accounted for as transactions between entities under common control.
以下对我们的财务状况和经营成果 (results of operations) 的讨论与分析，应当与本招股说明书其他部分所载的我们经审计的合并财务报表及相关附注和其他财务信息一并阅读。除历史合并财务信息外，以下讨论还包含反映我们计划、估计和判断的前瞻性陈述。我们的实际结果可能与前瞻性陈述中讨论的结果存在重大差异。读者应查阅”关于前瞻性陈述的警示性说明”以了解对前瞻性陈述的讨论，以及”风险因素”以了解可能导致实际结果与以下讨论与分析及本招股说明书其他部分所载或所暗示的前瞻性陈述中描述的结果存在重大差异的因素。我们经审计的合并财务报表及相关附注按追溯合并法编制以反映所示各期间公司合并情况，因为对 xAI 和 X Holdings 的收购按共同控制下实体间的交易处理。
Our Mission Our mission is to build the systems and technologies necessary to make life multiplanetary, to understand the true nature of the universe, and to extend the light of consciousness to the stars. To do this, we have formed the most ambitious, vertically integrated innovation engine on (and off) Earth with unmatched capabilities to rapidly manufacture and launch space-based communications that connect the world, to harness the Sun to power a truth- seeking artificial intelligence that advances scientific discovery, and ultimately to build a base on the Moon and cities on other planets.
我们的使命 我们的使命是构建使生命多行星化、理解宇宙真实本质并将意识之光延伸至群星所必需的系统与技术。为此，我们已组建地球（及地球之外）最具雄心、垂直整合度最高的创新引擎，具备无可比拟的能力，可以快速制造和发射连接全球的天基通信系统，利用太阳能驱动追求真相的人工智能以推动科学发现，并最终在月球建立基地、在其他行星建立城市。
Starship Flight Test Overview Founded in 2002, SpaceX is the only company building the integrated hardware and software infrastructure of the future across space, connectivity, and AI. At our core, we are builders. We design, manufacture, launch, and operate products and services built on cutting-edge technologies, including the world's most advanced rockets and spacecraft. We safely and reliably transport astronauts, satellites, and other payloads on missions that benefit life on Earth. Since 2023, we have launched more than 80% of mass to orbit for the world each year with an over 99% mission success rate with Falcon rockets. We also operate a high-speed, low-latency global broadband data and communications network powered by approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to millions of consumer, enterprise, and government customers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets, as of March 31, 2026. Using our dedicated satellite-to-mobile constellation, we offer connectivity services, supplementing terrestrial networks and substantially reducing mobile "dead zones" across approximately 30 countries.
Starship 试飞概览 SpaceX 成立于 2002 年，是全球唯一一家在太空、连接和 AI 三大领域构建未来一体化软硬件基础设施的公司。在本质上，我们是建造者。我们设计、制造、发射并运营基于尖端技术构建的产品与服务，包括世界上最先进的火箭和航天器。我们安全、可靠地为造福地球生命的任务运送宇航员、卫星和其他有效载荷。自 2023 年以来，我们每年以 Falcon 火箭承担了全球超过 80% 的入轨质量，任务成功率超过 99%。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们还运营着一个由约 9,600 颗低地球轨道 (LEO) Starlink 宽带与移动卫星驱动的高速、低延迟全球宽带数据与通信网络，向 164 个国家、地区及其他市场的数百万消费者、企业和政府客户提供连接服务。借助专用的星地直连卫星星座，我们提供连接服务，补充地面网络，并在约 30 个国家大幅减少手机”信号盲区”。
With the potential to improve both space exploration and life on Earth, AI accelerates SpaceX's mission to make life multiplanetary, to understand the true nature of the universe, and to extend the light of consciousness to the stars.
AI 在改善太空探索和地球生活两方面均具潜力，正加速 SpaceX 实现使生命多行星化、理解宇宙真实本质并将意识之光延伸至群星的使命。
xAI, which was founded in 2023 and acquired by SpaceX in early 2026, is now an integral pillar of our vertically integrated company. We are rapidly constructing AI compute infrastructure--starting on Earth with the goal of extending to space--at industry-leading pace and cost efficiency. Our infrastructure supports training and inference for Grok, which has emerged as one of the world's most advanced frontier models. Grok is designed as a truth- seeking AI model, built on our founder Elon Musk's mission to enable humanity to understand the universe. We believe that accomplishing this mission requires a truth-seeking approach to AI. We define truth seeking as the active, relentless pursuit of what is objectively true about reality, and grounded in evidence, logic, empirical data, and first principles thinking. Our goal is to understand and explain what the universe appears to be doing, as accurately as current knowledge allows. Within two years of its initial model release, Grok achieved frontier-level performance in scientific reasoning, as measured by its GPQA Diamond score, an industry benchmark that evaluates AI models on a standardized set of questions written and validated by experts, on a faster timeline than reported by other leading model providers. Grok also benefits from integration with X, our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform, which serves as a foundational distribution and data engine for our AI ecosystem and further enhances Grok's truth-seeking objective.
xAI 成立于 2023 年，并于 2026 年初被 SpaceX 收购，现已成为我们垂直整合公司不可或缺的支柱。我们正以行业领先的速度和成本效率快速建设 AI 计算基础设施——始于地球并以延伸至太空为目标。我们的基础设施支持 Grok 的训练与推理，Grok 已成为全球最先进的前沿模型之一。Grok 被设计为追求真相的 AI 模型，其根基源于创始人 Elon Musk 让人类理解宇宙的使命。我们认为完成这一使命需要以追求真相的方法对待 AI。我们将追求真相定义为：积极、不懈地探求客观真实，并以证据、逻辑、经验数据和第一性原理思考为根基。我们的目标是在当前知识允许的范围内，尽可能准确地理解和解释宇宙的运作。Grok 在其首次发布后两年内便在科学推理方面达到前沿水平（以 GPQA Diamond 分数衡量，该行业基准评估 AI 模型对一组由专家撰写并验证的标准化问题的回答），其速度快于其他领先模型提供商所报告的进度。Grok 还受益于与 X 的集成——X 是我们的实时信息、娱乐与言论自由平台，为我们的 AI 生态系统提供基础性的分发与数据引擎，并进一步强化 Grok 追求真相的目标。
We believe that space represents the largest economic frontier in human history, unlocking unprecedented opportunities in orbit and on Earth. Earth has limits, so we must build infrastructure and industries in space, expanding human capabilities to improve life on Earth and to establish life beyond. Connectivity infrastructure in space is designed to help everyone on Earth have access to education, healthcare, entertainment, and communications, and to enable people to overcome many traditional limits, such as physical and political borders.
我们认为，太空代表着人类历史上最大的经济疆域，可在轨道及地球上释放前所未有的机会。地球有其极限，因此我们必须在太空中建设基础设施和产业，扩展人类能力以改善地球生活，并在地球之外建立生命。太空中的连接基础设施旨在帮助地球上的每个人获取教育、医疗、娱乐和通信服务，使人们能够跨越物理与政治边界等许多传统限制。
We believe AI infrastructure in space can utilize the virtually limitless power of the Sun and thereby enable the use of AI as a transformative force for understanding the universe and improving the daily lives of all humans. We believe the convergence of these areas will enable an unprecedented expansion in the global economy, leading to an age of abundance. Our innovations and technological advancements are redefining industries on Earth, while we aim to create new ones on the Moon, Mars, and beyond. We are truly building the infrastructure of the future.
我们认为，太空中的 AI 基础设施可以利用近乎无穷的太阳能，从而使 AI 成为理解宇宙并改善全人类日常生活的变革性力量。我们相信，这些领域的融合将推动全球经济实现前所未有的扩张，进入富足时代。我们的创新和技术进步正在重新定义地球上的产业，同时我们致力于在月球、火星及更远处创造新的产业。我们正在真正地构建未来的基础设施。
SpaceX is the only company that has cracked the code on accessing space at scale, revolutionizing an industry characterized by decades of stagnation, risk aversion, and economically perverse cost structures. SpaceX upended this paradigm through the application of first-principles thinking, which rejects industry assumptions and builds solutions based on the fundamental laws of physics. Our intense, mission-driven, engineering-first culture and focus on extreme vertical integration have propelled us to achieve what many deemed impossible. We have demonstrated the ability to achieve groundbreaking technological innovations with speed, quality control, and precision. We pioneered high-cadence, reliable, and affordable access to space with our Falcon family of rockets, with a goal to transform the rocket launch industry into airline-like operations. In 2015, we established at least a 10-year lead over the industry by successfully landing our first Falcon 9 booster back from space before anyone else. We have continued to invest significantly in further increasing our lead by pursuing full and rapid reusability at scale, including investing over $15 billion in our next-generation rocket, Starship.
SpaceX 是唯一破解大规模进入太空之道的公司，颠覆了一个长期停滞、规避风险、成本结构严重扭曲的行业。SpaceX 运用第一性原理思考——拒绝行业既有假设，基于基本物理定律构建解决方案——颠覆了这一范式。我们高强度、使命驱动、工程优先的文化与对极致垂直整合的专注，使我们实现了许多人视为不可能的事。我们证明了以速度、质量控制和精度实现突破性技术创新的能力。我们以 Falcon 系列火箭开创了高频次、可靠、可负担的太空进入方式，目标是将火箭发射行业转变为类似航空公司的运营。2015 年，我们在所有同行之前成功从太空回收了首枚 Falcon 9 助推器，从而至少建立起对行业十年的领先。我们继续大力投入以进一步扩大领先优势，追求大规模的完全且快速可重复使用——包括向我们的下一代火箭 Starship 投入超过 150 亿美元。
We believe rocket launches and landings should be as routine and commonplace as airplanes taking off and landing.
我们认为，火箭发射与着陆应当像飞机起降一样常态化、日常化。
To achieve this sort of cadence, our iterative approach emphasizes rapid designing, testing, and process optimization, putting flight hardware in the flight environment as often as possible. This allows us to accelerate our learning by repeatedly using and improving our systems.
