This has resulted in a significantly higher flight rate at costs that are much lower than launch programs that existed before SpaceX. For example, according to NASA, the first version of Falcon 9 in 2010 had a launch cost of approximately $2,700 per kilogram, which represented a reduction of approximately 85% compared to the historical average launch cost per kilogram of $18,500. The first version of Falcon Heavy in 2018 further reduced this cost to approximately $1,400 per kilogram, a reduction of approximately 92% compared to the historical average cost. With the future deployment of Starship, which is designed to be the world's first fully and rapidly reusable spacecraft, we aim to further reduce the cost to reach orbit by 99% or more relative to the historical average launch cost. Central to our cost advantage is the reusability of key hardware--most notably boosters--which we recover, refurbish, and refly many times instead of discarding after single use. This dramatically lowers per-launch costs by minimizing hardware replacement expenses and spreading fixed production costs across repeated uses. Space flight that historically cost billions per launch now costs in the tens of millions, fundamentally reducing the cost of space access, providing the opportunity to build new enterprises in space.
为达到这样的飞行频率，我们的迭代方法强调快速设计、测试和流程优化，尽可能频繁地让飞行硬件进入飞行环境。这使我们能够通过反复使用和改进系统来加速学习。这带来了远高于 SpaceX 之前任何发射项目的飞行频率，而成本却显著更低。例如，根据 NASA 资料，2010 年首版 Falcon 9 的发射成本约为每千克 2,700 美元，相比每千克 18,500 美元的历史平均发射成本降低了约 85%。2018 年首版 Falcon Heavy 进一步将该成本降至约每千克 1,400 美元，相比历史平均成本降低约 92%。随着设计为全球首款完全可快速重复使用航天器的 Starship 在未来部署，我们计划将入轨成本相对历史平均发射成本进一步降低 99% 或以上。降低成本的核心是关键硬件——尤其是助推器——的可重复使用：我们回收、翻新并反复重飞，而非用一次即弃。这通过最小化硬件更换支出并将固定生产成本摊销至多次使用之上，大幅降低了单次发射成本。历史上每次发射动辄数十亿美元的太空飞行如今已降至数千万美元级，从根本上降低了太空进入成本，为在太空中创建新企业提供了机会。
Similarly, xAI has cracked the code in the complexities of building and scaling AI compute infrastructure, becoming the first company to deploy a coherent gigawatt-scale AI training cluster. We believe the combination of our proprietary AI infrastructure capability, our truth-seeking frontier model, Grok, and our access to real-time data on X creates a formidable competitive advantage, allowing us to maintain a leading position in the development of advanced artificial intelligence. This advantage stems from our complete vertical integration and the common vision infused by our founder, Elon Musk. In just a few years, we have demonstrated an ability to build coherent compute at scale and rapid speed with lower cost. COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. We believe speed is a competitive advantage. In order to bring compute clusters online as fast as possible, we employ a vertically integrated, nimble approach to construction. At COLOSSUS, we brought online the first cluster of approximately 100,000 H100 processors, approximately 130 megawatts of compute power, in just 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory. At COLOSSUS II, we brought online the first cluster of approximately 110,000 GB200 processors, approximately 210 megawatts of compute power, even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years.
类似地，xAI 已破解了构建并扩展 AI 计算基础设施这一复杂难题，成为首家部署吉瓦级一致性 AI 训练集群的公司。我们认为，自主 AI 基础设施能力、追求真相的前沿模型 Grok，以及对 X 实时数据的访问相结合，构成了我们强大的竞争优势，使我们在先进人工智能发展中保持领先地位。这一优势源自我们完整的垂直整合以及创始人 Elon Musk 注入公司的共同愿景。在短短数年内，我们便展示了以更低成本、快速大规模构建一致性算力的能力。COLOSSUS 与 COLOSSUS II 合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦的算力，另有额外电力容量用于数据中心运营。我们相信速度本身就是一种竞争优势。为尽快上线计算集群，我们采用垂直整合、灵活机动的建设方式。在 COLOSSUS，我们将一座现有工厂的厂房改造再利用，仅用 122 天就上线了约 10 万颗 H100 处理器、约 130 兆瓦算力的首个集群。在 COLOSSUS II，我们用更短的 91 天上线了约 11 万颗 GB200 处理器、约 210 兆瓦算力的首个集群。作为对照，行业基准是一座 100 兆瓦绿地数据中心通常需要约两年才能上线。
Furthermore, in the case of COLOSSUS II, following the initial cluster, we brought online the second cluster of 110,000 GB300 processors and 220 megawatts of compute power in 64 days, demonstrating our ability to rapidly scale our facilities once built. We expect that once fully operational, the next phase of expansion at COLOSSUS II will bring online at least 220,000 additional GB300 processors and over 400 additional megawatts of compute power. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis.
此外，在 COLOSSUS II，首个集群上线后，我们仅用 64 天便上线了 11 万颗 GB300 处理器、220 兆瓦算力的第二个集群，证明了一旦设施建成便能迅速扩张的能力。我们预计 COLOSSUS II 下一阶段全面运行后将额外上线至少 22 万颗 GB300 处理器、超过 400 兆瓦的算力。我们在成本效率上也实现了显著改善，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本（按每兆瓦计）显著低于行业基准。
We are able to deploy power and compute significantly faster than other AI companies through first-principles thinking, behind-the-meter power generation, coupled with what we believe is the world's largest network of sustainable battery storage systems, and innovations in advanced liquid cooling, high-density rack layouts, and efficient networking. Our facilities also incorporate innovative design features that limit the effects on regional electricity pricing for neighbors and include advanced water cleaning, reclamation, and recycling processes to support sustainable operations. We partner with utilities and communities to connect to and enhance the grid over time, and do so while pledging to cover costs of all new power delivery infrastructure upgrades to service our data centers, including adequate network upgrade costs, to ensure that these expenses are not passed on to the ordinary household. Our ability to rapidly and cost-effectively scale with the latest processors keeps us ahead of competitors who deploy traditional and more expensive methods. As a result, we believe COLOSSUS II became one of the world's first data centers to deploy GB200s and GB300s, the most advanced AI processors available at the time, at significant scale, and is currently powering training for our next frontier models, including Grok-5. Furthermore, through our Terafab initiative together with Tesla to build a manufacturing facility capable of producing 1 terawatt per year of compute hardware, we intend to further extend our vertical integration to chip design and manufacturing to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, optimize compute performance, and potentially reduce overall compute costs. Intel, which has the ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale, also joined the Terafab project in early April 2026. Our shovels-to-tokens approach allows us to train and iterate our frontier models at high velocity, accelerating development cycles, eliminating external bottlenecks, and driving rapid, continuous improvements in model performance.
通过第一性原理思考、表后自发电以及我们认为世界规模最大的可持续电池储能系统网络，加上先进液冷、高密度机柜布局与高效网络方面的创新，我们能比其他 AI 公司更快地部署电力与算力。我们的设施还采用创新设计以限制对周边地区电价的影响，并配备先进的水净化、回收与再利用工艺以支持可持续运营。我们与公用事业公司及社区合作，逐步接入并增强电网，并承诺承担为服务我们数据中心而进行的所有新增供电基础设施升级费用，包括足量的电网升级成本，以确保这些费用不会转嫁给普通家庭。我们能以更快速度、更低成本部署最新一代处理器，使我们领先于采用传统、更昂贵方法部署的竞争对手。因此，我们认为 COLOSSUS II 已成为全球率先大规模部署 GB200 和 GB300（当时最先进的 AI 处理器）的数据中心之一，并正在为我们下一代前沿模型（包括 Grok-5）的训练提供算力。此外，通过与 Tesla 共同推进 Terafab 计划，建设一座年产 1 太瓦算力硬件的制造工厂，我们打算将垂直整合进一步延伸至芯片设计与制造，以缓解 SpaceX 未来潜在的芯片短缺、优化算力性能，并可能整体降低算力成本。具备大规模设计、制造并封装超高性能芯片能力的 Intel 也于 2026 年 4 月初加入 Terafab 项目。我们”从铁锹到 token”的全链路方式使我们能以高速度训练并迭代前沿模型，加速开发周期、消除外部瓶颈，并推动模型性能的快速、持续提升。
We were the first private company to develop and launch a liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit with the successful launch of Falcon 1 in 2008. In 2019, we were the first to begin deploying a large-scale LEO broadband satellite constellation. In February 2026, we acquired xAI, the first company to build a gigawatt-scale AI training cluster and largest coherent supercomputer. The graphic below illustrates key milestones for our business.
2008 年 Falcon 1 成功发射，我们成为第一家研制并将液体燃料火箭送入轨道的民营公司。2019 年，我们率先开始部署大规模 LEO 宽带卫星星座。2026 年 2 月，我们收购了 xAI——首家构建吉瓦级 AI 训练集群和最大一致性超级计算机的公司。下图展示了我们业务的关键里程碑。
Our Repeatable Business Model Our business model is built on a repeatable, engineering-driven framework that combines our unparalleled launch capabilities, extreme vertical integration, rapid iteration, and disciplined capital investment to create durable, large- scale businesses. We execute this framework through the following core principles:
我们的可复制商业模式 我们的商业模式建立在一套可复制、工程驱动的框架之上，将无与伦比的发射能力、极致垂直整合、快速迭代与有纪律的资本投资相结合，以构建持久、大规模的业务。我们通过以下核心原则执行这一框架：
1.Leverage our unparalleled launch capabilities to enable massive scale. Our rockets--with unmatched launch cadence, best-in-class reliability, and dramatically reduced cost-to-orbit--are the foundation that we expect will enable us to create economic opportunities in space and deliver a diversified portfolio of services.
1. 借助无与伦比的发射能力实现大规模扩张。 我们的火箭——具备无可匹敌的发射频率、行业最佳的可靠性以及大幅降低的入轨成本——是我们预期能够在太空中创造经济机会并交付多元化服务组合的基础。
Our launch capabilities enable large-scale deployment of assets that would not otherwise be economically viable.
我们的发射能力使大规模部署原本经济上不可行的资产成为可能。
2.Identify and create new trillion-dollar market opportunities. We focus on market opportunities that are useful for humanity and that present trillion-dollar opportunities, including global broadband and mobile connectivity for consumers, enterprises, and governments; and AI applications and computational infrastructure.
2. 识别并创造新的万亿美元级市场机会。 我们专注于对人类有用且呈现万亿美元机会的市场，包括面向消费者、企业和政府的全球宽带与移动连接，以及 AI 应用与算力基础设施。
We prioritize opportunities where structural inefficiencies or legacy technological limitations have constrained supply.
我们优先选择因结构性低效或传统技术限制而供给受限的机会。
3.Design a solution with world-class engineering and first-principles thinking. We apply physics-based engineering and first-principles thinking to design products and systems from the ground up--boiling things down to the most fundamental truths and reasoning up from there. This helps us drive massive, step-function improvements in performance, scalability, and cost.
3. 以世界级工程和第一性原理思考设计解决方案。 我们运用基于物理的工程方法与第一性原理思考，从零开始设计产品与系统——将事物拆解到最基本的真相，并由此向上推理。这帮助我们在性能、可扩展性和成本上实现大规模、阶跃式的改进。
4.Apply "The Algorithm" (make less dumb, delete, optimize, accelerate, automate). We operate under a set of core execution principles that we refer to as "The Algorithm," a five-step iterative process that we use as our guiding principles day-to-day. We make the requirements less dumb, delete unnecessary processes or parts (embracing the principle that the best part is no part), only then optimize the necessary processes or parts, and then accelerate cycle time (many entities have launched once; no one other than us has ever launched over 100 times per year), and automate only proven processes after the first four steps are completed. We apply the Algorithm across every aspect of our organization, creating a cultural and operational standard of excellence that has defined SpaceX since inception.
4. 应用”算法”（让需求别傻、删减、优化、加速、自动化）。 我们在被我们称为”算法”（The Algorithm）的一套核心执行原则下运作——这是一套五步迭代流程，作为我们日常工作的指导原则。我们先让需求别傻，然后删除不必要的流程或零件（拥抱”最好的零件就是没有零件”的原则），然后再优化必要的流程或零件，接着加速循环时间（许多机构只发射过一次，除我们之外没有任何机构每年发射超过 100 次），最后只在前四步完成后才将经验证的流程自动化。我们将这一算法贯穿组织的方方面面，形成自 SpaceX 成立以来定义我们的文化与运营卓越标准。
5.Vertically integrate all the way to the end customer. We design and manufacture a significant portion of our components in-house, including engines, avionics, structures, and software, even producing the "tools that make the tools," enabling us to test, fail, and iterate rapidly. We can then release newer, more advanced hardware with speed and cost efficiency.
5. 直至终端客户的完全垂直整合。 我们自行设计和制造大部分组件，包括发动机、航电、结构和软件，甚至自制”用于制造工具的工具”，使我们能够快速测试、失败并迭代。然后我们可以以速度和成本效率发布更新、更先进的硬件。
6.Continuously drive cost down and throughput up. Through rocket reusability, manufacturing at scale, advanced automation, and rigorous operational discipline, we continuously reduce unit costs while increasing launch cadence, satellite network, and AI hosting capacity.
6. 持续降本增效。 通过火箭可重复使用、规模化制造、先进自动化和严格的运营纪律，我们在持续降低单位成本的同时，提升发射频率、卫星网络规模和 AI 托管能力。
7.Generate significant cash flow and reinvest in the future. As our businesses scale, they generate significant cash flow, which we reinvest into nascent market opportunities--driving a self-reinforcing cycle of constant innovation and potentially creating significant additional value.
7. 产生可观现金流并再投资于未来。 随着我们的业务规模扩大，会产生可观现金流，我们再将其投入新生市场机会——形成持续创新的自我强化循环，并可能创造大量额外价值。
Segments in Our Vertically-Integrated Innovation Engine We have three reportable segments in our vertically integrated innovation engine: Space, Connectivity, and AI. In our Space segment, we design, manufacture, and launch reusable rockets to provide high cadence, reliable, and affordable access to space at unprecedented scale. In our Connectivity segment, we operate a worldwide high-speed, low-latency broadband data and communications network powered by over 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to millions of consumer, enterprise, and government customers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets. In our AI segment, we operate a highly vertically integrated AI platform spanning our truth-seeking frontier model Grok, AI solutions for consumer and enterprise customers, X-- our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform--and AI computational infrastructure.
我们垂直整合创新引擎中的业务分部 我们的垂直整合创新引擎包含三个可报告分部 (reportable segments)：太空 (Space)、连接 (Connectivity) 和 AI。在太空分部，我们设计、制造和发射可重复使用的火箭，以前所未有的规模提供高频次、可靠且经济的太空进入。在连接分部，我们运营一个由超过 9,600 颗 LEO Starlink 宽带与移动卫星驱动的全球高速、低延迟宽带数据与通信网络，在 164 个国家、地区和其他市场为数百万消费者、企业和政府客户提供连接。在 AI 分部，我们运营一个高度垂直整合的 AI 平台，涵盖追求真相的前沿模型 Grok、面向消费者和企业客户的 AI 解决方案、X（我们的实时信息、娱乐与言论自由平台）以及 AI 算力基础设施。
Our financial results reflect the strength of our operating model and our ability to create and scale multiple new businesses:
我们的财务结果反映了运营模式的强大和我们创造并扩展多个新业务的能力：
•For the three months ended March 31, 2026, we generated revenue on a consolidated basis of $4,694 million, loss from operations of $(1,943) million and Adjusted EBITDA of $1,127 million. In 2025, we generated revenue on a consolidated basis of $18,674 million, loss from operations of $(2,589) million and Adjusted EBITDA of $6,584 million. Our Space and Connectivity segments contributed the substantial majority of our consolidated revenue in the three months ended March 31, 2026 and the year ended December 31, 2025, demonstrating the benefits of their scale and operating leverage in our vertically integrated business model;
• 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，我们在合并基础上实现收入 46.94 亿美元，经营亏损 (19.43) 亿美元，调整后 EBITDA 11.27 亿美元。2025 年，我们在合并基础上实现收入 186.74 亿美元，经营亏损 (25.89) 亿美元，调整后 EBITDA 65.84 亿美元。在截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月以及截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日的全年，我们的太空与连接分部贡献了合并收入的大部分，体现了垂直整合商业模式中规模化与运营杠杆带来的益处；
•For the three months ended March 31, 2026, our Space segment generated revenue of $619 million, loss from operations of $(662) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(351) million. In 2025, our Space segment generated revenue of $4,086 million, loss from operations of $(657) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $653 million. Additionally, our Space segment funded $930 million and $3,004 million in research and development expense during the three months ended March 31, 2026 and the year ended December 31, 2025, respectively, for our next-generation Starship launch vehicle program. Starship is designed to enable a step- function change in our launch capability across reusability, payload capacity, and launch cadence, and is the key enabler of our long-term growth strategy by unlocking entirely new categories of missions;
• 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，我们的太空分部实现收入 6.19 亿美元，经营亏损 (6.62) 亿美元，分部调整后 EBITDA (3.51) 亿美元。2025 年，太空分部实现收入 40.86 亿美元，经营亏损 (6.57) 亿美元，分部调整后 EBITDA 6.53 亿美元。此外，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月和 2025 年全年，太空分部分别为下一代 Starship 运载火箭计划投入研发费用 9.30 亿美元和 30.04 亿美元。Starship 旨在使我们的发射能力在可重复使用性、有效载荷能力和发射频率上实现阶跃式跃迁，是我们长期增长战略的关键推动者，可以解锁全新类别的任务；
•For the three months ended March 31, 2026, our Connectivity segment generated revenue of $3,257 million, income from operations of $1,188 million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $2,087 million. Our Connectivity segment, primarily driven by Starlink, generated revenue of $11,387 million, income from operations of $4,423 million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $7,168 million in 2025, representing year-over-year growth of 49.8%, 120.4%, and 86.2%, respectively, benefiting from subscriber growth, increasing enterprise adoption, and continued improvement in network efficiency;
• 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，我们的连接分部实现收入 32.57 亿美元，经营利润 11.88 亿美元，分部调整后 EBITDA 20.87 亿美元。主要由 Starlink 驱动的连接分部 2025 年实现收入 113.87 亿美元、经营利润 44.23 亿美元和分部调整后 EBITDA 71.68 亿美元，分别同比增长 49.8%、120.4% 和 86.2%，受益于订阅用户增长、企业采用提升以及网络效率持续改善；
•In our newly acquired AI segment, we plan to prioritize growth and investment to capture significant opportunities in AI applications and compute infrastructure. For the three months ended March 31, 2026, our AI segment generated revenue of $818 million, loss from operations of $(2,469) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(609) million. In 2025, our AI segment generated revenue of $3,201 million, loss from operations of $(6,355) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(1,237) million, reflecting its earlier stage of development and continued investments to support long-term growth opportunities in AI; and •For the three months ended March 31, 2026, capital expenditures for our Space segment was $1,052 million, for our Connectivity segment was $1,332 million and for our AI segment was $7,723 million. In 2025, capital expenditures for our Space segment was $3,832 million, for our Connectivity segment was $4,178 million and for our AI segment was $12,727 million.
• 在新近收购的 AI 分部，我们计划优先推动增长与投资，以把握 AI 应用和算力基础设施中的重大机会。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，AI 分部实现收入 8.18 亿美元，经营亏损 (24.69) 亿美元，分部调整后 EBITDA (6.09) 亿美元。2025 年，AI 分部实现收入 32.01 亿美元，经营亏损 (63.55) 亿美元，分部调整后 EBITDA (12.37) 亿美元，反映其较早期的发展阶段以及为支持 AI 长期增长机会而进行的持续投资；以及 • 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，我们的资本性支出 (capital expenditures) 中，太空分部为 10.52 亿美元，连接分部为 13.32 亿美元，AI 分部为 77.23 亿美元。2025 年，资本性支出中，太空分部为 38.32 亿美元，连接分部为 41.78 亿美元，AI 分部为 127.27 亿美元。
Segment Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP measure. Please refer to the section titled "--Non-GAAP Financial Measures" for additional information on our non-GAAP financial measures, including reconciliations of Segment Adjusted EBITDA to segment income (loss) from operations, the most directly comparable GAAP measure.
分部调整后 EBITDA 是非 GAAP 指标。请参阅”——非 GAAP 财务指标 (Non-GAAP Financial Measures)“一节了解关于我们非 GAAP 财务指标的更多信息，包括分部调整后 EBITDA 与最直接可比的 GAAP 指标——分部经营利润（亏损）——之间的勾稽 (reconciliation)。
Space Since our founding in 2002, SpaceX has cracked the code on accessing space at scale, transforming an industry characterized by decades of stagnation, risk aversion, and economically perverse cost structures. We design, manufacture, launch, and refurbish reusable launch vehicles that provide cost-efficient, reliable, and high-cadence access to space for our own purposes as well as for third-party commercial and government customers. In 2025, we launched from four primary launch pads in the United States and successfully recovered boosters across seven landing facilities including autonomous drone ships and catch towers based on the vehicle type and mission profile.
太空业务 自 2002 年成立以来，SpaceX 已破解大规模进入太空的难题，颠覆了一个长期停滞、规避风险、成本结构严重扭曲的行业。我们设计、制造、发射并翻新可重复使用的运载火箭，为自身业务及第三方商业和政府客户提供经济高效、可靠和高频次的太空进入。2025 年，我们从美国境内四个主要发射场进行了发射，并根据飞行器类型和任务剖面，在包括自主无人驳船和接收塔在内的七个着陆设施成功回收助推器。
Our extensive vertical integration and end-to-end control over the entire value chain, from design to launch to operations, allows us to achieve unprecedented speed and cost efficiency.
我们广泛的垂直整合以及对从设计到发射、运营全价值链的端到端控制，使我们能够实现前所未有的速度和成本效率。
Falcon 9 First Stage Booster Landing As of March 31, 2026, SpaceX had launched a total mass to orbit of approximately 7,400 metric tons with an over 99% mission success rate across our Falcon rockets. We have completed approximately 650 orbital space launches, and over 540 of those launches were completed by a flight-proven Falcon rocket. In 2025 alone, SpaceX completed 170 missions across Falcon and Starship vehicles and 159 flight-proven booster launches with an over 99% success rate on attempted booster recoveries. We launched over 2,200 metric tons, representing over 80% of mass to orbit for the world in 2025. With the first successful launch of Falcon 1 in 2008, we became the first private company to successfully launch a liquid-fueled rocket to Earth's orbit. Just two years later, in 2010, the commercial debut of the Falcon 9 rocket revolutionized space access by delivering unprecedented cost efficiency. For example, according to NASA, the first version of Falcon 9 in 2010 reduced launch cost to approximately $2,700 per kilogram, which represented a reduction of approximately 85% compared to the historical average launch cost per kilogram of $18,500. The first version of Falcon Heavy in 2018 further reduced this cost to $1,400 per kilogram, a reduction of approximately 92% compared to the historical average. We have also reduced our internal cost of launch through a combination of engineering improvements, manufacturing efficiencies, and economies of scale--most notably, through our ability to drive more frequent reuse of rockets.
Falcon 9 一级助推器着陆 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，SpaceX 用 Falcon 系列火箭累计将约 7,400 公吨质量送入轨道，任务成功率超过 99%。我们已完成约 650 次轨道航天发射，其中超过 540 次由经过飞行验证的 Falcon 火箭完成。仅 2025 年一年，SpaceX 就在 Falcon 与 Starship 飞行器上完成了 170 次任务，其中 159 次为经过飞行验证的助推器发射，助推器回收尝试的成功率超过 99%。2025 年我们发射的入轨质量超过 2,200 公吨，占全球当年入轨质量的 80% 以上。2008 年 Falcon 1 首次成功发射，我们成为第一家成功将液体燃料火箭送入地球轨道的民营公司。仅两年后，即 2010 年 Falcon 9 火箭的商业首飞，通过提供前所未有的成本效率彻底变革了太空进入方式。例如，据 NASA 资料，2010 年的首版 Falcon 9 将发射成本降至约每千克 2,700 美元，相比每千克 18,500 美元的历史平均发射成本降低约 85%。2018 年首版 Falcon Heavy 将该成本进一步降至每千克 1,400 美元，相比历史平均降低约 92%。我们还通过工程改进、制造效率与规模经济的结合——尤其是通过更频繁地重复使用火箭——降低了内部发射成本。
We generate Space revenue primarily through launch and mission services of Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, and Dragon provided to commercial and government customers. We fly to LEO, MEO, GEO, lunar, and interplanetary trajectories, as well as the International Space Station. Our Space segment revenue is derived from fixed-price contracts related to the development and provision of launch services for both commercial customers and governmental agency space programs, either at a "point in time" or "over time."
我们的太空收入 (revenue) 主要来自向商业和政府客户提供的 Falcon 9、Falcon Heavy 和 Dragon 的发射与任务服务。我们可执飞 LEO、MEO、GEO、月球及行星际轨道，以及国际空间站任务。我们太空分部的收入来源于与商业客户及政府机构太空项目相关的发射服务开发与提供的固定价格合同，按”时点法 (point in time)“或”时段法 (over time)“确认。
We manage our Space segment to support our businesses and those of our customers. We plan launches and allocate payloads in advance, although it can be difficult to manage the timing of customer payload arrivals. When an expected customer payload for a planned launch is not available, we instead use launch capacity for our satellites. As a result, we adjust expected launch payloads frequently, impacting period-to-period financial comparison. For a majority of customer payloads, revenue and costs are primarily recognized at the launch or deployment of the customer's spacecraft to its intended orbit, with some revenues and costs being recognized over time. For launches dedicated to deploying our Starlink satellites, we capitalize the associated costs within our Connectivity segment and depreciate them over time, and we do not recognize revenue for those launches in our Space segment. We allocate a significant amount of launch capacity to our Connectivity segment, and expect to allocate a significant amount to our AI segment in the future. Our Space segment revenue only reflects customer launches and other customer activities. As a result, notwithstanding an increasing launch cadence, our Space segment has relatively lower revenue scale and revenue growth compared to our other segments, though its financial results do not reflect the foundational strategic value that it provides to us in bolstering the growth of our Connectivity and AI segments.
我们对太空分部进行管理以支持自身及客户业务。我们提前规划发射并分配有效载荷，尽管管理客户有效载荷到货时机可能较为困难。当计划发射的预期客户载荷不可用时，我们会用该发射能力发射自有卫星。因此，我们经常调整预期发射载荷，从而影响期间间的财务可比性。对于大部分客户载荷，收入和成本主要在客户航天器被发射或部署至预定轨道时确认，部分收入和成本按时段法确认。对于专门用于部署 Starlink 卫星的发射，我们将相关成本资本化进入连接分部并按时间折旧，且不在太空分部确认该次发射的收入。我们将大量发射能力分配给连接分部，并预计未来将向 AI 分部分配大量发射能力。我们的太空分部收入仅反映客户发射及其他客户活动。因此，尽管发射频率在增加，但相比其他分部，太空分部的收入规模和收入增长相对较低，其财务结果并未反映它在支撑我们连接与 AI 分部增长方面所提供的基础性战略价值。
Connectivity. Starlink provides global access to high-speed internet, including underserved rural and remote communities worldwide. As of March 31, 2026, we had approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, providing broadband connectivity to approximately 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets. We also provide satellite-to-mobile texting and over-the-top voice services to approximately 7.4 million monthly unique devices across approximately 30 countries.
连接业务。 Starlink 提供高速互联网的全球接入，包括服务全球范围内服务不足的农村和偏远社区。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们在 LEO 部署了约 9,600 颗 Starlink 宽带与移动卫星，为 164 个国家、地区和其他市场约 1,030 万 Starlink 订阅用户 (Subscribers) 提供宽带连接。我们还在约 30 个国家向约 740 万月度独立设备提供卫星到手机的短信和 OTT 语音服务。
Starlink Mini •Starlink Consumer Broadband. We operate the world's largest and most advanced space-based internet broadband service with median latency at approximately 25 milliseconds as of March 31, 2026. We provide fiber-like download speeds--at a median of 225 Mbps during peak hours for residential users as of March 31, 2026--and the technological capability to provide service everywhere on Earth, including the poles. This service quality is enabled by our vast network of approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, which accounted for approximately 75% of all active maneuverable satellites in orbit as of March 31, 2026. We expect to commence deploying our next-generation V3 satellites, designed to offer one Tbps of downlink capacity per satellite, using Starship in the second half of 2026. We expect that a single Starship launch will be capable of deploying up to 60 V3 satellites to LEO, representing a potential twenty-fold increase in Starlink downlink capacity deployed relative to a Falcon 9 launch. As of March 31, 2026, we had approximately 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers, up approximately 105% from 5.0 million subscribers a year prior. We charge our Starlink Subscribers a monthly subscription fee, which varies based on geographic market and download speed, plus typically a one-time upfront terminal cost.
Starlink Mini • Starlink 消费者宽带。 我们运营着全球规模最大、最先进的天基互联网宽带服务，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，中位延迟约为 25 毫秒。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们为住宅用户提供光纤级的下载速度——高峰时段中位数为 225 Mbps，并具备在地球任何地方（包括两极）提供服务的技术能力。这一服务质量得益于我们由约 9,600 颗 LEO Starlink 宽带与移动卫星组成的庞大网络，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，这些卫星约占在轨全部活动可机动卫星的 75%。我们预计将于 2026 年下半年开始使用 Starship 部署下一代 V3 卫星，其设计目标是单颗卫星提供 1 Tbps 下行容量。我们预计单次 Starship 发射可将多达 60 颗 V3 卫星送入 LEO，相对于一次 Falcon 9 发射，Starlink 部署下行容量有望提升约二十倍。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们拥有约 1,030 万 Starlink 订阅用户，相比一年前的 500 万订阅用户增长约 105%。我们向 Starlink 订阅用户收取月度订阅费，金额因地理市场和下载速度而异，外加通常一次性的终端预付费用。
•Enterprise Solutions. SpaceX is a critical partner to a wide array of enterprises. We offer Starlink's high- speed, low-latency, reliable internet services to enterprise customers across industries including construction, agriculture, retail, telecom, hospitality, aviation, maritime, and land mobility. Starlink's unique capabilities are well‑suited for deployments across field offices, remote worksites, research stations, drilling rigs, rural hospitals, aircraft, cruise ships, trains, and hotels. Our enterprise customers include companies such as United Airlines, Carnival, Maersk, and John Deere, among others. We also serve a broad fixed‑site customer base across industries such as retail and financial services that require high availability for critical operations as well as reliable connectivity in remote or hard-to-serve locations. As companies continue to invest in secure and resilient networks and backup systems to keep critical infrastructure online--such as point‑of‑sale and payment processing systems--we often start as a backup solution and then transition to being the primary solution. Our enterprise contracts are based on a combination of subscriptions, data consumption, capacity, or other pricing models depending on each customer's particular needs. Since 2023, no Starlink Enterprise customer having contributed more than $750,000 of annual revenue has voluntarily discontinued their service, demonstrating the strong performance and value of our offering. This is despite the ability of our customers to cancel the service at any time.
• 企业解决方案。 SpaceX 是众多企业的关键合作伙伴。我们为建筑、农业、零售、电信、酒店、航空、海事和陆上交通等行业的企业客户提供 Starlink 高速、低延迟、可靠的互联网服务。Starlink 的独特能力非常适合野外办公室、偏远工地、研究站、钻井平台、农村医院、飞机、邮轮、火车和酒店等场景的部署。我们的企业客户包括 United Airlines、Carnival、Maersk 和 John Deere 等公司。我们也服务于零售和金融服务等需要为关键运营提供高可用性以及在偏远或难以服务地点保持可靠连接的固定站点客户。随着公司继续投资于安全可靠的网络和备份系统以保持销售点和支付处理等关键基础设施在线，我们经常先作为备用方案进入，然后转为主用方案。我们的企业合同根据每位客户的具体需求，采用订阅、数据用量、容量或其他定价模式的组合。自 2023 年以来，任何年收入贡献超过 75 万美元的 Starlink 企业客户均未主动停止服务，体现了我们服务的强劲表现与价值。这是在客户可以随时取消服务的情况下取得的。
•Government Solutions. For our government customers, we provide high-speed, resilient connectivity for public services, social impact, humanitarian efforts, and disaster response in even the most remote and challenging environments. Examples include support for the FEMA in coordinating disaster recovery after hurricanes and wildfires, the NOAA for at-sea testing and environmental monitoring, the Government of the Philippines for linking remote islands, schools, and public institutions, the Government of Jamaica for improving digital access in remote and maritime areas, and the Government of Ecuador for supporting education and healthcare connectivity in isolated communities. Separately with Starshield, we have leveraged our commercial LEO satellite constellation engineering learnings and operational experiences to develop a secure, dedicated satellite network designed specifically for United States Government customers and national security applications.
• 政府解决方案。 对于政府客户，我们在最偏远和最具挑战的环境中提供高速、有韧性的连接，用于公共服务、社会影响、人道援助及灾害响应。例如，为 FEMA 协调飓风和野火后的灾后恢复提供支持，为 NOAA 提供海上测试与环境监测，为菲律宾政府连接偏远岛屿、学校和公共机构，为牙买加政府改善偏远和海事区域的数字接入，以及为厄瓜多尔政府支持偏远社区的教育和医疗连接。另外，我们通过 Starshield，将商业 LEO 卫星星座的工程经验与运营经验，发展为专为美国政府客户和国家安全应用而设计的安全专用卫星网络。
•Starlink Mobile. We provide satellite-to-mobile connectivity, supplementing terrestrial networks and substantially reducing mobile "dead zones" across approximately 30 countries. We partner with MNOs including major wireless carriers like T-Mobile in the United States, and other international operators including One NZ, Optus, Telstra, Rogers, KDDI, Salt, Entel, Kyivstar, and VMO2. Through these partnerships, we enable consumers, businesses, and public-sector customers to use their existing phones in more places, support critical connectivity during disasters and power outages, and open new applications for low-bandwidth mobile and IoT devices. Our current capabilities under our "V1" constellation (consisting of approximately 650 V1 Mobile satellites in orbit) include light data, text messaging (SMS), and over-the-top voice services (e.g., WhatsApp and FaceTime). We are developing more comprehensive satellite-to-mobile services, including broadband data and IoT connectivity, which are expected to deliver resilient, infrastructure-independent connectivity worldwide and enable 5G connectivity. We have partnerships with approximately 30 MNOs on six continents, covering an area that is home to approximately 1.9 billion people. We charge MNOs either a fixed fee or a per-mobile user fee-based amount, which is typically passed through to the customer via the carrier as an "add-on" feature.
• Starlink Mobile。 我们提供星地直连移动连接，补充地面网络并在约 30 个国家大幅减少手机”信号盲区”。我们与移动网络运营商 (MNO) 合作，包括美国的主要无线运营商 T-Mobile，以及 One NZ、Optus、Telstra、Rogers、KDDI、Salt、Entel、Kyivstar 和 VMO2 等国际运营商。通过这些合作关系，我们使消费者、企业和公共部门客户能够在更多地点使用现有手机，在灾害和停电期间支持关键连接，并为低带宽移动和 IoT 设备开启新的应用。我们目前在 V1 星座（由在轨约 650 颗 V1 移动卫星组成）下的能力包括轻量数据、短消息 (SMS) 和 OTT 语音服务（例如 WhatsApp 和 FaceTime）。我们正在开发更全面的星地直连移动服务，包括宽带数据和 IoT 连接，预计将提供有韧性、独立于地面基础设施的全球连接并支持 5G 连接。我们在六大洲与约 30 家 MNO 合作，覆盖约 19 亿人口居住的区域。我们向 MNO 收取固定费用或按移动用户计费，通常由运营商作为”附加”功能转嫁给客户。
We generate revenue in our Connectivity segment primarily through subscription fees from consumer subscribers.
我们连接分部的收入主要来自消费者订阅用户的订阅费。
We drive consumer revenue through monthly subscription fees based on geographic market and download speed, recognizing revenue ratably over the service period, plus typically a one-time sale of a kit. In addition, we generate revenue from enterprises through contracts structured as a combination of subscriptions, data consumption, and capacity, or on a percentage-of-completion basis, depending on each customer's particular needs. We generate government revenue via long term contracts for Starshield, a secure satellite network designed specifically for government customers and national security applications. We also earn Starlink Mobile revenue through revenue- sharing arrangements with MNO partners, based on connectivity services included in their plans.
我们根据地理市场和下载速度按月订阅费向消费者收费，并在服务期内按时段比例确认收入，外加通常一次性的套件销售。此外，我们根据每位企业客户的具体需求，通过订阅、数据用量与容量的组合，或按完工百分比 (percentage-of-completion) 的方式确认企业收入。我们通过专为政府客户和国家安全应用设计的安全卫星网络 Starshield 的长期合同，获得政府收入。我们还通过与 MNO 合作伙伴的收入分成安排，按其套餐中包含的连接服务获得 Starlink Mobile 收入。
In 2025, revenue from consumer subscribers represented over 60% of Connectivity segment revenue. We expect revenue from consumer subscribers, as well as enterprise and government customers, to be the primary driver of Connectivity segment growth, and that Starlink Mobile will become a significant new contributor of Connectivity segment revenue.
2025 年，消费者订阅用户收入占连接分部收入的 60% 以上。我们预期消费者订阅用户以及企业和政府客户的收入将是连接分部增长的主要驱动力，且 Starlink Mobile 将成为连接分部收入的重要新贡献来源。
AI. We operate a highly vertically integrated AI platform spanning gigawatt-scale AI compute infrastructure, our truth-seeking frontier AI model, Grok, AI solutions for consumer and enterprise customers, and X, our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform. We believe AI is rapidly converging toward AGI, where human cognitive capabilities can be replicated and scaled at machine speeds, profoundly augmenting human productivity. Once an AGI system exists, its true value derives from the ability to create limitless duplicates of human-like intelligence, necessitating vast computational resources and cost-efficient deployment to achieve meaningful scale. Without large-scale, power-efficient infrastructure, AGI cannot be deployed broadly or economically--making such infrastructure a critical strategic differentiator.
AI 业务。 我们运营一个高度垂直整合的 AI 平台，涵盖吉瓦级 AI 算力基础设施、追求真相的前沿 AI 模型 Grok、面向消费者和企业客户的 AI 解决方案，以及 X——我们的实时信息、娱乐与言论自由平台。我们认为 AI 正迅速向 AGI 收敛——届时人类的认知能力可以被复制并以机器速度扩展，从而显著放大人类生产力。一旦存在 AGI 系统，其真正价值来自其创造无数类人智能副本的能力，因此需要海量算力以及成本高效的部署才能实现有意义的规模。如果缺乏大规模、高能效的基础设施，AGI 就无法被广泛或经济地部署——这使得此类基础设施成为关键的战略差异化要素。
COLOSSUS II Facility in Memphis, Tennessee •AI Compute Infrastructure. xAI has established a leading position in building and scaling terrestrial AI compute infrastructure, becoming the first company to deploy a coherent gigawatt-scale AI training cluster. Our AI compute facilities, COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II, collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. Our first-principles thinking enables us to build coherent compute at scale and at rapid speed with lower costs than most other companies in the industry. We brought the first cluster of COLOSSUS online in 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory, and the first cluster of COLOSSUS II online even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis. This dual speed and cost advantage stems from our complete vertical integration and the shared culture infused by our founder, Mr. Musk, across our Space, Connectivity, and AI segments. The addition of Terafab, an initiative together with Tesla to build a manufacturing facility capable of producing 1 terawatt per year of compute hardware, aims to further extend our vertical integration to chip design and manufacturing to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, optimize compute performance, and potentially reduce overall compute costs.
位于田纳西州孟菲斯的 COLOSSUS II 设施 • AI 算力基础设施。 xAI 在地面 AI 算力基础设施的建设与扩展方面已建立领先地位，成为首家部署吉瓦级一致性 AI 训练集群的公司。我们的 AI 算力设施 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦算力，另有额外电力容量可用于数据中心运营。我们的第一性原理思考使我们能以低于业内大多数公司的成本，大规模、快速地构建一致性算力。COLOSSUS 第一个集群我们用 122 天上线（复用了一座现有工厂的厂房），COLOSSUS II 第一个集群上线更快，仅用 91 天。作为对照，行业基准是一座 100 兆瓦绿地数据中心通常需要约两年才能上线。我们在成本效率上也实现显著改进，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本（按每兆瓦计）显著低于行业基准。这种速度与成本双重优势，源自我们的完整垂直整合，以及创始人 Musk 先生在太空、连接和 AI 三大分部注入的共享文化。Terafab——我们与 Tesla 共同推进、年产 1 太瓦算力硬件的制造工厂——的加入旨在将垂直整合进一步延伸至芯片设计与制造，以缓解 SpaceX 未来潜在的芯片短缺、优化算力性能并可能整体降低算力成本。
Intel, which has the ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale, has also joined the Terafab project. We believe that the key constraints in the continued growth of AI are physical--chip manufacturing, data center infrastructure, and power generation; the future of AI will be determined by the control of the physical stack.
具备大规模设计、制造并封装超高性能芯片能力的 Intel 也已加入 Terafab 项目。我们认为，AI 持续增长的关键约束在于物理层面——芯片制造、数据中心基础设施和发电能力；AI 的未来将由对物理堆栈的掌控决定。
•Truth-Seeking Frontier Model. xAI has developed one of the world's most advanced, truth-seeking frontier models with Grok. Since launching Grok-1 in November 2023, we have released four major versions and notable variations thereof, achieving one of the fastest iteration cycles in the industry, culminating in Grok-4.3 (April 2026). Building on this trajectory, we expect to continue scaling Grok through subsequent generations.
• 追求真相的前沿模型。 xAI 凭借 Grok 开发出全球最先进、追求真相的前沿模型之一。自 2023 年 11 月推出 Grok-1 以来，我们已发布四个主要版本及其重要变体，实现了行业内最快的迭代周期之一，并以 Grok-4.3（2026 年 4 月）为最新成果。基于这一轨迹，我们预计将继续在后续世代中扩展 Grok。
Ongoing training of next‑generation models is expected to scale toward multiple trillions of parameters, which could represent a step change in reasoning in depth and overall intelligence. In this context, the number of parameters refers to the scale of the model, where parameters are the internal numerical values, such as "weights," that are adjusted during training to enable the model to recognize patterns and relationships in data.
下一代模型的持续训练预计将扩展至数万亿参数 (parameters)，这可能代表着推理深度和整体智能上的阶跃式变化。在此语境下，参数数量指模型的规模，参数即在训练过程中被调整以使模型识别数据中模式和关系的内部数值（如”权重”）。
A larger number of parameters generally allows the model to capture more complex relationships, store greater amounts of knowledge, and achieve higher levels of reasoning capability. Within two years of its initial model release, Grok achieved frontier-level performance in scientific reasoning, as measured by its GPQA Diamond score, an industry benchmark that evaluates AI models on a standardized set of questions written and validated by experts, on a faster timeline than reported by other leading model providers. This accelerated rate of innovation stems from our highly vertically integrated stack: full ownership of training infrastructure, access to the world's most powerful compute clusters, and relentless focus on truth seeking and real-world utility. A key competitive differentiator is Grok's deep integration with X, enabling proprietary access to a real-time information stream of approximately 350 million daily posts, which enhances freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness for Grok. This direct, real-time access to the information and human discourse on X enhances Grok's truth-seeking capabilities by grounding outputs in up-to-date knowledge and diverse viewpoints. We believe that this combination of compute infrastructure scale and the massive dataset available to us through X, subject to some limitations for certain content, has allowed us to achieve industry-leading performance and provide model outputs that analyze real-time information on global events. We expect that our compute infrastructure and direct access to real-time data via X constitute substantial performance advantages for Grok that will result in increasingly rapid and dramatic iteration cycles.
更多的参数通常使模型能够捕捉更复杂的关系、储存更多知识并达到更高的推理能力。Grok 在其首次发布后两年内便在科学推理方面达到前沿水平（以 GPQA Diamond 分数衡量，该行业基准评估 AI 模型对一组由专家撰写并验证的标准化问题的回答），其速度快于其他领先模型提供商所报告的进度。这种加速创新源自我们高度垂直整合的堆栈：完全自有训练基础设施、可访问全球最强算力集群，以及对追求真相和现实效用的不懈专注。一个关键的竞争差异化要素是 Grok 与 X 的深度集成——使我们能专有访问每日约 3.5 亿条帖子的实时信息流，从而增强 Grok 的时效性、相关性与情境感知能力。这种对 X 上信息和人类对话的直接、实时访问，通过将输出根植于最新知识与多元观点，强化了 Grok 的求真能力。我们认为，算力基础设施的规模与通过 X 可获取的庞大数据集（对某些内容存在一定限制）相结合，使我们能够实现行业领先的性能，并提供能分析全球事件实时信息的模型输出。我们预计算力基础设施和通过 X 直接获取实时数据将构成 Grok 的重要性能优势，进而实现日益迅速和显著的迭代周期。
•Consumer and Enterprise Applications. We leverage our leading frontier models and compute infrastructure to deliver consumer and enterprise applications. In under six months, we developed Grok Voice, a real-time speech engine, including in multilingual performance. Our image and video generation system, Imagine, produced approximately 10 billion images and over 2 billion videos per month, on average, for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. Together with Tesla, we are also developing Macrohard, an agentic AI platform designed to be capable of fully emulating digital workflows and augmenting human operation of computers-- from coding and product development to management and entire business processes--using sophisticated autonomous agents. We believe Macrohard will have the potential to fundamentally transform how companies are structured and operate, thereby allowing dramatic increases in human productivity. In addition, we believe our existing government relationships and track record as large government contractors are a structural advantage as governments become significant consumers of AI applications.
• 消费者与企业应用。 我们利用领先的前沿模型和算力基础设施，向消费者和企业交付应用。在不到六个月内，我们开发了 Grok Voice 这一实时语音引擎，包括多语言性能。我们的图像和视频生成系统 Imagine，在截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的季度中，平均每月生成约 100 亿张图像和超过 20 亿个视频。我们还与 Tesla 共同开发 Macrohard——一个 agentic AI 平台，旨在通过复杂的自主智能体完全模拟数字工作流并增强人类对计算机的操作，从编码、产品开发到管理及整个业务流程。我们相信 Macrohard 有潜力从根本上变革公司的组织和运营方式，从而大幅提升人类生产力。此外，我们认为，随着政府成为 AI 应用的重要用户，我们已有的政府关系以及作为大型政府承包商的过往业绩，构成了我们的结构性优势。
Our integrated AI platforms across Grok and X have over 1.3 billion supported accounts active in the last twelve months ended March 31, 2026, including approximately 550 million MAUs, up from over 1.1 billion supported accounts and approximately 520 million MAUs as of December 31, 2025. Of our MAUs, we had approximately 117 million MAUs that used Grok's AI features as of March 31, 2026. While MAUs provide an estimated measure of the size and engagement of our user base, we are focused on revenue and operating margin, and manage our business with the objective of driving sustainable revenue growth and profitability rather than with the primary objective of growing or maintaining MAU levels.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的过去 12 个月内，我们在 Grok 与 X 上的整合 AI 平台拥有超过 13 亿支持账户处于活跃状态，包括约 5.5 亿月活跃用户 (MAU)，相比 2025 年 12 月 31 日的 11 亿支持账户和约 5.2 亿 MAU 有所增长。在 MAU 中，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，约 1.17 亿 MAU 使用了 Grok 的 AI 功能。虽然 MAU 提供了对用户基础规模与参与度的估算性度量，但我们关注收入和经营利润率，并以推动可持续收入增长与盈利能力为目标管理业务，而不以增长或维持 MAU 水平为主要目标。
We also monetize user activity through high-impact advertising inventory on X. We believe X's scale, real-time engagement, and integration with Grok provide a differentiated foundation for building a unified user experience across communication, content discovery, commerce, and financial services, among others. For enterprises that advertise on X, we offer large-scale user engagement, real-time content, and advanced AI- driven performance marketing tools. For enterprises, we offer tailored deployments of Grok customized to specific workflows and security needs through Grok Business and Grok Enterprise, sold on license-, consumption-, or outcome-based pricing models.
我们还通过 X 上具有高影响力的广告库存对用户活动进行变现。我们认为 X 的规模、实时参与度及与 Grok 的集成，为构建跨通信、内容发现、商业和金融服务等的统一用户体验提供了独特基础。对于在 X 上投放广告的企业，我们提供大规模用户参与、实时内容以及先进的 AI 驱动效果营销工具。对于企业客户，我们通过 Grok Business 和 Grok Enterprise 提供针对特定工作流和安全需求定制部署的 Grok，按许可证、用量或绩效定价模式销售。
Our Capital Allocation and Funding Strategy Since our beginning, we have managed through multiple investment cycles. We initially raised capital to fund what is now our Space segment, which generates revenue from commercial and government customers while serving as the backbone for our Connectivity segment. We invested in our Connectivity segment as we generated Segment Adjusted EBITDA from our Space segment, along with additional equity capital that we raised externally, creating a segment that generates predictable and recurring revenue from consumer, enterprise, and government customers. We continue to invest meaningfully in both our Space and Connectivity segments to build out the infrastructure of the future through our next-generation Starship launch platform and our expanded Starlink broadband and mobility networks.
我们的资本配置与融资策略 自创立以来，我们经历了多个投资周期。我们最初募集资本以资助现在的太空分部——该分部从商业和政府客户处产生收入，同时也是连接分部的支柱。在太空分部产生分部调整后 EBITDA 的同时，我们外部募集了额外股权资本，对连接分部进行投资，使其成为面向消费者、企业和政府客户提供可预测、经常性收入的分部。我们继续对太空和连接分部进行重大投资，通过下一代 Starship 发射平台和扩展的 Starlink 宽带与移动网络来构建未来的基础设施。
We have a stellar track record of capital allocation and value creation in Space and Connectivity. Since SpaceX's founding in 2002, we have raised over $9 billion of equity capital to fund the development and growth of these two business segments. The Space segment became Segment Adjusted EBITDA positive on a sustained basis beginning in 2018 and the Connectivity segment became in aggregate Segment Adjusted EBITDA positive on a sustained basis beginning in 2023. In 2025, our Space segment generated a loss from operations of $(657) million and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $653 million, including the impact of funding $3,004 million in research and development expense for our next-generation Starship launch vehicle program. In 2025, our Connectivity segment generated income from operations of $4,423 million and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $7,168 million.
我们在太空和连接业务的资本配置和价值创造方面拥有出色的过往业绩。自 2002 年 SpaceX 创立以来，我们累计募集了超过 90 亿美元的股权资本，用于这两个业务分部的发展和增长。太空分部自 2018 年起持续实现分部调整后 EBITDA 正值，连接分部自 2023 年起合计持续实现分部调整后 EBITDA 正值。2025 年，太空分部产生经营亏损 (6.57) 亿美元和分部调整后 EBITDA 6.53 亿美元，其中包括为下一代 Starship 运载火箭计划投入 30.04 亿美元研发费用的影响。2025 年，连接分部产生经营利润 44.23 亿美元和分部调整后 EBITDA 71.68 亿美元。
We acquired xAI in February 2026, which forms the basis of our AI segment. We expect to allocate substantial capital to expand our compute infrastructure, and we expect a multi-year investment horizon before these deployments translate into sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA. During this investment period, our capital expenditures will scale as quickly as we are able to deploy power and compute to address the $26.5 trillion potential market opportunity for AI. We plan to access a range of debt and equity financing solutions available to us as a public company to fund future investments in growth and to maintain strong liquidity. We aim to maintain an investment grade credit rating.
我们于 2026 年 2 月收购 xAI，这构成了我们 AI 分部的基础。我们预期会投入大量资本扩展算力基础设施，并预期在这些部署转化为持续为正的 AI 分部调整后 EBITDA 之前会有数年投资周期。在此投资期内，我们的资本性支出将随我们部署电力和算力以应对 26.5 万亿美元 AI 潜在市场机会的速度扩张。我们计划利用作为上市公司可获得的多种债权与股权融资工具，为未来增长投资提供资金并维持强劲的流动性。我们的目标是维持投资级信用评级。
Segment Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP measure. Please refer to the section titled "--Non-GAAP Financial Measures" for additional information on our non-GAAP financial measures, including reconciliations of Segment Adjusted EBITDA to segment income (loss) from operations, the most directly comparable GAAP measure.
分部调整后 EBITDA 是非 GAAP 指标。请参阅”——非 GAAP 财务指标”一节了解关于我们非 GAAP 财务指标的更多信息，包括分部调整后 EBITDA 与最直接可比的 GAAP 指标——分部经营利润（亏损）——之间的勾稽。
Key Business Metrics We use the following key business metrics to evaluate our business, measure our performance, identify trends, formulate business plans, and make strategic decisions.
关键业务指标 我们使用以下关键业务指标来评估业务、衡量绩效、识别趋势、制定业务计划并做出战略决策。
Space In our Space segment, we use mass to orbit and launches as key business metrics to measure our scale and throughput. Mass to orbit and launches grow more rapidly than Space segment revenue because these metrics include our internal constellation deployments from which we do not recognize inter-segment revenue.
太空业务 在太空分部，我们以入轨质量 (mass to orbit) 和发射次数作为衡量规模与吞吐的关键业务指标。入轨质量和发射次数的增长快于太空分部收入，因为这些指标涵盖我们自有星座的部署，而我们不就此确认分部间收入。
Mass to Orbit: Mass to orbit is the total kilograms of payload that we deploy to orbit in a given period, and is a key indicator of SpaceX's capacity and scalability that supports Space revenue and drives expansion across our Connectivity and AI segments. We calculate this metric by summing verified mass, including Starlink satellites, customer payloads, and development cargo, from all successful orbital and flight tests. This measure excludes failed or scrubbed attempts. We increased mass to orbit from 1,210 metric tons in 2023 to 1,699 metric tons in 2024 to 2,213 metric tons in 2025, and from 450 metric tons in the three months ended March 31, 2025 to 556 metric tons in the three months ended March 31, 2026. In 2023, 2024, and 2025, mass to orbit included 205, 282, and 312 metric tons attributable to customer payloads, respectively, and 1,005, 1,418, and 1,901 metric tons attributable to internal payloads, respectively (the amounts presented may not add up to the corresponding totals due to rounding). Falcon 9 launches contribute steadily at an average capacity of 13 metric tons per mission since 2023 to various orbits while we transition to Starship. As the most powerful launch system ever developed, we expect that Starship V3 will be able to carry a payload of 100 metric tons, with future generations of Starship being designed to double this payload.
入轨质量： 入轨质量是指我们在给定期间内部署至轨道的有效载荷总公斤数，是 SpaceX 产能与可扩展性的关键指标，支撑太空收入并驱动连接和 AI 分部的扩张。我们通过加总所有成功轨道飞行与试飞中已验证的质量（包括 Starlink 卫星、客户载荷和开发载荷）计算该指标。该指标不包括失败或终止的尝试。我们入轨质量从 2023 年的 1,210 公吨增至 2024 年的 1,699 公吨、2025 年的 2,213 公吨，从截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 450 公吨增至截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 556 公吨。2023、2024 和 2025 年，入轨质量中分别有 205、282 和 312 公吨归属客户载荷，1,005、1,418 和 1,901 公吨归属内部载荷（由于四舍五入，所列数字可能与总额不一致）。在过渡到 Starship 的过程中，自 2023 年起 Falcon 9 持续以平均每次任务 13 公吨的运力执飞各类轨道。作为有史以来最强大的发射系统，我们预期 Starship V3 将能携带 100 公吨载荷，未来世代的 Starship 设计将使该载荷加倍。
Launches: Launches are a key measure of our operational scale, which in turn supports our revenue growth and mission to expand humanity's presence in space. Launches in a period represent the sum of all successful orbital and flight tests across our rockets, including internal Starlink deployments, development tests, and launches for our third-party customers, and excluding any cancellations or scrubs that occurred in that period. Falcon 9 is the most active orbital launch vehicle today, with approximately 620 orbital space launches as of March 31, 2026, and an over 99% mission success rate. During the three months ended March 31, 2026, we launched 40 Falcon rockets, of which 39 were flight-proven booster launches, and in 2025, we launched 165 Falcon 9 rockets, of which 157 were flight-proven booster launches. While we have steadily increased our Falcon 9 launch cadence over recent years, we expect Falcon 9 launches to decrease over time. While Falcon 9 currently drives the majority of our launch activity, we expect Starship, which is designed to be the world's first fully, rapidly, reusable launch vehicle, to become a larger contributor to our launch volume as it enters operational service. To date, we have executed 11 Starship flight tests to advance our goal of rapidly and fully reusable orbital capability, a breakthrough we believe will transform our launch economics and benefit both our business and customers who rely on our launch services. We have also scheduled a 12th flight test, which will debut the next generation Starship vehicle and Super Heavy booster, powered by the next evolution of our Raptor engine and launching from a newly designed pad at Starbase. We allocate a significant amount of launch capacity to our Connectivity segment, and expect to allocate a significant amount to our AI segment in the future. Our Space segment revenue only reflects our customer launches and customer activities.
发射次数： 发射次数是衡量我们运营规模的关键指标，进而支撑我们的收入增长以及扩展人类太空存在的使命。一个期间内的发射次数代表所有火箭成功轨道发射和试飞的总和，包括内部 Starlink 部署、开发测试以及为第三方客户进行的发射，不包括该期间内发生的任何取消或终止。Falcon 9 是当今最活跃的轨道运载火箭，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日已进行约 620 次轨道航天发射，任务成功率超过 99%。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，我们发射了 40 枚 Falcon 火箭，其中 39 枚为经过飞行验证的助推器发射；2025 年我们发射了 165 枚 Falcon 9 火箭，其中 157 枚为经过飞行验证的助推器发射。虽然近年来我们持续提升 Falcon 9 的发射频率，但我们预期 Falcon 9 的发射次数将随时间逐步下降。虽然目前 Falcon 9 驱动了我们大部分发射活动，但我们预期被设计为全球首款完全、快速可重复使用运载火箭的 Starship，将在投入运行后成为我们发射量的更大贡献者。迄今为止，我们已完成 11 次 Starship 试飞，以推进实现快速、完全可重复使用入轨能力的目标——我们认为这一突破将变革发射经济性，惠及我们自身业务以及依赖我们发射服务的客户。我们还安排了第 12 次试飞，将首飞下一代 Starship 飞行器和 Super Heavy 助推器，由我们新一代 Raptor 发动机提供动力，并从 Starbase 新设计的发射台发射。我们将大量发射能力分配给连接分部，并预计未来将向 AI 分部分配大量发射能力。我们的太空分部收入仅反映客户发射和客户活动。
__________________ (1)With respect to Falcon launches, the number of launches for the years ended December 31, 2023, 2024, and 2025 totaled 96, 134, and 165, respectively, of which customer launches totaled 33, 45, and 43, respectively, and internal launches totaled 63, 89, and 122, respectively.
__________________ (1) 关于 Falcon 发射，截至 2023、2024 和 2025 年 12 月 31 日年度，发射次数分别合计为 96、134 和 165 次，其中客户发射分别为 33、45 和 43 次，内部发射分别为 63、89 和 122 次。
The number of Falcon launches for the three months ended March 31, 2025 and 2026 totaled 36 and 40, respectively, of which customer launches totaled 12 and 7, respectively, and internal launches totaled 24 and 33, respectively. We designate a launch as a "customer launch"
截至 2025 年和 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，Falcon 发射次数分别合计为 36 和 40 次，其中客户发射分别为 12 和 7 次，内部发射分别为 24 和 33 次。当外部客户载荷构成主要载荷（即主要目的是交付该客户载荷），且任务参数（如发射窗口、轨道参数、任务剖面）围绕该主要载荷的需求设计时，我们将该次发射认定为”客户发射”。
if an external customer payload constitutes the primary payload (i.e., where the principal objective is to deliver the customer payload) and the mission parameters (e.g., launch window, orbital parameters, mission profile) are designed around the primary payload's requirements.
（续上）若外部客户载荷构成主要载荷（即主要目的是交付该客户载荷），且任务参数（如发射窗口、轨道参数、任务剖面）围绕该主要载荷的需求设计，则我们将该次发射认定为”客户发射”。
To date, all Starship launches have been classified as internal.
迄今为止，所有 Starship 发射均被归类为内部发射。
Connectivity In our Connectivity segment, we view Starlink Subscribers and Starlink Subscriber ARPU as key business metrics to evaluate our growth and monetization.
连接业务 在连接分部，我们将 Starlink 订阅用户数 (Starlink Subscribers) 和 Starlink 订阅用户 ARPU 作为评估我们增长与变现的关键业务指标。
Starlink Subscribers: We define a Starlink Subscriber as a unique Service Line that is directly assigned to a Starlink.com account registered to a person or entity that does not have a direct, negotiated agreement with the Starlink sales team. A Service Line is an individual instance of Starlink broadband internet service provisioned under a subscription plan, generally associated with a specific Starlink terminal or group of terminals, and billed according to Starlink's service plans and terms of service. The number of Service Lines is distinct from the number of unique devices, account holders, end users or physical persons. An individual, household, or business may share a single Service Line among multiple end-users. Likewise, an individual, household, or business may maintain multiple service lines (e.g., both a Residential Service Line and a separate Roam Service Line, which would be defined as two separate Service Lines and therefore two Starlink Subscribers).
Starlink 订阅用户： 我们将 Starlink 订阅用户定义为：直接分配给在 Starlink.com 上注册的、由未与 Starlink 销售团队签订直接协商协议的个人或实体所持账户的唯一 Service Line（服务线）。Service Line 是在订阅计划下开通的 Starlink 宽带互联网服务的单一实例，通常与特定 Starlink 终端或一组终端关联，并按 Starlink 服务计划与服务条款计费。Service Line 数量与唯一设备数量、账户持有人数、终端用户数或自然人数量不同。一个人、家庭或企业可在多个终端用户间共享一条 Service Line。同样，一个人、家庭或企业可保有多条 Service Line（例如 Residential Service Line 和 Roam Service Line 分别计为两条 Service Line，因此对应两个 Starlink 订阅用户）。
We use this measure to assess the adoption of Starlink as we expand within and across geographies and business segments. Starlink Subscribers includes both Personal (e.g., Residential and Roam) and Business (e.g., Local Priority and Global Priority) subscription plans, but does not include managed enterprise and government customers with contracts in domains including aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites and government entities. We calculate Starlink Subscribers for a period as the number of unique Service Lines at the end of the period. Starlink Subscribers totaled approximately 10.3 million and 5.0 million, up 105% and 91% on a year-over-year basis, in the quarters ended March 31, 2026 and March 31, 2025, respectively.
我们使用该指标评估 Starlink 在地域和业务分部内及跨域的采用情况。Starlink 订阅用户包括个人计划（如 Residential 和 Roam）和商务计划（如 Local Priority 和 Global Priority），但不包括在航空、海事、陆上交通、固定站点及政府实体等领域签订合同的托管型企业和政府客户。我们按期末唯一 Service Line 数计算一个期间的 Starlink 订阅用户数。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日和 2025 年 3 月 31 日的季度末，Starlink 订阅用户数分别约为 1,030 万和 500 万，同比分别增长 105% 和 91%。
Starlink Subscriber ARPU: We calculate ARPU as service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers during the period divided by (i) the average number of Starlink Subscribers during the period and by (ii) the number of months in the period. Our strategy is focused on driving sustainable revenue growth and expanding our margins through operational efficiencies and technological advancements, rather than prioritizing increases in ARPU. This approach aligns with our long-term vision of expanding global connectivity and market access. We generally expect Starlink Subscriber ARPU to continue to decline over the next few years as the portion of our subscriber base outside North America continues to grow, as we add lower priced service plans, and as we adjust the monthly service plan fees we charge for broadband offerings. However, we expect these dynamics to be offset by increased scale and technological advancement in our launch, satellite, and user terminal operations, ultimately supporting overall revenue growth and cost reduction. Our Starlink Subscriber monthly ARPU decreased from $86 per month for the three months ended March 31, 2025 to $66 per month for the three months ended March 31, 2026 and from $91 per month in 2024 to $81 per month in 2025. These decreases were driven primarily by international expansion and the addition of lower priced service plans.
Starlink 订阅用户 ARPU： 我们按期间内来自 Starlink 订阅用户的服务收入除以 (i) 期间内 Starlink 订阅用户平均数量及 (ii) 期间月数，计算 ARPU。我们的战略是通过运营效率提升和技术进步推动可持续收入增长并扩展利润率，而非以提升 ARPU 为优先。这一方式与我们扩展全球连接和市场触达的长期愿景一致。我们总体预期，随着北美以外的订阅用户占比持续扩大、我们增加价格更低的服务计划、以及我们对宽带产品的月度服务费进行调整，未来几年 Starlink 订阅用户 ARPU 将继续下降。然而，我们预期这些动态会被发射、卫星和用户终端运营规模的扩大及技术进步所抵消，最终支撑整体收入增长和成本下降。我们的 Starlink 订阅用户月度 ARPU 从截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的每月 86 美元下降至截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的每月 66 美元，并从 2024 年的每月 91 美元下降至 2025 年的每月 81 美元。这些下降主要由国际扩张和增加价格更低的服务计划所驱动。
AI Nameplate Compute Draw: We calculate Nameplate Compute Draw for a period as the number of GPUs installed in our data centers at the end of the period multiplied by their respective all-in power draw. Nameplate Compute Draw reflects installed capacity and does not represent actual power consumption or utilization. It does not include power we install and use for our supporting infrastructure such as cooling systems, power distribution losses, lighting, security systems, or facility-level overhead. Our Nameplate Compute Draw increased to 1.0 gigawatt as of March 31, 2026 as we brought COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II online. We use this metric to assess our ability to deploy and scale compute capacity.
AI 业务 标称算力消耗 (Nameplate Compute Draw)： 我们按期末数据中心安装的 GPU 数量乘以其各自的整机功率消耗，计算一个期间的标称算力消耗。该指标反映已安装容量，并不代表实际功率消耗或利用率。它不包括我们为冷却系统、配电损耗、照明、安防系统或设施级一般开销等支持基础设施安装并使用的功率。我们随着 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 上线，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的标称算力消耗增至 1.0 吉瓦。我们使用该指标评估部署和扩展算力能力的水平。
Segment Income (Loss) from Operations Space Income (Loss) from Operations Space loss from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $592 million to $(662) million compared to $(70) million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily driven by an accelerated investment in development of the Starship vehicle as well as launch facilities to support future Starship launches, and a decrease in revenue from customer launches, partially offset by a decrease in cost of revenue, selling, general, and administrative expenses and impairment.
分部经营利润（亏损） 太空分部经营利润（亏损） 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的太空经营亏损较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 (70) 百万美元增加 5.92 亿美元至 (6.62) 亿美元，主要由 Starship 飞行器的加速研发投入及为支持未来 Starship 发射的发射设施投入驱动，并受客户发射收入下降影响，部分被营业成本 (cost of revenue)、销售、一般及行政费用 (SG&A) 和资产减值 (impairment) 的下降所抵消。
Space income (loss) from operations for the year ended December 31, 2025 decreased by $678 million to $(657)
截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日的全年，太空经营利润（亏损）较截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的 2,100 万美元减少 6.78 亿美元至 (6.57)
million compared to $21 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, while Space income (loss) from operations for the year ended December 31, 2024 increased by $22 million to $21 million for the year ended December 31, 2024 compared to $(1) million for the year ended December 31, 2023. The year-over-year decrease in 2025 was primarily driven by an accelerated investment in development of the Starship vehicle as well as launch facilities to support future Starship launches, partially offset by an increase in revenue and decrease in cost of revenue.
亿美元，而截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的太空经营利润（亏损）相较截至 2023 年 12 月 31 日全年的 (100) 万美元增加 2,200 万美元至 2,100 万美元。2025 年的同比下降主要由 Starship 飞行器的加速研发投入及为支持未来 Starship 发射的发射设施投入驱动，部分被收入增加和营业成本下降所抵消。
Connectivity Income (Loss) from Operations Connectivity income from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $155 million to $1,188 million compared to $1,033 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily driven by increased revenue from our consumer subscribers (composed of 104.7% growth in Starlink Subscribers, offset by a 22.9% decline in Starlink Subscriber ARPU, primarily due to international expansion and the addition of lower priced service plans) and enterprise business, partially offset by higher depreciation of capitalized launch and satellite costs due to the increase in Starlink flights, as well as higher operating expenses including ground operating costs and international expansion costs to support and drive subscriber growth.
连接分部经营利润（亏损） 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的连接经营利润较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 10.33 亿美元增加 1.55 亿美元至 11.88 亿美元，主要由消费者订阅用户收入增加（Starlink 订阅用户增长 104.7%，部分被 Starlink 订阅用户 ARPU 下降 22.9% 抵消，ARPU 下降主要由国际扩张和增加价格更低的服务计划造成）和企业业务驱动，部分被因 Starlink 发射增加导致的资本化发射与卫星成本折旧上升，以及为支持并推动订阅用户增长而上升的运营费用（包括地面运营成本和国际扩张成本）所抵消。
Connectivity income from operations for 2025 increased by $2,417 million to $4,423 million compared to $2,006 million for the year ended December 31, 2024 while Connectivity income from operations for the year ended December 31, 2024 increased by $1,537 million to $2,006 million compared to $469 million for the year ended December 31, 2023. The year-over-year increase in 2025 was primarily driven by increased revenue from growth of our consumer and enterprise customers by $2,378 million and $1,410 million, respectively, partially offset by higher depreciation of capitalized launch and satellite costs due to the increase in Starlink flights, as well as higher marketing and international expansion costs to drive subscriber growth.
2025 年连接经营利润较截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的 20.06 亿美元增加 24.17 亿美元至 44.23 亿美元，而截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的连接经营利润相较截至 2023 年 12 月 31 日全年的 4.69 亿美元增加 15.37 亿美元至 20.06 亿美元。2025 年的同比增长主要由消费者和企业客户增长带来的收入增加 23.78 亿美元和 14.10 亿美元分别驱动，部分被因 Starlink 发射增加导致的资本化发射与卫星成本折旧上升、以及为推动订阅用户增长而上升的营销和国际扩张成本所抵消。
AI Income (Loss) from Operations AI loss from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $1,533 million to $(2,469) million compared to $(936) million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily driven by higher cloud computing and GPU depreciation costs, data center infrastructure and employee expenses, partially offset by higher revenue.
AI 分部经营利润（亏损） 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 AI 经营亏损较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 (9.36) 亿美元增加 15.33 亿美元至 (24.69) 亿美元，主要由云计算和 GPU 折旧成本、数据中心基础设施和员工费用上升驱动，部分被收入增加所抵消。
AI loss from operations for 2025 increased by $4,794 million to $(6,355) million compared to $(1,561) million for the year ended December 31, 2024, while AI loss from operations for the year ended December 31, 2024 decreased by $2,412 million to $(1,561) million compared to $(3,973) million for the year ended December 31, 2023. The increase in 2025 was primarily driven by higher cloud computing costs, facilities-related costs and employee expenses, partially offset by higher revenue.
2025 年 AI 经营亏损较截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的 (15.61) 亿美元增加 47.94 亿美元至 (63.55) 亿美元，而截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的 AI 经营亏损相较截至 2023 年 12 月 31 日全年的 (39.73) 亿美元减少 24.12 亿美元至 (15.61) 亿美元。2025 年的增加主要由云计算成本、设施相关成本和员工费用上升驱动，部分被收入增加所抵消。
Segment Adjusted EBITDA Segment Adjusted EBITDA is defined as segment income (loss) from operations excluding (i) depreciation and amortization, (ii) share-based compensation, (iii) restructuring charges and (iv) impairment.
分部调整后 EBITDA 分部调整后 EBITDA 的定义是：分部经营利润（亏损）扣除 (i) 折旧与摊销 (depreciation and amortization)、(ii) 股权激励 (share-based compensation)、(iii) 重组费用 (restructuring charges) 及 (iv) 资产减值。
Space Segment Adjusted EBITDA Space Segment Adjusted EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $575 million to $(351)
太空分部调整后 EBITDA 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的太空分部调整后 EBITDA 较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 2.24 亿美元减少 5.75 亿美元至 (3.51)
million compared to $224 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily driven by an accelerated investment in development of the Starship vehicle as well as launch facilities to support future Starship launches, and a decrease in revenue from customer launches, partially offset by a decrease in cost of revenue, selling, general, and administrative expenses.
亿美元，主要由 Starship 飞行器的加速研发投入及为支持未来 Starship 发射的发射设施投入驱动，并受客户发射收入下降影响，部分被营业成本与 SG&A 的下降所抵消。
Space Segment Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 decreased by $501 million to $653 million compared to $1,154 million in 2024, while Space Segment Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 increased by $157 million to $1,154 million compared to $997 million in 2023. The year-over-year decrease in 2025 was primarily driven by an accelerated investment in development of the Starship vehicle, as well as launch facilities to support future Starship launches, partially offset by an increase in NASA Cargo Resupply Services (CRS) for additional missions to the International Space Station, along with increased revenue from a U.S. Department of War contract. Our Space Segment Adjusted EBITDA is also driven by the reusability and efficiency of our rockets, which boosts cadence and reliability and supports a diversified base of commercial and government customers. These efforts have created a strong foundation for our Space Segment Adjusted EBITDA, and we believe position us to unlock further high-value opportunities in the expanding space economy.
2025 年太空分部调整后 EBITDA 较 2024 年的 11.54 亿美元减少 5.01 亿美元至 6.53 亿美元；2024 年太空分部调整后 EBITDA 较 2023 年的 9.97 亿美元增加 1.57 亿美元至 11.54 亿美元。2025 年的同比下降主要由 Starship 飞行器的加速研发投入及为支持未来 Starship 发射的发射设施投入驱动，部分被因执行额外国际空间站任务而新增的 NASA 货物补给服务 (CRS) 收入和来自美国国防部 (U.S. Department of War) 合同的收入增长所抵消。我们的太空分部调整后 EBITDA 还得益于火箭的可重复使用和效率，从而提升了发射频率和可靠性，并支撑了多元化的商业和政府客户基础。这些努力为太空分部调整后 EBITDA 奠定了坚实基础，我们相信能让我们在不断扩张的太空经济中解锁更多高价值机会。
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $469 million to $2,087 million compared to $1,618 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily driven by higher revenue from growth in consumer and enterprise revenue. Consumer revenue was composed of 104.7% growth in Starlink Subscribers, offset by a 22.9% decline in Starlink Subscriber ARPU, primarily due to international expansion and the addition of lower priced service plans. Enterprise and government revenue had an increase primarily driven by the growth in our aviation, maritime, mobility, and other enterprise business, partially offset by a decrease in our government business. These increases in revenue were offset by higher operating expenses for international expansion, and higher research and development costs.
连接分部调整后 EBITDA 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的连接分部调整后 EBITDA 较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 16.18 亿美元增加 4.69 亿美元至 20.87 亿美元，主要由消费者和企业收入增长带来的更高收入驱动。消费者收入由 Starlink 订阅用户 104.7% 的增长构成，被 Starlink 订阅用户 ARPU 22.9% 的下降所抵消，ARPU 下降主要由国际扩张和增加价格更低的服务计划造成。企业和政府收入有所增加，主要由航空、海事、移动及其他企业业务增长驱动，部分被政府业务下降所抵消。这些收入增加被国际扩张运营费用上升和研发成本上升所抵消。
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 increased by $3,319 million to $7,168 million compared to $3,849 million in 2024 while Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 increased by $2,247 million to $3,849 million compared to $1,602 million in 2023. The year-over-year increase in 2025 was primarily driven by higher revenue from growth in our consumer and enterprise customers, partially offset by higher marketing and international expansion costs to grow our subscribers, as well as higher research and development costs for our next- generation product development. We have driven our strong sequential Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA growth by expanding the scale and efficiency of our LEO satellite constellations and our highly verticalized supply chain, which has delivered major cost reductions in user terminal production.
2025 年连接分部调整后 EBITDA 较 2024 年的 38.49 亿美元增加 33.19 亿美元至 71.68 亿美元；2024 年连接分部调整后 EBITDA 较 2023 年的 16.02 亿美元增加 22.47 亿美元至 38.49 亿美元。2025 年的同比增长主要由消费者和企业客户增长带来的收入增加驱动，部分被为推动订阅用户增长而上升的营销和国际扩张成本，以及为下一代产品开发而上升的研发成本所抵消。我们通过扩展 LEO 卫星星座的规模与效率以及高度垂直化的供应链，推动了连接分部调整后 EBITDA 的强劲连续增长，并在用户终端生产上实现了重大成本下降。
AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $497 million to $(609)
AI 分部调整后 EBITDA 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 AI 分部调整后 EBITDA 较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 (1.12) 亿美元减少 4.97 亿美元至 (6.09)
million compared to $(112) million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, primarily driven by higher cloud compute and data center infrastructure and operating costs, and employee compensation expenses, partially offset by higher revenue.
亿美元，主要由云计算、数据中心基础设施和运营成本以及员工薪酬费用上升驱动，部分被收入增加所抵消。
AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 decreased by $1,584 million to $(1,237) million compared to $347 million in 2024 while AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 decreased by $875 million to $347 million, compared to $1,222 million in 2023. The decrease in 2025 was primarily driven by higher cloud computing costs, facilities- related costs and employee expenses, partially offset by higher revenue. AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA is primarily driven by our strategy to rapidly and cost-effectively scale compute infrastructure. We expect to continue to expand our terrestrial data centers, and to launch orbital data centers, and we expect a multi-year investment horizon before these deployments translate into sustained positive Segment Adjusted EBITDA for our AI segment.
2025 年 AI 分部调整后 EBITDA 较 2024 年的 3.47 亿美元减少 15.84 亿美元至 (12.37) 亿美元，2024 年 AI 分部调整后 EBITDA 较 2023 年的 12.22 亿美元减少 8.75 亿美元至 3.47 亿美元。2025 年的下降主要由云计算成本、设施相关成本和员工费用上升驱动，部分被收入增加所抵消。AI 分部调整后 EBITDA 主要由我们快速且具备成本效率地扩展算力基础设施的策略驱动。我们预期将继续扩张地面数据中心并发射轨道数据中心，并预计在这些部署转化为 AI 分部持续为正的分部调整后 EBITDA 之前会有多年的投资周期。
Segment Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP measure. Please refer to the section titled "--Non-GAAP Financial Measures" for additional information on our non-GAAP financial measures, including reconciliations of Segment Adjusted EBITDA to segment income (loss) from operations, the most directly comparable GAAP measure.
分部调整后 EBITDA 是非 GAAP 指标。请参阅”——非 GAAP 财务指标”一节了解关于我们非 GAAP 财务指标的更多信息，包括分部调整后 EBITDA 与最直接可比 GAAP 指标——分部经营利润（亏损）——之间的勾稽。
Capital Expenditures The following table presents our capital expenditures by segment:
资本性支出 下表列示了我们按分部划分的资本性支出：
| | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | Three Months Ended March 31,| | Year Ended December 31, (in millions)| 2026| | 2025| | 2025| | 2024| | 2023 Space ..................................................| $1,052| | $759| | $3,832| | $2,032| | $1,497 Connectivity .......................................| 1,332| | 814| | 4,178| | 3,498| | 2,455 AI ........................................................| 7,723| | 2,567| | 12,727| | 5,633| | 463 Total Capital Expenditures .................| $10,107| | $4,140| | $20,737| | $11,163| | $4,415 Space Capital Expenditures Space capital expenditures for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased $293 million to $1,052 million compared to $759 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025. The increase was primarily driven by increased investment in our launch site infrastructure for Starship.
| | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | 截至 3 月 31 日的三个月| | 截至 12 月 31 日的年度 （百万美元）| 2026| | 2025| | 2025| | 2024| | 2023 太空 ..................................................| $1,052| | $759| | $3,832| | $2,032| | $1,497 连接 .......................................| 1,332| | 814| | 4,178| | 3,498| | 2,455 AI ........................................................| 7,723| | 2,567| | 12,727| | 5,633| | 463 资本性支出合计 .................| $10,107| | $4,140| | $20,737| | $11,163| | $4,415 太空资本性支出 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的太空资本性支出较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 7.59 亿美元增加 2.93 亿美元至 10.52 亿美元。增长主要由对 Starship 发射场基础设施的投资增加驱动。
Space capital expenditures for 2025 increased $1,800 million to $3,832 million compared to $2,032 million in 2024, while Space capital expenditures for 2024 increased $535 million to $2,032 million compared to $1,497 million in 2023\. The increase in each year-over-year period was primarily driven by increased investment in our launch site infrastructure for Starship.
2025 年太空资本性支出较 2024 年的 20.32 亿美元增加 18.00 亿美元至 38.32 亿美元；2024 年太空资本性支出较 2023 年的 14.97 亿美元增加 5.35 亿美元至 20.32 亿美元。每年的同比增长主要由对 Starship 发射场基础设施的投资增加驱动。
Connectivity Capital Expenditures Connectivity capital expenditures for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased $518 million to $1,332 million compared to $814 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025. The increase was primarily driven by higher satellite and ground equipment costs as we continue to increase our number of satellites and grow our satellite network.
连接资本性支出 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的连接资本性支出较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 8.14 亿美元增加 5.18 亿美元至 13.32 亿美元。增长主要由我们持续增加卫星数量并扩大卫星网络而带来的更高卫星和地面设备成本驱动。
Connectivity capital expenditures for 2025 increased $680 million to $4,178 million compared to $3,498 million in 2024, while Connectivity capital expenditures for 2024 increased $1,043 million to $3,498 million compared to $2,455 million in 2023. The increase in each year-over-year period was primarily driven by higher satellite and ground equipment costs as we continue to increase our number of satellites and grow our satellite network.
2025 年连接资本性支出较 2024 年的 34.98 亿美元增加 6.80 亿美元至 41.78 亿美元；2024 年连接资本性支出较 2023 年的 24.55 亿美元增加 10.43 亿美元至 34.98 亿美元。每年的同比增长主要由我们持续增加卫星数量并扩大卫星网络而带来的更高卫星和地面设备成本驱动。
AI Capital Expenditures AI capital expenditures for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased $5,156 million to $7,723 million compared to $2,567 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025. The increase was primarily driven by investments in the rapid expansion of our terrestrial data centers, including the development, construction, and equipping of new facilities and supporting infrastructure.
AI 资本性支出 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 AI 资本性支出较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 25.67 亿美元增加 51.56 亿美元至 77.23 亿美元。增长主要由对地面数据中心快速扩张的投资驱动，包括新设施及配套基础设施的开发、建造与配置。
AI capital expenditures for 2025 increased $7,094 million to $12,727 million compared to $5,633 million in 2024, while AI capital expenditures for 2024 increased $5,170 million to $5,633 million compared to $463 million in 2023\. This increase was primarily driven by significant investments in the rapid expansion of our terrestrial data centers, including the development, construction, and equipping of new facilities and supporting infrastructure.
2025 年 AI 资本性支出较 2024 年的 56.33 亿美元增加 70.94 亿美元至 127.27 亿美元；2024 年 AI 资本性支出较 2023 年的 4.63 亿美元增加 51.70 亿美元至 56.33 亿美元。该增长主要由对地面数据中心快速扩张的重大投资驱动，包括新设施及配套基础设施的开发、建造与配置。
Drivers of Our Performance Developing Starship. Starship is our next-generation vehicle that we expect will dramatically expand our launch capability through full and rapid reusability combined with unprecedented mass to orbit capability. As the most powerful launch system ever developed, we expect that Starship V3 will be able to carry a payload of 100 metric tons, and that future generations could reach 200 metric tons, potentially as soon as Starship V4. Starship is central to our goal of unlocking growth through our unique vertically integrated business model. Starship is expected to be the only vehicle with fully reusable first and second stages, which is critical to reducing launch costs and increasing launch cadence. We believe that Starship can eventually reduce the cost to reach orbit by 99% or more relative to the historical average launch cost per kilogram according to NASA of $18,500, establishing a scalable path to creating the infrastructure of the future, such as orbital AI compute.
我们业绩的驱动因素 开发 Starship。 Starship 是我们的下一代飞行器，预期通过完全且快速可重复使用以及前所未有的入轨质量能力，大幅扩展我们的发射能力。作为有史以来最强大的发射系统，我们预期 Starship V3 将能携带 100 公吨载荷，未来世代可能达到 200 公吨，最早可能在 Starship V4 实现。Starship 是我们通过独特的垂直整合商业模式解锁增长这一目标的核心。Starship 预期将是唯一一款一级和二级均完全可重复使用的飞行器，这对于降低发射成本和提升发射频率至关重要。我们认为 Starship 最终能将入轨成本相对 NASA 给出的每千克 18,500 美元历史平均发射成本降低 99% 或以上，建立一条可规模化路径，构建例如轨道 AI 算力等未来基础设施。
We have already demonstrated catching and reusing the first stage booster for Starship through our innovative "chopsticks" method to catch the booster. To date, we have executed 11 Starship flight tests. We have also scheduled a 12th flight test, which will debut the next generation Starship vehicle and Super Heavy booster. This next-generation Starship introduces major changes for better orbital performance and reusability. We plan to demonstrate key development milestones of catching the upper stage and demonstrating in-orbit propellant transfer capabilities. These milestones will be the key unlocks for a rapidly reusable rocket that we expect will take hundreds of thousands of tons of mass to orbit to drive growth in our Connectivity and AI segments, and allow us to develop the lunar economy and eventually to reach Mars. We expect Starship to commence payload delivery to orbit in the second half of 2026 following additional flight tests. For additional information about this risk, please refer to "Risk Factors--Risks Related to Our Business--Any failure or delay in the development of Starship at scale or in achieving the required launch cadence, reusability and capabilities thereafter would delay or limit our ability to execute our growth strategy, including the deployment of next-generation satellites, global satellite-to-mobile connectivity, and orbital AI compute, which could materially adversely affect our business, financial condition, results of operations, and future prospects" in this prospectus.
我们已经通过创新的”筷子”接收方法展示了对 Starship 一级助推器的接收和重复使用。迄今为止，我们已执行 11 次 Starship 试飞。我们还安排了第 12 次试飞，将首飞下一代 Starship 飞行器和 Super Heavy 助推器。这一下一代 Starship 引入了为更佳轨道性能与可重复使用而做出的重大改动。我们计划展示接收上面级以及在轨推进剂转移能力等关键开发里程碑。这些里程碑将是实现快速可重复使用火箭的关键突破，我们预期它将把数十万吨质量送入轨道，从而推动连接和 AI 分部的增长，并使我们能够发展月球经济并最终抵达火星。我们预计 Starship 将在额外试飞后，于 2026 年下半年开始向轨道交付有效载荷。关于该风险的更多信息，请参阅本招股说明书中”风险因素——业务相关风险——Starship 大规模开发或其后实现所需的发射频率、可重复使用性和能力的任何失败或延迟，将延迟或限制我们执行增长战略（包括下一代卫星部署、全球星地直连和轨道 AI 算力）的能力，这可能对我们的业务、财务状况、经营成果和未来前景产生重大不利影响”。
Launch Costs and Cadence. Our launch costs and cadence underpin the foundational competitive advantage that enables the performance of each of our segments. The reusability of our launch vehicles meaningfully reduces the cost per kilogram to orbit by eliminating or limiting the need to manufacture new vehicles for every mission.
