发射成本与频率。 我们的发射成本与频率支撑每个业务分部表现的基础性竞争优势。我们运载火箭的可重复使用通过消除或限制每次任务都需制造新飞行器的需要，显著降低了每千克入轨成本。
Reusability also enables higher launch cadence by shortening the time between flights, as vehicles can be rapidly reflown after their return. These factors enable performance in our Connectivity segment by supporting faster and more cost‑effective deployment of our satellite constellations. We expect they will support our AI segment as we aim to deploy a large fleet of orbital AI compute. We expect continued enhancements to our launch infrastructure and launch vehicles, including Starship, to drive cost down and throughput up, extending these benefits to our businesses, as well as to our third‑party customers who rely on our launch capabilities. As we continue to reduce launch costs and increase launch cadence, we expect to transform the rocket launch industry into airline-like operations, enabling continuous and affordable access to space. Period-to-period comparisons of launch costs and cadence are impacted by factors out of our control, including timing of delivery of customer payloads which impacts the mix of customer and internal payloads and related financial reporting, or weather which can delay a launch from one period to another.
可重复使用还通过缩短两次飞行之间的间隔时间提升发射频率，因为飞行器返回后可迅速再飞。这些因素通过支持更快、更具成本效率地部署卫星星座，支撑了连接分部的表现。我们预期它们将支撑 AI 分部，使我们能够部署大型轨道 AI 算力机队。我们预期对发射基础设施和包括 Starship 在内的运载火箭的持续改进将推动成本下降、吞吐提升，并将这些益处延伸至我们的业务及依赖我们发射能力的第三方客户。随着我们继续降低发射成本并提升发射频率，我们预期将把火箭发射行业转变为类似航空公司的运营，从而实现持续且可负担的太空进入。期间间的发射成本与频率比较会受到我们无法控制因素的影响，包括客户载荷交付时机（影响客户与内部载荷的混合及相关财务报告），或天气（可能将发射从一个期间推迟到另一个期间）。
Increasing Satellite Capacity. The scale, reliability, and capacity of our LEO broadband and mobile satellite constellations drive our Connectivity segment's growth and operating performance. In 2025, launching and operating higher-throughput satellites supported Starlink's service quality and customer reach by increasing available network capacity and improving service consistency during peak usage periods. As of March 31, 2026, we operated over 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, with the majority composed of our second-generation, V2 Mini satellites. We expect to commence deploying our next-generation V3 satellites, designed to offer one Tbps of downlink capacity per satellite, using Starship in the second half of 2026 and expect that a single Starship launch will be capable of deploying up to 60 V3 satellites to LEO, representing a twenty-fold increase in Starlink downlink capacity deployed relative to a Falcon 9 launch.
提升卫星容量。 我们 LEO 宽带与移动卫星星座的规模、可靠性与容量驱动着连接分部的增长与经营业绩。2025 年，发射并运行更高吞吐量的卫星，通过增加可用网络容量并改善高峰使用期间的服务一致性，支撑了 Starlink 的服务质量和客户覆盖。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们在 LEO 运营超过 9,600 颗 Starlink 宽带与移动卫星，其中大多数为第二代 V2 Mini 卫星。我们预计将于 2026 年下半年开始用 Starship 部署下一代 V3 卫星，其设计目标是单颗卫星提供 1 Tbps 下行容量；我们预期单次 Starship 发射可将多达 60 颗 V3 卫星送入 LEO，相对于一次 Falcon 9 发射，Starlink 部署下行容量提升二十倍。
We also provide satellite-to-mobile connectivity, supplementing terrestrial networks and substantially reducing mobile "dead zones" in approximately 30 countries. Since January 2025, we have grown our constellation from approximately 360 mobile V1 Mobile satellites to approximately 650 mobile V1 Mobile satellites. Through this constellation and in partnership with more than 30 mobile network operators, we provided data, over-the-top voice, and messaging services to approximately 7.4 million monthly unique devices across approximately 30 countries.
我们还提供星地直连移动连接，补充地面网络，并在约 30 个国家大幅减少手机”信号盲区”。自 2025 年 1 月以来，我们的星座已从约 360 颗 V1 移动卫星扩展至约 650 颗 V1 移动卫星。通过该星座并与超过 30 家移动网络运营商合作，我们在约 30 个国家向约 740 万月度独立设备提供数据、OTT 语音和消息服务。
During 2025, we also entered into agreements to acquire 65 MHz of spectrum in the United States as well as certain global Mobile Satellite Service spectrum licenses from EchoStar for $19.6 billion of equity and cash consideration, as described below under "--Liquidity and Capital Resources--Material Cash Commitments." We expect the spectrum acquisition to close in November 2027, subject to required regulatory approvals and other closing conditions. We expect the wider bandwidth operations enabled by this spectrum purchase, together with our authorization to deploy 7,500 satellites including with the 2GHz spectrum band, will provide stronger support for current performance and potential future services, including broadband data and IoT connectivity, and is expected to enable 5G connectivity.
2025 年期间，我们还签署协议以约 196 亿美元股权和现金对价从 EchoStar 收购美国境内 65 MHz 频谱及若干全球移动卫星服务频谱许可，详情见下方”——流动性与资本资源——重大现金承诺”。我们预计该项频谱收购将于 2027 年 11 月完成交割，前提是获得所需监管批准及满足其他交割条件。我们预期此次频谱购入所带动的更宽带宽运营，与我们已获授权部署的 7,500 颗卫星（包括 2GHz 频段）相结合，将为当前性能和未来潜在服务（包括宽带数据和 IoT 连接）提供更强支持，并预期能支持 5G 连接。
These investments in satellite scale, per-satellite capacity, and expanded capabilities are instrumental to the growth and operating performance of our Connectivity segment, enabling us to onboard new users while improving service quality.
对卫星规模、单星容量与扩展能力的这些投资，对连接分部的增长和经营业绩至关重要，使我们能够在提升服务质量的同时引入新用户。
Increasing Starlink Brand Awareness and Acquiring New Subscribers. Our growth is driven in part by increased global awareness of Starlink's capabilities and our ability to convert that awareness into customer adoption. Trust, visibility, and demonstrated reliability are central to customer acquisition, particularly for those in remote and infrastructure-limited regions. Proven performance in rural, remote, and disaster-affected areas, along with strong brand awareness, reinforces Starlink's reputation as essential infrastructure, leading to higher adoption in new markets.
提升 Starlink 品牌知名度并获取新订阅用户。 我们的增长部分由全球对 Starlink 能力的认知提升以及我们将该认知转化为客户采用的能力驱动。信任、能见度和已证实的可靠性对客户获取至关重要，尤其是在偏远和基础设施受限地区。在农村、偏远和受灾区域的已证实表现，连同强劲的品牌知名度，强化了 Starlink 作为关键基础设施的声誉，进而带动新市场更高的采用率。
As of March 31, 2026, we had over 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, operating the world's most advanced broadband constellation providing internet connectivity to approximately 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets, collectively home to more than 3.3 billion people. We are focused on growing the number of Starlink Subscribers by expanding our consumer distribution network across thousands of authorized retail stores globally, and executing region-specific marketing campaigns to increase brand awareness. By clearly demonstrating Starlink's superior speed, low-latency, and ease of installation, we expect to drive meaningful subscriber growth.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们在 LEO 拥有超过 9,600 颗 Starlink 宽带与移动卫星，运营着全球最先进的宽带星座，向 164 个国家、地区及其他市场的约 1,030 万 Starlink 订阅用户提供互联网连接，这些市场合计居住超过 33 亿人。我们专注于通过在全球数千家授权零售店扩展消费者分销网络、并执行区域专属营销活动以提升品牌知名度，从而增加 Starlink 订阅用户数量。通过清晰展示 Starlink 的卓越速度、低延迟和易于安装，我们预期将带动订阅用户的显著增长。
Increasing Enterprise Customer Adoption. As we continue to grow our Starlink constellation and bandwidth, we see a large opportunity to grow the enterprise connectivity market by providing solutions that had not previously been available. Our network is global and can provide primary connectivity for on-the-move applications as well as a resilient backup option for enterprises serviced by land-based connectivity. We plan to deepen our penetration with enterprise and government customers through direct, vertical-specific acquisition strategies. In recent years, we have assembled dedicated sales and engineering teams to market and support fleet-wide conversions in aviation and maritime, customized deployments for land mobility, which we expect to continue to grow as consumers who experience Starlink begin to expect high-performance connectivity when traveling. We expect to enable more customized deployments for land mobility across existing use cases such as commercial trucking fleets, and new applications enabled by more connected devices. We also continue to develop specialized networks for secure government applications via Starshield. By leveraging proven performance in mission-critical environments and expanding through channel partners in select geographies, we expect to drive increased adoption among high-value enterprise and government accounts.
提升企业客户采用率。 随着我们继续扩大 Starlink 星座和带宽，我们看到通过提供过去无法获得的解决方案、扩大企业连接市场的巨大机会。我们的网络是全球性的，可为移动应用提供主连接，也可作为依赖陆基连接的企业的弹性备用方案。我们计划通过直接的、面向垂直行业的获客策略加深与企业和政府客户的渗透。近年来，我们组建了专门的销售和工程团队来推广和支持航空和海事领域的全机/全船改造、为陆上交通提供定制化部署——我们预期这些将持续增长，因为体验过 Starlink 的消费者在出行时开始期待高性能连接。我们预期将为陆上交通在现有用例（如商用卡车队）和由更多互联设备催生的新应用中实现更多定制化部署。我们也继续通过 Starshield 为安全的政府应用开发专用网络。通过利用关键任务环境中已证实的表现并在特定地区通过渠道伙伴扩展，我们预期将推动高价值企业和政府账户的采用率提升。
Accelerating Investment in Growth and Innovation. We are simultaneously developing and scaling a wide range of complex, capital‑intensive projects, including Starship and terrestrial and orbital AI compute. We believe speed is a competitive advantage, and periodically we decide to increase and accelerate our investments. For example, in 2025 we accelerated our timeline for Starship development, increasing R&D in our Space segment to $3,004 million, compared to $1,835 million in 2024. In our AI segment, in 2025 we successfully accelerated deployment of compute for the development of Grok, increasing R&D in our AI segment to $5,064 million, compared to $1,176 million in 2024. We believe pursuing multiple ambitious programs in parallel enables us to compound advantages across our vertically integrated innovation engine and unlock new large addressable markets over time. The timing of our investments is not fixed and may accelerate based on technical progress, market opportunity, or resource availability. As a result, our operating results, margins and profitability may fluctuate from period to period as we continue to prioritize execution speed, capacity expansion, and technological leadership over near‑term margin optimization. We believe that this approach maximizes long‑term value creation by allowing us to move faster than competitors, scale earlier in emerging markets, and reinforce durable competitive advantages that we expect to benefit our business over time.
加速对增长与创新的投资。 我们同时开发并扩展一系列复杂、资本密集的项目，包括 Starship、地面与轨道 AI 算力。我们认为速度是一种竞争优势，并会周期性地决定加大并加速投资。例如，2025 年我们加快了 Starship 的开发时间表，将太空分部研发支出从 2024 年的 18.35 亿美元增至 30.04 亿美元。在 AI 分部，2025 年我们成功加速了为 Grok 开发部署算力，将 AI 分部研发支出从 2024 年的 11.76 亿美元增至 50.64 亿美元。我们相信，同时推进多个雄心勃勃的项目使我们能够在垂直整合的创新引擎中累积优势，并随着时间推移解锁新的大型可寻址市场。我们投资的时间并非固定，可能基于技术进展、市场机会或资源可得性而加速。因此，由于我们持续将执行速度、产能扩张和技术领先置于近期利润率优化之上，我们的经营成果、利润率和盈利能力可能在期间间波动。我们认为该方式通过让我们比竞争对手更快行动、在新兴市场更早扩张、并强化我们预期将随时间惠及业务的持久竞争优势，最大化了长期价值创造。
Supply Chain and Manufacturing Efficiency for User Terminals. The operating performance of our Connectivity segment depends in part on the cost and availability of user terminals at scale. We are vertically integrated across terminal design, production, and support, including silicon, hardware, software, manufacturing, fulfillment, and operations, which enables us to control our means of production as well as rapidly iterate to continuously improve the performance of our user terminals and optimize product cost. Since our initial launch of our user terminal, we have optimized the design of our phased-array antennas, our self-aligning antenna responsible for connecting user equipment to our LEO satellite network, for manufacturability and high-volume scale. Over the past five years, we have significantly lowered production costs and have scaled terminal output to approximately 200,000 terminals per week. We plan to continue to further scale production significantly and make gains that improve margins, lower customer barriers, and broaden addressable markets.
用户终端的供应链与制造效率。 我们连接分部的经营业绩部分取决于用户终端的规模化成本和可获得性。我们在终端设计、生产和支持方面进行了垂直整合，包括芯片、硬件、软件、制造、履约和运营，使我们能够掌控生产手段，并快速迭代以持续改进用户终端性能和优化产品成本。自首次推出用户终端以来，我们已针对可制造性和大规模量产，优化了相控阵天线（即负责将用户设备连接至 LEO 卫星网络的自对准天线）的设计。在过去五年中，我们显著降低了生产成本，并将终端产量扩大到约每周 200,000 台。我们计划继续大幅扩大生产规模，并取得改善利润率、降低客户准入门槛、扩大可寻址市场的进展。
Scaling our AI Compute Rapidly and Efficiently. Our ability to rapidly and cost-effectively scale AI compute is a significant driver of our competitiveness. We view scaling of compute capacity through a simple lens: power availability and the powered shell together determine how quickly we can deploy compute, and our model and serving stack in that powered shell determines how efficiently we convert that compute into useful tokens. In order to scale our AI segment rapidly and efficiently, our strategy is extreme vertical integration, "from shovels to tokens."
快速、高效地扩展我们的 AI 算力。 我们快速、高效地扩展 AI 算力的能力是我们竞争力的重要驱动因素。我们以简单的视角看待算力容量的扩展：电力可得性和带电厂房 (powered shell) 共同决定我们能多快部署算力，而该带电厂房中的模型与服务堆栈决定我们将算力转化为有用 token 的效率。为快速、高效地扩展 AI 分部，我们的策略是极致垂直整合——“从铁锹到 token”。
Power Availability and Powered Shells. We have demonstrated an industry-leading ability to rapidly deploy large-scale data center infrastructure at unprecedented speed and cost efficiency. Our COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II data centers collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. We brought the first cluster of COLOSSUS online in 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory, and the first cluster of COLOSSUS II online even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis.
电力可得性与带电厂房。 我们已展现出行业领先的能力，以前所未有的速度和成本效率快速部署大规模数据中心基础设施。我们的 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 数据中心合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦算力，另有额外电力容量用于数据中心运营。COLOSSUS 第一个集群我们用 122 天上线（复用了一座现有工厂的厂房），COLOSSUS II 第一个集群上线更快，仅用 91 天。作为对照，行业基准是一座 100 兆瓦绿地数据中心通常需要约两年才能上线。我们在成本效率上也实现显著改进，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本（按每兆瓦计）显著低于行业基准。
COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II were brought online almost entirely through on-site power generation capabilities that we designed, built, and deployed ourselves. We view our proven ability to construct power infrastructure at this scale and speed as a significant competitive advantage. We partner closely with local utilities to fund grid infrastructure expansions and access excess capacity, while proactively curtailing our grid usage whenever required to prioritize community needs. Megapacks--utility-scale battery storage systems-- deliver critical redundancy and help stabilize operations during peak demand. Going forward, COLOSSUS II is expected to be primarily powered by a dedicated natural gas power plant, supplemented over time by additional grid capacity that we are directly funding through our local utility partners. Our comprehensive expertise across the full infrastructure stack--from power procurement and on-site generation to distribution and advanced cooling systems--enables us to translate available power into usable compute capacity with exceptional efficiency. As we continue to scale and optimize, we expect to drive further improvements in Power Usage Effectiveness. We expect these gains to accelerate the path from buildout to monetization.
COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 几乎完全依靠我们自行设计、建造并部署的现场发电能力上线。我们认为我们以这种规模和速度建设电力基础设施的已被证实的能力是一项重要的竞争优势。我们与当地公用事业公司密切合作，资助电网基础设施扩建并使用富余容量，并在需要时主动削减电网用量以优先满足社区需求。Megapack——公用事业规模电池储能系统——提供关键冗余并在用电高峰期帮助稳定运营。展望未来，COLOSSUS II 预计将主要由专用天然气发电厂供电，并随时间通过我们直接通过当地公用事业合作伙伴出资增加的电网容量进行补充。我们在整个基础设施堆栈（从电力采购、现场发电到配电与先进冷却系统）的综合专长，使我们能以卓越的效率将可用电力转化为可用算力。随着我们继续扩展和优化，我们预期将进一步改善电力使用效率 (Power Usage Effectiveness)。我们预期这些收益将加速从建设到变现的路径。
AI Token Generation Efficiency. We are highly vertically integrated. We design, own or lease, and install all of our powered shells and dedicated processor capacity. This full-stack ownership enables us to efficiently convert power capacity into usable compute, precisely control cluster configuration, and operate a true end-to-end system spanning infrastructure through to model deployment. Our operating performance depends on how effectively we utilize deployed compute once capacity comes online--specifically, our ability to convert raw infrastructure into reliable, high-throughput token generation at scale. Achieving this requires tight coordination across model training and inference workflows, hardware configuration, and data center operations so that utilization and throughput ramp efficiently as we expand. We believe we hold a meaningful efficiency advantage by tightly integrating the model layer directly with the compute layer. Unlike third-party environments that impose multiple abstraction layers, we run our serving stack close to the processors and optimize serving, networking, and cluster configuration as a single unified system. This "model-to-compute"
AI Token 生成效率。 我们具有高度垂直整合的能力。我们设计、自有或租赁并安装所有带电厂房和专用处理器容量。这种全栈自有使我们能高效地将电力容量转化为可用算力，精确控制集群配置，并运行一个真正的端到端系统——从基础设施一直贯通至模型部署。我们的经营业绩取决于容量上线后我们能多有效地利用已部署算力——具体而言，取决于我们将原始基础设施转化为可靠、高吞吐 token 生成的大规模能力。要实现这一点，需要模型训练与推理工作流、硬件配置和数据中心运营之间的紧密协作，以确保利用率和吞吐在扩张过程中高效爬升。我们认为，通过将模型层与算力层紧密集成，我们具有显著的效率优势。与会施加多层抽象的第三方环境不同，我们将服务堆栈部署在靠近处理器的位置，并将服务、网络和集群配置作为一个统一系统进行优化。这种”模型到算力”
integration reduces overhead, improves hardware utilization, and increases the proportion of available compute that is converted into delivered output tokens. Output tokens represent the final generated response delivered to the user, while total processing can be substantially higher when a request triggers additional inference-time reasoning steps. Because we control workload scheduling and serving logic, we can prioritize high token efficiency--intelligently balancing compute allocated to reasoning with strong final output to maintain or improve response quality. This end-to-end control, combined with our sourcing relationships with leading compute providers, gives us a performance-per-watt advantage and enables us to adopt new processor generations at scale more rapidly through a repeatable playbook for reconfiguration and recommissioning.
（续）的集成减少了开销、提升了硬件利用率，并提高了可用算力转化为已交付输出 token 的比例。输出 token 代表交付给用户的最终生成响应，而总处理量在请求触发额外推理时步骤时可能显著更高。由于我们控制工作负载调度和服务逻辑，我们能够优先实现高 token 效率——智能地平衡分配给推理的算力与稳健的最终输出，以维持或改善响应质量。这种端到端控制，结合我们与领先算力供应商的采购关系，赋予我们每瓦性能优势，并使我们能够通过可重复的重配置和重新调试操作手册，更快地大规模采用新一代处理器。
Orbital AI Compute Has the Potential to Massively Increase Our Ability to Scale Our AI Compute, Accelerate Our Pace, and to Be More Cost Effective Relative to Terrestrial Options. We believe we are the only company with a commercially viable path to building orbital AI compute at scale. This is underpinned by our unique ability to launch substantial mass into orbit cost efficiently through reusable rockets and manufacture secure, reliable, and high performance satellites at low cost and high volume. We plan to develop orbital data centers to enable scaling of compute capacity for us and our customers that is independent of terrestrial power infrastructure constraints. Space offers the potential to access virtually limitless power and an operating environment that supports sustained high‑density compute, including structural advantages for power generation, cooling, and uninterrupted operations as capacity grows. We plan to employ a modular shell approach built around our scalable satellite constellation, which enables compute capacity to be deployed and expanded efficiently as capacity requirements grow. The architecture also supports shorter refresh cycles at the token layer, as we can upgrade compute as successive chip generations arrive, increasing token output per unit of installed capacity. Our goal over time is to launch 100 gigawatts of compute to space each year. If operated continuously, the generation resources used to support 100 gigawatts of compute could generate approximately one-fifth of the annual power production in the United States, which was 4.4 thousand terawatt hours in 2025, according to the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA). We expect space‑based compute to massively increase AI compute scale, while also improving token economics.
轨道 AI 算力有可能大幅增加我们扩展 AI 算力的能力、加速我们的步伐，并相较地面方案更具成本效益。 我们认为我们是唯一具有商业可行路径来大规模构建轨道 AI 算力的公司。这得益于我们通过可重复使用火箭以高成本效率将大量质量送入轨道的独特能力，以及以低成本、高产量制造安全、可靠、高性能卫星的能力。我们计划开发轨道数据中心，使我们和客户能够独立于地面电力基础设施约束扩展算力容量。太空提供了访问近乎无穷电力的潜力，以及一个支持持续高密度算力的运营环境，包括在容量增长时发电、冷却和不间断运营方面的结构性优势。我们计划采用围绕可扩展卫星星座构建的模块化厂房方法，使算力容量在需求增长时能够高效部署和扩展。该架构还支持在 token 层进行更短的刷新周期，因为我们可以随着后续芯片世代到来升级算力，从而提升单位已安装容量的 token 产出。我们的长期目标是每年向太空发射 100 吉瓦算力。如果持续运营，用于支撑 100 吉瓦算力的发电资源可产生约美国年度发电量五分之一的电能——据美国能源信息署 (EIA)，2025 年美国发电量为 4.4 千 TWh。我们预期天基算力将大幅扩展 AI 算力规模，同时改善 token 经济性。
Ability to Increase Revenue from our Consumer User Base. Our performance depends in part on our ability to effectively increase revenue from our over 1.3 billion accounts active in the last twelve months ended March 2026, including approximately 550 million monthly active AI users across Grok and X through multiple complementary monetization channels:
提升来自消费者用户基础的收入能力。 我们的业绩部分取决于我们能否通过多个互补的变现渠道，有效地从截至 2026 年 3 月过去 12 个月内超过 13 亿活跃账户（包括 Grok 与 X 上约 5.5 亿月度活跃 AI 用户）中提升收入：
Growing our Advertising Platform. Advertising remains a core monetization channel for our AI segment, with revenue driven by our ability to deliver highly relevant ads. We aim to grow advertising revenue per user by strengthening performance advertising, expanding AI‑driven targeting and measurement, and introducing richer ad formats and creative tools. A central focus of ours is making ads feel like content--contextually relevant, aligned with user interests, and integrated into real‑time conversations. Grok increasingly supports this strategy by helping advertisers with campaign creation, creative optimization, and alignment with trending topics and user intent. While these factors help us drive advertising revenue, the pricing of our advertising products is also affected by other factors, including the global economy and the highly competitive nature of our industry. We believe continued investment in AI‑powered advertising will further improve advertiser ROI while further enhancing user experience.
扩展我们的广告平台。 广告仍是我们 AI 分部的核心变现渠道，收入由我们交付高度相关广告的能力驱动。我们通过强化效果广告、扩展 AI 驱动的定向与测量、引入更丰富的广告形式和创意工具来提升每用户广告收入。我们关注的核心是让广告体验起来更像内容——情境相关、与用户兴趣契合并融入实时对话。Grok 通过帮助广告主创建活动、优化创意以及与热门话题和用户意图保持一致，越来越多地支持这一策略。虽然这些因素有助于推动广告收入，但我们广告产品的定价也受其他因素影响，包括全球经济和我们行业的高度竞争性质。我们认为持续投资 AI 驱动的广告将进一步提升广告主 ROI，同时进一步改善用户体验。
Conversion of Users to Paid Subscribers. In parallel, we are focused on converting a greater portion of our user base into paying subscribers through our X subscription (Premium and Premium+) and Grok subscription offerings. Subscribers benefit from enhanced functionality, exclusive features, and access to our latest AI models. As of March 31, 2026, we reached approximately 6.3 million active paid subscribers, which was comprised of approximately 4.4 million X Premium and Premium+ paid subscribers and approximately 1.9 million SuperGrok, SuperGrok Heavy and SuperGrok Lite paid subscribers. We plan to continue adding new features and functionality while releasing increasingly capable Grok models to increase the penetration rate of our subscriber base. Our AI segment has demonstrated exceptional model velocity: since launching Grok, we have developed leading frontier models at a far faster rate of innovation than others. We believe this pace of innovation strengthens the value proposition of our subscription offerings and supports long‑term subscriber growth.
将用户转化为付费订阅者。 同时，我们专注于通过 X 订阅（Premium 和 Premium+）和 Grok 订阅服务，将用户基础中更多比例转化为付费订阅者。订阅者可获得增强功能、专属特性以及对我们最新 AI 模型的访问。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们达到约 630 万活跃付费订阅者，其中约 440 万为 X Premium 与 Premium+ 付费订阅者，约 190 万为 SuperGrok、SuperGrok Heavy 和 SuperGrok Lite 付费订阅者。我们计划在发布功能日益强大的 Grok 模型的同时持续增加新功能和特性，以提升订阅基础的渗透率。我们的 AI 分部展现了出色的模型迭代速度：自推出 Grok 以来，我们以远高于他人的创新速度开发了领先的前沿模型。我们认为这一创新速度强化了我们订阅服务的价值主张，并支撑长期订阅者增长。
Progress Toward the Everything App and New Monetization Channels. We aim to evolve X into an "Everything App," integrating real-time information, communications, media, payments, banking, commerce and more within one consumer experience. This can increase the usefulness of X, and therefore increase the usage and monetization potential of X. We have rapid product launch velocity, with a frequent cadence of new features and products launched since 2023, including features such as long‑form video, improved group interactions, and creator tools. We plan to further broaden the value proposition of X through offerings like Money, a product we launched in beta in November 2025, which aims to expand platform utility by enabling payments and other financial services. We released X Chat in November 2025, which features end-to-end encryption and has no connection to advertising, unlike other services. We intend to further embed Grok throughout the platform to enhance discovery, analysis of posts, user support, and personalization, making core workflows more useful and reducing friction for users to adopt paid features.
迈向”万能应用”的进展与新变现渠道。 我们旨在将 X 演变为一款”万能应用 (Everything App)“，将实时信息、通信、媒体、支付、银行、商业等更多内容整合至一个消费者体验中。这可以增加 X 的实用性，从而提升 X 的使用度和变现潜力。自 2023 年以来，我们以高频次推出新功能和产品，包括长视频、改进的群组互动和创作者工具等。我们计划通过 Money 等服务进一步拓宽 X 的价值主张——Money 于 2025 年 11 月以测试版推出，旨在通过支付和其他金融服务扩展平台的实用性。我们于 2025 年 11 月发布了 X Chat，具备端到端加密，与广告无任何关联（不同于其他服务）。我们打算将 Grok 进一步嵌入平台各处，以增强发现、帖子分析、用户支持与个性化，使核心工作流更有用，并降低用户采用付费功能的阻力。
Growing Enterprise and Government Adoption of Our AI Offerings. Our future growth and financial performance depend in part on our ability to increase adoption and usage of our AI offerings among enterprise and government customers. We have launched Grok Business, Grok Enterprise, Grok API, and xAI Gov, products that we believe will be attractive to enterprises and governments, and we expect substantial opportunities to acquire new customers.
扩大企业和政府对我们 AI 产品的采用。 我们未来的增长和财务表现部分取决于我们能否在企业和政府客户中提升 AI 产品的采用与使用。我们已推出 Grok Business、Grok Enterprise、Grok API 和 xAI Gov 等我们认为对企业和政府具有吸引力的产品，并预期有大量获取新客户的机会。
We are also partnering with Cursor to advance Grok and potentially to create jointly-owned coding and knowledge work AI models, trained on our compute infrastructure. Over time, we also believe enterprises and governments will present significant opportunities for revenue expansion as they deploy our models more broadly across their organizations, adopt new capabilities, and build and operate solutions using our API. We also intend to continue to offer our compute infrastructure to third-party customers. Our ability to realize these expansion opportunities depends on continued innovation, reliable performance, and meeting evolving technical, security, and compliance requirements.
我们还正与 Cursor 合作以推进 Grok，并可能共同创建在我们算力基础设施上训练的、共同拥有的编码和知识工作 AI 模型。随着时间推移，我们也相信企业和政府将提供重大收入扩张机会——它们会在组织内更广泛地部署我们的模型、采用新能力，并使用我们的 API 构建和运营解决方案。我们还计划继续向第三方客户提供我们的算力基础设施。我们实现这些扩张机会的能力取决于持续创新、可靠的性能以及满足不断演变的技术、安全和合规要求。
Components of Results of Operations Description of Our Segments Space Revenue \- Space Space segment generates revenue primarily through (i) Launch Services for the deployment of payloads to their intended orbits for both commercial and government customers utilizing Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy, and (ii)
经营成果各部分 我们分部的描述 太空收入 — 太空 太空分部的收入主要来自 (i) 利用 Falcon 9 和 Falcon Heavy 为商业和政府客户将载荷部署到预定轨道的发射服务 (Launch Services)，以及 (ii)
Launch and Development for the development of spacecraft and provision of launch and mission services for government agency space programs utilizing Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, Starship, and Dragon. Launch Services revenue is derived from fixed-price contracts that range from one to five years. Launch and Development revenue is derived from fixed-price contracts that can range from one to fourteen years.
利用 Falcon 9、Falcon Heavy、Starship 和 Dragon 为政府机构太空项目提供航天器开发以及发射和任务服务的发射与开发 (Launch and Development)。发射服务收入来自期限为一到五年的固定价格合同。发射与开发收入来自期限可达一到十四年的固定价格合同。
The Company recognizes Launch Services revenue at a point in time, due to the interchangeability of flight hardware and minimal unique engineering costs. Revenue and costs are deferred and not recognized until upon the launch or deployment of the customer's payload to their intended orbit.
由于飞行硬件可互换且独特的工程成本极少，公司按时点法 (point in time) 确认发射服务收入。收入和成本被递延，直到客户载荷被发射或部署至预定轨道时才确认。
The Company recognizes Launch and Development revenue over time as the Company's performance on the contract creates an asset with no alternative use and the Company has an enforceable right to payment for performance to date. The Company measures progress on these contracts using the cost-to-cost input method, which the Company believes represents the most appropriate measure towards satisfaction of its performance obligation.
公司按时段法 (over time) 确认发射与开发收入，因为公司在合同上的履约创造了无替代用途的资产，且公司对迄今为止的履约具有可强制执行的收款权利。公司使用成本对成本投入法 (cost-to-cost input method) 计量这些合同的进度，公司认为该方法最合适地反映其履约义务的履行进度。
For launches of our Starlink satellites, the Company does not recognize any inter-segment revenue, rather those launch costs are capitalized in satellites in Property, plant, and equipment, net. We allocate a significant amount of launch capacity to our Connectivity segment, and expect to allocate a significant amount to our AI segment in the future. Our Space segment revenue only reflects our customer launches and customer activities.
对于 Starlink 卫星的发射，公司不确认任何分部间收入；这些发射成本被资本化进入”不动产、厂房和设备净额”中的卫星科目。我们将大量发射能力分配给连接分部，并预期未来将向 AI 分部分配大量发射能力。我们的太空分部收入仅反映客户发射和客户活动。
Revenue from Launch Services recognized at point in time and revenue from Launch and Development recognized over time as a percentage of total Space segment revenue are as follows:
按时点法确认的发射服务收入和按时段法确认的发射与开发收入占太空分部总收入的比例如下：
| | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | | Three Months Ended March 31,| | Year Ended December 31, | | 2026| | 2025| | 2025| | 2024| | 2023 Launch Services ...............................| | 53.3%| | 65.4%| | 63.0%| | 68.2%| | 55.2% Launch & Development ...................| | 46.7%| | 34.6%| | 37.0%| | 31.8%| | 44.8% Space ................................................| | 100.0%| | 100.0%| | 100.0%| | 100.0%| | 100.0% We expect Space revenue growth to continue to be lower than total company revenue growth as our internal business continues to absorb most of the growth in our launch capacity. In addition, we expect Launch and Development to represent a larger portion of our Space revenue as we continue to serve our long-term contracts for our government customers. From period to period, Space revenue will vary based on the mix of launches used for customers and our own businesses.
| | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | | 截至 3 月 31 日的三个月| | 截至 12 月 31 日的年度 | | 2026| | 2025| | 2025| | 2024| | 2023 发射服务 ...............................| | 53.3%| | 65.4%| | 63.0%| | 68.2%| | 55.2% 发射与开发 ...................| | 46.7%| | 34.6%| | 37.0%| | 31.8%| | 44.8% 太空 ................................................| | 100.0%| | 100.0%| | 100.0%| | 100.0%| | 100.0% 我们预期太空收入增长将继续低于公司整体收入增长，因为我们内部业务持续吸纳了我们大部分发射能力增长。此外，我们预期随着我们继续履行面向政府客户的长期合同，发射与开发将在太空收入中占据更大比例。从期间到期间，太空收入将根据用于客户和自身业务的发射比例而变化。
Expenses - Space Cost of Revenue The Company's Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy are composed of boosters (also known as first stages), second stages, Merlin engines, and fairings. Boosters, fairings, and Merlin engines are reusable and are classified as property, plant, and equipment and are depreciated to cost of revenue. The second stages are not reusable and are recorded to cost of revenue when they are launched for Launch Services revenue transactions or assigned for Launch and Development revenue transactions. Dragon is comprised of a fully reusable capsule that is classified as Property, plant, and equipment, net and is depreciated to cost of revenue. Starship is comprised of a booster, ship, and Raptor engines and is currently in the development stage. A majority of Starship costs are currently expensed to Research and development as incurred. Raptor engines are expensed when used in test flights.
费用 — 太空 营业成本 公司的 Falcon 9 和 Falcon Heavy 由助推器（也称一级）、二级、Merlin 发动机和整流罩构成。助推器、整流罩和 Merlin 发动机可重复使用，归类为不动产、厂房和设备，并按折旧计入营业成本。二级不可重复使用，在用于发射服务收入交易时被发射或在分配至发射与开发收入交易时，被记录为营业成本。Dragon 由完全可重复使用的载人舱组成，归类为不动产、厂房和设备净额，按折旧计入营业成本。Starship 由助推器、飞船和 Raptor 发动机构成，目前处于研发阶段。Starship 大部分成本在发生时计入研发费用。Raptor 发动机在试飞中使用时计入费用。
Space segment's cost of revenue includes second stages flown related to the Company's Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy launches, launch operations and overhead, depreciation (inclusive of booster, Merlin engine, and fairing depreciation), employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation) for our operations teams, launch testing and overhead, engineering costs, inventory excess and obsolescence, shared costs incurred in the production of launch hardware, and ongoing product support.
太空分部的营业成本包括：与公司 Falcon 9 和 Falcon Heavy 发射相关的飞行后二级、发射运营和管理开销、折旧（含助推器、Merlin 发动机和整流罩折旧）、运营团队员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、发射测试与管理开销、工程成本、存货过剩与陈旧、生产发射硬件中发生的共享成本以及持续的产品支持。
We expect Space cost of revenue to increase both in absolute dollars and as a percentage of revenue based on our expected mix of Launch Services and Launch and Development. From period to period, Space segment cost of revenue will vary based on the mix of customer and internal launches.
我们预期太空营业成本将基于我们对发射服务和发射与开发的预期比例，在绝对金额和占收入比例上均有所增加。从期间到期间，太空分部的营业成本将根据客户与内部发射的比例而变化。
Research and Development Space segment's research and development ("R&D") expenses mainly relate to the development, build, and testing of Starship. Starship costs consist of test flight hardware, Raptor engines, employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation), tooling and equipment expenses, depreciation for R&D equipment, and allocated overhead. R&D also includes certain expenses related to the development of features and modules created through engineering services for the Company's Falcon vehicles, where the Company retains the associated intellectual property.
研发费用 太空分部的研发 (“R&D”) 费用主要涉及 Starship 的开发、制造和测试。Starship 成本包括试飞硬件、Raptor 发动机、员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、工装和设备费用、研发设备折旧及分摊的间接费用。研发费用还包括与 Falcon 飞行器通过工程服务创建的特性和模块开发相关的某些费用，相关知识产权由公司保留。
We expect Space research and development to increase both in absolute dollars and as a percentage of revenue in 2026, as we invest in the development and commercialization of Starship, and to moderate both in absolute dollars and as a percentage of revenue once Starship is commercialized by delivering payload to orbit. At commercialization, Starship costs generally will be capitalized and then depreciated in cost of revenue of the segment associated with the payload delivered.
我们预期 2026 年太空研发费用在绝对金额和占收入比例上均会增加，因为我们投资于 Starship 的开发与商业化；并预期一旦 Starship 通过将载荷送入轨道实现商业化后，研发费用在绝对金额和占收入比例上均将放缓。商业化后，Starship 成本一般将被资本化，然后通过所交付载荷对应分部的营业成本进行折旧。
Selling, General, and Administrative Space segment's selling, general, and administrative ("SG&A") expenses include allocated employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation) for our sales, facilities, legal, finance, information technology, human resources, and other administrative employees, depreciation, and corporate aircraft costs.
销售、一般及行政费用 太空分部的销售、一般及行政 (“SG&A”) 费用包括为销售、设施、法律、财务、信息技术、人力资源和其他行政员工分摊的员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、折旧及公司飞机成本。
We expect Space segment's SG&A to increase in absolute dollars to support growth of our business, and to decrease as a percentage of revenue as we continue to work to reduce operating costs as a percentage of revenue.
我们预期太空分部的 SG&A 将在绝对金额上增加以支持业务增长，并随着我们持续降低运营成本占收入比例而下降。
Impairment Space impairment includes impairment losses on fixed assets due to anomalies on the Company's flight vehicles and launch sites, which occur outside our normal business operations.
资产减值 太空资产减值包括公司飞行器和发射场出现异常导致的固定资产减值损失，这些异常发生在我们正常业务运营之外。
Connectivity Revenue \- Connectivity Connectivity segment generates revenue from (i) the broadband and mobile connectivity services provided through Starlink and (ii) the sale of the Starlink Kit (inclusive of the terminal). The Company provides connectivity services and Starlink Kits to consumers or enterprise and government customers.
连接收入 — 连接 连接分部的收入来自 (i) 通过 Starlink 提供的宽带和移动连接服务，以及 (ii) Starlink Kit 销售（含终端）。公司向消费者或企业和政府客户提供连接服务和 Starlink Kit。
The Company recognizes revenue from broadband and mobile connectivity services over time as the customer simultaneously receives and consumes the benefits provided. The Company generates service revenue from (i)
公司按时段法确认宽带和移动连接服务收入，因为客户同时接收并消费所提供的利益。公司从以下来源产生服务收入：(i)
fixed-price services that require advance or recurring monthly payments by the customer or (ii) variable-priced services based on actual data consumption. The amounts received from customers for advanced payments for broadband and mobile connectivity services are recognized either ratably over the subscription term or based on actual data consumption. The Company's broadband contracts are generally month-to-month and the revenue recognized for these recurring consumer customers is equal to the amount billed in that month. The Company's mobile connectivity agreements are generally multi-year contractual obligations that range from one to five years, although the customer can generally terminate at any time.
需要客户预付或按月经常性付款的固定价格服务，或 (ii) 按实际数据用量定价的变价服务。客户为宽带和移动连接服务预付的金额，要么在订阅期内按比例确认，要么按实际数据用量确认。公司的宽带合同通常为按月签订，对这些经常性消费者客户确认的收入等于当月计费金额。公司的移动连接协议通常为期一至五年的多年期合同义务，尽管客户通常可随时终止。
The Company recognizes revenue over time for certain contracts related to our Starshield business that are multi- year in nature. For revenue that is recognized over time, we use the cost-to-cost input method. The Company records revenue based upon costs (such as materials and labor hours) incurred to date relative to the total estimated cost at completion.
公司对与 Starshield 业务相关的某些多年期合同按时段法确认收入。对于按时段法确认的收入，我们使用成本对成本投入法。公司按截至当前发生的成本（如材料和人工小时数）相对于总预计完工成本的比例记录收入。
The Company records revenue for the Starlink Kit upon delivery to the customer, or in the instance of certain enterprise customers, when it is installed. Starlink Kit revenue is reported net of sales returns, credits, and chargebacks.
公司在 Starlink Kit 交付给客户时确认收入，或对于某些企业客户，在安装时确认。Starlink Kit 收入按扣除销售退回、抵扣和退单后的净额报告。
Expenses - Connectivity Cost of Revenue Connectivity segment's cost of revenue includes depreciation (inclusive of launch, satellite, and ground infrastructure costs), Starlink Kit costs, shipping and handling costs, ground operating expenses, employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation) for our engineering and operations teams, payment processor fees, warranty expense, inventory excess and obsolescence, and customs and duties.
费用 — 连接 营业成本 连接分部的营业成本包括折旧（含发射、卫星和地面基础设施成本）、Starlink Kit 成本、运输与处理成本、地面运营费用、工程和运营团队员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、支付处理费、保修费用、存货过剩与陈旧、关税和税费。
We expect Connectivity cost of revenue to increase in absolute dollars as we grow our revenue, and to decrease as a percentage of revenue as we continue to drive efficiencies in our next-generation satellites, Starlink Kits, and ground infrastructure.
我们预期随着我们扩大收入，连接营业成本将在绝对金额上增加；并随着我们持续推动下一代卫星、Starlink Kit 和地面基础设施的效率提升，连接营业成本占收入比例将下降。
Research and Development Connectivity segment's R&D expenses mainly relate to the development, build, and testing of our next-generation satellites, Starlink Kits, and ground infrastructure. These costs include employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation), contractor compensation expenses, equipment lease expenses, depreciation for R&D equipment, and allocated overhead.
研发费用 连接分部的研发费用主要涉及下一代卫星、Starlink Kit 和地面基础设施的开发、制造和测试。这些成本包括员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、承包商薪酬费用、设备租赁费用、研发设备折旧及分摊的间接费用。
We expect Connectivity research and development to increase in absolute dollars as we grow our revenue, and to decrease as a percentage of revenue as we scale our business.
我们预期随着我们扩大收入，连接研发费用将在绝对金额上增加；并随着我们扩大业务规模，连接研发费用占收入比例将下降。
Selling, General, and Administrative Connectivity segment's SG&A expenses include allocated employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation) for our sales, facilities, legal, finance, information technology, human resources, and other administrative employees, licensing and regulatory fees, marketing expenses, depreciation, and bad debt expense.
销售、一般及行政费用 连接分部的 SG&A 费用包括为销售、设施、法律、财务、信息技术、人力资源和其他行政员工分摊的员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、许可和监管费用、营销费用、折旧及坏账费用。
We expect Connectivity SG&A to increase in absolute dollars and as a percentage of revenue in 2026 as we introduce marketing spend to support growth of our business, and to decrease as a percentage of revenue over time as we continue to work to reduce operating costs as a percentage of revenue.
我们预期 2026 年连接 SG&A 在绝对金额和占收入比例上均会增加，因为我们引入营销支出以支持业务增长；并随着我们持续努力降低运营成本占收入比例，预期占收入比例将随时间下降。
Impairment Connectivity impairment includes costs related to discontinuation of a product line for Starlink Kits that is non- recurring.
资产减值 连接资产减值包括与某条 Starlink Kit 产品线停产相关的非经常性成本。
AI Revenue \- AI AI segment generates revenue from the sale of digital platform services, including advertising, subscription, and licensing services offered to consumers and enterprise customers.
AI 收入 — AI AI 分部的收入来自向消费者和企业客户提供的数字平台服务的销售，包括广告、订阅和许可服务。
The Company generates revenue from (i) the sale of ad products displayed on its X platform, and (ii) providing AI solutions and infrastructure, which includes subscription-related offerings, data licensing arrangements, and API access to Grok models.
公司的收入来自 (i) 在其 X 平台上展示的广告产品销售，以及 (ii) 提供 AI 解决方案和基础设施，包括订阅相关产品、数据许可安排以及 Grok 模型的 API 访问。
Revenue for advertising services is recognized in the period when advertising is delivered as evidenced by a person engaging with an ad on the Company's platforms in a manner satisfying the types of engagement selected by the advertisers. The Company's contract terms for advertising services are typically cancellable short-term arrangements. We experience seasonality in our advertising revenues. Overall advertising spend tends to be highest in the fourth quarter of each year due in large part to end-of-year advertiser spending and lowest in the first quarter of each year.
广告服务的收入在广告以广告主选定的参与类型证明用户与广告产生互动的期间确认。公司广告服务的合同条款通常为可取消的短期安排。我们的广告收入存在季节性。整体广告支出往往在每年第四季度最高（主要因年终广告主支出），第一季度最低。
Revenue for AI solutions and infrastructure includes: (i) premium subscriptions on X and Grok which is recognized ratably over the period of the subscription term (ranging from month-to-month to one year), (ii) data licensing revenue which is generally recognized ratably over the period (from month-to-month to two years) in which the Company provides data as the customer consumes and benefits from the use of the licensed data, (iii) revenue from providing API access to Grok models recognized ratably over the contract term (typically month-to-month or up to one year) for stand-ready access or as services are consumed for usage based arrangements.
AI 解决方案和基础设施收入包括：(i) X 和 Grok 上的高级订阅，按订阅期间（从按月到一年）的时间比例确认；(ii) 数据许可收入，通常在公司提供数据期间（从按月到两年）按比例确认，因为客户在使用所许可数据时同时接收并受益；(iii) 提供 Grok 模型 API 访问的收入，对于待用访问按合同期（通常按月或至多一年）的比例确认，或对于按用量付费安排，在服务消费时确认。
Expenses - AI Cost of Revenue AI segment's cost of revenue includes infrastructure costs, revenue share expenses, payment processor fees, payments to creators, amortization of acquired intangible assets, and allocated labor and overhead costs.
费用 — AI 营业成本 AI 分部的营业成本包括基础设施成本、收入分成费用、支付处理费、给创作者的支付、收购无形资产的摊销以及分摊的劳动力和间接费用。
Infrastructure costs consist primarily of costs related to data center facilities, including lease and hosting costs, related support, maintenance, energy, and bandwidth costs, depreciation of servers and networking equipment, public cloud hosting costs, and employee compensation costs (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation) for our operations teams.
基础设施成本主要包括与数据中心设施相关的成本，包括租赁和托管成本、相关支持、维护、能源和带宽成本、服务器和网络设备的折旧、公有云托管成本，以及运营团队员工薪酬成本（包括工资、福利和股权激励）。
We expect AI cost of revenue to increase in absolute dollars as we grow our revenue, and to decrease as a percentage of revenue as we monetize our products and as we expand our service offerings for AI solutions.
我们预期随着我们扩大收入，AI 营业成本将在绝对金额上增加；并随着我们对产品进行变现以及扩展 AI 解决方案的服务范围，AI 营业成本占收入比例将下降。
Research and Development AI segment's R&D expenses mainly relate to the training of Grok, our leading frontier model, development, build, and testing of our next-generation AI-enabled products and data center costs to train AI-enabled products. These costs include cloud computing expenses, employee compensation expenses (including salaries, benefits, and share- based compensation), power generation costs, and depreciation of data center assets, including processors, equipment lease expenses, and networking equipment.
研发费用 AI 分部的研发费用主要涉及对我们领先前沿模型 Grok 的训练，对下一代 AI 化产品的开发、构建和测试，以及训练 AI 化产品所需的数据中心成本。这些成本包括云计算费用、员工薪酬费用（包括工资、福利和股权激励）、发电成本、以及数据中心资产折旧（包括处理器、设备租赁费用和网络设备）。
We expect AI R&D expenses to increase, both in absolute dollars and as a percentage of revenue, as we invest in compute infrastructure for Grok. Additionally, AI R&D expenses may increase as a result of the compute agreement with Cursor.
我们预期随着我们对 Grok 的算力基础设施投入增加，AI 研发费用在绝对金额和占收入比例上均会增加。此外，AI 研发费用可能因与 Cursor 的算力协议而增加。
Selling, General, and Administrative AI segment's SG&A expenses consist primarily of employee compensation expenses (including salaries, benefits, and share-based compensation) for our sales, sales support, marketing, finance, legal, information technology, human resources and other administrative employees. In addition, SG&A expenses include fees and costs for professional services, including consulting, content moderation, third-party legal and accounting services and facilities costs and other supporting overhead costs that are not allocated to other departments.
销售、一般及行政费用 AI 分部的 SG&A 费用主要包括销售、销售支持、营销、财务、法律、信息技术、人力资源和其他行政员工的员工薪酬费用（包括工资、福利和股权激励）。此外，SG&A 费用还包括专业服务费用（包括咨询、内容审核、第三方法律和会计服务）、设施成本以及其他未分配至其他部门的支持性间接费用。
We expect AI SG&A to increase in absolute dollars to support growth of our business, and to decrease as a percentage of revenue as we continue to work to reduce operating costs as a percentage of revenue. Additionally, AI SG&A may increase as a result of the compute agreement with Cursor.
我们预期 AI SG&A 将在绝对金额上增加以支持业务增长，并随着我们持续降低运营成本占收入比例而下降。此外，AI SG&A 可能因与 Cursor 的算力协议而增加。
Restructuring Charges AI restructuring charges are the result of the acquisition of Twitter in October 2022 by X Holdings. The charges include workforce restructuring for former Twitter employees, as well as impairment and early termination penalties as a result of consolidation of Twitter's various office leases.
重组费用 AI 重组费用源自 X Holdings 于 2022 年 10 月对 Twitter 的收购。这些费用包括对原 Twitter 员工的人员重组，以及因整合 Twitter 各办公租约而产生的资产减值与提前终止罚款。
Impairment AI impairment includes a one-time impairment of the Twitter brand when Twitter was rebranded to X in July 2023.
资产减值 AI 资产减值包括 2023 年 7 月 Twitter 更名为 X 时对 Twitter 品牌的一次性减值。
Other Corporate Expenses Interest Expense Interest expense includes interest expense related to our borrowings, amortization of associated debt issuance costs, undrawn fees, and finance leases. Interest expense is reflected net of capitalized interest.
其他公司费用 利息费用 利息费用包括与我们借款相关的利息费用、相关债务发行成本的摊销、未提取费用以及融资租赁相关的利息费用。利息费用在扣除资本化利息后反映。
Interest Income Interest income includes interest income earned on cash and cash equivalents and marketable securities, and dividend income from our investments in mutual funds.
利息收入 利息收入包括来自现金、现金等价物和有价证券的利息收入，以及来自共同基金投资的股息收入。
Other Income (Expense), Net Other income (expense), net consists of gain or loss on digital assets, gain or loss on foreign currency transactions, and loss on extinguishment of debt.
其他收入（费用）净额 其他收入（费用）净额包括数字资产的收益或损失、外币交易的收益或损失以及债务清偿损失。
Provision for (Benefit from) Income Taxes The provision for (benefit from) income taxes consists primarily of income taxes in certain federal, state, local and foreign jurisdictions in which we conduct business. Foreign jurisdictions typically have different statutory tax rates from those in the United States. Accordingly, our effective tax rates may vary depending on the impact of the valuation allowance as well as the relative proportion of foreign income to domestic income, generation of tax credits, changes in the valuation of our deferred tax assets and liabilities, and changes in tax laws.
所得税费用（收益）准备 所得税费用（收益）准备主要包括我们在经营业务的特定联邦、州、地方和外国司法管辖区的所得税。外国司法管辖区的法定税率通常不同于美国。因此，我们的有效税率可能根据估值备抵的影响以及外国收入与国内收入的相对比例、税收抵免的产生、递延所得税资产和负债估值的变动以及税法变动而有所不同。
Comparison of the three months ended March 31, 2026 and 2025 Consolidated Results of Operations The following table sets forth our consolidated financial statements data for the periods indicated:
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日和 2025 年的三个月的比较 合并经营成果 下表列示了所述期间的合并财务报表数据：
| | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | Three Months Ended March 31,| | 2026 vs. 2025 Change (in millions)| 2026| | 2025| | $ Change| | % Change Revenue ...............................................................| $4,694| | $4,067| | $627| | 15.4% Costs and expenses| | | | | | | Cost of revenue ..............................................| 2,388| | 1,962| | 426| | 21.7% Research and development .............................| 3,514| | 1,557| | 1,957| | 125.7% Selling, general, and administrative ...............| 746| | 493| | 253| | 51.3% Restructuring charges (credits) .......................| (11)| | 4| | (15)| | NM Impairment .....................................................| --| | 24| | (24)| | NM Total costs and expenses ...........................| 6,637| | 4,040| | 2,597| | 64.3% Income (loss) from operations ............................| (1,943)| | 27| | (1,970)| | NM Interest expense ...................................................| (664)| | (447)| | (217)| | 48.5% Interest income ....................................................| 213| | 117| | 96| | 82.1% Other expense, net ...............................................| (1,876)| | (211)| | (1,665)| | 789.1% Loss before income taxes ....................................| (4,270)| | (514)| | (3,756)| | 730.7% Provision for income taxes ..................................| 6| | 14| | (8)| | (57.1)% Net loss ................................................................| $(4,276)| | $(528)| | $(3,748)| | 709.8% _________________ NM -- Absolute percentage comparisons from positive to negative values or to zero values are considered not meaningful.
| | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | 截至 3 月 31 日的三个月| | 2026 对 2025 变动 （百万美元）| 2026| | 2025| | $ 变动| | % 变动 收入 ...............................................................| $4,694| | $4,067| | $627| | 15.4% 成本和费用| | | | | | | 营业成本 ..............................................| 2,388| | 1,962| | 426| | 21.7% 研发 .............................| 3,514| | 1,557| | 1,957| | 125.7% 销售、一般及行政 ...............| 746| | 493| | 253| | 51.3% 重组费用（抵免）.......................| (11)| | 4| | (15)| | NM 资产减值 .....................................................| --| | 24| | (24)| | NM 成本和费用合计 ...........................| 6,637| | 4,040| | 2,597| | 64.3% 经营利润（亏损）............................| (1,943)| | 27| | (1,970)| | NM 利息费用 ...................................................| (664)| | (447)| | (217)| | 48.5% 利息收入 ....................................................| 213| | 117| | 96| | 82.1% 其他费用净额 ...............................................| (1,876)| | (211)| | (1,665)| | 789.1% 所得税前亏损 ....................................| (4,270)| | (514)| | (3,756)| | 730.7% 所得税准备 ..................................| 6| | 14| | (8)| | (57.1)% 净亏损 ................................................................| $(4,276)| | $(528)| | $(3,748)| | 709.8% _________________ NM —— 由正值到负值或到零的绝对百分比比较被视为不具有意义。
Revenue Revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $627 million, or 15.4%, compared to the three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to an increase in revenue from our Connectivity segment of $782 million as our Starlink Subscriber base continued to grow as well as an increase in revenue from our AI segment of $91 million from higher X and Grok subscriptions, partially offset by a decrease in revenue from our Space segment of $246 million due to lower Launch Services missions and timing of work for government contracts.
收入 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的收入较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月增加 6.27 亿美元，增幅 15.4%。该增长主要由于：连接分部收入增加 7.82 亿美元（Starlink 订阅用户基础持续增长）以及 AI 分部收入增加 9,100 万美元（X 和 Grok 订阅增加），部分被太空分部收入减少 2.46 亿美元（发射服务任务减少及政府合同工作时序）所抵消。
Cost of Revenue Cost of revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $426 million, or 21.7%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to an increase in costs in our Connectivity segment of $437 million driven by an increase in depreciation related to the number of satellites placed into orbit and higher operating costs of $5 million in our AI segment, partially offset by a decrease in cost of revenue from our Space segment of $16 million due to less customer launches.
营业成本 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的营业成本较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 4.26 亿美元，增幅 21.7%。该增长主要由于：连接分部成本增加 4.37 亿美元（与入轨卫星数量相关的折旧增加），AI 分部运营成本增加 500 万美元，部分被太空分部因客户发射减少而使营业成本下降 1,600 万美元所抵消。
Research and Development Research and development expense for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $1,957 million, or 125.7%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to higher costs in our AI segment of $1,471 million driven by depreciation of GPU hardware, and the cost of cloud computing and data center infrastructure expenses as a result of our AI data center expansions and higher costs from our Space segment of $404 million driven by accelerated investment in our Starship vehicle and related facilities.
研发 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的研发费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 19.57 亿美元，增幅 125.7%。该增长主要由于：AI 分部成本增加 14.71 亿美元（由 GPU 硬件折旧、AI 数据中心扩张带来的云计算和数据中心基础设施费用上升所驱动），以及太空分部成本增加 4.04 亿美元（由 Starship 飞行器和相关设施的加速投资驱动）。
Selling, General, and Administrative Selling, general, and administrative expense for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $253 million, or 51.3%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to higher employee-related costs and professional fees for our AI segment of $163 million as our AI business grew rapidly, higher marketing and international expansion costs of $79 million and $23 million, respectively, for our Connectivity segment. These increases were partially offset by lower expenses of $18 million in our Space segment.
销售、一般及行政 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的销售、一般及行政费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 2.53 亿美元，增幅 51.3%。该增长主要由于：AI 分部因业务快速增长导致员工相关成本和专业服务费上升 1.63 亿美元；连接分部营销和国际扩张成本分别增加 7,900 万美元和 2,300 万美元。这些增长被太空分部费用下降 1,800 万美元部分抵消。
Restructuring Charges (Credits)
重组费用（抵免）
Restructuring charges (credits) for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $15 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This decrease was primarily due to change in estimated settlement amounts for former Twitter employees as part of the workforce reduction program implemented in 2022.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的重组费用（抵免）较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 1,500 万美元。该下降主要由于 2022 年实施的员工裁减计划下，对原 Twitter 员工预估和解金额的变动。
Impairment Impairment for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $24 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. The impairment in the three months ended March 31, 2025 was related to a post- landing anomaly in our Space segment. There was no impairment for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
资产减值 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的资产减值较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 2,400 万美元。截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月内的减值与太空分部一次着陆后异常相关。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月没有减值。
Income (Loss) from Operations Income (loss) from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $1,970 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025 driven by the factors described above.
经营利润（亏损） 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的经营利润（亏损）较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 19.70 亿美元，由上文所述因素驱动。
Interest Expense Interest expense for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $217 million, or 48.5%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to additional debt raised by the Company and other financing arrangements entered into during the period by our AI segment.
利息费用 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的利息费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 2.17 亿美元，增幅 48.5%。该增长主要由于公司在当期新增的债务以及 AI 分部签订的其他融资安排。
Interest Income Interest income for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $96 million, or 82.1%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to an increase in interest income earned from cash equivalents and marketable securities.
利息收入 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的利息收入较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 9,600 万美元，增幅 82.1%。该增长主要由于从现金等价物和有价证券获得的利息收入增加。
Other Income (Expense), Net Other expense, net for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $1,665 million, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to the loss on extinguishment of debt and unrealized loss on digital assets.
其他收入（费用）净额 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的其他费用净额较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 16.65 亿美元。该增长主要由于债务清偿损失以及数字资产未实现损失。
Provision for (Benefit from) Income Taxes Provision for income taxes for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $8 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This decrease was primarily due to the change in the mix of our jurisdictional earnings subject to different tax rates.
所得税费用（收益）准备 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的所得税准备较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 800 万美元。该下降主要由于受不同税率约束的不同司法管辖区的盈利构成变动。
Net Income (Loss)
净利润（亏损）
Net loss for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $3,748 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025 driven by the factors described above.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的净亏损较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 37.48 亿美元，由上文所述因素驱动。
Segment Results Space | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | Three Months Ended March 31,| | 2026 vs. 2025 Change (in millions)| 2026| | 2025| | $ Change| | % Change Revenue .............................................................| $619| | $865| | $(246)| | (28.4)% Costs and expenses| | | | | | | Cost of revenue ...................................................| 281| | 297| | (16)| | (5.4)% Research and development .............................| 930| | 526| | 404| | 76.8% Selling, general, and administrative ...............| 70| | 88| | (18)| | (20.5)% Impairment .....................................................| --| | 24| | (24)| | NM Total costs and expenses ................................| $1,281| | $935| | $346| | 37.0% Loss from operations .................................| $(662)| | $(70)| | $(592)| | 845.7% _________________ NM -- Absolute percentage comparisons from positive to negative values or to zero values are considered not meaningful.
分部业绩 太空 | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | 截至 3 月 31 日的三个月| | 2026 对 2025 变动 （百万美元）| 2026| | 2025| | $ 变动| | % 变动 收入 .............................................................| $619| | $865| | $(246)| | (28.4)% 成本和费用| | | | | | | 营业成本 ...................................................| 281| | 297| | (16)| | (5.4)% 研发 .............................| 930| | 526| | 404| | 76.8% 销售、一般及行政 ...............| 70| | 88| | (18)| | (20.5)% 资产减值 .....................................................| --| | 24| | (24)| | NM 成本和费用合计 ................................| $1,281| | $935| | $346| | 37.0% 经营亏损 .................................| $(662)| | $(70)| | $(592)| | 845.7% _________________ NM —— 由正值到负值或到零的绝对百分比比较被视为不具有意义。
Revenue Revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased $246 million, or 28.4%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This decrease was primarily driven by a decrease in Launch Services revenue of $236 million and a decrease of $10 million in Launch and Development revenue. The decrease in Launch Services revenue is due to a decrease in customer launches period over period. While total Falcon launches increased by 4 from 36 for the three months ended March 31, 2025 to 40 for the three months ended March 31, 2026, Launch Services missions decreased by 4 over the same period. Launch and Development revenue decreased due to timing of work performed on government contracts.
收入 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的收入较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 2.46 亿美元，降幅 28.4%。该下降主要由发射服务收入减少 2.36 亿美元和发射与开发收入减少 1,000 万美元驱动。发射服务收入下降的原因是同比期间客户发射减少。虽然 Falcon 总发射次数从截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 36 次增加至截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 40 次（增加 4 次），但同期发射服务任务减少 4 次。发射与开发收入下降是由于政府合同上履行工作的时序所致。
Cost of Revenue Cost of revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $16 million, or 5.4%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This decrease was primarily due to the decrease in customer launches and timing of work on government contracts of $34 million, offset by an increase of $10 million in inventory excess and obsolescence reserves and $10 million in launch hardware disposals for damaged Falcon fairings.
营业成本 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的营业成本较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 1,600 万美元，降幅 5.4%。该下降主要由客户发射减少和政府合同工作时序导致 3,400 万美元的下降所驱动，被存货过剩与陈旧准备增加 1,000 万美元以及因 Falcon 整流罩受损导致的发射硬件报废 1,000 万美元所抵消。
Research and Development Research and development for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $404 million, or 76.8%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily driven by higher production costs of $194 million, higher engineering costs of $95 million, and higher test and launch costs of $62 million, due to the accelerated investment in development of the Starship vehicle and continued development of production and launch facilities to support future Starship launches.
研发 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的研发费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 4.04 亿美元，增幅 76.8%。该增长主要由生产成本增加 1.94 亿美元、工程成本增加 9,500 万美元、测试与发射成本增加 6,200 万美元所驱动，原因是 Starship 飞行器开发的加速投资以及为支持未来 Starship 发射而持续开发生产和发射设施。
Selling, General, and Administrative Selling, general, and administrative for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $18 million, or 20.5%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This decrease was primarily due to lower allocated general and administrative overhead of $13 million.
销售、一般及行政 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的销售、一般及行政费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 1,800 万美元，降幅 20.5%。该下降主要由于分摊的一般及行政间接费用降低 1,300 万美元。
Impairment Impairment for the three months ended March 31, 2026 decreased by $24 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This decrease was primarily due to a non-recurring impairment loss on a Falcon 9 booster due to a post-landing anomaly during the three months ended March 31, 2025. There was no impairment for the three months ended March 31, 2026.
资产减值 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的资产减值较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月减少 2,400 万美元。该下降主要由于截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的三个月内一枚 Falcon 9 助推器因着陆后异常产生的非经常性减值损失。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月没有减值。
Loss from Operations Space loss from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $592 million compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025 driven by the factors described above.
经营亏损 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的太空经营亏损较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 5.92 亿美元，由上文所述因素驱动。
Connectivity | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | Three Months Ended March 31,| | 2026 vs. 2025 Change (in millions)| 2026| | 2025| | $ Change| | % Change Revenue .............................................................| $3,257| | $2,475| | $782| | 31.6% Costs and expenses| | | | | | | Cost of revenue ..............................................| $1,651| | $1,214| | $437| | 36.0% Research and development .............................| 205| | 123| | 82| | 66.7% Selling, general, and administrative ...............| 213| | 105| | 108| | 102.9% Total costs and expenses ................................| $2,069| | $1,442| | $627| | 43.5% Income from operations .............................| $1,188| | $1,033| | $155| | 15.0% Revenue Revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $782 million, or 31.6%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily driven by an increase of $656 million in revenue from our consumer subscribers, composed of 104.7% growth in Starlink Subscribers, offset by an 22.9% decline in Starlink Subscriber ARPU, primarily due to international expansion and the addition of lower priced service plans.
连接 | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|---|--- | 截至 3 月 31 日的三个月| | 2026 对 2025 变动 （百万美元）| 2026| | 2025| | $ 变动| | % 变动 收入 .............................................................| $3,257| | $2,475| | $782| | 31.6% 成本和费用| | | | | | | 营业成本 ..............................................| $1,651| | $1,214| | $437| | 36.0% 研发 .............................| 205| | 123| | 82| | 66.7% 销售、一般及行政 ...............| 213| | 105| | 108| | 102.9% 成本和费用合计 ................................| $2,069| | $1,442| | $627| | 43.5% 经营利润 .............................| $1,188| | $1,033| | $155| | 15.0% 收入 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的收入较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 7.82 亿美元，增幅 31.6%。该增长主要由消费者订阅用户收入增加 6.56 亿美元驱动（由 Starlink 订阅用户 104.7% 的增长构成，被 Starlink 订阅用户 ARPU 22.9% 的下降所抵消，ARPU 下降主要由国际扩张和增加价格更低的服务计划造成）。
In addition, enterprise and government revenue had an increase of $126 million primarily driven by the growth in our aviation, maritime, and other enterprise business of $209 million, our mobile connectivity business of $85 million, partially offset by a decrease of $175 million in our government connectivity business.
此外，企业和政府收入增加 1.26 亿美元，主要由航空、海事及其他企业业务增长 2.09 亿美元、移动连接业务增长 8,500 万美元驱动，部分被政府连接业务下降 1.75 亿美元所抵消。
Cost of Revenue Cost of revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $437 million, or 36.0%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to higher depreciation of $276 million from capitalized launch and satellite costs, higher operating expenses of $140 million mainly driven by ground operating costs of $50 million, customer support and installation costs of $42 million, payment processor fees of $19 million, freight costs of $15 million, and warranty costs of $12 million.
营业成本 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的营业成本较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 4.37 亿美元，增幅 36.0%。该增长主要由于：资本化发射和卫星成本的折旧增加 2.76 亿美元；运营费用增加 1.40 亿美元，主要由地面运营成本 5,000 万美元、客户支持和安装成本 4,200 万美元、支付处理费 1,900 万美元、运输成本 1,500 万美元和保修成本 1,200 万美元驱动。
Research and Development Research and development for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $82 million, or 66.7%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily due to higher costs for the next-generation production development of satellites of $62 million, Starlink Kits of $8 million, and ground equipment of $14 million.
研发 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的研发费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 8,200 万美元，增幅 66.7%。该增长主要由下一代卫星生产开发成本增加 6,200 万美元、Starlink Kit 成本增加 800 万美元、地面设备成本增加 1,400 万美元所驱动。
Selling, General, and Administrative Selling, general, and administrative for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $108 million, or 102.9%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025. This increase was primarily driven by higher marketing costs of $79 million and higher international expansion costs of $23 million, partially offset by lower bad debt expense of $9 million.
销售、一般及行政 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的销售、一般及行政费用较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 1.08 亿美元，增幅 102.9%。该增长主要由营销成本增加 7,900 万美元和国际扩张成本增加 2,300 万美元驱动，部分被坏账费用下降 900 万美元所抵消。
Income from Operations Connectivity income from operations for the three months ended March 31, 2026 increased by $155 million, or 15.0%, compared to the prior three months ended March 31, 2025 driven by the factors described above.
经营利润 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的连接经营利润较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日的前三个月增加 1.55 亿美元，增幅 15.0%，由上文所述因素驱动。
