截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年经营活动提供的净现金较截至 2023 年 12 月 31 日的全年 45.20 亿美元增加 12.56 亿美元至 57.76 亿美元。该增长主要由剔除非现金项目后较高的净利润驱动，部分被存货、应收账款、预付费用和其他资产因收入增加和 Starlink Kit 生产而减少 6.28 亿美元所抵消。
Investing Activities Net cash used in investing activities increased by $12,554 million from $4,170 million during the three months ended March 31, 2025 to $16,724 million during the three months ended March 31, 2026. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in capital expenditures of $5,967 million related to the build out of data centers and related infrastructure, and space launch facilities and related infrastructure, as well as an increase in purchases of marketable securities of $7,489 million in the period. This increase was partially offset by an increase in cash received from product rebates of $1,195 million.
投资活动 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月投资活动使用的净现金较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 41.70 亿美元增加 125.54 亿美元至 167.24 亿美元。该增长主要由与数据中心及相关基础设施、太空发射设施及相关基础设施建设相关的资本性支出增加 59.67 亿美元，以及当期有价证券购买增加 74.89 亿美元所驱动。该增长部分被产品退款增加 11.95 亿美元所抵消。
Net cash used in investing activities increased by $8,779 million from $10,796 million during the year ended December 31, 2024 to $19,575 million during the year ended December 31, 2025. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in capital expenditures of $9,574 million related to the build out of data centers and related infrastructure, and space launch facilities and related infrastructure, partially offset by a net increase in cash received from marketable securities of $1,264 million.
截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日全年投资活动使用的净现金较截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的 107.96 亿美元增加 87.79 亿美元至 195.75 亿美元。该增长主要由与数据中心及相关基础设施、太空发射设施及相关基础设施建设相关的资本性支出增加 95.74 亿美元驱动，部分被有价证券净现金收入增加 12.64 亿美元所抵消。
Net cash used in investing activities increased by $5,929 million from $4,867 million during the year ended December 31, 2023 to $10,796 million during the year ended December 31, 2024. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in capital expenditures of $6,748 million related to the build out of data centers and related infrastructure, and space launch facilities and related infrastructure, partially offset by an increase in cash received for the maturities of marketable securities of $981 million.
截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年投资活动使用的净现金较截至 2023 年 12 月 31 日全年的 48.67 亿美元增加 59.29 亿美元至 107.96 亿美元。该增长主要由与数据中心及相关基础设施、太空发射设施及相关基础设施建设相关的资本性支出增加 67.48 亿美元驱动，部分被有价证券到期收到的现金增加 9.81 亿美元所抵消。
Financing Activities Net cash provided by financing activities increased by $6,771 million from $354 million during the three months ended March 31, 2025 to $7,125 million during the three months ended March 31, 2026. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in proceeds from the SpaceX Bridge Loan and other financing arrangements of $17,950 million and proceeds from sale of our capital stock of $7,420 million, partially offset by an increase in payment on existing debt obligations and debt extinguishment costs of $14,703 million from the proceeds from the SpaceX Bridge Loan as well as an increase in repurchases of our capital stock of $3,838 million following the xAI Merger.
融资活动 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月融资活动提供的净现金较截至 2025 年 3 月 31 日三个月的 3.54 亿美元增加 67.71 亿美元至 71.25 亿美元。该增长主要由 SpaceX Bridge Loan 及其他融资安排所得款增加 179.50 亿美元和股本出售所得款增加 74.20 亿美元驱动，部分被使用 SpaceX Bridge Loan 所得款偿还现有债务义务和债务清偿成本的支付增加 147.03 亿美元，以及 xAI 合并后我们股本回购增加 38.38 亿美元所抵消。
Net cash provided by financing activities increased by $14,520 million from $11,830 million during the year ended December 31, 2024 to $26,350 million during the year ended December 31, 2025. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in proceeds from debt and other financing arrangements for our AI segment of $16,055 million and proceeds from sale of our capital stock of $5,706 million, partially offset by an increase in repayments on debt and other financing arrangements for our AI segment of $6,781 million.
截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日全年融资活动提供的净现金较截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年的 118.30 亿美元增加 145.20 亿美元至 263.50 亿美元。该增长主要由 AI 分部债务和其他融资安排所得款增加 160.55 亿美元和股本出售所得款增加 57.06 亿美元驱动，部分被 AI 分部债务和其他融资安排还款增加 67.81 亿美元所抵消。
Net cash provided by financing activities increased by $11,408 million from $422 million during the year ended December 31, 2023 to $11,830 million during the year ended December 31, 2024. This increase was primarily driven by an increase in proceeds from the sale of our capital stock of $12,327 million, partially offset by an increase in the buyback of common and preferred shares by the Company of $104 million.
截至 2024 年 12 月 31 日全年融资活动提供的净现金较截至 2023 年 12 月 31 日全年的 4.22 亿美元增加 114.08 亿美元至 118.30 亿美元。该增长主要由股本出售所得款增加 123.27 亿美元驱动，部分被公司普通股和优先股回购增加 1.04 亿美元所抵消。
Critical Accounting Estimates The preparation of financial statements and related disclosures in conformity with GAAP and the Company's discussion and analysis of its financial condition and operating results require the Company's management to make judgments, assumptions and estimates that affect the amounts reported. Note 2, "Summary of Significant Accounting Policies" of the Notes to audited consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this prospectus describes the significant accounting policies and methods used in the preparation of the Company's consolidated financial statements. Management bases its estimates on historical experience and on various other assumptions it believes to be reasonable under the circumstances, the results of which form the basis for making judgments about the carrying values of assets and liabilities.
关键会计估计 按 GAAP 编制财务报表和相关披露以及公司财务状况和经营成果的讨论与分析，要求公司管理层做出影响所报告金额的判断、假设和估计。本招股说明书其他部分经审计合并财务报表附注 2”重要会计政策摘要”中描述了用于编制公司合并财务报表的重要会计政策和方法。管理层在情况下根据历史经验和其认为合理的各种其他假设进行估计，其结果构成对资产和负债账面价值做出判断的基础。
Revenue Recognition Space contract revenue is derived from fixed-price contracts related to the development and provision of launch services for the deployment of spacecraft and other payloads to their intended orbit for both commercial customers and governmental agency space programs. Connectivity contract revenue for Starshield customers is mostly derived from fixed-price contracts related to the development of a secure satellite network designed specifically for government and national security applications.
收入确认 太空合同收入来自与商业客户和政府机构太空项目相关的航天器及其他载荷的发射服务的开发和提供的固定价格合同。面向 Starshield 客户的连接合同收入主要来自与专为政府客户和国家安全应用设计的安全卫星网络开发相关的固定价格合同。
The Company recognizes revenue over time when the Company's performance on the contract creates an asset with no alternative use and when the Company has an enforceable right to payment for performance to date. The Company measures progress on these contracts using the cost-to-cost input method, as the Company believes this represents the most appropriate measure towards satisfaction of its performance obligation. Under the cost-to-cost input method, the Company records revenue based upon costs (such as materials and labor hours) incurred to date relative to the total estimated cost at completion.
公司按时段法确认收入，前提是公司在合同上的履约创造了无替代用途的资产，且公司对迄今为止的履约具有可强制执行的收款权利。公司使用成本对成本投入法计量这些合同的进度，公司认为该方法最合适地反映其履约义务的履行进度。在成本对成本投入法下，公司按截至当前发生的成本（如材料和人工小时数）相对于总预计完工成本的比例记录收入。
The Company's contracts recognized over time using the cost-to-cost input method are complex and require the Company to estimate the total costs to perform over the term of the contracts, as well as the measurement of progress towards completion for each performance obligation. For Space contracts, developing the estimated total cost at completion for each performance obligation requires the use of significant management judgment, including assumptions regarding launch timing, labor hours, allocation of shared costs for launch vehicles that have been identified as reusable for multiple launches, as well as expected technological changes to launch vehicles and spacecraft. For Connectivity contracts, developing the estimated total cost at completion for each performance obligation requires the use of significant management judgment, including assumptions regarding labor hours, allocation of shared costs used in the production of satellites, satellite material costs, as well as expected technological changes to satellites. Material changes in estimated contract revenue or costs at completion and the resulting changes in contract profit could have a material impact on the Company's financial condition and operating results.
公司按时段法、使用成本对成本投入法确认的合同较为复杂，要求公司估计在合同期内执行的总成本，以及衡量每项履约义务朝完工的进度。对于太空合同，开发每项履约义务的预计完工总成本需要管理层做出重要判断，包括发射时机、人工小时数、被识别为可重复使用于多次发射的运载火箭共享成本分摊以及对运载火箭和航天器预期技术变化的假设。对于连接合同，开发每项履约义务的预计完工总成本需要管理层做出重要判断，包括人工小时数、卫星生产中使用的共享成本分摊、卫星材料成本以及对卫星预期技术变化的假设。预计合同收入或完工成本以及由此产生的合同利润的重大变化，可能对公司的财务状况和经营成果产生重大影响。
The impact of net adjustments from contracts recognized over time using the cost-to-cost input method to our revenue and operating income was not material for the years ended December 31, 2025, 2024, and 2023 and for the three months ended March 31, 2026. If the combined gross margins for our contracts recognized over time using the cost-to-cost input method had been estimated to be higher or lower by 1% during 2025, it would have increased or decreased operating income for the year by approximately $110 million.
按时段法、使用成本对成本投入法确认的合同对我们截至 2025、2024 和 2023 年 12 月 31 日年度以及截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的收入和经营利润的净调整影响并不重大。如果我们按时段法、使用成本对成本投入法确认的合同的合计毛利率在 2025 年被高估或低估 1%，则当年的经营利润将增加或减少约 1.10 亿美元。
Property, Plant, and Equipment, Net Property, plant, and equipment, net is stated at cost less accumulated depreciation. The Company depreciates these assets primarily using the straight-line method over the estimated useful lives of the assets except flight vehicles and spacecraft, which are depreciated over the expected number of average flights for each flight vehicle and spacecraft.
不动产、厂房和设备净额 不动产、厂房和设备净额按成本扣除累计折旧列示。公司主要按资产估计使用年限采用直线折旧法对这些资产计提折旧，但飞行器和航天器除外，它们按每架飞行器和航天器预期平均飞行次数计提折旧。
Leasehold improvements are depreciated over the shorter of their estimated useful lives or the related lease term.
租赁改良按其估计使用年限或相关租赁期限中较短者折旧。
Determining the useful lives and the number of average flights a flight vehicle and spacecraft can fly require the Company to estimate the period over which we expect to recover the economic value of our property, plant, and equipment. For each of our flight vehicle hardware and spacecraft, we consider recovery and refurbishment success rates, refurbishment economics, customer acceptance limits that may prohibit the use of vehicles that have been flown more than a certain number of launches, expected future launches included in the mission manifest, as well as any anticipated retirement timing of certain flight vehicle and spacecraft models such as Falcon as a result of anticipated transition to Starship to determine the expected number of average flights for each vehicle.
确定使用年限以及飞行器和航天器可飞行的平均次数，要求公司估计预期可收回不动产、厂房和设备经济价值的期间。对于我们的每件飞行器硬件和航天器，我们考虑回收和翻新成功率、翻新经济性、可能禁止使用已飞超过一定次数的飞行器的客户接受度限制、任务清单中包括的预期未来发射，以及由于预期向 Starship 过渡而预期 Falcon 等特定飞行器和航天器型号的退役时机，来确定每架飞行器的预期平均飞行次数。
For our satellites assets, we consider factors such as on-orbit performance, orbit-raise timing, expected service capability, and the evolution of constellation density and technology.
对于我们的卫星资产，我们考虑在轨性能、轨道提升时间、预期服务能力以及星座密度和技术演变等因素。
When we determine that the useful lives or expected remaining flights of assets are shorter or longer than we had originally estimated, we adjust the rate of depreciation to reflect the assets' revised useful lives or number of remaining flights.
当我们确定资产的使用年限或预期剩余飞行次数比我们最初估计的更短或更长时，我们调整折旧率以反映资产的修订使用年限或剩余飞行次数。
The Company periodically evaluates impairment of its property, plant, and equipment assets whenever events or circumstances indicate that the carrying value of an asset or asset group may not be recoverable. Factors we consider to identify indicators of potential impairment include significant changes or planned changes in our use of certain property, plant and equipment, technological developments that reduce the utility of the existing assets, declines in forecasted cash flows, and significant negative industry or economic trends.
每当事件或情况表明资产或资产组的账面价值可能无法收回时，公司会定期评估不动产、厂房和设备资产的减值。我们识别潜在减值指标考虑的因素包括：我们对某些不动产、厂房和设备使用的重大变化或计划变化、降低现有资产效用的技术发展、预测现金流下降以及重大不利的行业或经济趋势。
Impairment is assessed at the lowest level for which identifiable cash flows are largely independent of the cash flows of other assets and liabilities. If estimated future cash flows are less than the carrying value of the asset or asset group, an impairment charge is recognized to the extent its carrying value exceeds its estimated fair value to cost of revenue or selling, general, and administrative expenses depending on the nature of the assets, or to impairment charges if the impairment is considered to be outside the normal course of business. For the years ended December 31, 2025, 2024, and 2023, and for the three months ended March 31, 2026, impairments on fixed assets were not material.
减值评估在可识别现金流大体上独立于其他资产和负债现金流的最低层级进行。如果估计未来现金流低于资产或资产组的账面价值，则在账面价值超过其估计公允价值的范围内确认减值费用，根据资产性质计入营业成本或销售、一般及行政费用，或者如果减值被认为发生在正常业务过程之外，则计入减值费用。截至 2025、2024 和 2023 年 12 月 31 日年度以及截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的三个月，固定资产减值不重大。
If the average remaining flights for our flight vehicle and spacecraft had been estimated to be five more or fewer flights, the impact to our operating income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and three months ended March 31, 2026 would not be material. If the average useful life of our satellite assets had been changed by one year, it would have an approximately $480 million and $170 million impact on our operating income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and three months ended March 31, 2026, respectively.
如果我们飞行器和航天器的平均剩余飞行次数被估计多或少五次，对我们截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日年度和截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月经营利润的影响不重大。如果我们卫星资产的平均使用年限变动一年，对我们截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日年度和截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月经营利润的影响分别约为 4.80 亿美元和 1.70 亿美元。
Legal and Other Contingencies The Company is subject to various legal proceedings and claims that arise in the ordinary course of business, the outcomes of which are inherently uncertain. The Company records a liability when it is probable a loss has been incurred and the amount is reasonably estimable, the determination of which requires significant judgment.
法律和其他或有事项 公司在日常业务过程中受到各种法律程序和索赔的约束，其结果本质上不确定。当损失很可能发生且金额可合理估计时，公司记录负债，这一确定需要重大判断。
Resolution of legal matters in a manner inconsistent with management's expectations could have a material impact on the Company's financial condition and operating results.
以与管理层预期不一致的方式解决法律事项可能对公司财务状况和经营成果产生重大影响。
Recent Accounting Pronouncements Refer to Note 2, Summary of Significant Accounting Policies, to the audited consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this prospectus.
近期会计公告 请参阅本招股说明书其他部分经审计合并财务报表附注 2”重要会计政策摘要”。
Quantitative and Qualitative Disclosures About Market Risk Foreign Currency Risk Our Connectivity and AI businesses operate in many countries and transact in multiple currencies. In general, we are a net receiver of currencies other than the U.S. dollar for our foreign subsidiaries. Accordingly, we are exposed to foreign currency risk both from fluctuations in exchange rates affecting foreign-currency denominated transactions and from the impact of translating the assets, liabilities, revenues, costs of revenue, and other operating expenses of our foreign subsidiaries into U.S. dollars. We have experienced, and will continue to experience, fluctuations in our net income as a result of gains (losses) on the settlement and the re-measurement of monetary assets and liabilities not denominated in our functional currencies. We do not hedge foreign currency risk and changes in exchange rates could have an adverse impact on our operating results and cash flows.
市场风险的定量与定性披露 外汇风险 我们的连接和 AI 业务在许多国家运营并以多种货币进行交易。一般而言，我们的外国子公司是非美元货币的净接收方。因此，我们既面临影响以外币计价交易的汇率波动带来的外汇风险，也面临将我们外国子公司的资产、负债、收入、营业成本和其他运营费用折算为美元所带来的影响。我们曾经历并将继续经历由于非功能货币计价货币性资产和负债的结算和重新计量产生的损益而导致的净利润波动。我们不对冲外汇风险，汇率变化可能对我们的经营成果和现金流产生不利影响。
We considered the historical trends in foreign currency exchange rates and determined that it is reasonably possible that adverse changes in foreign currency exchange rates of 10% for all currencies could be experienced in the near- term. These changes were applied to our total monetary assets and liabilities denominated in our non-functional currencies at the balance sheet date to compute the impact these changes would have had on our income (loss)
我们考虑了外汇汇率的历史趋势，认为近期所有货币 10% 的不利汇率变动是合理可能发生的。我们将这些变化应用到资产负债表日以非功能货币计价的全部货币性资产和负债，以计算这些变化对我们所得税前损益的影响。
before income taxes. These changes would have resulted in an immaterial gain or loss as of March 31, 2026 and December 31, 2025, respectively.
（续）这些变化对截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日和 2025 年 12 月 31 日的所得税前损益分别会产生不重大的损益。
Interest Rate Risk Our exposure to changes in interest rates relates primarily to our investment portfolio, interest income on cash and cash equivalents and our credit facilities.
利率风险 我们对利率变化的敞口主要与我们的投资组合、现金及现金等价物的利息收入以及我们的信贷额度相关。
Our cash and cash equivalents consist of cash, time deposits, money market funds, U.S. government and agency securities. Our investment policy and strategy are focused on preservation of capital and supporting our liquidity requirements. Changes in U.S. interest rates affect the interest earned on our cash and cash equivalents. A hypothetical 100 basis point increase or decrease in market interest rates would have resulted in an immaterial increase or decrease in interest income for the year ended December 31, 2025 and three months ended March 31, 2026.
我们的现金及现金等价物包括现金、定期存款、货币市场基金、美国政府和机构债券。我们的投资政策和策略专注于资本保值和支持流动性需求。美国利率变化会影响我们现金及现金等价物所赚取的利息。市场利率假设上升或下降 100 个基点，对截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日年度和截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日三个月的利息收入将产生不重大的增加或减少。
The effective interest rate on outstanding borrowings under the SpaceX Bridge Loan was 4.58% as of March 31, 2026. A hypothetical 100 basis point increase in U.S. interest rates would increase annual interest expense by approximately $200 million.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，SpaceX Bridge Loan 未偿借款的有效利率为 4.58%。美国利率假设上升 100 个基点将使年度利息费用增加约 2.00 亿美元。

业务 / Business

449 块 · 1 标题 · 445 段落 · 英文 45,112 词 · 中文 99,349 字
English (Original)
中文译文
BUSINESS
业务
"You want to wake up in the morning and think the future is going to be great--and that's what being a space-faring civilization is all about. It's about believing in the future and thinking that the future will be better than the past. And I can't think of anything more exciting than going out there and being among the stars."
「你希望早上醒来时觉得未来会变得伟大——这就是成为太空文明的全部意义所在。这关乎对未来的信念，关乎相信未来会比过去更好。我想不出还有什么比走出去、置身群星之间更令人兴奋的事。」
--Elon Musk Our Mission Our mission is to build the systems and technologies necessary to make life multiplanetary, to understand the true nature of the universe, and to extend the light of consciousness to the stars. To do this, we have formed the most ambitious, vertically integrated innovation engine on (and off) Earth with unmatched capabilities to rapidly manufacture and launch space-based communications that connect the world, to harness the Sun to power a truth- seeking artificial intelligence that advances scientific discovery, and ultimately to build a base on the Moon and cities on other planets.
——Elon Musk 我们的使命 我们的使命是构建使生命跨行星化、理解宇宙真正本质、并将意识之光延伸至群星所必需的系统与技术。为此，我们打造了地球上（以及地球之外）最具雄心的、垂直整合的创新引擎，拥有无与伦比的能力，能够快速制造并发射连接全球的太空通信系统，利用太阳能驱动追求真理的人工智能以推进科学发现，并最终在月球上建立基地、在其他行星上建立城市。
Overview Founded in 2002, SpaceX is the only company building the integrated hardware and software infrastructure of the future across space, connectivity, and AI. At our core, we are builders. We design, manufacture, launch, and operate products and services built on cutting-edge technologies, including the world's most advanced rockets and spacecraft. We safely and reliably transport astronauts, satellites, and other payloads on missions that benefit life on Earth. Since 2023, we have launched more than 80% of mass to orbit for the world each year with an over 99% mission success rate with Falcon rockets. We also operate a high-speed, low-latency global broadband data and communications network powered by approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to millions of consumer, enterprise, and government customers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets, as of March 31, 2026. Using our dedicated satellite-to-mobile constellation, we offer connectivity services, supplementing terrestrial networks and substantially reducing mobile "dead zones" across approximately 30 countries.
概述 SpaceX 创立于 2002 年，是唯一一家在太空、连接和 AI 三大领域同时构建未来一体化软硬件基础设施的公司。在核心层面，我们是建造者。我们设计、制造、发射并运营基于尖端技术的产品和服务，包括世界上最先进的火箭和航天器。我们安全、可靠地运送宇航员、卫星和其他有效载荷，执行造福地球生命的任务。自 2023 年以来，我们每年用 Falcon 火箭把全球超过 80% 的入轨质量送上太空，任务成功率超过 99%。我们同时运营一张由约 9,600 颗位于近地轨道 (LEO, Low Earth Orbit) 的 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星驱动的高速、低延迟全球宽带数据与通信网络，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，已在 164 个国家、地区及其他市场为数百万消费者、企业和政府客户提供连接服务。借助我们专用的卫星直连移动 (satellite-to-mobile) 星座，我们在大约 30 个国家提供连接服务，补充地面网络，大幅减少移动「盲区」。
With the potential to improve both space exploration and life on Earth, AI accelerates SpaceX's mission to make life multiplanetary, to understand the true nature of the universe, and to extend the light of consciousness to the stars.
凭借同时改善太空探索和地球生活的潜力，AI 加速了 SpaceX 让生命跨行星化、理解宇宙真正本质、并将意识之光延伸至群星的使命。
xAI, which was founded in 2023 and acquired by SpaceX in early 2026, is now an integral pillar of our vertically integrated company. We are rapidly constructing AI compute infrastructure--starting on Earth with the goal of extending to space--at industry-leading pace and cost efficiency. Our infrastructure supports training and inference for Grok, which has emerged as one of the world's most advanced frontier models. Grok is designed as a truth- seeking AI model, built on our founder Elon Musk's mission to enable humanity to understand the universe. We believe that accomplishing this mission requires a truth-seeking approach to AI. We define truth seeking as the active, relentless pursuit of what is objectively true about reality, and grounded in evidence, logic, empirical data, and first principles thinking. Our goal is to understand and explain what the universe appears to be doing, as accurately as current knowledge allows. Within two years of its initial model release, Grok achieved frontier-level performance in scientific reasoning, as measured by its GPQA Diamond score, an industry benchmark that evaluates AI models on a standardized set of questions written and validated by experts, on a faster timeline than reported by other leading model providers. Grok also benefits from integration with X, our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform, which serves as a foundational distribution and data engine for our AI ecosystem and further enhances Grok's truth-seeking objective.
xAI 创立于 2023 年，并于 2026 年初被 SpaceX 收购，如今已成为我们这家垂直整合公司不可或缺的支柱。我们正以行业领先的速度和成本效率快速建设 AI 算力基础设施——从地球开始，目标是延伸至太空。我们的基础设施为 Grok 提供训练和推理支持，Grok 已成长为全球最先进的前沿模型之一。Grok 被设计为追求真理的 AI 模型，建立在我们创始人 Elon Musk「让人类理解宇宙」的使命之上。我们相信完成这一使命需要以追求真理的方式做 AI。我们将「追求真理」定义为：主动、不懈地追求关于现实客观为真的东西，并以证据、逻辑、经验数据和第一性原理思维为根基。我们的目标是在当前知识允许的范围内，尽可能准确地理解和解释宇宙正在做什么。在首个模型发布后的两年内，Grok 在科学推理上达到了前沿水平——以 GPQA Diamond 分数衡量，该分数是行业基准，使用由专家撰写并验证的标准化问题集来评测 AI 模型——这一时间比其他主要模型提供方所披露的更快。Grok 还得益于与 X 的整合，X 是我们的实时信息、娱乐和言论自由平台，是我们 AI 生态系统的基础分发渠道和数据引擎，并进一步增强了 Grok 的追求真理目标。
We believe that space represents the largest economic frontier in human history, unlocking unprecedented opportunities in orbit and on Earth. Earth has limits, so we must build infrastructure and industries in space, expanding human capabilities to improve life on Earth and to establish life beyond. Connectivity infrastructure in space is designed to help everyone on Earth have access to education, healthcare, entertainment, and communications, and to enable people to overcome many traditional limits, such as physical and political borders.
我们相信太空是人类历史上最大的经济前沿，能在轨道上和地球上释放前所未有的机会。地球有其极限，因此我们必须在太空中构建基础设施和产业，扩展人类能力，以改善地球上的生活并在地球之外建立生命。位于太空的连接基础设施旨在帮助地球上每一个人获取教育、医疗、娱乐和通信，使人们得以突破许多传统限制，例如物理边界和政治边界。
We believe AI infrastructure in space can utilize the virtually limitless power of the Sun and thereby enable the use of AI as a transformative force for understanding the universe and improving the daily lives of all humans. We believe the convergence of these areas will enable an unprecedented expansion in the global economy, leading to an age of abundance. Our innovations and technological advancements are redefining industries on Earth, while we aim to create new ones on the Moon, Mars, and beyond. We are truly building the infrastructure of the future.
我们相信位于太空的 AI 基础设施可以利用太阳几乎无限的能量，从而把 AI 变成理解宇宙、改善所有人类日常生活的变革性力量。我们相信这些领域的交汇将推动全球经济出现前所未有的扩张，迎来一个富足时代。我们的创新和技术进步正在重新定义地球上的产业，与此同时，我们的目标是在月球、火星及更远处创造新的产业。我们正在真正构建未来的基础设施。
SpaceX is the only company that has cracked the code on accessing space at scale, revolutionizing an industry characterized by decades of stagnation, risk aversion, and economically perverse cost structures. SpaceX upended this paradigm through the application of first-principles thinking, which rejects industry assumptions and builds solutions based on the fundamental laws of physics. Our intense, mission-driven, engineering-first culture and focus on extreme vertical integration have propelled us to achieve what many deemed impossible. We have demonstrated the ability to achieve groundbreaking technological innovations with speed, quality control, and precision. We pioneered high-cadence, reliable, and affordable access to space with our Falcon family of rockets, with a goal to transform the rocket launch industry into airline-like operations. In 2015, we established at least a 10-year lead over the industry by successfully landing our first Falcon 9 booster back from space before anyone else. We have continued to invest significantly in further increasing our lead by pursuing full and rapid reusability at scale, including investing over $15 billion in our next-generation rocket, Starship.
SpaceX 是唯一一家破解了大规模进入太空难题的公司，彻底改变了一个长期停滞、风险规避、成本结构在经济上扭曲的行业。SpaceX 通过运用第一性原理思维颠覆了这一范式——拒绝接受行业既定假设，基于物理学基本规律构建解决方案。我们紧张的、使命驱动的、工程优先的文化，加上对极致垂直整合的专注，推动我们实现了许多人认为不可能的事。我们已证明能以速度、质量管控和精度实现突破性技术创新。我们用 Falcon 系列火箭开创了高频次、可靠且可负担的太空进入方式，目标是把火箭发射行业改造成航空公司式的运营。2015 年，我们率先成功回收 Falcon 9 一级助推器（在所有人之前），由此确立了至少 10 年的行业领先地位。我们持续大量投资以扩大领先优势，追求大规模的完全且快速可重复使用 (reusability)，包括为下一代火箭 Starship 投入超过 150 亿美元。
We believe rocket launches and landings should be as routine and commonplace as airplanes taking off and landing.
我们相信，火箭的发射和着陆应该像飞机起飞和降落一样常规和普遍。
To achieve this sort of cadence, our iterative approach emphasizes rapid designing, testing, and process optimization, putting flight hardware in the flight environment as often as possible. This allows us to accelerate our learning by repeatedly using and improving our systems. This has resulted in a significantly higher flight rate at costs that are much lower than launch programs that existed before SpaceX. For example, according to NASA, the first version of Falcon 9 in 2010 had a launch cost of approximately $2,700 per kilogram, which represented a reduction of approximately 85% compared to the historical average launch cost per kilogram of $18,500. The first version of Falcon Heavy in 2018 further reduced this cost to approximately $1,400 per kilogram, a reduction of approximately 92% compared to the historical average cost. With the future deployment of Starship, which is designed to be the world's first fully and rapidly reusable spacecraft, we aim to further reduce the cost to reach orbit by 99% or more relative to the historical average launch cost. Central to our cost advantage is the reusability of key hardware--most notably boosters--which we recover, refurbish, and refly many times instead of discarding after single use. This dramatically lowers per-launch costs by minimizing hardware replacement expenses and spreading fixed production costs across repeated uses. Space flight that historically cost billions per launch now costs in the tens of millions, fundamentally reducing the cost of space access, providing the opportunity to build new enterprises in space.
为了达到这种发射节奏，我们的迭代方式强调快速设计、测试和流程优化，尽可能频繁地把飞行硬件放进飞行环境中。这让我们能通过反复使用并改进系统来加速学习。结果是远高于 SpaceX 之前的发射方案的飞行频率，成本却低得多。例如，根据 NASA 数据，2010 年第一代 Falcon 9 的发射成本约为每公斤 2,700 美元，相比每公斤 18,500 美元的历史平均发射成本降低了约 85%。2018 年第一代 Falcon Heavy 又把这一成本降至每公斤约 1,400 美元，相比历史平均降低了约 92%。随着未来 Starship 的部署——其被设计为全球首款完全且快速可重复使用的航天器——我们的目标是相对历史平均发射成本，把进入轨道的成本再降低 99% 以上。我们成本优势的核心是关键硬件——尤其是助推器——的可回收性：我们将其回收、翻新并多次复飞，而不是一次性丢弃。这通过最小化硬件更换支出并将固定生产成本摊销在多次使用上，大幅降低了单次发射成本。历史上每次发射成本高达数十亿美元的太空飞行，如今已降至几千万美元，从根本上降低了进入太空的成本，为在太空中建立新企业提供了机会。
Similarly, xAI has cracked the code in the complexities of building and scaling AI compute infrastructure, becoming the first company to deploy a coherent gigawatt-scale AI training cluster. We believe the combination of our proprietary AI infrastructure capability, our truth-seeking frontier model, Grok, and our access to real-time data on X creates a formidable competitive advantage, allowing us to maintain a leading position in the development of advanced artificial intelligence. This advantage stems from our complete vertical integration and the common vision infused by our founder, Elon Musk. In just a few years, we have demonstrated an ability to build coherent compute at scale and rapid speed with lower cost. COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. We believe speed is a competitive advantage. In order to bring compute clusters online as fast as possible, we employ a vertically integrated, nimble approach to construction. At COLOSSUS, we brought online the first cluster of approximately 100,000 H100 processors, approximately 130 megawatts of compute power, in just 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory. At COLOSSUS II, we brought online the first cluster of approximately 110,000 GB200 processors, approximately 210 megawatts of compute power, even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years.
类似地，xAI 也破解了构建和扩展 AI 算力基础设施的复杂难题，成为首家部署完整 gigawatt（吉瓦）级 AI 训练集群的公司。我们相信，自有的 AI 基础设施能力、追求真理的前沿模型 Grok，以及对 X 实时数据的访问，三者结合构成了强大的竞争优势，让我们能在先进人工智能的发展中保持领先地位。这一优势来自我们彻底的垂直整合和创始人 Elon Musk 注入的共同愿景。短短数年内，我们已证明了以更低成本、快速地构建大规模、协同算力的能力。COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦的算力，另外还有可用于数据中心运营的额外电力容量。我们相信速度本身就是竞争优势。为了尽快让算力集群上线，我们采用垂直整合、敏捷的建设方式。在 COLOSSUS，我们仅用 122 天就让首个集群——约 100,000 颗 H100 处理器、约 130 兆瓦算力——上线，并改造利用了一座现有工厂的外壳。在 COLOSSUS II，我们用更短的 91 天就让首个集群——约 110,000 颗 GB200 处理器、约 210 兆瓦算力——上线。作为参照，行业基准下让一座 100 兆瓦的全新数据中心上线大约需要两年。
Furthermore, in the case of COLOSSUS II, following the initial cluster, we brought online the second cluster of 110,000 GB300 processors and 220 megawatts of compute power in 64 days, demonstrating our ability to rapidly scale our facilities once built. We expect that once fully operational, the next phase of expansion at COLOSSUS II will bring online at least 220,000 additional GB300 processors and over 400 additional megawatts of compute power. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis.
此外，在 COLOSSUS II 上，继首个集群之后，我们用 64 天就让 110,000 颗 GB300 处理器、220 兆瓦算力的第二个集群上线，证明了一旦设施建成我们能快速扩容。我们预计 COLOSSUS II 的下一阶段扩张全部投产后，将再上线至少 220,000 颗 GB300 处理器和超过 400 兆瓦的额外算力。我们还在成本效率上展现了显著进步，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本按每兆瓦计算大幅低于行业基准。
We are able to deploy power and compute significantly faster than other AI companies through first-principles thinking, behind-the-meter power generation, coupled with what we believe is the world's largest network of sustainable battery storage systems, and innovations in advanced liquid cooling, high-density rack layouts, and efficient networking. Our facilities also incorporate innovative design features that limit the effects on regional electricity pricing for neighbors and include advanced water cleaning, reclamation, and recycling processes to support sustainable operations. We partner with utilities and communities to connect to and enhance the grid over time, and do so while pledging to cover costs of all new power delivery infrastructure upgrades to service our data centers, including adequate network upgrade costs, to ensure that these expenses are not passed on to the ordinary household. Our ability to rapidly and cost-effectively scale with the latest processors keeps us ahead of competitors who deploy traditional and more expensive methods. As a result, we believe COLOSSUS II became one of the world's first data centers to deploy GB200s and GB300s, the most advanced AI processors available at the time, at significant scale, and is currently powering training for our next frontier models, including Grok-5. Furthermore, through our Terafab initiative together with Tesla to build a manufacturing facility capable of producing 1 terawatt per year of compute hardware, we intend to further extend our vertical integration to chip design and manufacturing to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, optimize compute performance, and potentially reduce overall compute costs. Intel, which has the ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale, also joined the Terafab project in early April 2026. Our shovels-to-tokens approach allows us to train and iterate our frontier models at high velocity, accelerating development cycles, eliminating external bottlenecks, and driving rapid, continuous improvements in model performance.
我们能够比其他 AI 公司显著更快地部署电力和算力，依靠的是第一性原理思维、表后 (behind-the-meter) 自建发电，叠加我们认为是全球最大规模的可持续电池储能系统网络，以及在先进液冷、高密度机柜布局和高效组网方面的创新。我们的设施还包含创新的设计特性，以限制对周边地区电价的影响，并采用先进的水净化、回收和循环利用流程支持可持续运营。我们与公用事业公司和社区合作，长期对接和强化电网，并承诺承担所有为我们的数据中心提供新增电力输送基础设施升级所需的成本，包括足够的电网升级费用，以确保这些支出不会转嫁给普通居民。我们能够以最新的处理器快速、低成本扩张，让我们领先于那些采用传统、更昂贵方式部署的竞争者。因此，我们相信 COLOSSUS II 是全球最早在大规模上部署 GB200 和 GB300（当时可用的最先进 AI 处理器）的数据中心之一，目前正在为我们下一代前沿模型（包括 Grok-5）的训练提供算力。此外，通过我们与 Tesla 联合发起的 Terafab 项目，目标是建设每年可生产 1 太瓦算力硬件的制造设施，我们计划把垂直整合进一步延伸到芯片设计与制造，以缓解 SpaceX 未来可能的芯片短缺、优化算力性能并可能降低整体算力成本。Intel 拥有大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片的能力，也于 2026 年 4 月初加入了 Terafab 项目。我们的「从铲子到 tokens」一体化方式让我们能高速训练和迭代前沿模型，加快研发周期，消除外部瓶颈，推动模型性能持续快速改进。
In pursuing our mission, SpaceX has created new opportunities across our three foundational competitive advantages, Space, Connectivity, and AI:
在推进使命的过程中，SpaceX 围绕太空 (Space)、连接 (Connectivity) 和 AI 三大基础竞争优势创造了新的机会：
•Space. Launch is one of our foundational competitive advantages. We were the first private company to develop and launch a liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit (2008), the first private company to successfully dock a private spacecraft with the International Space Station (2012), the first company to propulsively land (2015) and refly an orbital-class rocket booster (2017), the first to begin deploying a large-scale LEO broadband satellite constellation (2019), and the first private company to launch astronauts to orbit, allowing American astronauts to again fly to and from the International Space Station on an American launch vehicle (2020). As of March 31, 2026, SpaceX had completed approximately 650 orbital space launches, and over 540 of those launches were completed by a flight-proven Falcon rocket, drastically reducing the cost of access to space. We are the only private company that is certified by NASA to send human missions to orbit. We are currently developing Starship, designed to be the world's most powerful launch vehicle. Starship is designed to be a fully and rapidly reusable transportation system capable of carrying larger payloads farther and at lower marginal cost per launch than our current Falcon rockets. Our unparalleled launch capabilities power every aspect of our business.
• 太空 (Space)。发射是我们的基础竞争优势之一。我们是第一家研制并发射液体燃料火箭入轨的私营公司 (2008 年)，第一家将私营航天器成功对接国际空间站的公司 (2012 年)，第一家以推进方式回收 (2015 年) 并复飞 (2017 年) 一级轨道级火箭助推器的公司，第一家开始部署大规模近地轨道宽带卫星星座的公司 (2019 年)，以及第一家将宇航员送入轨道、让美国宇航员再次乘坐美国发射载具往返国际空间站的私营公司 (2020 年)。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，SpaceX 已完成约 650 次轨道级太空发射，其中超过 540 次由经过飞行验证的 Falcon 火箭执行，大幅降低了进入太空的成本。我们是唯一一家获得 NASA 认证执行载人入轨任务的私营公司。我们目前正在研发 Starship，目标是打造全球最强大的发射载具。Starship 被设计为完全且快速可重复使用的运输系统，能比我们当前的 Falcon 火箭运送更大的有效载荷、抵达更远的距离，且单次发射边际成本更低。我们无可匹敌的发射能力为业务的方方面面提供动力。
•Connectivity. Since activating service for customers in 2020, Starlink has rapidly expanded global access to high-speed internet, prioritizing underserved rural and remote communities worldwide. While building terrestrial networks in such communities can be prohibitively expensive, Starlink is capable of delivering broadband connectivity anywhere on Earth with just a Starlink Kit. As of March 31, 2026, we had approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, operating the world's most advanced broadband constellation providing internet connectivity to approximately 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets. In January 2024, we also began deploying our Starlink Mobile constellation that utilizes separate Starlink satellites with satellite-to-mobile capabilities, substantially reducing mobile "dead zones" around the world. As of March 31, 2026, our dedicated satellite-to- mobile constellation of approximately 650 V1 Mobile satellites provides satellite-to-mobile data, over-the-top voice, and messaging services to approximately 7.4 million monthly unique devices across approximately 30 countries.
• 连接 (Connectivity)。自 2020 年为客户启动服务以来，Starlink 已快速扩展全球高速互联网接入，优先服务于全球服务不足的乡村和偏远社区。在此类社区铺设地面网络成本可能极其高昂，而 Starlink 只需一套 Starlink Kit 即可在地球上任何地方提供宽带连接。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们在近地轨道拥有约 9,600 颗 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星，运营着全球最先进的宽带星座，在 164 个国家、地区及其他市场为约 1,030 万 Starlink 订阅用户提供互联网连接。2024 年 1 月，我们还开始部署 Starlink Mobile 星座，使用具备卫星直连移动能力的独立 Starlink 卫星，大幅减少全球移动「盲区」。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们由约 650 颗 V1 Mobile 卫星组成的专用卫星直连移动星座，在约 30 个国家为约 740 万个月度独立设备提供卫星直连移动数据、Over-the-Top 语音和消息服务。
•AI. We were the first company to deploy a coherent gigawatt-scale AI training cluster. We own and operate what we believe to be the largest AI training data center clusters on Earth, consisting of hundreds of thousands GPUs--all in the same spirit that enabled us to launch Grok faster than any other leading foundational AI model --while maintaining full vertical integration from on-site power generation and water reclamation to GPU deployment. In under two years, we have established a dual advantage in both cost efficiency and deployment speed at scale. By owning the compute infrastructure and vertically integrating across the full AI stack, we can train and iterate our frontier models at lower cost and higher velocity and accelerate development cycles. This eliminates external bottlenecks and drives rapid, continuous improvements in model performance. The addition of the Terafab initiative aims to further extend our control to the foundational processor layer. We believe that the key constraints in the continued growth of AI are physical--chip manufacturing, data center infrastructure, and power generation; the future of AI will be determined by the control of the physical stack. We believe no other AI company has better control over the full physical stack than SpaceX. We believe this combination of our state-of-the-art AI compute infrastructure, our truth-seeking frontier model, and our access to real-time data on X creates a significant strategic advantage. Our integrated AI platforms across Grok and X have over 1.3 billion supported accounts active in the last twelve months ended March 31, 2026, including approximately 550 million MAUs, up from over 1.1 billion supported accounts and approximately 520 million MAUs as of December 31, 2025, and generating approximately 350 million daily posts. Of our MAUs, we had approximately 117 million MAUs that used Grok's AI features as of March 31, 2026. Grok's deep integration with X enables freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness that we believe is a competitive differentiator.
• AI。我们是首家部署完整 gigawatt 级 AI 训练集群的公司。我们拥有并运营着我们认为是全球最大的 AI 训练数据中心集群，包含数十万颗 GPU——这一切秉承的精神，与让我们比其他主要基础 AI 模型更快推出 Grok 的精神一脉相承——并保持从现场发电、水回收到 GPU 部署的全面垂直整合。在不到两年的时间里，我们已在大规模成本效率和部署速度两方面建立了双重优势。通过自有算力基础设施并跨整个 AI 栈进行垂直整合，我们能以更低成本、更高速度训练并迭代前沿模型，加快研发周期。这消除了外部瓶颈，推动模型性能持续快速改进。Terafab 项目的加入旨在将我们的控制力进一步延伸到底层处理器层。我们相信，AI 持续增长的关键约束是物理性的——芯片制造、数据中心基础设施和发电；AI 的未来将由对物理栈的控制力决定。我们相信没有其他 AI 公司比 SpaceX 对完整物理栈拥有更强的控制力。我们相信这种最先进 AI 算力基础设施、追求真理的前沿模型以及对 X 实时数据的访问的组合，构成了重大的战略优势。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的过去十二个月内，我们整合的 Grok 与 X AI 平台拥有超过 13 亿活跃支持账户，其中包括约 5.5 亿月活用户 (MAU)，相比 2025 年 12 月 31 日的超过 11 亿支持账户和约 5.2 亿 MAU 有所增长，并产生约 3.5 亿条日发帖。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，在我们的 MAU 中约有 1.17 亿 MAU 使用了 Grok 的 AI 功能。Grok 与 X 的深度整合带来了我们认为是竞争性差异点的时效性、相关性和上下文感知能力。
This direct, real-time access to the information and human discourse on X enhances Grok's truth-seeking capabilities by grounding outputs in up-to-date knowledge and diverse viewpoints. As a result, we believe Grok can deliver the most objective and relevant insights and best serve high-frequency, high-value use cases across consumer and enterprise AI applications.
这种对 X 上信息和人类讨论的直接、实时访问，通过将输出建立在最新知识和多元观点之上，增强了 Grok 追求真理的能力。因此，我们相信 Grok 能够提供最客观且最相关的洞察，并最好地服务于消费者和企业 AI 应用中高频、高价值的场景。
For complex reasoning and agentic workloads, compute is directly correlated with the quality of intelligence and task completion speed. Over the long-term, however, we expect Earth's finite resources will not be able to sustain the immense computational demands of advanced AI models. Sustainably satisfying this compute demand will require space-based infrastructure that utilizes the ultimate fusion energy source: the Sun. We believe we are the only company with a commercially viable path to building orbital AI compute at scale, due to our unique ability to launch substantial mass into orbit through reusable, cost-efficient rockets, to manufacture secure, reliable, and high-performance satellites at low cost and high volume, and to manage large-scale constellations. We expect that owning scalable, power-efficient infrastructure to train and operate frontier models will be the most important driver for AI differentiation as AI systems converge toward artificial general intelligence ("AGI")--which has the potential to unlock large-scale productivity gains, scientific discovery, and societal abundance.
对复杂推理和 agent 类工作负载而言，算力直接关联着智能的质量和任务完成速度。但从长期看，我们预计地球的有限资源将无法支撑先进 AI 模型巨大的算力需求。可持续地满足这一算力需求，将需要利用终极聚变能源——太阳——的太空基础设施。我们相信我们是唯一具备商业可行路径在太空中大规模构建轨道 AI 算力的公司，因为我们独有以下能力：通过可重复使用、低成本的火箭把大量质量送入轨道；以低成本、高产量制造安全可靠、高性能的卫星；以及管理大规模星座。我们预计，随着 AI 系统朝着通用人工智能 (AGI) 收敛——AGI 有潜力释放大规模生产力提升、科学发现和社会富足——拥有可扩展、能效高的基础设施来训练和运行前沿模型将成为 AI 差异化最重要的驱动力。
We have created distinct new markets across the space, connectivity, and AI industries by building the integrated hardware and software infrastructure of the future and by combining our broad range of capabilities. For example, SpaceX's recent acquisition of xAI unites SpaceX's launch capabilities and global connectivity network with xAI's AI development capabilities. Specifically, we believe SpaceX's reusable rockets, scaled satellite manufacturing, and operational expertise can enable the cost-effective and rapid deployment of massive AI compute satellite constellations--with potentially millions of satellites--for orbital data centers. We believe these AI compute satellites in Sun-synchronous orbit will be able to handle energy-intensive AI workloads, such as inference demand, at far greater scale and efficiency than terrestrial alternatives, with Starlink providing low-latency, global connectivity linking these orbital AI systems to people around the world and delivering real-time intelligence. Our goal is to leverage our launch leadership, global connectivity network, and AI expertise to allow us to continue building the integrated infrastructure of the future on Earth, the Moon, Mars, and beyond to benefit humanity.
我们通过构建未来的一体化软硬件基础设施并整合多方面能力，在太空、连接和 AI 行业创造了独特的新市场。例如，SpaceX 最近对 xAI 的收购，把 SpaceX 的发射能力和全球连接网络与 xAI 的 AI 研发能力联结起来。具体而言，我们相信 SpaceX 的可重复使用火箭、规模化卫星制造和运营经验，能让大规模 AI 算力卫星星座——可能多达数百万颗——以低成本和快速方式部署，作为轨道数据中心。我们相信处于太阳同步轨道上的这些 AI 算力卫星，将能以远超地面替代方案的规模和效率处理能耗密集的 AI 工作负载（例如推理需求），Starlink 则提供低延迟全球连接，将这些轨道 AI 系统与全球用户连接起来，并实时交付智能。我们的目标是利用我们的发射领先地位、全球连接网络和 AI 能力，让我们能继续在地球、月球、火星及更远处构建未来的一体化基础设施，以造福人类。
We have an intense, mission-driven, engineering-first culture that seeks to achieve what many have deemed impossible. "The Algorithm," as it is known internally, is a five-step iterative process that emphasizes making the requirements less dumb, deleting unnecessary processes or parts (embracing the principle that the best part is no part), only then optimizing the necessary processes or parts, accelerating cycle time, and automating only proven processes. We strive to make the incredible and extraordinary accessible and repeatable, and we have grown rapidly by continuously leveraging our core strengths, including:
我们拥有紧张的、使命驱动的、工程优先的文化，致力于实现许多人认为不可能的事。我们内部称之为「The Algorithm（算法）」的五步迭代流程强调：让需求不那么愚蠢、删除不必要的流程或零件（拥抱「最好的零件就是没有零件」原则），然后再优化必要的流程或零件、加快循环周期，最后只对已验证的流程进行自动化。我们努力让令人难以置信和非凡的事物变得可获得且可复制，并通过持续撬动核心优势实现了快速增长，这些核心优势包括：
•Global leadership in orbital launch services;
• 在轨道发射服务领域的全球领导地位；
•Unrivaled satellite and connectivity platform across design, manufacturing, deployment, and operations;
• 跨设计、制造、部署和运营的、无可匹敌的卫星与连接平台；
•Truth-seeking AI model enhanced by real-time data;
• 由实时数据增强的追求真理的 AI 模型；
•Extreme vertical integration enabling high velocity and superior cost efficiency at scale;
• 极致的垂直整合，带来大规模的高速度与卓越的成本效率；
•Unique ability to scale new trillion-dollar markets across Space, Connectivity, and AI;
• 跨太空、连接和 AI 拓展新万亿美元级市场的独特能力；
•Business models that are incredibly difficult to replicate; and •Our mission-driven culture and world-class talent.
• 极难复制的商业模式；以及 • 我们使命驱动的文化和世界级人才。
We have a stellar track record of capital allocation and value creation in Space and Connectivity. Since SpaceX's founding in 2002, we have raised over $9 billion of equity capital to fund the development and growth of these two business segments. The Space segment became Segment Adjusted EBITDA positive on a sustained basis beginning in 2018 and the Connectivity segment became in aggregate Segment Adjusted EBITDA positive on a sustained basis beginning in 2023. In 2025, our Space segment generated a loss from operations of $(657) million and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $653 million, including the impact of funding $3,004 million in research and development expense for our next-generation Starship launch vehicle program. In 2025, our Connectivity segment generated income from operations of $4,423 million and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $7,168 million.
我们在太空和连接业务上拥有出色的资本配置和价值创造记录。自 SpaceX 2002 年成立以来，我们已为这两个业务板块的研发和增长募集了超过 90 亿美元的股权资本。太空板块自 2018 年起持续实现 Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为正，连接板块自 2023 年起在合计层面持续实现 Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为正。2025 年，太空板块产生 (6.57) 亿美元的运营亏损和 6.53 亿美元的 Segment Adjusted EBITDA，其中包含为下一代 Starship 发射载具项目支出的 30.04 亿美元研发费用。2025 年，连接板块实现 44.23 亿美元的运营利润和 71.68 亿美元的 Segment Adjusted EBITDA。
Our financial results reflect the strength of our operating model and our ability to create and scale multiple new businesses:
我们的财务结果反映了我们运营模式的稳健性和创造并扩展多个新业务的能力：
•For the three months ended March 31, 2026, we generated revenue on a consolidated basis of $4,694 million, loss from operations of $(1,943) million and Adjusted EBITDA of $1,127 million. In 2025, we generated revenue on a consolidated basis of $18,674 million, loss from operations of $(2,589) million and Adjusted EBITDA of $6,584 million. Our Space and Connectivity segments contributed the substantial majority of our consolidated revenue in the three months ended March 31, 2026 and the year ended December 31, 2025, demonstrating the benefits of their scale and operating leverage in our vertically integrated business model;
• 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月内，我们的综合收入为 46.94 亿美元，运营亏损为 (19.43) 亿美元，调整后 EBITDA 为 11.27 亿美元。2025 年，我们的综合收入为 186.74 亿美元，运营亏损为 (25.89) 亿美元，调整后 EBITDA 为 65.84 亿美元。在截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月和截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日止年度内，太空和连接板块贡献了我们综合收入的绝大部分，体现了垂直整合业务模式中规模和运营杠杆的效益；
•For the three months ended March 31, 2026, our Space segment generated revenue of $619 million, loss from operations of $(662) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(351) million. In 2025, our Space segment generated revenue of $4,086 million, loss from operations of $(657) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $653 million Additionally, our Space segment funded $930 million and $3,004 million in research and development expense during the three months ended March 31, 2026 and the year ended December 31, 2025, respectively, for our next-generation Starship launch vehicle program. Starship is designed to enable a step- function change in our launch capability across reusability, payload capacity, and launch cadence, and is the key enabler of our long-term growth strategy by unlocking entirely new categories of missions;
• 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月内，太空板块收入为 6.19 亿美元，运营亏损为 (6.62) 亿美元，Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为 (3.51) 亿美元。2025 年，太空板块收入为 40.86 亿美元，运营亏损为 (6.57) 亿美元，Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为 6.53 亿美元。此外，太空板块在截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月和截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日止年度，分别为下一代 Starship 发射载具项目投入了 9.30 亿美元和 30.04 亿美元的研发费用。Starship 被设计为在可回收性、有效载荷容量和发射频率三方面带来跃迁式提升，并通过解锁全新类别的任务，成为我们长期增长战略的关键推动者；
•For the three months ended March 31, 2026, our Connectivity segment generated revenue of $3,257 million, income from operations of $1,188 million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $2,087 million. Our Connectivity segment, primarily driven by Starlink, generated revenue of $11,387 million, income from operations of $4,423 million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $7,168 million in 2025, representing year-over-year growth of 49.8%, 120.4%, and 86.2%, respectively, benefiting from subscriber growth, increasing enterprise adoption, and continued improvement in network efficiency;
• 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月内，我们的连接板块收入为 32.57 亿美元，运营利润为 11.88 亿美元，Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为 20.87 亿美元。我们的连接板块（主要由 Starlink 驱动）在 2025 年的收入为 113.87 亿美元，运营利润为 44.23 亿美元，Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为 71.68 亿美元，同比分别增长 49.8%、120.4% 和 86.2%，受益于订阅用户增长、企业渗透率提升和网络效率持续改善；
•In our newly acquired AI segment, we plan to prioritize growth and investment to capture significant opportunities in AI applications and compute infrastructure. For the three months ended March 31, 2026, our AI segment generated revenue of $818 million, loss from operations of $(2,469) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(609) million. In 2025, our AI segment generated revenue of $3,201 million, loss from operations of $(6,355) million, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA of $(1,237) million, reflecting its earlier stage of development and continued investments to support long-term growth opportunities in AI; and •For the three months ended March 31, 2026, capital expenditures for our Space segment was $1,052 million, for our Connectivity segment was $1,332 million and for our AI segment was $7,723 million. In 2025, capital expenditures for our Space segment was $3,832 million, for our Connectivity segment was $4,178 million and for our AI segment was $12,727 million.
• 在我们新收购的 AI 板块，我们计划优先投入增长和投资以抓住 AI 应用和算力基础设施中的重大机会。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月内，AI 板块收入为 8.18 亿美元，运营亏损为 (24.69) 亿美元，Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为 (6.09) 亿美元。2025 年，AI 板块收入为 32.01 亿美元，运营亏损为 (63.55) 亿美元，Segment Adjusted EBITDA 为 (12.37) 亿美元，反映其较早期的发展阶段以及为支持 AI 长期增长机会而进行的持续投资；以及 • 截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月内，太空板块的资本支出为 10.52 亿美元、连接板块为 13.32 亿美元、AI 板块为 77.23 亿美元。2025 年，太空板块的资本支出为 38.32 亿美元、连接板块为 41.78 亿美元、AI 板块为 127.27 亿美元。
Segment Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP measure. Please refer to the section titled "Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations--Non-GAAP Financial Measures" for additional information on our non-GAAP financial measures, including reconciliations of Segment Adjusted EBITDA to segment income (loss) from operations, the most directly comparable GAAP measure.
Segment Adjusted EBITDA 是一项非 GAAP 指标。请参阅「管理层对财务状况和经营结果的讨论与分析——非 GAAP 财务指标」一节，了解我们非 GAAP 财务指标的更多信息，包括 Segment Adjusted EBITDA 与最直接可比 GAAP 指标 segment income (loss) from operations 的对账。
Why This Matters Now For the entirety of its existence, human civilization has lived on a single celestial body: Earth. The current paradigm, in which human civilization is confined to one planet, exposes humanity to existential threats that are unpredictable and uncontrollable on a planetary scale. These threats include naturally occurring catastrophic events--such as asteroid impacts, volcanic activity, or solar fluctuations--as well as man-made global conflicts. Geological and astronomical records indicate a non-zero probability of extinction-level events occurring over periods measurable in millions of years. Reliance on a single planetary home constitutes a single point of failure and carries existential risk with a probability of one that must be solved. By moving beyond the only home we have ever known, we ensure species-level redundancy and that the light of consciousness will not be tied to a single planet subject to the inevitable hazards of a harsh and vast universe. We do not want humans to have the same fate as dinosaurs. We want to give them a reason to look ahead with excitement, with the prospect that we are entering an age of abundance with an endlessly prosperous and exciting future.
为什么此刻重要 在其全部存在期间，人类文明都生活在唯一一颗天体上：地球。当前这种把人类文明限定在一颗行星上的范式，让人类暴露在难以预测和控制的、行星级的存在性威胁之下。这些威胁包括自然发生的灾难性事件——例如小行星撞击、火山活动或太阳波动——以及人为的全球冲突。地质与天文记录显示，以百万年为尺度，发生灭绝级事件的概率不为零。仅依赖单一行星家园构成了单点故障，并以概率为 1 的方式承载着必须解决的存在性风险。通过走出我们唯一所知的家园，我们确保了物种级别的冗余，并确保意识之光不会被绑定在一颗注定要承受严酷宇宙不可避免危害的单一行星上。我们不希望人类与恐龙同样的命运。我们希望给他们一个理由，让他们带着兴奋向前看，期待我们正进入一个无尽繁荣、激动人心的富足时代。
Artist Visualization of Life on Mars For decades, a reality where humanity travels between the planets and the stars has felt tantalizingly close but still locked in the pages and screens of science fiction. We are capable of better understanding the universe, exploring the universe, and ultimately making life multiplanetary across the universe. We are becoming a civilization with the ability to reach beyond Earth's cradle and begin to inhabit other worlds. While we remain dedicated to this fundamental mission, our progress in accessing space continues to yield opportunities that enrich life on Earth.
火星生活的艺术家想象图 几十年来，人类在行星与群星之间穿行的现实感觉近在咫尺，却依然被锁在科幻小说的书页和银幕里。我们有能力更好地理解宇宙、探索宇宙，并最终让生命跨越宇宙、变得跨行星化。我们正在成为一个有能力走出地球摇篮、开始居住于其他世界的文明。我们仍致力于这一根本使命，与此同时，我们在进入太空方面的进展持续带来丰富地球生活的机会。
We believe our steps into the expanse will be accelerated by the rapid emergence of AI. As humanity moves into the unknown, we believe AI will be our greatest tool for innovation and navigation, helping us better understand day-to- day life and the universe, and master the complexity of establishing new civilizations in the far-flung reaches of space. For AI to help us understand the universe, we believe it must be able to discard the often popular, but wrong, in favor of the unpopular, but true. By combining the innate human desire to seek truth and explore with our breakthrough technologies, we believe humanity will eventually reach new frontiers across the universe, while enhancing the quality and resilience of life on Earth.
我们相信，AI 的快速涌现将加速我们迈入辽阔宇宙的步伐。当人类走向未知，我们相信 AI 将成为我们最伟大的创新和导航工具，帮助我们更好地理解日常生活和宇宙，掌握在太空遥远疆域建立新文明的复杂性。要让 AI 帮助我们理解宇宙，我们认为它必须能够摒弃流行却错误的东西，选择不流行但正确的东西。通过把人类与生俱来的寻找真理和探索的渴望与我们的突破性技术相结合，我们相信人类最终将抵达宇宙中的新边疆，同时提升地球生活的质量和韧性。
The rapid emergence of the AI era intensifies the urgency of our mission, as AI has the potential to accelerate not only space exploration, but also transformative societal advancements on Earth. However, AI's ability to revolutionize human potential is directly dependent on meeting exponentially increasing resource demands. On Earth, the massive expansion of data center capacity to support growing compute demand is significantly outpacing electricity generation, which was effectively flat in the United States for approximately 15 years, growing at a compound annual growth rate of 0.1% from 2008 to 2023. Despite the recent increase in electricity demand from AI data centers, electricity generation in the United States has grown at an annual rate of less than 3% between 2023 and 2025, while electricity generation in China has grown at approximately twice that rate in the same time period.
AI 时代的快速涌现强化了我们使命的紧迫性，因为 AI 不仅有潜力加速太空探索，也有潜力推动地球上的变革性社会进步。然而，AI 释放人类潜能的能力直接取决于能否满足指数级增长的资源需求。在地球上，数据中心容量为支持不断增长的算力需求而进行的大规模扩张，正在显著超过发电增速；美国发电量在大约 15 年间基本持平，从 2008 年到 2023 年的年复合增长率仅为 0.1%。尽管 AI 数据中心近期推高了电力需求，美国 2023 年至 2025 年间发电量的年增长率仍低于 3%，而同期中国的发电量增速约为这一水平的两倍。
U.S. compute demand has already outpaced available power supply with estimated demand of 62 gigawatts in 2025 exceeding the power generation of 49 gigawatts, according to industry sources. We expect the gap between demand for compute and power supply to continue to widen meaningfully as AI compute needs proliferate. Such structural power shortages are expected to intensify over the coming years. This supply and demand imbalance is already imposing unsustainable strains on terrestrial power grids, supply chains, and the environment. The Sun contains approximately 99.8% of the solar system's energy and, as a result, we believe it is the only truly scalable solution to terrestrial energy constraints in the age of AI. Harnessing this energy in space is considerably more efficient than on land. Space-based solar arrays can generate more than five times the energy per unit area of terrestrial solar due to continuous illumination, lack of atmospheric interference, and optimal orientation. SpaceX is well-positioned to capture this space-based solar energy through our ability to rapidly access Sun-synchronous orbit through our satellite manufacturing scale and launch capability. As a result, we are expanding our footprint and harnessing the vast resources of space that are essential to sustaining technological development. Our goal is to ensure that AI becomes a force for human flourishing and a benefit to civilization, rather than a catalyst for terrestrial resource depletion and instability. We believe owning scalable, power-efficient infrastructure to train and operate frontier models will be the most important competitive differentiator as AI systems converge toward AGI--which has the potential to unlock large-scale productivity gains, scientific discovery, and societal abundance.
据行业来源，美国算力需求已经超过可用电力供应，2025 年估算需求 62 吉瓦，超过 49 吉瓦的发电量。我们预计随着 AI 算力需求持续扩散，算力需求与电力供应之间的差距会继续显著扩大。这类结构性电力短缺预计将在未来几年加剧。这种供需失衡已经对地面电网、供应链和环境造成不可持续的压力。太阳含有太阳系约 99.8% 的能量，因此我们认为它是在 AI 时代真正可扩展地解决地面能源约束的唯一方案。在太空中利用这种能量比在陆地上效率高得多。由于持续的光照、不受大气干扰且取向最优，太空太阳能阵列每单位面积能产生的能量是地面太阳能的五倍以上。SpaceX 凭借规模化卫星制造和发射能力，能够快速进入太阳同步轨道，处于捕获这种太空太阳能的有利位置。因此，我们正在扩展自己的疆域，并利用对维持技术发展至关重要的太空浩瀚资源。我们的目标是确保 AI 成为推动人类繁荣、造福文明的力量，而不是地球资源枯竭和不稳定的催化剂。我们相信，随着 AI 系统朝着 AGI 收敛——AGI 有潜力释放大规模生产力提升、科学发现和社会富足——拥有可扩展、能效高的基础设施来训练和运行前沿模型将成为最重要的竞争差异点。
We believe space represents the largest economic frontier in human history. Our unmatched launch cadence has massively increased access to space, enabling rapid and reliable missions for humans, cargo, and satellites--creating unprecedented opportunities for innovation, scientific discovery, and global connectivity. SpaceX has always been a mission-driven company, founded with the goal of making humanity multiplanetary. By dramatically reducing the cost of access to space, we have been able to expand our mission to address some of the Earth's most pressing challenges, including bridging the digital divide by aiming to connect over three billion unconnected people to the internet and humanity's collective knowledge. Starlink is our groundbreaking solution for global internet connectivity, delivering high-speed, low-latency access to the most remote and underserved corners of the world-- from Antarctica's frozen wilderness to vast oceans and towering mountaintops--overcoming barriers posed by traditional terrestrial infrastructure. Starlink's unparalleled global reach has the potential to enable society to educate billions of people, to help lift entire communities out of poverty, and to provide essential connectivity to schools, hospitals, and critical services, fostering a more equitable and informed future for humanity. We support essential applications such as education in rural and underserved regions, telemedicine for hard-to-reach patients, seamless connectivity for aviation and maritime users, and resilient communications during natural disasters. For example, during the 2023 Maui wildfires, which devastated Lahaina and left thousands without power or cellular service, Starlink rapidly deployed over 650 terminals to restore high-speed internet connectivity, enabling first responders, humanitarian organizations, and survivors to coordinate relief efforts, access aid resources, communicate with family, and support recovery in areas where traditional infrastructure had completely failed. During Hurricanes Helene and Milton in 2024 in the southeastern United States, our Starlink terminals provided a rapid lifeline for communication and recovery when traditional cell towers, broadband lines, and power infrastructure were knocked out for days or weeks by widespread damage caused by flooding and high winds.
我们相信太空是人类历史上最大的经济前沿。我们无可匹敌的发射频率已大幅提升进入太空的能力，使得载人、货运和卫星任务能快速可靠地执行——为创新、科学发现和全球连接创造前所未有的机会。SpaceX 一直是使命驱动的公司，创立的目标是让人类跨行星化。通过大幅降低进入太空的成本，我们得以把使命扩展到应对地球上一些最紧迫的挑战，包括弥合数字鸿沟——目标是把超过 30 亿尚未接入互联网的人连接到互联网和人类的集体知识。Starlink 是我们解决全球互联网连接问题的突破性方案，为世界上最偏远和服务最不足的角落提供高速低延迟接入——从南极冰原荒野到广阔海洋和高耸山顶——克服传统地面基础设施带来的障碍。Starlink 无与伦比的全球覆盖有潜力让社会教育数十亿人、帮助整个社区脱贫，并为学校、医院和关键服务提供基本连接，培育更平等、更明智的人类未来。我们支持包括乡村和服务不足地区的教育、远程医疗、航空和航海用户的无缝连接、自然灾害期间的弹性通信等关键应用。例如在 2023 年毛伊岛野火（摧毁了 Lahaina 并让数千人失去电力和蜂窝服务）期间，Starlink 快速部署了超过 650 台终端以恢复高速互联网连接，使应急人员、人道主义组织和幸存者能够协调救援工作、获取援助资源、与家人沟通，并在传统基础设施完全瘫痪的地区支援恢复。在 2024 年美国东南部的 Helene 和 Milton 飓风期间，当传统蜂窝塔、宽带线路和电力基础设施被大范围洪水和大风破坏导致数日或数周中断时，我们的 Starlink 终端为通信和恢复提供了快速生命线。
Our AI technology also has the ability to elevate the quality of life for people and communities around the world.
我们的 AI 技术也有能力提升全球人民和社区的生活质量。
We believe AI has the potential to revolutionize human potential--from advanced manufacturing and infrastructure development to scientific research and medicine--delivering tangible real-world benefits for individuals, organizations, and governments. For example, AI systems can expedite scientific discovery for researchers, aid healthcare professionals in precise medical analysis and diagnosis, and empower educators to craft tailored learning experiences for students. Moreover, these technologies can optimize Earth's resource allocation, enhance disaster response strategies, and drive efficiencies in transportation and energy systems.
我们相信 AI 有潜力革命性地释放人类潜能——从先进制造、基础设施开发，到科学研究和医学——为个人、组织和政府提供切实可见的现实收益。例如，AI 系统可以加快研究人员的科学发现，帮助医疗专业人员进行精准的医学分析和诊断，并赋能教育者为学生量身打造学习体验。此外，这些技术还能优化地球资源配置、提升灾难响应策略，并提高交通和能源系统的效率。
We believe that our current space efforts will catalyze transformative breakthroughs that could reshape terrestrial industries and lead to the emergence of new trillion-dollar markets on the Moon, Mars, and beyond. In particular, we believe our goal of establishing a lunar presence will enable terawatt-scale annual AI compute growth, support deeper space exploration and industrialization, and serve as a stepping stone to establishing a civilization on Mars.
我们相信，当前的太空努力将催化变革性突破，重塑地面产业，并在月球、火星及更远处催生新的万亿美元市场。特别是我们认为，建立月球存在的目标将支持每年太瓦级的 AI 算力增长、支持更深远的太空探索和工业化，并作为在火星建立文明的踏脚石。
Due to technological advancements that we are working towards, such as in-space propellant transfer, we believe our Starship vehicle will be capable of landing massive amounts of cargo on the Moon. Once there, we believe it will be possible to establish a permanent presence for scientific and manufacturing pursuits. For example, we believe that factories on the Moon will be able to take advantage of lunar resources to manufacture millions of AI compute satellites and deploy them farther into space. Our goal is to establish a sustainable lunar presence for scientific exploration, industrialization, and as a stepping stone to Mars, serving as a proving ground for habitats, resource utilization, and Starship systems essential for long-term human survival beyond Earth.
由于我们正在推进的技术进步（例如在轨推进剂转移），我们相信 Starship 载具将能向月球投送大量货物。一旦到达月球，我们相信有可能建立长期存在以开展科学研究和制造。例如，我们相信月球工厂将能够利用月球资源制造数百万颗 AI 算力卫星并将其向太空更深处部署。我们的目标是为科学探索和工业化建立可持续的月球存在，并作为通往火星的踏脚石——作为栖息地、资源利用和 Starship 系统的验证场，这些都对人类在地球之外的长期生存至关重要。
We believe the next paradigm shift for humanity is the creation of a resilient, perpetually expanding spacefaring civilization that drives continuous innovation across new frontiers, ultimately propelling us to Kardashev Type II status--a civilization that harnesses the full energy output of our Sun. In the near term, we expect space-enabled technologies to enhance life on Earth through greater global connectivity and breakthroughs forged in the harsh environments of our solar system, leading to accelerating progress in energy and AI. As we build infrastructure in the Earth's orbit, and potentially on the Moon, Mars and beyond, we believe we are capable of unlocking an era of unprecedented economic expansion, while also contributing to the safeguards of humanity's future against existential risk.
我们相信人类的下一个范式转变是创建一个有韧性、永续扩张的太空文明，在新边疆持续推动创新，最终把我们推向卡尔达肖夫 II 型（Kardashev Type II）状态——即一个能够利用我们恒星全部能量输出的文明。短期内，我们预计太空技术将通过更强的全球连接以及在太阳系恶劣环境中锻造的突破来改善地球生活，带动能源和 AI 进展的加速。当我们在地球轨道，以及可能的月球、火星及更远处建设基础设施时，我们相信我们能够开启一个前所未有的经济扩张时代，同时也为人类未来抵御存在性风险贡献保障。
Who We Are Our mission is to build the systems and technologies necessary to make life multiplanetary, to understand the true nature of the universe, and to extend the light of consciousness to the stars. To do this, we've formed the most ambitious, vertically integrated innovation engine on (and off) Earth. We are combining the most transformative and critical technologies in human history, including reusable rockets, a fully global internet service, satellite-to-mobile communications that enable connectivity everywhere, our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform, and a truth-seeking AI system designed to accelerate scientific discovery and augment human capabilities.
我们是谁 我们的使命是构建使生命跨行星化、理解宇宙真正本质并将意识之光延伸至群星所必需的系统和技术。为此，我们打造了地球上（以及地球之外）最具雄心的、垂直整合的创新引擎。我们正在结合人类历史上最具变革性和最关键的技术，包括可重复使用火箭、覆盖全球的互联网服务、让连接无处不在的卫星直连移动通信、我们的实时信息、娱乐和言论自由平台，以及一套旨在加速科学发现和增强人类能力、追求真理的 AI 系统。
These capabilities form a self-reinforcing ecosystem: launch systems deploy and maintain the satellite network, which delivers ubiquitous connectivity and vast data flows; the platform surfaces real-time information and supports open discourse; and AI processes data at scale to drive breakthroughs in physics, materials science, and space exploration. Together, they create a foundation for the development of the infrastructure of the future and the ultimate goal of establishing a self-sustaining human presence on other planets.
这些能力构成自我强化的生态系统：发射系统部署并维护卫星网络，卫星网络提供无处不在的连接和海量数据流；平台呈现实时信息并支持开放讨论；AI 大规模处理数据，推动物理、材料科学和太空探索的突破。它们共同构成构建未来基础设施的基础，最终目标是在其他行星上建立可自我维持的人类存在。
SpaceX designs, manufactures, launches, and operates the world's most advanced rockets and spacecraft. We safely and reliably transport astronauts, satellites, and other payloads on missions that benefit life on Earth. Since 2023, we have launched more than 80% of mass to orbit for the world each year with an over 99% mission success rate. We believe our unparalleled launch capabilities represent the foundational competitive advantage that enables all other parts of our business. We operate a high-speed, low-latency broadband data and communications network powered by approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, delivering connectivity to millions of consumer, enterprise, and government customers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets, as of March 31, 2026. We also built one of the world's most advanced models in under two years and are rapidly scaling the associated AI compute infrastructure--starting on Earth with the goal of extending to space--at industry- leading pace and cost efficiency. We believe that space represents the largest economic frontier in human history and that AI is a transformative force for understanding the universe. Together, we believe that space and AI will enable an age of abundance that will lead to an unprecedented expansion in the global economy. We are the only company that has the foundational infrastructure across hardware and software necessary to drive transformative innovation across space, connectivity, and AI. Our technological advancements are redefining industries on Earth, while aiming to create new ones on the Moon, Mars, and beyond.
SpaceX 设计、制造、发射并运营世界上最先进的火箭和航天器。我们安全、可靠地运送宇航员、卫星和其他有效载荷，执行造福地球生命的任务。自 2023 年以来，我们每年向轨道运送的质量超过全球的 80%，任务成功率超过 99%。我们相信无与伦比的发射能力是我们业务所有其他部分的基础竞争优势。我们运营着一张由约 9,600 颗近地轨道 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星驱动的高速、低延迟宽带数据与通信网络，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，在 164 个国家、地区及其他市场为数百万消费者、企业和政府客户提供连接。我们也在两年不到的时间内构建了世界上最先进的模型之一，并以行业领先的速度和成本效率快速扩展相关 AI 算力基础设施——从地球开始，目标是延伸到太空。我们相信太空是人类历史上最大的经济前沿，AI 是理解宇宙的变革性力量。我们相信太空和 AI 的结合将开启一个富足时代，推动全球经济前所未有的扩张。我们是唯一拥有跨太空、连接和 AI 推动变革性创新所需的基础软硬件设施的公司。我们的技术进步正在重新定义地球上的产业，同时致力于在月球、火星及更远处创造新的产业。
Our Unparalleled Launch Capabilities Since our founding in 2002, SpaceX has cracked the code on accessing space at scale, transforming an industry characterized by decades of stagnation, risk aversion, and economically perverse cost structures. We design, manufacture, launch, and refurbish reusable launch vehicles that provide cost-efficient, reliable, and high-cadence access to space for our own purposes as well as for third-party commercial and government customers. In 2025, we launched from four primary launch pads in the United States and successfully recovered boosters across seven landing facilities including autonomous drone ships and catch towers based on the vehicle type and mission profile.
我们无与伦比的发射能力 自 2002 年成立以来，SpaceX 已破解了大规模进入太空的难题，改变了一个长期停滞、风险规避、成本结构在经济上扭曲的行业。我们设计、制造、发射并翻新可重复使用的发射载具，为自身和第三方商业、政府客户提供低成本、可靠且高频次的太空进入方式。2025 年，我们从美国境内四个主要发射台执行发射，并根据载具类型和任务剖面，在包括自主无人船和回收塔在内的七个着陆设施成功回收助推器。
Our extensive vertical integration and end-to-end control over the entire value chain, from design to launch to operations, allows us to achieve unprecedented speed and cost efficiency.
我们广泛的垂直整合以及对从设计到发射再到运营整个价值链的端到端把控，让我们能够实现前所未有的速度和成本效率。
As of March 31, 2026, SpaceX had launched a total mass to orbit of approximately 7,400 metric tons with an over 99% mission success rate across our Falcon rockets. We have completed approximately 650 orbital space launches, and over 540 of those launches were completed by a flight-proven Falcon rocket. In 2025 alone, SpaceX completed 170 missions across Falcon and Starship vehicles and 159 flight-proven booster launches with an over 99% success rate on attempted booster recoveries. We launched over 2,200 metric tons, representing over 80% of mass to orbit for the world in 2025. With the first successful launch of Falcon 1 in 2008, we became the first private company to successfully launch a liquid-fueled rocket to Earth's orbit. Just two years later, in 2010, the commercial debut of the Falcon 9 rocket revolutionized space access by delivering unprecedented cost efficiency. For example, according to NASA, the first version of Falcon 9 in 2010 reduced launch cost to approximately $2,700 per kilogram, which represented a reduction of approximately 85% compared to the historical average launch cost per kilogram of $18,500. The first version of Falcon Heavy in 2018 further reduced this cost to $1,400 per kilogram, a reduction of approximately 92% compared to the historical average. We have also reduced our internal cost of launch through a combination of engineering improvements, manufacturing efficiencies, and economies of scale--most notably, through our ability to drive more frequent reuse of rockets.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，SpaceX 通过 Falcon 系列火箭累计向轨道投送的总质量约为 7,400 公吨，任务成功率超过 99%。我们已完成约 650 次轨道级太空发射，其中超过 540 次由经过飞行验证的 Falcon 火箭执行。仅 2025 年一年，SpaceX 就用 Falcon 和 Starship 载具完成了 170 次任务，包含 159 次飞行验证助推器发射，已尝试的助推器回收成功率超过 99%。我们投送了超过 2,200 公吨，占 2025 年全球轨道投送质量的 80% 以上。2008 年 Falcon 1 首次成功发射，我们成为第一家将液体燃料火箭成功送入地球轨道的私营公司。仅两年后，2010 年 Falcon 9 火箭的商业首飞通过前所未有的成本效率革新了太空进入方式。例如，根据 NASA 数据，2010 年第一代 Falcon 9 将发射成本降至约每公斤 2,700 美元，相比每公斤 18,500 美元的历史平均水平降低了约 85%。2018 年第一代 Falcon Heavy 又将这一成本降至每公斤约 1,400 美元，相比历史平均降低了约 92%。我们还通过工程改进、制造效率和规模经济，特别是更频繁的火箭重复使用能力，进一步降低了内部发射成本。
In December 2015, we achieved what many deemed impossible: landing a rocket launched to space back on Earth.
2015 年 12 月，我们实现了许多人认为不可能的事：让发射进入太空的火箭着陆回地球。
By 2017, we were routinely recovering and reusing the Falcon 9 first-stage booster post-launch, delivering another step-function drop in space access costs via groundbreaking reusability. As of March 31, 2026, our Falcon 9 rockets have demonstrated the ability to refly a first-stage 34 times. Since 2020, our Dragon spacecraft has safely flown 78 crewmembers from 20 countries. With the future deployment of Starship, which is designed to be the world's first fully and rapidly reusable spacecraft, we aim to reduce the cost to reach orbit by 99% or more relative to the historical average launch cost, establishing the most affordable and scalable path to creating new opportunities in space, such as orbital AI compute and Mars exploration.
到 2017 年，我们已能在发射后常规回收并重复使用 Falcon 9 一级助推器，通过突破性的可重复使用性带来太空进入成本的又一次跃迁式下降。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们的 Falcon 9 火箭已展示出第一级最多复飞 34 次的能力。自 2020 年以来，我们的 Dragon 航天器已安全运送来自 20 个国家的 78 名机组成员。随着未来 Starship 的部署——其被设计为全球首款完全且快速可重复使用的航天器——我们的目标是相对历史平均发射成本把进入轨道的成本降低 99% 以上，从而建立起最经济、最具可扩展性的路径，在太空中创造诸如轨道 AI 算力和火星探索等新机会。
Booster Reusability Enables Increasing Launch Rates Our principal launch vehicles and spacecraft include:
助推器可回收性推动发射率上升 我们的主要发射载具和航天器包括：
•Falcon 9. As the world's first orbital-class rapidly reusable rocket, Falcon 9 was first launched in 2010 and has a payload capacity to LEO of approximately 23 metric tons when fully expendable. Falcon 9 has completed approximately 620 orbital space launches as of March 31, 2026, and an over 99% mission success rate, making it the most active orbital launch vehicle today. In 2025 alone, we launched 165 Falcon 9 rockets, of which 157 were flight-proven booster launches, and during the three months ended March 31, 2026, we launched 40 Falcon rockets, of which 39 were flight-proven booster launches.
• Falcon 9。作为全球首款轨道级快速可重复使用火箭，Falcon 9 首次发射于 2010 年，在完全一次性使用配置下对近地轨道的有效载荷能力约为 23 公吨。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，Falcon 9 已完成约 620 次轨道级太空发射，任务成功率超过 99%，使其成为当今最活跃的轨道发射载具。仅 2025 年，我们就发射了 165 枚 Falcon 9 火箭，其中 157 次为飞行验证助推器发射；在截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日止三个月内，我们发射了 40 枚 Falcon 火箭，其中 39 次为飞行验证助推器发射。
•Falcon Heavy. Falcon Heavy first launched in 2018 when it put a Tesla Roadster and its mannequin passenger, known as Starman, into orbit around the Sun. With a payload capacity to LEO of approximately 64 metric tons, Falcon Heavy is a partially reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle designed to deliver large payloads to orbit.
• Falcon Heavy。Falcon Heavy 于 2018 年首飞，那次发射将一辆 Tesla Roadster 和被称为 Starman 的人偶乘客送入绕太阳的轨道。Falcon Heavy 对近地轨道的有效载荷能力约为 64 公吨，是一款部分可重复使用的超重型运载火箭，设计用于将大型有效载荷投送入轨。
Falcon Heavy is one of the most powerful operational rockets in the world measured by liftoff thrust, with 11 launches as of March 31, 2026 and a 100% mission success rate.
以起飞推力衡量，Falcon Heavy 是世界上最强大的在役火箭之一，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日已完成 11 次发射，任务成功率为 100%。
•Dragon. Launched by Falcon 9 in 2012, our Dragon spacecraft became the first commercial spacecraft to deliver cargo to and from the International Space Station and, eight years later, the first privately built vehicle to fly humans to the orbiting laboratory. Since its first flight, Dragon has visited the International Space Station over 50 times, and restored America's ability to launch astronauts. Dragon has also supported all of NASA's private astronaut missions to the International Space Station, flown the first all-commercial astronaut crew, completed the first human spaceflight over the Earth's polar regions, and supported the first-ever commercial spacewalk.
• Dragon。Dragon 航天器于 2012 年由 Falcon 9 发射，成为首款向国际空间站运送货物并返回的商业航天器，并在 8 年后成为首款将人类送往这一轨道实验室的私营建造载具。自首次飞行以来，Dragon 已造访国际空间站超过 50 次，并恢复了美国发射宇航员的能力。Dragon 还支持了 NASA 所有的国际空间站私营宇航员任务，搭载了首支全商业宇航员机组，完成了首次跨越地球极地的载人航天飞行，并支持了有史以来首次商业太空行走。
•Starship. First launched in 2023, Starship is designed to be a fully reusable, super heavy-lift launch vehicle.
• Starship。Starship 于 2023 年首飞，被设计为完全可重复使用的超重型运载火箭。
Starship V3 is designed to deliver 100 metric tons to Earth's orbit in a fully reusable configuration while enabling rapid turnaround times akin to commercial aviation. Future generations of Starship are being designed to double this payload capacity. To date, we have executed 11 Starship flight tests. We have also scheduled a 12th flight test, which will debut the next generation Starship vehicle and Super Heavy booster, powered by the next evolution of our Raptor engine and launching from a newly designed pad at Starbase. We expect Starship to commence payload delivery to orbit in the second half of 2026. We have achieved innovative milestones such as catching a booster using "chopstick" arms on the same tower it launched from. We expect this capability will facilitate rapid refurbishment and reuse, allowing for multiple launches per day at reduced costs.
Starship V3 被设计为在完全可重复使用配置下向地球轨道投送 100 公吨，并实现类似商业航空的快速周转时间。未来几代 Starship 正在被设计成把这一有效载荷能力翻倍。截至目前，我们已执行 11 次 Starship 飞行测试。我们还安排了第 12 次飞行测试，将首次启用下一代 Starship 载具和 Super Heavy 助推器，由下一代 Raptor 引擎驱动，从 Starbase 全新设计的发射台发射。我们预计 Starship 将在 2026 年下半年开始向轨道投送有效载荷。我们已经实现了创新的里程碑，例如用「筷子」机械臂在发射所用的同一座塔上抓回助推器。我们预计这一能力将促成快速翻新和重复使用，让每天能进行多次低成本发射。
Upon achieving rocket reusability, we recognized the immense potential of our launch business to enable new revenue streams, as our launch capacity would eventually outstrip demand from traditional space customers alone.
在实现火箭可重复使用之后，我们意识到发射业务有巨大潜力来开辟新的收入来源，因为我们的发射运力终将超过仅传统太空客户的需求。
This realization, along with our efforts to make life multiplanetary, drove us to reimagine what was possible when access to space became more affordable. Rather than asking what was being done in space, we asked what large- scale global need could be better served from space. This led to the development of Starlink, our global satellite internet constellation, consisting of thousands of LEO satellites designed to provide high-speed, low-latency broadband connectivity to underserved areas worldwide. Although the concept of using satellites for global internet connectivity dates back decades, technical challenges and the prohibitive cost of accessing space historically rendered attempts to provide such connectivity economically unviable. Within three years of our first satellite launch in 2019, we solved the technical and production challenges of the satellites, and within five years, we had deployed the largest LEO constellation in existence. Today, Starlink is the sole low-latency network available globally.
这一认识，加上我们让生命跨行星化的努力，驱动我们重新构想——当进入太空变得更经济时——什么才是可能的。我们没有问太空里在做什么，而是问什么样的大规模全球需求可以由太空更好地服务。这导致了 Starlink 的诞生——我们的全球卫星互联网星座，由数千颗近地轨道卫星组成，旨在为全球服务不足地区提供高速低延迟宽带连接。尽管利用卫星提供全球互联网连接的概念可以追溯几十年，技术挑战和高昂的太空进入成本历史上使得提供此类连接在经济上不可行。在 2019 年首次卫星发射后的三年内，我们解决了卫星的技术与生产挑战，五年内部署了现存最大的近地轨道星座。如今 Starlink 是全球唯一可用的低延迟网络。
As the leader in space access, our launch operations are an important and expanding competitive advantage. By combining increasing launch cadence, expanding cargo capacity, and declining unit costs--driven by rapid reusability--we have generated a compounding competitive advantage. This not only fortifies our core business, but also provides vast new market opportunities uniquely enabled by space.
作为太空进入领域的领跑者，我们的发射运营是重要且不断扩大的竞争优势。通过结合不断上升的发射频率、不断扩大的载荷能力以及在快速可重复使用驱动下不断下降的单位成本，我们已经形成了复利式的竞争优势。这不仅巩固了核心业务，也提供了仅由太空独家催生的广阔新市场机会。
Our Leading Capabilities Across Space, Connectivity, and AI Space. While our launch capabilities support our other businesses, such as Starlink Consumer Broadband and Starlink Mobile, we also sell launches to third-party customers. We offer launch services to commercial, civil, and government customers through our reusable Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy rockets for satellite, cargo, and crew missions.
