We fly to LEO, MEO, GEO, lunar, and interplanetary trajectories, as well as the International Space Station. We are the primary launch provider for the U.S. government. In 2025, we launched 11 of 12 National Security Space Launch ("NSSL") medium and heavy lift missions and all five U.S. crew and cargo missions to the International Space Station for NASA. We serve commercial and government customers--including NASA, the National Reconnaissance Office ("NRO"), Axiom Space, SES, Eutelsat, and Oneweb. We charge our customers based on the type of rocket, mass to orbit, size of payload, and type of service, such as whether the launch is dedicated to a single customer or part of a "rideshare" with other customers.
我们在太空、连接和 AI 领域的领先能力 太空。虽然我们的发射能力支持其他业务（如 Starlink Consumer Broadband 和 Starlink Mobile），但我们也向第三方客户出售发射服务。我们通过可重复使用的 Falcon 9 和 Falcon Heavy 火箭，为商业、民用和政府客户提供卫星、货运和载人任务的发射服务。我们飞往近地轨道 (LEO)、中地球轨道 (MEO, Medium Earth Orbit)、地球同步轨道 (GEO)、月球及行星际轨迹，以及国际空间站。我们是美国政府的主要发射服务提供方。2025 年，我们执行了 12 次国家安全太空发射（National Security Space Launch，简称「NSSL」）中型和重型任务中的 11 次，并执行了所有 5 次为 NASA 飞往国际空间站的美国载人和货运任务。我们服务于包括 NASA、国家侦察局 (NRO)、Axiom Space、SES、Eutelsat 和 Oneweb 在内的商业和政府客户。我们按火箭类型、入轨质量、有效载荷大小及服务类型（例如该发射是单一客户专属还是与其他客户共享的「rideshare」拼车）向客户收费。
Starship is our next-generation reusable rocket vehicle that we expect will expand our launch capability dramatically through full and rapid reusability combined with currently unprecedented mass to orbit capability. As the most powerful launch system ever developed, we expect that Starship V3 will be able to carry a payload of 100 metric tons, and that future generations could reach 200 metric tons, potentially as soon as Starship V4. Starship is designed to deliver our next-generation satellites to orbit, long-haul point-to-point transportation on Earth, the cargo and crew necessary to develop a base on the Moon and a city on Mars for research and human spaceflight development.
Starship 是我们的下一代可重复使用火箭载具，我们预计它将通过完全且快速可重复使用结合当前史无前例的入轨质量能力，大幅扩展我们的发射能力。作为有史以来最强大的发射系统，我们预计 Starship V3 将能携带 100 公吨的有效载荷，而未来几代——可能最早在 Starship V4 上——可达到 200 公吨。Starship 被设计用于把我们下一代卫星投送入轨、进行地球上长程点对点运输，以及搭载在月球建立基地和在火星建造城市以进行研究和载人航天发展所需的货物和机组。
Connectivity. Starlink provides global access to high-speed internet, including underserved rural and remote communities worldwide. As of March 31, 2026, we had approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, providing broadband connectivity to approximately 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers across 164 countries, territories, and other markets. We also provide satellite-to-mobile texting and over-the-top voice services to approximately 7.4 million monthly unique devices across approximately 30 countries.
连接。Starlink 提供全球高速互联网接入，包括全球服务不足的乡村和偏远社区。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们在近地轨道拥有约 9,600 颗 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星，为 164 个国家、地区及其他市场的约 1,030 万 Starlink 订阅用户提供宽带连接。我们还在约 30 个国家为约 740 万个月度独立设备提供卫星直连移动短信和 Over-the-Top 语音服务。
•Starlink Consumer Broadband. We operate the world's largest and most advanced space-based internet broadband service with median latency at approximately 25 milliseconds as of March 31, 2026. We provide fiber-like download speeds--at a median of 225 Mbps during peak hours for residential users as of March 31, 2026--and the technological capability to provide service everywhere on Earth, including the poles. This service quality is enabled by our vast network of approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit, which accounted for approximately 75% of all active maneuverable satellites in orbit as of March 31, 2026. We expect to commence deploying our next-generation V3 satellites, designed to offer one Tbps of downlink capacity per satellite, using Starship in the second half of 2026. We expect that a single Starship launch will be capable of deploying up to 60 V3 satellites to LEO, representing a potential twenty-fold increase in Starlink downlink capacity deployed relative to a Falcon 9 launch. As of March 31, 2026, we had approximately 10.3 million Starlink Subscribers, up approximately 105% from 5.0 million subscribers a year prior. We charge our Starlink Subscribers a monthly subscription fee, which varies based on geographic market and download speed, plus typically a one-time upfront terminal cost.
• Starlink Consumer Broadband。我们运营着全球最大、最先进的天基互联网宽带服务，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日中位时延约 25 毫秒。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们为住宅用户在高峰时段提供光纤级的下载速度，中位约为 225 Mbps，并具备在地球任何地方（包括两极）提供服务的技术能力。这种服务质量得益于我们由约 9,600 颗近地轨道 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星组成的庞大网络，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，这些卫星约占在轨所有可机动卫星的 75%。我们预计将于 2026 年下半年开始用 Starship 部署下一代 V3 卫星，每颗卫星设计提供 1 Tbps 的下行容量。我们预计一次 Starship 发射将能向近地轨道部署多达 60 颗 V3 卫星，相比一次 Falcon 9 发射，Starlink 部署的下行容量可能增长二十倍。截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们拥有约 1,030 万 Starlink 订阅用户，相比一年前的 500 万订阅用户增长了约 105%。我们向 Starlink 订阅用户按月收取订阅费——根据所在地理市场和下载速度变化——通常另外加上一次性的终端预付费用。
•Enterprise Solutions. SpaceX is a critical partner to a wide array of enterprises. We offer Starlink's high- speed, low-latency, reliable internet services to enterprise customers across industries including construction, agriculture, retail, telecom, hospitality, aviation, maritime, and land mobility. Starlink's unique capabilities are well‑suited for deployments across field offices, remote worksites, research stations, drilling rigs, rural hospitals, aircraft, cruise ships, trains, and hotels. Our enterprise customers include companies such as United Airlines, Carnival, Maersk, and John Deere, among others. We also serve a broad fixed‑site customer base across industries such as retail and financial services that require high availability for critical operations as well as reliable connectivity in remote or hard-to-serve locations. As companies continue to invest in secure and resilient networks and backup systems to keep critical infrastructure online--such as point‑of‑sale and payment processing systems--we often start as a backup solution and then transition to being the primary solution. Our enterprise contracts are based on a combination of subscriptions, data consumption, capacity, or other pricing models depending on each customer's particular needs. Since 2023, no Starlink Enterprise customer having contributed more than $750,000 of annual revenue has voluntarily discontinued their service, demonstrating the strong performance and value of our offering. This is despite the ability of our customers to cancel the service at any time.
• 企业解决方案 (Enterprise Solutions)。SpaceX 是众多企业的关键合作伙伴。我们向涉及建筑、农业、零售、电信、酒店、航空、海运和陆地移动等行业的企业客户提供 Starlink 的高速、低延迟、可靠互联网服务。Starlink 的独特能力非常适合在野外办公室、远程作业现场、研究站、钻井平台、乡村医院、飞机、邮轮、火车和酒店部署。我们的企业客户包括 United Airlines、Carnival、Maersk 和 John Deere 等公司。我们还服务广泛的固定站点客户，覆盖零售和金融服务等行业——这些行业需要为关键业务保持高可用性，并在偏远或难以服务的地点拥有可靠连接。随着企业持续投资于安全且有弹性的网络和备份系统，以让 POS 和支付处理等关键基础设施保持在线，我们经常先作为备份方案，再过渡为首要方案。我们的企业合同基于订阅、数据消耗、容量或其他定价模式的组合，视每位客户具体需求而定。自 2023 年以来，年贡献收入超过 75 万美元的 Starlink Enterprise 客户没有一家主动停止服务，体现了我们方案的强劲表现和价值。即便如此，我们的客户随时可以取消该服务。
•Government Solutions. For our government customers, we provide high-speed, resilient connectivity for public services, social impact, humanitarian efforts, and disaster response in even the most remote and challenging environments. Examples include support for the FEMA in coordinating disaster recovery after hurricanes and wildfires, the NOAA for at-sea testing and environmental monitoring, the Government of the Philippines for linking remote islands, schools, and public institutions, the Government of Jamaica for improving digital access in remote and maritime areas, and the Government of Ecuador for supporting education and healthcare connectivity in isolated communities. Separately with Starshield, we have leveraged our commercial LEO satellite constellation engineering learnings and operational experiences to develop a secure, dedicated satellite network designed specifically for United States Government customers and national security applications.
• 政府解决方案 (Government Solutions)。对于政府客户，我们在最偏远和最具挑战的环境中，为公共服务、社会影响、人道主义工作和灾难响应提供高速、有韧性的连接。例子包括：支持 FEMA 协调飓风和野火后的灾后恢复，支持 NOAA 进行海上测试和环境监测，支持菲律宾政府连接偏远岛屿、学校和公共机构，支持牙买加政府改善偏远和海上地区的数字接入，以及支持厄瓜多尔政府为偏远社区的教育和医疗连接提供保障。另外通过 Starshield，我们将商业近地轨道卫星星座的工程经验和运营经验，开发为专为美国政府客户和国家安全应用打造的安全专用卫星网络。
•Starlink Mobile. We provide satellite-to-mobile connectivity, supplementing terrestrial networks and substantially reducing mobile "dead zones" across approximately 30 countries. We partner with MNOs including major wireless carriers like T-Mobile in the United States, and other international operators including One NZ, Optus, Telstra, Rogers, KDDI, Salt, Entel, Kyivstar, and VMO2. Through these partnerships, we enable consumers, businesses, and public-sector customers to use their existing phones in more places, support critical connectivity during disasters and power outages, and open new applications for low-bandwidth mobile and IoT devices. Our current capabilities under our "V1" constellation (consisting of approximately 650 V1 Mobile satellites in orbit) include light data, text messaging (SMS), and over-the-top voice services (e.g., WhatsApp and FaceTime). We are developing more comprehensive satellite-to-mobile services, including broadband data and IoT connectivity, which are expected to deliver resilient, infrastructure-independent connectivity worldwide and enable 5G connectivity. We have partnerships with approximately 30 MNOs on six continents, covering an area that is home to approximately 1.9 billion people. We charge MNOs either a fixed fee or a per-mobile user fee-based amount, which is typically passed through to the customer via the carrier as an "add-on" feature.
• Starlink Mobile。我们提供卫星直连移动连接，补充地面网络，并在大约 30 个国家大幅减少移动「盲区」。我们与运营商合作，包括美国的 T-Mobile 等主要无线运营商，以及其他国际运营商如 One NZ、Optus、Telstra、Rogers、KDDI、Salt、Entel、Kyivstar 和 VMO2。通过这些合作，我们让消费者、企业和公共部门客户能在更多地方使用现有手机、在灾难和断电期间保持关键连接，并为低带宽移动和物联网设备开拓新的应用。我们目前 V1 星座（在轨约 650 颗 V1 Mobile 卫星）支持的能力包括轻量数据、短信 (SMS) 和 Over-the-Top 语音服务（如 WhatsApp 和 FaceTime）。我们正在开发更全面的卫星直连移动服务，包括宽带数据和物联网连接，预计将在全球范围内提供有韧性、独立于地面基础设施的连接，并实现 5G 连接。我们在六大洲与大约 30 家移动网络运营商建立了合作，覆盖区域居住人口约 19 亿。我们向运营商收取固定费用或按移动用户计费的费用，通常由运营商以「附加」功能形式转嫁给最终客户。
Our Global Starlink Subscriber Base AI. We operate a highly vertically integrated AI platform spanning gigawatt-scale AI compute infrastructure, our truth-seeking frontier AI model, Grok, AI solutions for consumer and enterprise customers, and X, our real-time information, entertainment, and free speech platform. We believe AI is rapidly converging toward AGI, where human cognitive capabilities can be replicated and scaled at machine speeds, profoundly augmenting human productivity. Once an AGI system exists, its true value derives from the ability to create limitless duplicates of human-like intelligence, necessitating vast computational resources and cost-efficient deployment to achieve meaningful scale. Without large-scale, power-efficient infrastructure, AGI cannot be deployed broadly or economically--making such infrastructure a critical strategic differentiator.
我们的全球 Starlink 订阅用户基础 AI。我们运营着一个高度垂直整合的 AI 平台，涵盖吉瓦级 AI 算力基础设施、追求真理的前沿 AI 模型 Grok、面向消费者和企业客户的 AI 解决方案，以及实时信息、娱乐和言论自由平台 X。我们相信 AI 正快速朝 AGI 收敛——届时人类认知能力将能被以机器速度复制和扩展，深刻地放大人类生产力。一旦 AGI 系统存在，其真正价值来自于无限复制类人智能的能力，这需要海量算力资源和成本高效的部署才能达到有意义的规模。如果没有大规模、能效高的基础设施，AGI 就无法被广泛或经济地部署——这使得此类基础设施成为关键的战略差异点。
•AI Compute Infrastructure. xAI has established a leading position in building and scaling terrestrial AI compute infrastructure, becoming the first company to deploy a coherent gigawatt-scale AI training cluster. Our AI compute facilities, COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II, collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. Our first-principles thinking enables us to build coherent compute at scale and at rapid speed with lower costs than most other companies in the industry. We brought the first cluster of COLOSSUS online in 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory, and the first cluster of COLOSSUS II online even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis. This dual speed and cost advantage stems from our complete vertical integration and the shared culture infused by our founder, Mr. Musk, across our Space, Connectivity, and AI segments. The addition of Terafab, an initiative together with Tesla to build a manufacturing facility capable of producing 1 terawatt per year of compute hardware, aims to further extend our vertical integration to chip design and manufacturing to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, optimize compute performance, and potentially reduce overall compute costs.
• AI 算力基础设施。xAI 在构建和扩展地面 AI 算力基础设施方面已确立领先地位，是首家部署完整 gigawatt 级 AI 训练集群的公司。我们的 AI 算力设施 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦算力，并有额外的电力容量可用于数据中心运营。我们的第一性原理思维让我们能以低于行业大多数公司的成本快速地大规模构建协同算力。我们用 122 天就让 COLOSSUS 的首个集群上线，并改造了一座现有工厂的外壳，COLOSSUS II 首个集群上线则更快，仅 91 天。作为参照，行业基准下让一座 100 兆瓦的全新数据中心上线大约需要两年。我们在成本效率上也展现了显著进步，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本按每兆瓦计算大幅低于行业基准。这种速度与成本的双重优势来自我们彻底的垂直整合，以及创始人 Musk 先生在太空、连接和 AI 三大板块注入的共同文化。Terafab——我们与 Tesla 联合发起、计划每年生产 1 太瓦算力硬件的制造项目——的加入旨在把我们的垂直整合进一步延伸到芯片设计和制造，以缓解 SpaceX 未来可能的芯片短缺、优化算力性能并可能降低整体算力成本。
Intel, which has the ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale, has also joined the Terafab project. We believe that the key constraints in the continued growth of AI are physical--chip manufacturing, data center infrastructure, and power generation; the future of AI will be determined by the control of the physical stack.
Intel 拥有大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片的能力，也已加入 Terafab 项目。我们相信 AI 持续增长的关键约束是物理性的——芯片制造、数据中心基础设施和发电；AI 的未来将由对物理栈的控制力决定。
•Truth-Seeking Frontier Model. xAI has developed one of the world's most advanced, truth-seeking frontier models with Grok. Since launching Grok-1 in November 2023, we have released four major versions and notable variations thereof, achieving one of the fastest iteration cycles in the industry, culminating in Grok-4.3 (April 2026). Building on this trajectory, we expect to continue scaling Grok through subsequent generations.
• 追求真理的前沿模型。xAI 通过 Grok 开发了全球最先进的、追求真理的前沿模型之一。自 2023 年 11 月推出 Grok-1 以来，我们发布了四个主要版本及其重要变体，迭代周期是业内最快的之一，最终在 2026 年 4 月达到 Grok-4.3。基于这一轨迹，我们预计将通过后续几代继续扩展 Grok。
Ongoing training of next‑generation models is expected to scale toward multiple trillions of parameters, which could represent a step change in reasoning in depth and overall intelligence. In this context, the number of parameters refers to the scale of the model, where parameters are the internal numerical values, such as "weights," that are adjusted during training to enable the model to recognize patterns and relationships in data.
下一代模型的持续训练预计将朝着数万亿参数规模扩展，这可能代表推理深度和整体智能上的跃迁。在此语境下，参数数量指模型的规模——参数是训练期间被调整的内部数值（例如「权重」），使模型能够识别数据中的模式和关系。
A larger number of parameters generally allows the model to capture more complex relationships, store greater amounts of knowledge, and achieve higher levels of reasoning capability. Within two years of its initial model release, Grok achieved frontier-level performance in scientific reasoning, as measured by its GPQA Diamond score, an industry benchmark that evaluates AI models on a standardized set of questions written and validated by experts, on a faster timeline than reported by other leading model providers. This accelerated rate of innovation stems from our highly vertically integrated stack: full ownership of training infrastructure, access to the world's most powerful compute clusters, and relentless focus on truth seeking and real-world utility. A key competitive differentiator is Grok's deep integration with X, enabling proprietary access to a real-time information stream of approximately 350 million daily posts, which enhances freshness, relevance, and contextual awareness for Grok. This direct, real-time access to the information and human discourse on X enhances Grok's truth-seeking capabilities by grounding outputs in up-to-date knowledge and diverse viewpoints. We believe that this combination of compute infrastructure scale and the massive dataset available to us through X, subject to some limitations for certain content, has allowed us to achieve industry-leading performance and provide model outputs that analyze real-time information on global events. We expect that our compute infrastructure and direct access to real-time data via X constitute substantial performance advantages for Grok that will result in increasingly rapid and dramatic iteration cycles.
参数数量更大通常使模型能捕捉更复杂的关系、储存更多知识，并达到更高的推理能力水平。在首个模型发布后的两年内，Grok 在科学推理上达到了前沿水平——以 GPQA Diamond 分数衡量，该分数是行业基准，使用由专家撰写并验证的标准化问题集来评测 AI 模型——这一时间比其他主要模型提供方所披露的更快。这种加速的创新源自我们高度垂直整合的栈：完全拥有训练基础设施、可访问全球最强大的算力集群，并对追求真理和现实世界效用持续专注。一项关键竞争差异点是 Grok 与 X 的深度整合，使其能够独家访问每日约 3.5 亿条实时信息流，从而增强 Grok 的时效性、相关性和上下文感知能力。这种对 X 上信息和人类讨论的直接、实时访问，通过将输出建立在最新知识和多元观点之上，增强了 Grok 追求真理的能力。我们相信，这种算力基础设施规模与通过 X 可获取的海量数据集（在某些内容上有一定限制）的结合，让我们能实现行业领先的性能，并提供分析全球事件实时信息的模型输出。我们预计我们的算力基础设施和通过 X 直接访问实时数据的能力，构成了 Grok 的重大性能优势，将带来越来越快速、越来越显著的迭代周期。
•Consumer and Enterprise Applications. We leverage our leading frontier models and compute infrastructure to deliver consumer and enterprise applications. In under six months, we developed Grok Voice, a real-time speech engine, including in multilingual performance. Our image and video generation system, Imagine, produced approximately 10 billion images and over 2 billion videos per month, on average, for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. Together with Tesla, we are also developing Macrohard, an agentic AI platform designed to be capable of fully emulating digital workflows and augmenting human operation of computers-- from coding and product development to management and entire business processes--using sophisticated autonomous agents. We believe Macrohard will have the potential to fundamentally transform how companies are structured and operate, thereby allowing dramatic increases in human productivity. In addition, we believe our existing government relationships and track record as large government contractors are a structural advantage as governments become significant consumers of AI applications.
• 消费者与企业应用。我们利用领先的前沿模型和算力基础设施提供消费者和企业应用。在不到六个月时间内，我们开发了 Grok Voice——一个具备多语言性能的实时语音引擎。我们的图像与视频生成系统 Imagine，在截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的季度内，平均每月生成约 100 亿张图像和超过 20 亿条视频。我们还与 Tesla 一起开发 Macrohard——一个 agentic AI 平台，旨在能够完全模拟数字工作流并增强人类对计算机的操作，从编程和产品开发到管理乃至整个业务流程，使用复杂的自主 agent。我们相信 Macrohard 有潜力从根本上改变公司组织和运作的方式，从而显著提升人类生产力。此外，我们相信我们既有的政府关系和作为大型政府承包商的过往业绩，是结构性优势——尤其当政府越来越多地成为 AI 应用的重要消费者时。
Our integrated AI platforms across Grok and X have over 1.3 billion supported accounts active in the last twelve months ended March 31, 2026, including approximately 550 million MAUs, up from over 1.1 billion supported accounts and approximately 520 million MAUs as of December 31, 2025. Of our MAUs, we had approximately 117 million MAUs that used Grok's AI features as of March 31, 2026.
截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日的过去十二个月内，我们整合的 Grok 与 X AI 平台拥有超过 13 亿活跃支持账户，其中包括约 5.5 亿 MAU，相比 2025 年 12 月 31 日的超过 11 亿支持账户和约 5.2 亿 MAU 有所增长。在我们的 MAU 中，截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日约有 1.17 亿 MAU 使用了 Grok 的 AI 功能。
We also monetize user activity through high-impact advertising inventory on X. We believe X's scale, real-time engagement, and integration with Grok provide a differentiated foundation for building a unified user experience across communication, content discovery, commerce, and financial services, among others. For enterprises that advertise on X, we offer large-scale user engagement, real-time content, and advanced AI- driven performance marketing tools. For enterprises, we offer tailored deployments of Grok customized to specific workflows and security needs through Grok Business and Grok Enterprise, sold on license-, consumption-, or outcome-based pricing models.
我们还通过 X 上高影响力的广告库存对用户活动进行变现。我们相信 X 的规模、实时互动以及与 Grok 的整合提供了差异化的基础，可以构建跨通信、内容发现、商务和金融服务等的统一用户体验。对于在 X 投放广告的企业，我们提供大规模用户互动、实时内容和先进的 AI 驱动效果营销工具。对于企业客户，我们通过 Grok Business 和 Grok Enterprise 提供针对特定工作流和安全需求定制的 Grok 部署，并按授权、消费或基于成果的定价模型销售。
Collaboration with Tesla SpaceX and Tesla developed the early foundation of a strong and constructive partnership through a series of limited but successful commercial engagements. Our relationship with Tesla evolved meaningfully following Tesla's January 2026 commitment to invest in xAI--an investment that, upon SpaceX's acquisition of xAI, was converted into an equity interest in SpaceX. Tesla and xAI continue to build upon their longstanding collaborative relationship by evaluating future strategic opportunities between the companies.
与 Tesla 的合作 SpaceX 和 Tesla 通过一系列有限但成功的商业合作奠定了强健、建设性合作的早期基础。在 2026 年 1 月 Tesla 承诺投资 xAI 之后，我们与 Tesla 的关系发生了实质性演进——这笔投资在 SpaceX 收购 xAI 后转化为对 SpaceX 的股权权益。Tesla 与 xAI 继续在长期合作关系基础上推进，评估两家公司之间的未来战略机会。
One expected area of collaboration is an AI project called Macrohard. We expect Macrohard to benefit from running on both state-of-the-art processors and cost efficient, next-generation Tesla processors, a critical advantage of our vertical integration.
预期的合作领域之一是名为 Macrohard 的 AI 项目。我们预计 Macrohard 将受益于在最先进处理器和成本高效的下一代 Tesla 处理器上运行——这是我们垂直整合的关键优势。
Another expected area of collaboration is Terafab, an announced AI chip manufacturing initiative designed to vertically integrate the design, fabrication, and deployment of advanced logic and memory chips. We believe this initiative will alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX and optimize compute performance. We expect Terafab to be the world's largest chip manufacturing facility. Our strategy for Terafab is to vertically integrate across the design of lithography masks, fabrication of logic and memory chips, and design of advanced packaging in a single closed-loop plant. Conducting all these activities end-to-end in a single facility enables rapid testing and iterations, allowing us to improve chip design and scale manufacturing faster. We expect that our speed and cost advantage from vertical integration will allow us to scale efficiently in AI chip manufacturing towards our long-term goal of producing one terawatt of compute each year. We are partnering to build Terafab in order to support growth in two kinds of chips-- one type optimized for terrestrial edge and inference to be used primarily in Tesla's Optimus robots and vehicles, and another type optimized for the space environment to be used in our orbital compute infrastructure. While Terafab is intended to expand our internal chip manufacturing capabilities, we expect to continue sourcing a significant portion of our compute hardware from third-party suppliers. We view Terafab as complementary to these relationships, enabling us to augment our access to compute hardware at massive scale and further complete our highly vertically integrated compute platform by extending our control to the foundational chip layer. We believe that the key constraints in the continued growth of AI are physical--chip manufacturing, data center infrastructure, and power generation; the future of AI will be determined by the control of the physical stack.
另一个预期合作领域是 Terafab——一个已宣布的 AI 芯片制造项目，旨在垂直整合先进逻辑芯片和存储芯片的设计、制造与部署。我们相信这一项目将缓解 SpaceX 未来可能的芯片短缺并优化算力性能。我们预计 Terafab 将成为全球最大的芯片制造设施。我们对 Terafab 的策略是在单一闭环工厂中垂直整合光刻掩膜设计、逻辑芯片与存储芯片制造，以及先进封装设计。把所有这些活动在同一座设施内端到端进行，可以快速测试和迭代，让我们能更快地改进芯片设计并扩大制造规模。我们预计垂直整合带来的速度与成本优势将让我们能在 AI 芯片制造上高效扩张，向每年生产一太瓦算力的长期目标前进。我们与合作伙伴一起建设 Terafab，目的是支持两类芯片的增长——一类针对地面边缘和推理优化，主要用于 Tesla 的 Optimus 机器人和车辆；另一类针对太空环境优化，用于我们的轨道算力基础设施。Terafab 旨在扩展我们内部的芯片制造能力，但我们仍计划从第三方供应商持续采购相当一部分算力硬件。我们认为 Terafab 与这些关系是互补的，让我们能在巨大规模上增强算力硬件的获取能力，并通过把控制力延伸至底层芯片层进一步完善高度垂直整合的算力平台。我们相信 AI 持续增长的关键约束是物理性的——芯片制造、数据中心基础设施和发电；AI 的未来将由对物理栈的控制力决定。
We believe that we are better positioned than other AI companies given our unique control over the full physical stack. We plan to explore other areas of strategic collaboration with Tesla in the future.
我们相信，凭借对完整物理栈的独特控制力，我们比其他 AI 公司更具优势。我们计划未来探索与 Tesla 的其他战略合作领域。
Collaboration with Cursor On April 19, 2026, we entered into a compute agreement with Cursor. Cursor develops and operates an AI-native integrated development environment that enables professional software developers and engineering teams to write, edit, review, and refactor code using LLM-powered agents and workflows integrated via its proprietary model harness. In 2025, Cursor launched Composer, its own LLM trained for software development. It recently released Composer 2, which offers improvements in coding performance at lower cost. We believe the compute agreement and any acquisition of Cursor (described below), if completed, will extend our strategy to vertically integrate compute infrastructure, models, and applications, can help accelerate our development of AI-native software tools, and combined with our significant compute capacity, will help strengthen our position in AI-assisted developer productivity. We expect to accelerate the development of our existing AI models, including Grok, through our collaboration with Cursor.
与 Cursor 的合作 2026 年 4 月 19 日，我们与 Cursor 签订了算力协议。Cursor 开发并运营一款 AI 原生集成开发环境，使专业软件开发者和工程团队能通过基于 LLM 的 agent 与工作流（通过其专有的模型 harness 整合）来编写、编辑、审查和重构代码。2025 年，Cursor 推出了 Composer——其自研、用于软件开发的 LLM。最近发布了 Composer 2，在更低成本下提供更优的编码性能。我们相信，算力协议以及任何对 Cursor 的收购（如下述）若得以完成，将延展我们对算力基础设施、模型和应用进行垂直整合的战略，可帮助加速我们对 AI 原生软件工具的开发；结合我们庞大的算力容量，也将帮助强化我们在 AI 辅助开发者生产力领域的地位。我们期望通过与 Cursor 的合作加速 Grok 等现有 AI 模型的研发。
Under the compute agreement, we will provide Cursor with certain GPU cluster compute capacity for use in connection with specified development, training, improvement and other activities related to AI models and other technology and intellectual property. In exchange, Cursor will contribute certain personnel, data and datasets, documentation, technical know-how, workflows, prompts, specifications and software code. We will collaborate with Cursor to improve our existing models, including Grok, and potentially to jointly develop AI models and related model-specific deliverables. Each party retains ownership of its pre-existing and independently developed intellectual property (including, in the case of SpaceX, Grok) and related improvements and derivatives, including where they are utilized in connection with joint development activities. Any jointly developed models will be jointly owned, and each party will have a broad right to use, reproduce, modify, distribute, license, commercialize and otherwise exploit them without an obligation to account to the other party.
根据算力协议，我们将向 Cursor 提供一定的 GPU 集群算力，用于指定的 AI 模型及其他技术、知识产权相关的开发、训练、改进及其他活动。作为交换，Cursor 将贡献某些人员、数据和数据集、文档、技术诀窍、工作流、prompts、规格和软件代码。我们将与 Cursor 合作改进现有模型（包括 Grok），并可能联合开发 AI 模型和相关模型特定交付物。各方对其自身先前存在或独立开发的知识产权（包括 SpaceX 的 Grok）以及相关改进和衍生品保留所有权——即便其在联合开发活动中被使用。任何联合开发的模型将共同拥有，各方均有广泛权利使用、复制、修改、分发、许可、商业化及以其他方式利用这些模型，且无须向另一方报账。
We also entered into an option agreement pursuant to which we have the right, but not the obligation, to acquire Cursor. The option agreement generally provides that we may exercise the call option at any time during the 30-day period following the earlier of (i) seven trading days following the completion of this offering and (ii) September 30, 2026\. Exercise of the call option is in our sole discretion and subject to further approval by our board of directors.
我们还签订了一项期权协议，根据该协议我们有权但无义务收购 Cursor。期权协议总体规定：我们可以在以下两者较早发生之日后的 30 天期间内随时行使认购权：(i) 本次发行完成后第 7 个交易日；以及 (ii) 2026 年 9 月 30 日。行使认购权由我们自行决定，并须经董事会进一步批准。
Cursor is also subject to certain exclusivity obligations under the option agreement.
根据期权协议，Cursor 还须受某些排他性义务约束。
If we exercise the call option, we would simultaneously execute a merger agreement with Cursor, pursuant to which, following satisfaction of the closing conditions set forth in the merger agreement, including receipt of requisite regulatory approvals, Cursor would become our subsidiary, and, as a result, we would acquire all of Cursor's cash, intellectual property, personnel, customer contracts and other assets. As of January 31, 2026 (Cursor's fiscal year- end), Cursor had $3.1 billion of total assets, primarily comprising $2.7 billion of cash and cash equivalents, and $0.55 billion of total liabilities. The purchase price would primarily be allocated to goodwill on our balance sheet.
如果我们行使认购权，我们将同步签署与 Cursor 的合并协议，根据该协议，在合并协议中规定的交割条件（包括获得必要的监管批准）满足后，Cursor 将成为我们的子公司，因此我们将取得 Cursor 全部的现金、知识产权、人员、客户合同和其他资产。截至 2026 年 1 月 31 日（Cursor 的财年末），Cursor 的总资产为 31 亿美元——主要由 27 亿美元的现金及现金等价物构成——总负债为 5.5 亿美元。购买价款将主要在我们资产负债表上确认为商誉。
Cursor has historically earned some revenue by providing services to customers and, if we acquired Cursor, we may provide these or similar services to customers after the acquisition although at revenue levels that may vary significantly from historical performance. If we exercise the call option to acquire Cursor, we would expect to retain certain Cursor talent by committing to provide continuing employees with competitive compensation and retention- focused incentives designed to support the long-term value of SpaceX.
Cursor 历史上通过向客户提供服务获得部分收入；如果我们收购 Cursor，我们可能在收购后向客户提供这些或类似服务，但收入水平可能与历史业绩显著不同。如果我们行使认购权收购 Cursor，我们预计会通过为留任员工提供具竞争力的薪酬和以留任为目标、旨在支持 SpaceX 长期价值的激励，留住 Cursor 的部分核心人才。
The consideration for the acquisition of Cursor, if any, after the closing of this offering would consist of shares of our Class A common stock based on an implied equity value of Cursor of $60.0 billion, and the price of our Class A common stock that equals the volume-weighted average closing price thereof over the seven consecutive trading days immediately preceding the closing of the acquisition. If either (i) we decide to terminate the option agreement or (ii) Cursor is eligible to and decides to terminate due to our material breach of the option agreement (subject to notice and cure provisions), Cursor is entitled to a $1.5 billion termination fee under the option agreement and an $8.5 billion deferred services fee under the compute agreement. These fees are payable in cash (or Class A common stock, if this offering has not been consummated at the time the fees become payable).
本次发行完成后，对 Cursor 的收购对价（如有）将由我们的 A 类普通股股份构成，依据 Cursor 隐含股权价值 600 亿美元以及我们 A 类普通股价格（取收购交割前连续 7 个交易日的成交量加权平均收盘价）计算。如果 (i) 我们决定终止期权协议；或 (ii) Cursor 有资格并选择因我们对期权协议的重大违约（须经通知和补救条款约束）而终止，则 Cursor 有权根据期权协议获得 15 亿美元终止费，并根据算力协议获得 85 亿美元递延服务费。这些费用以现金支付（或在费用到期时本次发行尚未完成的情况下以 A 类普通股支付）。
Any shares of our Class A common stock issuable pursuant to the merger agreement would be issued in reliance upon the exemption from the registration requirements of the Securities Act provided by Section 4(a)(2) thereof. As a result, any such shares of Class A common stock would be deemed "restricted securities" as such term is defined under Rule 144 under the Securities Act. Such shares of Class A common stock would be eligible for resale only if registered under the Securities Act or if such resales qualify for an exemption from registration.
根据合并协议可发行的任何 A 类普通股股份将依赖 Securities Act 第 4(a)(2) 条规定的注册要求豁免发行。因此，任何此类 A 类普通股股份将被视为 Securities Act Rule 144 下定义的「受限证券」。此类 A 类普通股股份只有在依照 Securities Act 注册或此类转售符合注册豁免条件时，方可转售。
We have conducted preliminary due diligence on Cursor's business, technology and operations, and expect to continue such diligence in connection with any decision to exercise the call option. We cannot predict whether we will elect to exercise the call option or, if exercised, whether the acquisition will close on the anticipated terms, or at all.
我们已对 Cursor 的业务、技术和运营进行了初步尽职调查，并预计在任何行使认购权的决定相关的过程中继续此类尽调。我们无法预测是否会选择行使认购权，或者一旦行使，收购是否会按预期条款完成、或是否能完成。
Compute Services Agreements with Third Parties We believe our compute infrastructure and related strategy provides us with substantial flexibility in how we allocate and monetize capacity. We have the ability to use compute resources to support our proprietary AI applications (such as Grok 5, which is currently being trained at COLOSSUS II), while also providing access to select compute capacity to third-party customers. For example, in May 2026, we entered into Cloud Services Agreements with Anthropic, an AI research and development public benefit corporation, with respect to access to compute capacity across COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II. Pursuant to these agreements, the customer has agreed to pay us $1.25 billion per month through May 2029, with capacity ramping in May and June 2026 at a reduced fee.
与第三方的算力服务协议 我们相信我们的算力基础设施及相关战略在如何分配和变现容量方面给予我们极大的灵活性。我们既可以使用算力资源支持自有 AI 应用（例如目前正在 COLOSSUS II 上训练的 Grok 5），也可以向部分第三方客户提供算力容量。例如 2026 年 5 月，我们与 AI 研究与开发公益公司 Anthropic 签订了云服务协议，涉及 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 的算力容量。根据这些协议，客户同意在 2026 年 5 月和 6 月以较低费用启动容量，并在直至 2029 年 5 月期间每月向我们支付 12.5 亿美元。
The agreements may be terminated by either party upon 90 days' notice. The customer will retain ownership and intellectual property rights in its content, AI models, and related data. This structure allows us to monetize unused compute capacity in our infrastructure, while still permitting reallocation of the capacity for our own internal initiatives if needed in the future. We have sufficient capacity to provide compute for our own AI models, including support of our training and inference demands, and to satisfy the obligations under these agreements. We expect to enter into additional similar services contracts for compute capacity with third parties. To the extent we become compute constrained due internal and external utilization, we would need to expand our compute infrastructure. We believe this opportunity highlights the increasing importance of large-scale, frontier-level AI infrastructure and positions us as a differentiated provider of high-performance compute capacity to both internal and third-party AI workloads. We believe our dual monetization strategy provides multiple pathways to generate returns on invested capital.
协议可由任一方在 90 天通知后终止。客户对其内容、AI 模型及相关数据保留所有权和知识产权。这种结构允许我们对基础设施中未使用的算力进行变现，同时如未来内部需要仍可重新分配该容量。我们拥有足够的容量为自有 AI 模型（包括支持训练和推理需求）提供算力，并能履行这些协议项下的义务。我们预计将与第三方签订更多类似的算力容量服务合同。如果由于内部和外部使用导致我们算力紧张，我们将需要扩展算力基础设施。我们相信这一机会凸显了大规模、前沿级 AI 基础设施日益增长的重要性，并将我们定位为面向内部和第三方 AI 工作负载的差异化高性能算力提供方。我们相信这种双重变现策略为投入资本提供了多条回报路径。
Our Repeatable Business Model Our business model is built on a repeatable, engineering-driven framework that combines our unparalleled launch capabilities, extreme vertical integration, rapid iteration, and disciplined capital investment to create durable, large- scale businesses. We execute this framework through the following core principles:
我们可复制的商业模式 我们的商业模式建立在可复制、工程驱动的框架上，结合了无与伦比的发射能力、极致的垂直整合、快速迭代和审慎的资本投资，以创造持久、大规模的业务。我们通过以下核心原则执行这一框架：
1.Leverage our unparalleled launch capabilities to enable massive scale. Our rockets--with unmatched launch cadence, best-in-class reliability, and dramatically reduced cost-to-orbit--are the foundation that we expect will enable us to create economic opportunities in space and deliver a diversified portfolio of services.
1. 撬动无与伦比的发射能力以实现巨大规模。我们的火箭——具备无可比拟的发射频率、最佳的可靠性以及大幅降低的入轨成本——是我们预期能够在太空中创造经济机会并交付多元化服务组合的基础。
Our launch capabilities enable large-scale deployment of assets that would not otherwise be economically viable.
我们的发射能力让那些原本在经济上不可行的大规模资产部署成为可能。
2.Identify and create new trillion-dollar market opportunities. We focus on market opportunities that are useful for humanity and that present trillion-dollar opportunities, including global broadband and mobile connectivity for consumers, enterprises, and governments; and AI applications and computational infrastructure.
2. 识别并创造新的万亿美元市场机会。我们专注于对人类有用、且具有万亿美元规模的市场机会，包括面向消费者、企业和政府的全球宽带和移动连接，以及 AI 应用和算力基础设施。
We prioritize opportunities where structural inefficiencies or legacy technological limitations have constrained supply.
我们优先选择那些因结构性低效或遗留技术限制而供给受限的机会。
3.Design a solution with world-class engineering and first-principles thinking. We apply physics-based engineering and first-principles thinking to design products and systems from the ground up--boiling things down to the most fundamental truths and reasoning up from there. This helps us drive massive, step-function improvements in performance, scalability, and cost.
3. 用世界级工程和第一性原理思维设计解决方案。我们运用基于物理学的工程和第一性原理思维，从零开始设计产品和系统——把事物归结到最基本的真理，再向上推理。这帮助我们在性能、可扩展性和成本上推动巨大的、跃迁式的改进。
4.Apply "The Algorithm" (make less dumb, delete, optimize, accelerate, automate). We operate under a set of core execution principles that we refer to as "The Algorithm," a five-step iterative process that we use as our guiding principles day-to-day. We make the requirements less dumb, delete unnecessary processes or parts (embracing the principle that the best part is no part), only then optimize the necessary processes or parts, and then accelerate cycle time (many entities have launched once; no one other than us has ever launched over 100 times per year), and automate only proven processes after the first four steps are completed. We apply the Algorithm across every aspect of our organization, creating a cultural and operational standard of excellence that has defined SpaceX since inception.
4. 运用「The Algorithm」（让更少愚蠢、删除、优化、加速、自动化）。我们在被称为「The Algorithm」的核心执行原则下运作，这是一个我们日常作为指导原则使用的五步迭代流程。我们让需求不那么愚蠢、删除不必要的流程或零件（拥抱「最好的零件就是没有零件」原则），然后再优化必要的流程或零件，接着加快循环周期（许多机构发射过一次；除我们以外，没有谁每年发射过超过 100 次），最后在前四步完成后只对已验证的流程进行自动化。我们在组织的每个方面应用 The Algorithm，形成了自 SpaceX 创立以来定义我们的卓越文化和运营标准。
5.Vertically integrate all the way to the end customer. We design and manufacture a significant portion of our components in-house, including engines, avionics, structures, and software, even producing the "tools that make the tools," enabling us to test, fail, and iterate rapidly. We can then release newer, more advanced hardware with speed and cost efficiency.
5. 一直向终端客户做垂直整合。我们在内部设计和制造相当一部分组件，包括引擎、航电、结构和软件，甚至生产「制造工具的工具」，使我们能快速地测试、失败并迭代。我们因此能以速度和成本效率发布更新、更先进的硬件。
6.Continuously drive cost down and throughput up. Through rocket reusability, manufacturing at scale, advanced automation, and rigorous operational discipline, we continuously reduce unit costs while increasing launch cadence, satellite network, and AI hosting capacity.
6. 持续推动成本下降、产能上升。通过火箭可回收性、规模化制造、先进自动化和严格的运营纪律，我们持续降低单位成本，同时提高发射频率、卫星网络和 AI 托管容量。
7.Generate significant cash flow and reinvest in the future. As our businesses scale, they generate significant cash flow, which we reinvest into nascent market opportunities--driving a self-reinforcing cycle of constant innovation and potentially creating significant additional value.
7. 产生大量现金流并对未来再投资。随着业务规模扩大，它们产生大量现金流，我们将其再投入到萌芽期的市场机会，推动持续创新的自我强化循环，并可能创造重大的额外价值。
Starship is a powerful example of this business model in action. Upon achieving a fully and rapidly reusable design, we believe Starship will support a step-function increase in launch capacity and be capable of landing massive amounts of cargo on the Moon. Once there, we believe it will be possible to establish a permanent presence for scientific and manufacturing pursuits. For example, we believe that factories on the Moon could take advantage of lunar resources to manufacture millions of AI compute satellites and deploy them farther into space. Additionally, we are collaborating with NASA under the Artemis program to land humans on the Moon, with the goal of using Starship for transportation, which will be the first such mission since 1972.
Starship 是这一商业模式实践的有力例证。一旦实现完全且快速可重复使用的设计，我们相信 Starship 将支持发射能力的跃迁式提升，并能向月球投送大量货物。一旦到达月球，我们相信有可能建立长期存在以开展科学研究和制造。例如，我们相信月球工厂能利用月球资源制造数百万颗 AI 算力卫星并向太空更远处部署。此外，我们正与 NASA 在 Artemis 项目下合作把人类送上月球，目标是使用 Starship 作为运输工具——这将是自 1972 年以来首次此类任务。
We will continue leveraging our expanding launch capabilities, combined with our engineering and manufacturing expertise, to create and scale new markets in space for the benefit of humanity--on Earth, the Moon, Mars, and beyond.
我们将继续利用不断扩展的发射能力，结合工程和制造能力，为人类福祉在太空中创建并扩展新市场——在地球、月球、火星及更远处。
Our Engineering-First Culture We are able to achieve transformative technological breakthroughs because we accept only the laws of physics as the limiting factors to our work and mission. Our core approach is deeply rooted in first-principles thinking, which rejects any preconceived notions or experience-based norms. Our unparalleled track record demonstrates our capacity to execute space missions and achieve technological breakthroughs with speed and precision that others have not achieved. We have a track record of achieving what many have deemed impossible. Some of our industry- defining achievements and historic milestones include:
我们的工程优先文化 我们之所以能够实现变革性技术突破，是因为我们只接受物理学定律作为对我们工作和使命的限制因素。我们的核心方式深植于第一性原理思维，拒绝任何先入为主的观念或基于经验的常规。我们无可匹敌的过往业绩展示了执行太空任务并以他人未达到的速度和精度实现技术突破的能力。我们有过一系列他人认为不可能的成就。我们一些定义行业的成果和历史性里程碑包括：
•The first private company to develop and launch a liquid-fuel rocket to reach orbit (2008);
• 第一家研制并发射液体燃料火箭入轨的私营公司 (2008 年)；
•The first private company to successfully dock a private spacecraft with the International Space Station (2012);
• 第一家将私营航天器成功对接国际空间站的私营公司 (2012 年)；
•The first to successfully propulsively land (2015) and refly orbital-class rocket boosters (2017);
• 首次以推进方式回收 (2015 年) 并复飞 (2017 年) 轨道级火箭助推器；
•The first to begin deploying a large-scale LEO broadband satellite constellation (2019);
• 第一家开始部署大规模近地轨道宽带卫星星座 (2019 年)；
•The first private company to transport astronauts to orbit, returning America's ability to fly astronauts to and from the International Space Station (2020);
• 第一家将宇航员送入轨道、恢复美国宇航员往返国际空间站能力的私营公司 (2020 年)；
•The first to manufacture consumer-grade phased-array user terminals at scale (2022);
• 第一家大规模制造消费级相控阵 (phased array) 用户终端 (2022 年)；
•The first to deploy a large-scale LEO satellite-to-mobile constellation (2025);
• 第一家部署大规模近地轨道卫星直连移动星座 (2025 年)；
•The first to build a gigawatt-scale AI training cluster and largest coherent supercomputer (2026);
• 第一家构建吉瓦级 AI 训练集群和最大协同超级计算机 (2026 年)；
•The first gigawatt-scale Megapack battery installation (2026); and •The only company capable of building orbital AI compute at scale.
• 第一次进行吉瓦级 Megapack 电池安装 (2026 年)；以及 • 唯一能够大规模构建轨道 AI 算力的公司。
Our organizational philosophy fosters an engineering- and data-led culture that embraces failure as an essential learning opportunity and is maniacally focused on efficiency and speed. This culture allows us to deliberately move quickly to test new hardware, knowing that early failures provide more valuable data than protracted analysis. We view our factories as the machines that build the machines and maintain a relentless focus on our ability to move, fail, and fix fast.
我们的组织哲学培育出工程和数据主导的文化，将失败视为必不可少的学习机会，并对效率和速度有近乎狂热的专注。这种文化让我们能有意地快速移动以测试新硬件，因为我们知道早期失败比冗长分析提供了更有价值的数据。我们把工厂视作制造机器的机器，并对我们快速移动、快速失败、快速修复的能力保持不懈关注。
Our AI Compute Infrastructure Advantage and Growth Strategy We believe AI leadership will be defined by the ability to rapidly scale compute capacity to support exponential usage growth and frontier intelligence. There is a meaningful compounding benefit of greater usage, creating more data for training, driving improvements in model performance, and in turn leading to greater usage. We believe that our highly vertically integrated, shovels-to-tokens approach allows us to train and iterate our frontier models at lower cost and higher velocity, accelerating development cycles, eliminating external bottlenecks, and driving rapid, continuous improvements in model performance. This dynamic reinforces the criticality of scale and cost efficiency in compute infrastructure as the primary differentiator in the AI landscape. In addition, our leadership in compute infrastructure positions us to monetize not only AI software applications built on our models, but also the underlying compute that powers them. As we continue to scale our terrestrial and orbital compute infrastructure to support internal model development, training, and inference workloads, we intend to sell our high-performance compute capacity to a limited number of third party customers.
我们的 AI 算力基础设施优势与增长策略 我们相信，AI 领导力将由快速扩展算力以支持指数级使用增长和前沿智能的能力来定义。使用量增加会带来显著的复利效应：产生更多训练数据，推动模型性能改进，进而带来更多使用。我们相信我们高度垂直整合的「铲子到 tokens」一体化方式让我们能以更低成本、更高速度训练和迭代前沿模型，加速研发周期，消除外部瓶颈，推动模型性能持续快速改进。这一动态强化了算力基础设施的规模和成本效率作为 AI 领域主要差异点的关键性。此外，我们在算力基础设施上的领先地位让我们不仅能对基于我们模型的 AI 软件应用进行变现，也能对驱动这些应用的底层算力进行变现。在我们继续扩展地面和轨道算力基础设施以支持内部模型开发、训练和推理工作负载的同时，我们也计划向有限数量的第三方客户出售高性能算力容量。
Why Compute Matters. The training and inference demanded by advanced AI models require substantial computational resources. Greater compute capacity enables more intelligence by training new generations of models with increasing frequency and creating more capable models, ability to support inference, or usage, across a large and growing user base, and extraction of the highest performance from those models. As the AI user base expands, we also expect compute demand per user to increase significantly. Reasoning models introduced in 2024 demonstrated that allocating more computational resources during inference directly leads to higher-quality intelligence. AI agents popularized in 2026 demonstrated that allocating more computational resources enabled multi-step task execution, meaningfully increasing compute demand per human user interaction. In addition, compute infrastructure with end-to-end, cluster-level coherence through tight integration across software and hardware systems enables more efficient, stable, and higher-fidelity training and inference at scale--ultimately enhancing model intelligence and performance. Within inference, we expect computationally-intensive reasoning, agentic, and multi-modal workloads will continue to grow as a portion of overall usage. We therefore expect demand for compute will continue to increase across consumer, enterprise, and government applications as AI adoption accelerates. For example, U.S. compute demand has already outpaced available power supply with estimated demand of 62 gigawatts in 2025 exceeding the power generation of 49 gigawatts, according to industry sources. We expect the gap between demand for compute and power supply to continue to widen meaningfully as AI compute needs proliferate. Furthermore, we believe that third-party estimates on data center demand are constrained by the practical supply limitations that exist in a terrestrial context and the power shortage may be far greater than what research estimates suggest. We believe operators with superior model-to-compute integration--the ability to efficiently support and allocate compute across both training and inference workloads--are best positioned to win the AI race.
为什么算力重要。先进 AI 模型所需的训练和推理需要大量算力资源。更大的算力让我们能更频繁地训练新世代模型并打造能力更强的模型、支持大量且不断增长的用户基础的推理或使用，并从这些模型中榨取最高的性能。随着 AI 用户基础的扩大，我们也预计每用户算力需求会显著上升。2024 年引入的推理模型显示，在推理阶段分配更多算力直接带来更高质量的智能。2026 年流行起来的 AI agent 则显示，分配更多算力让多步任务执行成为可能，显著提升了每次人机交互所需算力。此外，算力基础设施通过软硬件系统的紧密整合实现端到端、集群级的协同性时，可以在大规模上实现更高效、稳定、保真度更高的训练和推理——最终增强模型智能与性能。在推理中，我们预计计算密集型的推理、agentic 和多模态工作负载将继续在整体使用中占比上升。因此，随着 AI 采用加速，我们预计消费者、企业和政府应用对算力的需求将继续增长。例如据行业来源，美国算力需求已超过可用电力供应，2025 年估算需求 62 吉瓦，已超过 49 吉瓦的发电量。我们预计随着 AI 算力需求扩散，算力需求与电力供应之间的差距将继续显著扩大。此外，我们相信第三方对数据中心需求的估计受地面环境实际供给限制的约束，电力短缺可能远比研究估计的更严重。我们相信，那些具备更强的模型–算力整合能力的运营方——即能够在训练和推理工作负载之间高效支持和分配算力——最有可能赢得 AI 竞赛。
Self-Reinforcing Network Effects Among Lower Cost Per Token, Model Quality, and User Adoption. AI systems are ultimately constrained or differentiated by the cost, speed, and scale at which they can generate and process tokens. A "token" represents the fundamental unit of data consumed and produced by modern AI models, for example corresponding to words, images, audio, or other modalities. It serves as the atomic unit through which models read, reason, and generate output. As such, tokens are the primary basis for measuring both the cost of training and cost of inference, making them a foundational economic metric in the AI space. Companies that can structurally reduce energy, compute, networking, and deployment costs per token will be positioned to train faster, iterate more rapidly, and ultimately manufacture greater intelligence, scale models more rapidly, and deliver increasingly powerful and accessible AI solutions. This creates a self-reinforcing advantage in which lower token costs drive greater model quality and user adoption, reinforcing AI leadership. This is because lower cost per token enables more frequent model training, larger and more sophisticated models, longer chains of processing for reasoning and agentic workloads, and significantly higher inference volumes at economically viable prices. This dynamic directly impacts model quality, responsiveness, and accessibility, while also determining the ability to serve the rising global demand across consumer, enterprise, and mission-critical AI applications. As AI systems scale toward increasingly complex reasoning tasks and higher usage intensity, improvement in cost per token enables meaningful advantages in performance quality, scaled distribution, and monetization. This is particularly true as the industry converges towards recursive self-improving learning that minimizes human intervention, which is highly token consumptive.
更低 token 单位成本、模型质量与用户采用之间的自我强化网络效应。AI 系统最终被其生成和处理 token 的成本、速度和规模所约束或差异化。「token」是现代 AI 模型消费和产出的基本数据单元——例如对应于词、图像、音频或其他模态。它是模型读取、推理和生成输出的原子单位。因此 token 是衡量训练成本和推理成本的主要基础，使其成为 AI 领域的基础经济指标。能够在每个 token 上结构性降低能源、算力、组网和部署成本的公司，将有能力更快训练、更快迭代，并最终制造更多智能、更快地扩展模型并交付越来越强大且可及的 AI 解决方案。这创造了自我强化的优势——更低的 token 成本推动更高的模型质量和更广的用户采用，进而强化 AI 领导力。这是因为更低的单 token 成本支持更频繁地训练模型、更大更复杂的模型、推理和 agentic 工作负载的更长处理链，以及在经济可行价格下显著更大的推理量。这一动态直接影响模型质量、响应速度和可及性，同时也决定了服务于消费者、企业和关键任务 AI 应用全球不断增长需求的能力。随着 AI 系统朝着越来越复杂的推理任务和更高的使用强度扩展，单 token 成本的改进可在性能质量、规模化分发和变现上带来显著优势。在行业向最小化人类介入的递归式自我改进学习收敛时尤其如此——这类学习本身对 token 消耗极高。
Cost of Compute is the Main Driver of Cost Per Token. The cost of compute is the primary driver of cost per token across both training and inference workloads. Each token processed by an AI model requires a quantifiable amount of computational effort. The total cost per token is determined by the efficiency, availability, and unit economics of the underlying compute resources. According to SemiAnalysis, for most AI companies without a build cost advantage, their total capital cost of building compute infrastructure derives approximately 30% from data center construction costs (including, but not limited to, the shell; mechanical, electrical, and plumbing ("MEP"); and grid interconnection) and approximately 70% from the cost of procuring processors and critical IT equipment. Ongoing operational costs of utilizing this compute infrastructure include the cost of power to run the processors, cost of maintaining those processors, and cost of delivering inference workloads to the end user. Improvement in the cost of building and operating this compute infrastructure--whether through lower data center construction cost, lower power infrastructure cost, shorter time to grid interconnection, or higher cluster-level throughput--translates directly into lower cost per token. Accordingly, for a given level of intelligence, we expect the long-term economics of AI companies to be driven by the ability to consistently deliver bleeding-edge compute at the lowest possible cost per token. Put simply, we view cost per token as a function of three primary inputs--the underlying AI model, the compute hardware, and energy, and we expect to have a competitive advantage in the latter two cost components.
算力成本是单 token 成本的主要驱动因素。算力成本是训练和推理两类工作负载中单 token 成本的主要驱动因素。AI 模型处理的每个 token 都需要可量化的计算量。单 token 总成本由底层算力资源的效率、可用性和单位经济决定。据 SemiAnalysis，对大多数不具备建设成本优势的 AI 公司而言，其构建算力基础设施的总资本成本中约 30% 来自数据中心建设成本（包括但不限于外壳、机电管道（MEP, mechanical, electrical, and plumbing）和电网接入），约 70% 来自采购处理器和关键 IT 设备的成本。运营这种算力基础设施的持续成本包括驱动处理器的电费、维护处理器的费用，以及向终端用户交付推理工作负载的成本。无论通过更低的数据中心建设成本、更低的电力基础设施成本、更短的电网接入时间还是更高的集群级吞吐——构建和运营这种算力基础设施的成本改善都会直接转化为更低的单 token 成本。因此，对于给定的智能水平，我们预计 AI 公司长期经济将由持续以可能最低的单 token 成本交付最尖端算力的能力驱动。简而言之，我们把单 token 成本看作三个主要输入的函数——底层 AI 模型、算力硬件和能源——并预计我们在后两项成本组成上具有竞争优势。
We believe we have a pathway over time that will significantly reduce compute hardware costs through continued vertical integration and development of proprietary chips, building on our experience designing custom silicon for our Starlink satellites. We also expect that the marginal cost of energy for our AI compute satellites will be minimal because our satellites are powered by solar arrays in space. By driving the energy component to minimal levels and pursuing improvements in compute hardware cost, we believe we can achieve a meaningfully lower overall cost per token in the future.
我们相信，通过持续的垂直整合和自研芯片开发（基于我们为 Starlink 卫星设计自定义硅芯片的经验），我们有一条长期能显著降低算力硬件成本的路径。我们还预计 AI 算力卫星的能源边际成本将极低，因为我们的卫星由太空中的太阳能阵列供电。通过把能源成分压到极低、并追求算力硬件成本的改进，我们相信未来可以达到显著更低的整体单 token 成本。
We Have a Dual Speed and Cost Advantage in Terrestrial AI Compute. We have established a leading position in building and scaling terrestrial AI compute infrastructure, becoming the first company to deploy a coherent gigawatt-scale AI training cluster. We own and operate what we believe to be the largest AI training data center clusters on Earth. Our AI compute facilities, COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II, collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. Our first- principles thinking enables us to build coherent compute at scale and at rapid speed with lower costs than most other companies in the industry. We brought the first cluster of COLOSSUS online in 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory, and the first cluster of COLOSSUS II online even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis. We are able to deploy power and compute significantly faster than other AI companies through first-principles thinking, behind- the-meter power generation, coupled with what we believe is the world's largest network of sustainable battery storage systems, and innovations in advanced liquid cooling, high-density rack layouts, and efficient networking.
我们在地面 AI 算力上具备速度与成本的双重优势。我们在构建和扩展地面 AI 算力基础设施方面已确立领先地位，是首家部署完整 gigawatt 级 AI 训练集群的公司。我们拥有并运营着我们认为是地球上最大的 AI 训练数据中心集群。我们的 AI 算力设施 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦算力，并有额外的电力容量可用于数据中心运营。我们的第一性原理思维让我们能以低于行业大多数公司的成本快速地大规模构建协同算力。我们用 122 天就让 COLOSSUS 的首个集群上线，改造了一座现有工厂的外壳；COLOSSUS II 首个集群上线则更快，仅用 91 天。作为参照，行业基准下让一座 100 兆瓦的全新数据中心上线约需两年。我们在成本效率上也展现了显著进步，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本按每兆瓦计算大幅低于行业基准。通过第一性原理思维、表后自建发电（叠加我们认为是全球最大规模的可持续电池储能系统网络）以及在先进液冷、高密度机柜布局和高效组网方面的创新，我们能比其他 AI 公司显著更快地部署电力和算力。
Our first-principles thinking and innovations in advanced liquid cooling, high-density rack layouts, and efficient networking enable rapid, cost-effective scaling with the latest processors--keeping us ahead of competitors deploying traditional methods. Faster deployments reinforce our cost advantage: we are able to access and bring online the highest performing hardware before our competitors, allowing us to sustain a token cost advantage. For example, we believe COLOSSUS II became one of the world's first data centers to deploy GB200s and GB300s at significant scale and is currently powering training for our next frontier models, including Grok-5. We have already proven in multiple large-scale terrestrial data centers that we have built not only faster than competitors in the industry, but also at a lower cost.
我们的第一性原理思维和在先进液冷、高密度机柜布局、高效组网方面的创新，让我们能以最新处理器进行快速、低成本的扩张——领先于采用传统方式部署的竞争者。更快的部署强化了我们的成本优势：我们能在竞争对手之前获取并让最高性能硬件上线，从而保持 token 成本优势。例如，我们相信 COLOSSUS II 是全球最早大规模部署 GB200 和 GB300 的数据中心之一，目前正在为我们下一代前沿模型（包括 Grok-5）训练提供算力。我们在多个大规模地面数据中心上已经证明：我们不仅比业内竞争者更快地建成，成本也更低。
We have a Unique Right to Win in Orbital AI. The Sun contains approximately 99.8% of the solar system's energy and offers what we believe is the only truly scalable solution to terrestrial energy constraints, as we expect the cost and availability of terrestrial energy sources over time will necessitate a transition to orbital AI solutions. The logical path forward is to move power-intensive AI workloads into orbit, where solar energy is near-constant and uninterrupted. With such accessibility to energy, we believe that our launch business will enable us to consistently activate the highest performing hardware before our competitors without such access, shrinking the timeline to useful tokens on bleeding-edge hardware and sustaining our token cost advantage. Manufacturing next-generation satellites and launching them into space in very large numbers is a core component of our plans. We believe we are the only company with a commercially viable path to building orbital AI compute at scale. This is underpinned by our unique ability to launch substantial mass into orbit cost-efficiently through reusable rockets and to manufacture secure, reliable, and high-performance satellites at low cost and high volume.
我们在轨道 AI 上拥有独特的胜出权利。太阳含有太阳系约 99.8% 的能量，并提供了我们认为唯一真正可扩展的地面能源约束解决方案——我们预计随着时间推移，地面能源源的成本和可用性将必然推动向轨道 AI 解决方案的过渡。逻辑上的前进路径是把高耗能 AI 工作负载移到太空，那里太阳能近乎恒定且不被打断。凭借如此对能源的可获取性，我们相信发射业务将让我们能在竞争对手之前持续激活最高性能硬件，缩短在尖端硬件上产生有用 token 的时间线，并维持 token 成本优势。大规模制造下一代卫星并将其大量发射到太空是我们计划的核心。我们相信我们是唯一具备商业可行路径大规模构建轨道 AI 算力的公司。这建立在我们独有的能力之上：通过可重复使用火箭以低成本将大量质量送入轨道，并以低成本、高产量制造安全可靠、高性能的卫星。
•Terrestrial compute leadership. We believe the same cost and build advantages that have underpinned our leadership in gigawatt-scale terrestrial data centers will enable us to innovate across other terrestrial data center formats such as modular data centers for inference. We believe our modular terrestrial data center architectures will provide a foundation for the deployment of compute infrastructure in orbit given similarities in form factor in contrast to a gigawatt-scale campus.
• 地面算力领先地位。我们相信，支撑我们吉瓦级地面数据中心领先地位的同样成本和建设优势，将让我们能在其他地面数据中心形态（如用于推理的模块化数据中心）上进行创新。我们相信，相较于吉瓦级园区，模块化地面数据中心架构在形态尺寸上与轨道部署相似，因而可为轨道算力基础设施的部署提供基础。
•Satellites. Just as we expect our expertise in terrestrial data centers will enable us to package AI compute into modular, satellite form factors, we expect our leadership in satellite communications to allow us to interconnect our fleet of AI compute satellites into a massive, coherent constellation of compute. For example, as of March 31, 2026, our constellation already incorporated over 23,000 inter-satellite lasers that create a dynamic mesh network in space, enabling traffic to route through orbit rather than relying solely on terrestrial backhaul infrastructure. We are designing next-generation, high-performance AI compute satellites built for high volume, low cost, and with the reliability required for long-duration operation in space.
• 卫星。正如我们预计地面数据中心的专长将让我们能把 AI 算力封装到模块化的卫星形态中，我们也预计卫星通信领域的领先地位将让我们能把 AI 算力卫星互联成一个庞大、协同的算力星座。例如截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，我们的星座已经包含超过 23,000 条星间激光，构成太空中的动态网状网络，使流量能通过轨道路由，而不只是依赖地面回程基础设施。我们正在设计下一代高性能 AI 算力卫星，目标是高产量、低成本，并具备在太空长期运行所需的可靠性。
•Starship. We expect each of our Starship V3 vehicles to carry 100 metric tons to Earth's orbit in a reusable configuration, and future generations could reach 200 metric tons in capacity, potentially as soon as Starship V4. Future generations of Starship are being designed to eventually deliver millions of tons to orbit and beyond per year. Delivering large amounts of mass to orbit at low cost will be critical to deploying AI compute satellites at scale.
• Starship。我们预计每艘 Starship V3 载具在可重复使用配置下能向地球轨道投送 100 公吨，未来几代——可能最早在 Starship V4 上——容量可达 200 公吨。未来几代 Starship 正在被设计为最终每年向轨道及更远处投送数百万吨。以低成本向轨道投送大量质量对大规模部署 AI 算力卫星至关重要。
We Believe Orbital AI Can Accelerate Time to Power and Reduce Token Costs. The Sun contains approximately 99.8% of the solar system's energy and offers what we believe is the only truly scalable solution to the challenge of accelerating demand for compute relative to terrestrial energy constraints. The logical path forward is to move power-intensive AI workloads into orbit, where solar energy is near-constant and uninterrupted. With such accessibility to energy, we believe that our launch business will enable us to consistently activate the highest performing hardware before our competitors without such access. We believe SpaceX is uniquely positioned to deploy and operate data centers in orbit that can eventually achieve a lower cost than terrestrial data centers over time due to our extreme vertically integrated approach across launch, satellite manufacturing at scale, network connectivity and terrestrial data center expertise.
我们相信轨道 AI 能加速电力供给的到位并降低 token 成本。太阳含有太阳系约 99.8% 的能量，是我们认为应对相对于地面能源约束的算力需求加速的唯一真正可扩展方案。逻辑上的前进路径是把高耗能 AI 工作负载移到太空，那里太阳能近乎恒定且不被打断。凭借如此对能源的可获取性，我们相信发射业务将让我们能在竞争对手之前持续激活最高性能硬件。我们相信凭借在发射、规模化卫星制造、网络连接和地面数据中心方面的极致垂直整合，SpaceX 处于独特位置，能在轨道部署和运营数据中心，并随时间推移最终比地面数据中心成本更低。
•Time to useful tokens on new generations of infrastructure. Although we have already demonstrated an ability to rapidly scale new generations of compute in terrestrial deployments, we believe orbital AI will accelerate our time to useful tokens on bleeding-edge AI infrastructure. Physical deployment of new hardware is expected to be enabled by our launch business, where we believe reusability and launch cost efficiency will drive rapid cycles of payload delivery. Rapid time to useful tokens on that hardware will be enabled by the Sun's near-constant, uninterrupted supply of power, which would circumvent terrestrial power infrastructure constraints such as power procurement, grid interconnections, and permitting. As new generations of AI infrastructure continue to deliver step-function improvements in token efficiency, we believe that maintaining an AI fleet consistently at the bleeding edge of the frontier curve has the potential to deliver a sustainable cost per token advantage relative to our competitors.
• 新一代基础设施上产生有用 token 的时间。虽然我们已经在地面部署中证明了能快速扩展新一代算力，我们相信轨道 AI 将加快尖端 AI 基础设施上产生有用 token 的时间。新硬件的物理部署预计将由我们的发射业务支撑——我们相信可重复使用性和发射成本效率将推动有效载荷投送的快速循环。该硬件上产生有用 token 的时间将由太阳近乎恒定、不被打断的电力供应支撑，绕开诸如电力采购、电网接入和许可证等地面电力基础设施约束。随着新一代 AI 基础设施持续在 token 效率上带来跃迁式改进，我们相信持续把 AI 舰队保持在前沿曲线的最尖端有潜力相对竞争对手交付可持续的单 token 成本优势。
•Construction, power, and cooling infrastructure. In orbit, construction costs are replaced by launch costs and satellite production costs. We expect reusable launch systems and high flight cadence will significantly reduce the cost per kilogram to orbit, enabling more efficient deployment of compute payloads to orbit, and eventually approach the cost of fuel. We believe our advanced satellite manufacturing capabilities enable us to build AI compute satellites at scale and lower cost than competitors. Other terrestrial data center construction costs such as building the shell, MEP, and grid interconnection are not applicable in space. As a result, once Starship and our AI compute satellites are fully deployed at scale, we believe that the initial deployment costs of in-orbit compute in the aggregate will be less than construction costs of others' terrestrial data centers.
• 建设、电力和制冷基础设施。在轨道上，建设成本被发射成本和卫星生产成本所替代。我们预计可重复使用的发射系统和高飞行频率将显著降低每公斤入轨成本，使算力载荷能更高效地投送入轨，并最终接近燃料成本。我们相信先进卫星制造能力让我们能以比竞争对手更低的成本规模化制造 AI 算力卫星。其他诸如建造外壳、机电管道 (MEP) 和电网接入的地面数据中心建设成本在太空中并不适用。因此，一旦 Starship 和 AI 算力卫星全面规模化部署，我们相信在轨算力的初始部署总成本将低于其他公司地面数据中心的建设成本。
•Cost to procure and service processors. The cost of processors is a significant cost for both terrestrial and orbital data centers. We do not believe that moving compute to space in and of itself will have a meaningful impact on the cost of procuring processors. However, we believe that diversifying our long-term access to the supply of processors, including through our Terafab initiative with Tesla and Intel, will be a key driver in reducing the overall cost of compute hardware over time. By combining internally manufactured, lower cost chips with those we source from third-party suppliers, we expect the overall cost of our processors to decline. In addition to reducing costs, we also expect that this hybrid sourcing strategy will help alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX. In addition, we intend to conduct intensive pre-deployment testing to reduce the rate of chip failure in space, as we do not anticipate servicing or repairing processors in space.
• 处理器采购和维护成本。处理器成本是地面和轨道数据中心的重要成本。我们不认为单纯把算力移到太空对采购处理器的成本会有重大影响。但我们相信，通过包括与 Tesla 和 Intel 合作的 Terafab 项目在内的方式来多元化我们对处理器供给的长期获取，将是长期降低算力硬件总体成本的关键驱动。把内部制造的、更低成本的芯片与从第三方供应商采购的芯片结合，我们预计处理器整体成本将下降。除降低成本外，我们也预计这种混合采购策略将帮助缓解 SpaceX 未来可能的芯片短缺。此外，我们计划进行密集的部署前测试以降低芯片在太空中的失效率，因为我们不预期在太空中维修或更换处理器。
•Ongoing operations. The total cost of operating data centers is heavily influenced by energy, cooling, and distribution requirements. In orbit, chips are expected to be powered by solar energy which is low cost and unlimited, and we expect to leverage radiative cooling architectures, which incur no operating costs compared to liquid or air cooling. Our integrated, space-based Starlink network architecture also enables more cost efficient routing of data between compute clusters and to end users on a global basis.
• 持续运营。运营数据中心的总成本严重受能源、制冷和分发需求影响。在轨道上，芯片预计将由低成本且无限的太阳能供电，且我们预计将采用辐射制冷架构——相比液冷或风冷不产生运营成本。我们整合的天基 Starlink 网络架构也使算力集群之间以及与全球终端用户之间的数据路由更具成本效率。
We Believe We Are Well-Positioned to Deliver Orbital AI Compute. We believe orbital AI compute is an incredibly difficult technical challenge that only we can solve at scale in the near term. We are the only company that has already accomplished the key technical challenges associated with evolving connectivity satellites into AI compute satellites. In our view, due to our proven experience, we are well-positioned to deliver a full-scale AI compute satellite constellation. Significant work remains, but we are confident in our singular leadership position.
我们相信我们处于交付轨道 AI 算力的有利位置。我们相信轨道 AI 算力是一个极难的技术挑战，短期内只有我们能在规模上解决。我们是唯一一家已经攻克把通信卫星演化为 AI 算力卫星相关关键技术挑战的公司。从我们经过验证的经验来看，我们处于交付全规模 AI 算力卫星星座的有利位置。仍有大量工作要做，但我们对自己独有的领先地位充满信心。
•We have unmatched satellite launch capabilities to enable deployment at scale. Our ability to launch mass at scale and low cost is our foundational competitive advantage. Deployment of 100 gigawatts per year via satellites carrying over 100 kilowatts of compute power per metric ton will require thousands of launches per year and the transport of approximately one million metric tons to orbit annually. The fully reusable nature of Starship positions us to be capable of launching this level of mass. We plan to leverage our PEZ dispenser system, an integrated payload deployment system for Starship, along with our experience in developing fully deployable single-unit systems that are designed to substantially reduce the risks associated with in-orbit assembly. Starlink Broadband V1 and V2 Mini satellites have already demonstrated launch survivability and high reliability under vibration, shock, g-loads, acoustic stress, and vacuum exposure, achieving 99.9% average uptime. Although introducing AI processors would traditionally increase component-level failure rates, we plan to subject compute hardware to extensive pre-deployment testing on Earth to identify early life failures before launch.
• 我们拥有无可匹敌的卫星发射能力以实现规模化部署。低成本规模化发射质量的能力是我们的基础竞争优势。每年部署 100 吉瓦——按每公吨携带 100 千瓦以上算力的卫星计算——将需要每年数千次发射，并把每年约 100 万公吨送上轨道。Starship 完全可重复使用的特性让我们具备发射这种质量级别的能力。我们计划利用 PEZ 分发系统——一套用于 Starship 的集成有效载荷部署系统——以及我们开发完全可部署的单元系统的经验，旨在大幅降低在轨组装相关的风险。Starlink Broadband V1 和 V2 Mini 卫星已在振动、冲击、过载、声学应力和真空暴露下展示了发射可生存性和高可靠性，达到了 99.9% 的平均正常运行时间。尽管引入 AI 处理器传统上会提高组件级失效率，我们计划让算力硬件在地面接受广泛的部署前测试，以在发射前识别早期失效。
•We have already solved many of the significant technical hurdles to evolving connectivity satellites into AI compute satellites. Through our leading expertise in connectivity satellites and Starlink's existing technical and operational capabilities--including constellation-scale satellite management, autonomous operations, over- the-air software updates, inter-satellite laser communications, mesh network deployment, radiation-hardened system design, proprietary chip development, and the ability to operate computers reliably in the space environment--we have already solved the hardest part in the development of AI compute satellites. AI compute satellites represent an evolution of spacecraft engineering already demonstrated at scale through Starlink's connectivity satellites, and we believe development of AI compute satellites will be easier for us than for anyone else. AI compute satellites must integrate high-density compute payloads developed with radiation- tolerant designs and components with high electrical power generation, advanced thermal management, and inter satellite networking. To source the electricity needed to power our AI processors, we aim to continuously scale our existing space-grade solar technologies through insourced process development and build a constellation in dawn-dusk Sun-synchronous orbit that delivers near-constant solar exposure. We expect solar cells optimized for the space environment will be produced at a rapid rate, with early satellites generating 100 kilowatts of compute power and scaling from there. In orbit, thermal control must be accomplished through radiation rather than convection and conduction. We plan to advance thermal control systems--many of which have been proven on Starlink--by using radiators, vapor chambers, active cooling loops, and coatings to dissipate the heat generated by AI hardware in space's vacuum. We will also utilize inter-satellite lasers pioneered by Starlink for mesh networking at scale, creating coherent computing clusters across free space instead of wired connections used in terrestrial data centers. Our existing Starlink constellation, with over 23,000 inter-satellite lasers, will be a crucial enabler of orbital AI compute, as its global network allows data from our AI compute satellites in Sun-synchronous orbit to reach ground stations anywhere on Earth. The SpaceX AI compute satellites will be designed for high rate, automated production to enable the scale of satellites needed for the large amounts of compute planned in space.
• 我们已经解决了将通信卫星演化为 AI 算力卫星过程中的许多重大技术障碍。凭借在通信卫星上的领先专长和 Starlink 现有的技术与运营能力——包括星座级卫星管理、自主运营、空中软件更新、星间激光通信、网状网络部署、抗辐射系统设计、自研芯片开发，以及在太空环境中可靠运行计算机的能力——我们已经解决了开发 AI 算力卫星过程中最难的部分。AI 算力卫星是航天器工程的演进，已在 Starlink 通信卫星上规模化展示，我们相信对我们而言开发 AI 算力卫星会比对任何其他公司都更容易。AI 算力卫星必须把以抗辐射设计和元件构建的高密度算力载荷，与高电力发电、先进的热管理和星间组网相结合。为给 AI 处理器供电，我们计划通过内部工艺开发持续扩展现有太空级太阳能技术，并构建处于黎明–黄昏太阳同步轨道的星座，以获得近恒定的太阳曝光。我们预计针对太空环境优化的太阳能电池将以高速率生产，早期卫星可产生 100 千瓦算力，并由此扩展。在轨道上，热控制必须通过辐射而非对流和传导实现。我们计划推进热控系统——其中许多已在 Starlink 上得到验证——使用散热板、蒸汽腔、主动冷却回路和涂层在太空真空中耗散 AI 硬件产生的热量。我们还将利用 Starlink 首创的星间激光进行大规模网状组网，在自由空间中跨多颗卫星构建协同算力集群，而不是像地面数据中心那样使用有线连接。我们现有的 Starlink 星座，配备超过 23,000 条星间激光，将是轨道 AI 算力的关键支撑——其全球网络让位于太阳同步轨道的 AI 算力卫星数据能到达地球上任何地方的地面站。SpaceX 的 AI 算力卫星将被设计为高速、自动化生产，以支持太空中规划所需的大量卫星部署。
There are material differences between connectivity satellites and AI compute satellites. Connectivity satellites are primarily designed for communications, with substantial onboard equipment dedicated to phased-array antennas, radio systems, and data transmission. In contrast, AI compute satellites are optimized for high- performance computing. Key differences include significantly larger solar arrays to support higher power requirements, substantially larger radiators for thermal management, different electronics centered on AI accelerators rather than communications processors, and the removal of much of the communications hardware.
通信卫星与 AI 算力卫星存在实质性差异。通信卫星主要为通信设计，机上大量设备用于相控阵天线、射频系统和数据传输。相反，AI 算力卫星针对高性能计算进行优化。关键差异包括：显著更大的太阳能阵列以支持更高电力需求、显著更大的散热器用于热管理、以 AI 加速器而非通信处理器为核心的不同电子设计，以及移除大部分通信硬件。
Our V3 satellite platform already incorporates proprietary chips, providing a strong foundation for the ability to operate AI-focused electronics in space, and we expect to begin deploying our orbital AI compute satellites as early as 2028.
我们的 V3 卫星平台已经搭载自研芯片，为在太空运行以 AI 为中心的电子设备奠定了坚实基础；我们预计最早在 2028 年开始部署轨道 AI 算力卫星。
The primary remaining challenge is one of scale. For example, a deployment rate of approximately 10 gigawatts per year would require a materially lower manufacturing and launch cadence, which we believe would still enable a commercially attractive AI compute business with strong economic returns. While our long-term vision includes the ambition of deploying up to 100 gigawatts of power to orbit annually, which would require the deployment of thousands of launches per year, assuming 100 kilowatts of compute power per metric ton and Starship capacity to orbit of 100 metric tons, we believe we can be economically successful at significantly more modest volumes.
剩下的主要挑战是规模。例如，每年约 10 吉瓦的部署速率将需要的制造和发射频率显著更低，我们相信这仍能形成具有强劲经济回报的、商业上有吸引力的 AI 算力业务。我们的长期愿景包括每年向轨道部署多达 100 吉瓦电力的雄心，按每公吨 100 千瓦算力和 Starship 入轨容量 100 公吨计算，这将需要每年数千次发射；但我们相信在显著更低的量级下也能在经济上取得成功。
Our 100 gigawatt annual power deployment goal is based on reasoned engineering analyses and design parameters developed through our ongoing design and development work on next-generation AI compute satellites. These analyses are based on currently available space-grade solar technology and do not require fundamental technological advances beyond existing capabilities. Specifically, we expect these satellites to leverage our already-designed V3 satellite platform. The core V3 satellite design is complete, and the AI compute satellites are expected to generate substantially more power than V3 satellites. This performance is expected to be achieved primarily through the use of significantly larger solar arrays. These satellites are targeted to generate approximately 100 kW of compute power per ton, which initially will require approximately five times the solar array output compared to V3 satellite designs.
我们 100 吉瓦的年度电力部署目标基于经过推理的工程分析和通过对下一代 AI 算力卫星持续设计和研发工作得出的设计参数。这些分析基于目前可用的太空级太阳能技术，不需要超出现有能力的根本性技术突破。具体而言，我们预计这些卫星将利用已设计完成的 V3 卫星平台。V3 卫星核心设计已完成，AI 算力卫星预计将产生远比 V3 卫星更多的电力。这种性能主要通过使用显著更大的太阳能阵列实现。这些卫星的目标是每吨产生约 100 kW 算力，初期所需的太阳能阵列输出约为 V3 卫星设计的五倍。
We currently do not anticipate material supply constraints for space-based solar panels, as global production capacity, including through our vertical integration efforts, is believed to be sufficient to meet its requirements.
我们目前不预期太空太阳能板出现重大供给约束，因为包括我们垂直整合努力在内的全球产能被认为足以满足其需求。
We are actively developing the manufacturing, launch cadence, and operational capabilities that we believe would be needed to support such launch rates.
我们正在积极开发我们相信支持此类发射速率所需的制造、发射频率和运营能力。
The precise solar collection area, total system mass per satellite, and on-orbit assembly requirements associated with this goal continue to be refined as part of our ongoing engineering efforts. In general, the approach contemplates larger deployable solar arrays on each satellite, with no significant on-orbit assembly currently anticipated.
与此目标相关的精确太阳能采集面积、每颗卫星的总系统质量和在轨组装要求，仍在作为我们持续工程工作的一部分不断细化。总体而言，方案设想是在每颗卫星上配置更大的可展开太阳能阵列，目前预期不进行大量在轨组装。
•We will use our proven Starlink in-orbit technology to optimize our orbital AI compute. In order to operate orbital AI compute satellites, we plan to build on our vast experience of operating approximately 9,600 Starlink broadband and mobile satellites in Low-Earth Orbit. In 2025 alone, Starlink satellites proactively performed over 1,000 automated collision avoidance maneuvers per day guided by this technology to safely and efficiently operate the constellation. This operating model gives us control over workload placement across Earth and space while maintaining resilience through redundancy and fail safe systems. To ensure optimized thermal management and power generation, we will design each satellite's solar arrays to face the sun for constant power while its housing radiator panels face cold deep space for radiative cooling. A high degree of controllability will allow the satellite to be optimized for brightness mitigation, disposal, and other modes of operation. As more advanced AI hardware becomes available, we plan to manage the lifecycle of deployed systems by shifting older hardware to lower intensity workloads as performance characteristics evolve, and retiring systems that are no longer needed through controlled end of life disposition, including transition to graveyard orbits where appropriate. These retirements may occur sooner than our estimates for the useful lives of our satellites, which estimates are based on engineering studies, historical on-orbit performance, propellant life, utilization patterns, design enhancements across generations, and planned transitions to newer satellite technology. Space based compute also introduces orbital debris risk, which we already manage at constellation scale through our autonomous collision avoidance system across Starlink. To date, we have not experienced any failures of our autonomous collision avoidance system that have resulted in satellite loss.
• 我们将利用经过验证的 Starlink 在轨技术来优化轨道 AI 算力。为运营轨道 AI 算力卫星，我们计划基于运营约 9,600 颗近地轨道 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星的丰富经验。仅 2025 年，Starlink 卫星在该技术指引下每日主动执行超过 1,000 次自动碰撞回避机动，安全高效地运营星座。这种运营模式让我们能在地球和太空范围内控制工作负载放置，同时通过冗余和故障保护系统保持韧性。为确保最优的热管理和发电，我们将设计每颗卫星的太阳能阵列朝向太阳以获得恒定电力，而其外壳上的散热板朝向寒冷的深空以进行辐射制冷。高度的可控性允许卫星针对亮度缓解、报废和其他运营模式进行优化。随着更先进的 AI 硬件可用，我们计划通过把较旧硬件转移到强度较低的工作负载（随性能特征演变）来管理已部署系统的生命周期，并通过受控的报废处置（包括在合适情况下迁移到墓地轨道）退役不再需要的系统。这些退役可能早于我们对卫星有用寿命的估计，这些估计基于工程研究、历史在轨表现、推进剂寿命、使用模式、代际设计增强以及向新一代卫星技术的计划过渡。天基算力还引入了轨道碎片风险，我们已经通过 Starlink 的自主碰撞回避系统在星座规模上管理这一风险。截至目前，我们的自主碰撞回避系统没有发生导致卫星损失的失效。
•We can manufacture our AI compute constellations at scale with rapid upgrade cycles. We have built one of the largest satellite manufacturing operations in the world with standardized bus architectures, rapid iteration cycles, and automotive-style production lines, enabling us to evolve bus architecture and subsystem design with limited reliance on third-party suppliers. Our highly vertically integrated approach will be key to our mass- scaling efforts and should allow us to deploy the latest AI processors. Our ability to quickly develop and deploy new generations of AI compute to orbit will be a key advantage in maintaining frontier performance of the constellation. We believe SpaceX will be the first and only company to manufacture satellites at the scale of automotive manufacturing.
• 我们可以规模化制造 AI 算力星座并进行快速升级周期。我们已建立全球最大的卫星制造业务之一，采用标准化卫星平台架构、快速迭代周期和汽车式生产线，使我们能在有限依赖第三方供应商的情况下演进总线架构和子系统设计。我们高度垂直整合的方式将是我们规模化努力的关键，并应让我们能部署最新的 AI 处理器。我们能快速向轨道开发并部署新一代 AI 算力的能力将是维持星座前沿性能的关键优势。我们相信 SpaceX 将是第一家也是唯一一家以汽车制造规模制造卫星的公司。
•We are building chip manufacturing capabilities to scale our access to AI compute hardware. We announced a collaboration with Tesla in March 2026 to build the Terafab initiative with a long-term goal of producing one terawatt of compute hardware each year. Intel joined the project in April 2026 and is expected to contribute its expertise in designing, fabricating, and packaging ultra-high-performance chips to help Terafab scale. In connection with such collaboration, we have agreed with Tesla on a general framework for the future development of Terafab. Any specific projects undertaken pursuant to this framework will be subject to separate negotiations and agreements (including any development timelines, milestones and capital expenditures) and have not yet been determined. Our strategy for Terafab is to vertically integrate across design of lithography masks, fabrication of logic and memory chips, design of advanced packaging and rapidly test and iterate in order to improve chip design and performance. With this internal manufacturing capability, we plan to alleviate potential future chip shortages at SpaceX, especially as we develop orbital AI at scale, and design chips that are optimized for the space environment. We expect that our speed and cost advantage from vertical integration will allow us to scale efficiently in AI chip manufacturing.
• 我们正在构建芯片制造能力以扩大对 AI 算力硬件的获取。我们于 2026 年 3 月宣布与 Tesla 合作启动 Terafab 项目，长期目标是每年生产一太瓦算力硬件。Intel 于 2026 年 4 月加入项目，预计将贡献其在设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片方面的专业能力，帮助 Terafab 扩张。结合此次合作，我们已与 Tesla 就 Terafab 未来发展达成总体框架。根据该框架开展的任何具体项目（包括任何开发时间表、里程碑和资本支出）将受单独谈判和协议约束，目前尚未确定。我们对 Terafab 的策略是在光刻掩膜设计、逻辑与存储芯片制造、先进封装设计方面进行垂直整合，并快速测试和迭代以改进芯片设计和性能。借助这种内部制造能力，我们计划缓解 SpaceX 未来可能的芯片短缺——尤其在我们规模化开发轨道 AI 时——并设计针对太空环境优化的芯片。我们预计垂直整合带来的速度与成本优势将让我们能在 AI 芯片制造上高效扩张。
