For our Starlink Mobile constellation, we currently have approximately 650 existing dedicated mobile satellites. We are developing more comprehensive satellite-to mobile services, which we refer to as our Starlink Mobile Gen2 services, including broadband data and IoT connectivity, which are expected to deliver resilient, infrastructure-independent connectivity worldwide and enable 5G connectivity.
提高星座容量。我们目前由约 9,600 颗 Starlink 宽带和移动卫星组成的星座（其中超过 3,000 颗于 2025 年部署）累计支持超过 700 Tbps 的下行容量。为通过连接板块服务更多客户，我们计划显著提升宽带和移动星座的容量。对 Starlink 宽带星座，我们将继续部署更多 V2 Mini 卫星，并预计在 2026 年下半年开始部署下一代 V3 卫星——每颗卫星设计提供 1 Tbps 的下行容量。我们预计 Starship 每次发射最多能部署 60 颗 V3 卫星，相比 Falcon 9 单次发射部署的下行容量提高二十倍，从而以显著更低成本更快地扩展 Starlink 宽带星座。对 Starlink Mobile 星座，目前我们有约 650 颗专用移动卫星。我们正在开发更全面的卫星直连移动服务——我们称之为 Starlink Mobile Gen2 服务——包括宽带数据和物联网连接，预计将在全球范围内提供有韧性、独立于地面基础设施的连接，并支持 5G 连接。
We plan to expand our mobile constellation by deploying our next-generation mobile V2 Mobile satellites in 2027 which, combined with the EchoStar spectrum acquisition and optimized 5G protocols, are expected to increase capacity by orders of magnitude compared to our first-generation constellation. In the U.S., the FCC approved the EchoStar license transfer in May 2026, and we separately expect to receive the remaining necessary U.S. regulatory authorizations in the second or third quarter of 2026. While these authorizations would be sufficient from a U.S.
我们计划于 2027 年部署下一代移动 V2 Mobile 卫星，这与 EchoStar 频谱收购和经过优化的 5G 协议相结合，预计相比第一代星座将带来数量级的容量提升。在美国，FCC 已于 2026 年 5 月批准了 EchoStar 许可证转让，我们预计将在 2026 年第二或第三季度获得剩余必要的美国监管授权。虽然这些授权在美国
regulatory perspective, we still require our V2 Mobile satellites to be in orbit and must complete the acquisition of the relevant spectrum from EchoStar before we can commence our planned commercial Gen2 service in the United States. Internationally, we have filed applications in nearly every country in which we intend to operate our Gen2 service, and approvals have been granted in a limited number of these jurisdictions to date. Each international jurisdiction presents its own regulatory process and timeline, and we cannot predict when or whether approvals will be granted in any given market. In addition, our Gen2 service is subject to ITU coordination requirements. We have an operational coordination agreement with EchoStar, which we expect to continue through 2026 and 2027, under which EchoStar has agreed to protect our lower-priority S-band V2 Mobile constellation. By prioritizing these step- change capacity increases in our satellite-to-mobile capabilities, we expect to both enhance high-speed, low-latency service quality in existing markets and provide services to previously capacity-limited and unserved regions, including dense urban areas and emerging markets.
监管角度已足够，但我们仍需要 V2 Mobile 卫星进入轨道，并必须完成从 EchoStar 收购相关频谱，才能在美国启动我们计划的商业 Gen2 服务。在国际层面，我们已在几乎每个我们打算运营 Gen2 服务的国家提交申请，但截至目前仅在有限数量的司法辖区获得了批准。每个国际辖区都有其自身的监管流程和时间表，我们无法预测在任何给定市场何时或是否会获得批准。此外，我们的 Gen2 服务受到 ITU 协调要求约束。我们与 EchoStar 有一份运营协调协议——我们预计该协议将延续到 2026 和 2027 年——根据该协议 EchoStar 同意保护我们较低优先级的 S 频段 V2 Mobile 星座。通过优先实施这些卫星直连移动能力上的跃迁式容量提升，我们预计既能在现有市场提升高速、低延迟服务质量，也能为此前因容量受限或未被服务的区域（包括密集城市区域和新兴市场）提供服务。
AI Grow consumer AI platform monetization. We plan to continue to grow revenue from our AI platform, the Grok application, by increasing monetization of our existing user base. We will leverage our unique combination of real‑time data, large‑scale distribution, leading foundational model, and hardware expertise to increase the number of Grok subscribers. Subscribers benefit from enhanced functionality, exclusive features, and access to our latest AI models. Since the introduction of our Grok subscription offering in 2025, we have increased the number of available features to add value to our subscribers, including providing access to our latest and enhanced AI tools. We plan to continue adding new features and functionality while releasing increasingly capable Grok models to increase the penetration rate of our subscriber base. Our AI segment has demonstrated exceptional model velocity: since launching Grok, we have developed leading frontier models at a far faster rate of innovation than others. We continue to invest in scaling Grok through subsequent generations, including Grok 5. Our roadmap for future models contains multi-trillion parameter models, which could represent a step change in reasoning depth and overall intelligence. We believe this pace of innovation strengthens the value proposition of our subscription offerings and supports long term subscriber growth. While our subscriber growth has been strong, we believe we are still early in increasing paid penetration across our Grok user base. We further believe there might be an incremental monetization opportunity by introducing advertising into our stand-alone Grok offering.
AI 增长消费者 AI 平台变现。我们计划通过提升对现有用户基础的变现，继续增长 AI 平台（Grok 应用）的收入。我们将利用实时数据、大规模分发、领先的基础模型和硬件专长这一独特组合，增加 Grok 订阅者数量。订阅者可享有增强的功能、独家特性以及最新 AI 模型的访问权。自 2025 年推出 Grok 订阅产品以来，我们已增加可用功能以为订阅者增加价值，包括提供最新和增强的 AI 工具访问权。我们计划继续增加新功能与特性，同时发布能力越来越强的 Grok 模型，提高订阅者基础的付费渗透率。我们的 AI 板块展现出卓越的模型迭代速度：自推出 Grok 以来，我们以远高于其他人的速度开发领先的前沿模型。我们继续投资把 Grok 通过后续几代扩展，包括 Grok 5。我们对未来模型的路线图包含数万亿参数模型，这可能代表推理深度和整体智能的跃迁。我们相信这种创新速度强化了订阅产品的价值主张，支撑长期订阅者增长。虽然订阅者增长强劲，但我们相信在提升 Grok 用户基础付费渗透率方面仍处早期。我们进一步相信，把广告引入独立的 Grok 产品可能存在增量变现机会。
Grow X monetization. We intend to drive X revenue growth by increasing engagement across our users, increasing X Premium subscriber conversion, growing advertising revenue per user, and diversifying our advertising base. We continue to evolve X into an "Everything App," integrating real-time information, communications, media, payments, banking, and more within one consumer app experience. This can improve the usefulness of X, and therefore increase the usage and monetization potential of X. We have demonstrated rapid product launch velocity, with frequent features and products launched since 2023, including Grok integration, long‑form video, audio and video calling, secure messaging, tool calling, long-form articles, and creator tools. We plan to further broaden the value proposition of X through offerings like Money, a product we launched in beta in November 2025, which aims to expand platform utility by enabling payments and other financial services. We updated X chat in 2025, featuring end-to-end encryption and no connection to our ad personalization, unlike other messaging services. We intend to further embed Grok throughout X to enhance discovery, analysis of posts, user support, and personalization, increasing the usefulness of X and further improving the value of a paid subscription.
增长 X 的变现。我们打算通过提升用户参与度、增加 X Premium 订阅者转化、提升每用户广告收入以及多样化广告主基础来推动 X 收入增长。我们继续把 X 演化为「万事 App（Everything App）」，在一个消费 App 体验内整合实时信息、通信、媒体、支付、银行等。这可以提升 X 的有用性，从而提高 X 的使用量和变现潜力。我们已展示出快速的产品推出速度——自 2023 年以来频繁推出功能和产品，包括 Grok 集成、长视频、音频和视频通话、安全消息、工具调用、长文章和创作者工具。我们计划通过 Money 等产品进一步扩展 X 的价值主张——Money 是我们 2025 年 11 月推出 beta 的产品，旨在通过支持支付和其他金融服务扩展平台效用。2025 年我们升级了 X chat，特点是端到端加密，且不与我们的广告个性化相连——这与其他消息服务不同。我们打算把 Grok 进一步嵌入到 X 中，以增强发现、帖子分析、用户支持和个性化，提升 X 的有用性并进一步改善付费订阅的价值。
We also expect to grow advertising revenue per user and to diversify our advertiser base over time because of X's compelling advertiser value proposition--large-scale user engagement, real-time content, and advanced AI-driven performance marketing tools. We intend to drive further advertising revenue growth by improving our performance advertising capabilities, embedding AI to optimize ad campaigns, and launching richer ad formats, including those that increase advertiser return on ad spending and their spend with us. In determining our advertising rates, we use an auction process in which advertisers bid to have their ads shown to the audience they are targeting, except for certain reserved inventory, which is sold on a fixed price basis. We provide advertisers with several engagement metrics, including: the number of impressions, price per ad, clicks, and conversions. Currently, Grok API access is not included in our advertising rates to advertisers. We do not currently sell or offer advertisers the ability to place ads on the Grok API.
我们也预计提升每用户广告收入并随着时间推移多样化广告主基础——这得益于 X 引人注目的广告主价值主张：大规模用户互动、实时内容和先进的 AI 驱动效果营销工具。我们打算通过改善效果广告能力、嵌入 AI 优化广告活动以及推出更丰富的广告形式（包括提升广告主广告支出回报和在我们这里支出的形式），进一步推动广告收入增长。在决定广告费率时，我们使用一种竞价流程，广告主竞价让其广告展示给目标受众——某些预留库存除外，按固定价格出售。我们向广告主提供多个互动指标，包括：展示次数、单次广告价格、点击量和转化量。当前 Grok API 访问不包含在我们对广告主的广告费率中。我们目前不向广告主销售或提供在 Grok API 上投放广告的能力。
We also expect X's real-time content stream and engagement feedback, subject to some limitations for certain content, to strengthen our advertising product performance and relevance, improving outcomes for both consumers and advertisers, and increasing retention. We also began a phased roll-out of our new advertising platform, including the new X Ads Manager, in April 2026. X Ads Manager is designed to help advertisers launch better campaigns faster, with AI-powered systems enabling more precise, relevant, and dynamic ad delivery and a centralized workflow for campaign creation, optimization, and real-time monitoring. Grok supports this strategy by helping advertisers with campaign creation, creative optimization, and alignment with trending topics and user intent.
我们也预计 X 的实时内容流和互动反馈（某些内容受一定限制）会增强广告产品性能与相关性，改善消费者和广告主的成效，提升留存。我们也在 2026 年 4 月开始分阶段推出新广告平台，包括新版 X Ads Manager。X Ads Manager 旨在帮助广告主更快推出更好的广告活动，通过 AI 驱动系统实现更精准、相关、动态的广告投放，并为广告活动创建、优化和实时监控提供集中化工作流。Grok 通过帮助广告主进行活动创建、创意优化以及与热门话题和用户意图对齐来支持这一策略。
Deepen enterprise and government adoption. We believe adoption of AI by both enterprise and government reflects a structural industry shift, with room for substantial long-term growth. Our Grok Business, Grok Enterprise, and xAI Gov offerings position us to scale in tandem with broader enterprise and governmental AI adoption. Our Grok API further extends our reach by enabling developers to integrate our models directly into their applications and workflows. We intend to further support our enterprise offerings with a specialized salesforce and forward deployed engineers, engineers who embed directly with a client to implement our solution, to support customer acquisition and expansion.
深化企业和政府采用。我们相信企业和政府对 AI 的采用反映了行业的结构性转变，长期有显著增长空间。我们的 Grok Business、Grok Enterprise 和 xAI Gov 产品让我们能够随更广泛的企业和政府 AI 采用并行扩展。Grok API 通过让开发者把模型直接集成进他们的应用和工作流，进一步扩大了我们的影响。我们打算通过专门的销售团队和前置部署工程师（直接驻扎客户处实施方案的工程师）进一步支持企业产品，支持客户获取和扩展。
Increase the scale of our terrestrial power and AI compute infrastructure. We plan to rapidly scale our terrestrial AI compute infrastructure through the continued deployment of large-scale clusters to support the training and inference of our AI models. To rapidly bring gigawatt-scale data centers online, we leverage world-class engineering, first-principles thinking and deep "shovels-to-tokens" vertical integration. Our AI compute facilities, COLOSSUS and COLOSSUS II, collectively provide approximately 1.0 gigawatt of compute power, with additional power capacity available for data center operations. COLOSSUS II will also provide the compute to train our next- generation Grok 5 AI model. We expect that once fully operational, the next phase of expansion at COLOSSUS II will represent an additional 400MW of compute capacity. Our first-principles thinking enables us to build coherent compute at scale and at rapid speed with lower costs than most other companies in the industry. We brought the first cluster of COLOSSUS online in 122 days, repurposing the shell of an existing factory, and the first cluster of COLOSSUS II online even faster in 91 days. As an illustrative comparison, an industry benchmark to bring online a 100 megawatt greenfield data center is approximately two years. We also demonstrated a significant improvement in cost efficiency, achieving data center construction costs for COLOSSUS II that are considerably lower than industry benchmarks on a per megawatt basis. As AI workloads increase in complexity and scale, data center operators face constraints related to power density, cooling, network bandwidth, supply chain management, construction expertise and capital deployment. Our experience in designing mission-critical hardware systems, optimizing power efficiency, and operating distributed infrastructure networks provides a differentiated foundation for continuing to grow and advance the next-generation compute platform. We believe that continued investment in our compute infrastructure is critical to supporting long-term consumer and enterprise growth as AI adoption accelerates, while also providing a powerful foundation for our transition to orbital AI compute at scale.
提高地面电力和 AI 算力基础设施的规模。我们计划通过持续部署大规模集群以支持 AI 模型的训练和推理，快速扩张地面 AI 算力基础设施。为快速让吉瓦级数据中心上线，我们运用世界级工程、第一性原理思维和「铲子到 tokens」的深度垂直整合。我们的 AI 算力设施 COLOSSUS 和 COLOSSUS II 合计提供约 1.0 吉瓦算力，另有可用于数据中心运营的额外电力容量。COLOSSUS II 还将提供训练我们下一代 Grok 5 AI 模型的算力。我们预计 COLOSSUS II 的下一阶段扩张一旦完全投产，将代表额外 400 MW 的算力容量。我们的第一性原理思维让我们能以低于行业大多数公司的成本快速地大规模构建协同算力。我们用 122 天就让 COLOSSUS 的首个集群上线，改造了一座现有工厂的外壳；COLOSSUS II 首个集群上线则更快，仅 91 天。作为参照，行业基准下让一座 100 兆瓦的全新数据中心上线约需两年。我们在成本效率上也展现了显著进步，COLOSSUS II 的数据中心建设成本按每兆瓦计算大幅低于行业基准。随着 AI 工作负载在复杂度和规模上不断增加，数据中心运营方面临电力密度、制冷、网络带宽、供应链管理、建设专长和资本部署相关约束。我们在设计关键任务硬件系统、优化电力效率和运营分布式基础设施网络方面的经验，为持续发展并推进下一代算力平台提供了差异化基础。我们相信，对算力基础设施的持续投资对于在 AI 采用加速时支撑长期消费者和企业增长至关重要，同时也为我们大规模过渡到轨道 AI 算力提供了强大基础。
In addition, our leadership in compute infrastructure positions us to monetize not only AI software applications built on our models, but also the underlying compute that powers them. As we continue to scale our terrestrial compute infrastructure to support internal model development, training, and inference workloads, we intend to sell our high- performance compute capacity to a limited number of third party customers.
此外，我们在算力基础设施上的领先地位让我们不仅能对基于我们模型的 AI 软件应用进行变现，也能对驱动这些应用的底层算力进行变现。在我们持续扩展地面算力基础设施以支持内部模型开发、训练和推理工作负载的同时，我们打算向有限数量的第三方客户出售高性能算力容量。
Deploy orbital AI compute at scale. We believe growth of the projected $26.5 trillion-dollar AI market will be constrained by Earth's inability to rapidly scale power generation, underscoring the challenge of achieving terawatt- scale compute without harming people and the environment. While we expect terrestrial power generation to continue to grow, we believe the physical, environmental, and regulatory constraints will prevent it from delivering the orders-of-magnitude increases needed to match future energy demands of the AI era. Power from the Sun, an enormous, free fusion reactor in the sky, represents approximately 99.8% of the solar system's energy and offers the only truly scalable solution to terrestrial energy constraints. By combining virtually unlimited solar power in space with our industry-leading launch costs and satellite manufacturing capabilities, we believe we can deliver compute over time at a fundamentally lower cost structure than is possible on Earth. By the end of the decade, we intend to deploy the first modular orbital AI compute shells and begin monetizing capacity through the sale of AI software and AI compute. We aim to launch 100 gigawatts of AI compute capacity on solar-powered satellites each year, equivalent to roughly one fifth of total annual U.S. power production in 2025. The amount of compute capacity we can launch depends on three components--payload, satellite capacity, and launch frequency. With respect to payload, Starship V3 is designed to deliver 100 metric tons to space in a fully reusable configuration while enabling rapid turnaround times, and future generations could reach 200 metric tons, potentially as soon as Starship V4. With respect to satellite capacity, we expect solar cells optimized for the space environment will be produced at a rapid rate, with early satellites generating 100 kilowatts of compute power and scaling from there. Finally, with respect to launch frequency, we expect to be able to scale to thousands of launches per year. Together, we expect these achievements will allow us to transport approximately one million metric tons to orbit annually, powering 100 gigawatts of AI compute. Such compute capacity will also play a critical role in advancing our human augmentation vision by expanding the reach, speed, and capability of AI beyond what is possible with terrestrial compute infrastructure alone.
大规模部署轨道 AI 算力。我们相信，预计 26.5 万亿美元 AI 市场的增长将受限于地球无法快速扩张电力生产——这凸显了在不伤害人和环境的前提下实现太瓦级算力的挑战。虽然我们预计地面发电将持续增长，但我们相信物理、环境和监管约束将阻止其交付出匹配 AI 时代未来能源需求所需的数量级提升。来自太阳——空中一个巨大、免费的聚变反应堆——的电力，约占太阳系能量的 99.8%，提供了对地面能源约束唯一真正可扩展的解决方案。通过把太空中近乎无限的太阳能与行业领先的发射成本和卫星制造能力相结合，我们相信我们能随时间推移以比地球上根本性更低的成本结构交付算力。在本十年结束前，我们打算部署首批模块化的轨道 AI 算力外壳，并开始通过 AI 软件和 AI 算力销售对容量进行变现。我们的目标是每年在太阳能驱动卫星上发射 100 吉瓦的 AI 算力容量——相当于美国 2025 年总年发电量的约五分之一。我们能发射的算力容量取决于三个因素——有效载荷、卫星容量和发射频率。在有效载荷方面，Starship V3 被设计为在完全可重复使用配置下向太空投送 100 公吨，并实现快速周转，未来几代——可能最早在 Starship V4 上——可达 200 公吨。在卫星容量方面，我们预计针对太空环境优化的太阳能电池将以高速率生产，早期卫星可产生 100 千瓦算力并由此扩展。最后在发射频率方面，我们预计能扩展到每年数千次发射。综合起来，我们预计这些成就将让我们每年向轨道投送约 100 万公吨，驱动 100 吉瓦 AI 算力。这种算力容量也将通过超越仅靠地面算力基础设施所能达到的范围、速度和能力，在推进我们的人类增强愿景中发挥关键作用。
We believe we are well-positioned to execute and deliver orbital AI compute to build the infrastructure of the future.
我们相信我们处于执行并交付轨道 AI 算力、构建未来基础设施的有利位置。
We believe orbital AI compute is an incredibly difficult challenge that only we can solve at scale in the near term.
我们相信轨道 AI 算力是一个极难的挑战，短期内只有我们能在规模上解决。
Design and manufacture our own chips. We plan to deepen our strategic collaboration with Tesla and Intel through Terafab. In connection with such collaboration, we have agreed with Tesla on a general framework for the future development of Terafab. Any specific projects undertaken pursuant to this framework will be subject to separate negotiations and agreements (including any development timelines, milestones and capital expenditures) and have not yet been determined. We expect Terafab to be the world's largest chip manufacturing facility, with the goal of eventually achieving one terawatt of annual compute production capacity. While Terafab is intended to expand our internal chip manufacturing capabilities, we expect to continue sourcing a significant portion of our compute hardware from third-party suppliers. We view Terafab as complementary to these relationships, enabling us to augment our access to compute hardware at massive scale and further complete our highly vertically integrated compute platform by extending our control to the foundational chip layer. By developing end-to-end capabilities spanning the design of lithography masks, fabrication of logic and memory chips, and advanced packaging, all in a vertically integrated closed-loop single plant, we will be able to more rapidly iterate to improve chip design and performance. We plan to design chips that are optimized for the space environment. This collaboration directly enables our planned orders-of-magnitude increases in AI compute deployment in orbit which would be constrained by pure reliance on external foundries. Leveraging shared engineering resources, intellectual property, and infrastructure across Tesla and SpaceX, as well as Intel's expertise in designing, fabricating and packaging ultra- high-performance chips at scale, Terafab is designed to create powerful ecosystem synergies that accelerate innovation cycles and reduce costs. Just as we manufacture approximately 80% of Starship in-house, we expect significant speed and cost advantages from Terafab's vertical integration. We believe this integration, if achieved, will provide us with a critical competitive advantage in the race to scale AI infrastructure, especially as we begin our orbital AI compute satellite deployments.
设计并制造我们自己的芯片。我们计划通过 Terafab 深化与 Tesla 和 Intel 的战略合作。结合此次合作，我们已与 Tesla 就 Terafab 未来发展达成总体框架。根据该框架开展的任何具体项目（包括任何开发时间表、里程碑和资本支出）将受单独谈判和协议约束，目前尚未确定。我们预计 Terafab 将成为全球最大的芯片制造设施，目标是最终达到每年一太瓦的算力产能。虽然 Terafab 旨在扩展我们内部的芯片制造能力，但我们预计将继续从第三方供应商采购相当一部分算力硬件。我们认为 Terafab 与这些关系是互补的，让我们能在巨大规模上增强对算力硬件的获取，并通过把控制延伸至底层芯片层进一步完善高度垂直整合的算力平台。通过在单一垂直整合的闭环工厂中开发跨光刻掩膜设计、逻辑与存储芯片制造及先进封装的端到端能力，我们将能更快迭代以改进芯片设计和性能。我们计划设计针对太空环境优化的芯片。这一合作直接支持了我们计划中的轨道 AI 算力部署的数量级提升——若仅依赖外部代工厂将受到约束。借助 Tesla 与 SpaceX 之间共享的工程资源、知识产权和基础设施，以及 Intel 在大规模设计、制造和封装超高性能芯片方面的专长，Terafab 旨在创造强大的生态协同效应，加速创新周期并降低成本。正如我们约 80% 的 Starship 在内部制造，我们预计 Terafab 的垂直整合将带来显著的速度与成本优势。我们相信若实现这种整合，将在扩展 AI 基础设施的竞赛中——尤其在我们开始轨道 AI 算力卫星部署时——给我们带来关键的竞争优势。
Launch digital human augmentation. In partnership with Tesla, we are developing Macrohard, an agentic platform designed to fully emulate digital workflows and augment human operation of computers--from coding and product development to management and entire business processes. Similar to how autonomous systems emulate human inputs to execute complex tasks, Macrohard is designed to augment how humans operate computers and tools to analyze, create, and manage workflows. Unlike other enterprise software and AI applications that primarily digitize workflows and systematize historical processes, our solutions are designed to operate as real-time, intelligence- driven extensions of the user. Macrohard aims to combine our frontier AI model with Tesla's physical AI prowess to achieve the goal of augmenting the operational functions of entire companies. We expect Macrohard to benefit from running on both state-of-the-art processors and cost efficient Tesla processors, a critical advantage of our vertical integration. We believe Macrohard has the potential to fundamentally transform how companies across all industries are structured and operate, thereby allowing dramatic increases in human productivity and prosperity.
推出数字人类增强。与 Tesla 合作，我们正在开发 Macrohard——一个 agentic 平台，旨在完全模拟数字工作流并增强人类对计算机的操作——从编程和产品开发到管理乃至整个业务流程。类似自主系统模拟人类输入来执行复杂任务，Macrohard 被设计为增强人类操作计算机和工具进行分析、创造和管理工作流的方式。与主要把工作流数字化、将历史流程系统化的其他企业软件和 AI 应用不同，我们的方案被设计为作为用户的实时、智能驱动延伸而运行。Macrohard 旨在把我们的前沿 AI 模型与 Tesla 的物理 AI 实力结合，达成增强整个公司运营职能的目标。我们预计 Macrohard 将受益于在最先进处理器和成本高效的 Tesla 处理器上运行——这是我们垂直整合的关键优势。我们相信 Macrohard 有潜力从根本上改变各行业公司组织和运作的方式，从而显著提升人类生产力和繁荣。
Future Markets We aim to build the infrastructure of the future in Space, leveraging our foundational competitive advantage, the ability to launch mass at scale. By opening access to space to industries on Earth, we can grow our business by creating new markets. Our technological capabilities enable us to repeatedly create new markets by pushing the boundaries of what space can support. As we continue to advance and scale, we expect to unlock new market opportunities. Over the long-term, we expect our Starship-enabled opportunities to include:
未来市场 我们的目标是利用我们的基础竞争优势——大规模发射质量的能力——在太空中构建未来基础设施。通过为地球产业打开太空进入通道，我们可以通过创造新市场来发展业务。我们的技术能力让我们能通过拓展太空所能支持的边界反复创造新市场。随着我们继续推进和扩张，我们预计将解锁新的市场机会。从长远看，我们预计 Starship 支持的机会包括：
•Point-to-point terrestrial travel. We plan to develop ultra-fast long-haul point-to-point Earth transport using Starship, enabling passengers and cargo to travel between major cities in a fraction of current transit times, revolutionizing global logistics and passenger travel with unprecedented speed and efficiency.
• 点对点地面运输。我们计划使用 Starship 开发超快速的长程点对点地球运输，使乘客和货物能以当前运输时间的一小部分在主要城市之间穿行——以前所未有的速度和效率革新全球物流和客运。
•Space tourism. With meaningful advances in space technology and the continued build-out of orbital flight infrastructure, we expect increasing interest in human space travel as it becomes easier and more common to access space.
• 太空旅游。随着太空技术的显著进步和轨道飞行基础设施的持续建设，我们预计随着进入太空变得更容易、更常见，对载人太空旅行的兴趣将增加。
•In-orbit manufacturing. We aim to establish in-space manufacturing facilities that leverage the unique microgravity conditions of space to produce materials, pharmaceuticals, and advanced components that are difficult or impossible to manufacture on Earth, opening new high-value industrial markets.
• 在轨制造。我们旨在建立利用太空独特微重力条件的在太空制造设施，生产在地球上难以或无法制造的材料、药品和先进组件，打开新的高价值工业市场。
•Passenger and cargo transport to the Moon and Mars. We intend to support large-scale passenger and cargo missions to the Moon and Mars, delivering the people, equipment, and supplies needed to establish permanent human settlements and accelerate the path to becoming a self-sustaining multiplanetary civilization.
• 月球和火星的载人与货运运输。我们打算支持大规模的月球和火星载人与货运任务，运送建立永久人类定居点所需的人员、设备和物资，加速成为可自给自足的跨行星文明的进程。
•Energy production on the Moon and Mars. We aim to develop large-scale solar energy production on the Moon and Mars, taking advantage of the thin atmosphere and constant solar exposure to generate power for manufacturing, habitats, and future infrastructure at scale.
• 月球和火星上的能源生产。我们旨在在月球和火星上发展大规模太阳能能源生产，利用稀薄大气和恒定太阳曝光，为制造、栖息地和未来基础设施大规模发电。
•Manufacturing capabilities on the Moon and Mars. We plan to build manufacturing infrastructure on the Moon and Mars that utilizes local resources to produce fuel, construction materials, and other essential resources, reducing dependence on Earth resupply and enabling sustainable long-term presence.
• 月球和火星上的制造能力。我们计划在月球和火星上建造制造基础设施，利用本地资源生产燃料、建造材料和其他基本资源，减少对地球补给的依赖并支持可持续的长期存在。
•Asteroid mining. We plan to pursue asteroid mining operations to extract metals and other critical resources from near-Earth and main-belt asteroids, providing abundant raw materials for space-based industries and reducing the need to launch mass from Earth.
• 小行星采矿。我们计划开展小行星采矿运营，从近地和主带小行星中提取金属和其他关键资源，为太空产业提供丰富原材料并减少从地球发射质量的需要。
Our Market Opportunity We believe space represents the largest economic frontier in human history. Our innovations and technological advancements are redefining existing industries and creating new market opportunities across Space, Connectivity and AI. We believe we have a distinct ability to identify, develop, and commercialize new multi-trillion-dollar markets that did not previously exist. We currently stand alone in our ability to deliver revolutionary breakthroughs across spaceflight and exploration, global connectivity, and artificial intelligence, enabling an age of abundance that we believe has the potential to propel an unprecedented expansion in the global economy.
我们的市场机会 我们相信太空是人类历史上最大的经济前沿。我们的创新和技术进步正在重新定义现有产业，并在太空、连接和 AI 领域创造新的市场机会。我们相信我们有独特的能力来识别、开发并商业化此前不存在的、新的多万亿美元市场。我们目前在跨太空飞行与探索、全球连接和人工智能交付革命性突破方面独树一帜，开启了我们相信有潜力推动全球经济前所未有扩张的富足时代。
By pioneering the world's first and only fleet of reusable rockets at scale, we revolutionized space access through dramatically lower cost and unmatched reliability. Lowering costs by orders of magnitude creates entirely new industries on Earth and in space that were technologically and economically infeasible for others to access historically. Our first trillion-dollar market was Starlink, a satellite service supported by our low-latency, high-speed LEO constellation that required the rapid, low-cost deployment of millions of kilograms of hardware into orbit. Our Starlink constellation powers a global connectivity platform capable of supporting broadband and mobile services, enabling high-speed internet access to homes, enterprises, governments, and mobile users across virtually any location on Earth. We believe our next trillion-dollar market is AI compute, which we contemplate will leverage our rockets and satellites for massive orbital deployment.
通过开创全球首支也是唯一一支大规模可重复使用火箭舰队，我们以大幅降低的成本和无可匹敌的可靠性革新了太空进入。把成本降低数量级在地球和太空中创造出此前在技术和经济上对其他人不可行的新产业。我们的第一个万亿美元市场是 Starlink——一项由低延迟、高速 LEO 星座支撑的卫星服务，它需要把数百万公斤硬件快速、低成本地部署到轨道。我们的 Starlink 星座驱动着一个能支持宽带和移动服务的全球连接平台，使家庭、企业、政府和移动用户在地球上几乎任何位置都能享有高速互联网接入。我们相信下一个万亿美元市场是 AI 算力——我们设想将利用火箭和卫星进行大规模轨道部署。
We believe we have identified the largest TAM in human history. We estimate that our quantifiable TAM is $28.5 trillion, consisting of $370 billion in Space from space-enabled solutions; $1.6 trillion in Connectivity across $870 billion in Starlink Broadband and $740 billion in Starlink Mobile as well as additional opportunities in enterprise and government; $26.5 trillion in AI across $2.4 trillion in AI infrastructure, $760 billion in consumer subscriptions, $600 billion in digital advertising, and $22.7 trillion in enterprise applications. For illustrative purposes of sizing our addressable market opportunity, we exclude China and Russia from our global estimates.
我们相信我们已识别出人类历史上最大的 TAM。我们估计可量化的 TAM 为 28.5 万亿美元，其中包括：太空领域来自天基方案的 3,700 亿美元；连接领域 1.6 万亿美元（其中 Starlink Broadband 8,700 亿美元、Starlink Mobile 7,400 亿美元，外加企业和政府方面的额外机会）；AI 领域 26.5 万亿美元（其中 AI 基础设施 2.4 万亿美元、消费者订阅 7,600 亿美元、数字广告 6,000 亿美元、企业应用 22.7 万亿美元）。出于衡量可触达市场机会的演示目的，我们的全球估计中排除了中国和俄罗斯。
In addition to the markets we serve today, we believe we are poised to catalyze transformative breakthroughs and create entirely new markets. Given these are longer-term opportunities at earlier stages of development, we do not quantify them in our TAM estimates; however, we believe that over time each of these markets could eventually represent multi-trillion-dollar economic opportunities. These new markets include long haul point-to-point terrestrial travel, space tourism, in-orbit manufacturing, asteroid mining, energy production and manufacturing on the Moon and Mars, and passenger and cargo transportation to the Moon and Mars.
除了今天服务的市场之外，我们相信我们有望催化变革性突破并创造全新的市场。鉴于这些是发展较早期阶段的长期机会，我们未在 TAM 估计中量化它们；不过我们相信随着时间推移，每一个市场都可能最终代表多万亿美元的经济机会。这些新市场包括长程点对点地面运输、太空旅游、在轨制造、小行星采矿、月球和火星上的能源生产与制造，以及月球和火星的载人与货运运输。
SpaceX's Estimated TAM by Segment Space While the size of the space market is massive for any company to address, our capabilities in space represent a foundational competitive advantage that allow us to address markets that represent significant portions of global gross domestic product ("GDP")--connectivity and AI. We estimate a total market opportunity of $370 billion across space-enabled solutions, with the lunar economy presenting a significant upside not included in the estimate.
SpaceX 按板块的预估 TAM 太空 虽然太空市场对任何公司而言规模都很大，但我们在太空的能力代表了一种基础竞争优势，让我们能切入构成全球 GDP 重要部分的市场——连接和 AI。我们估计天基方案的总市场机会为 3,700 亿美元，月球经济代表了未计入此估计的重大上行空间。
Space-Enabled Solutions. According to Novaspace, space-enabled solutions represented a $370 billion market in 2025, including spacecraft manufacturing, launch services, satellite operations, positioning, navigation and timing ("PNT") devices and value-added services, as well as uncontracted costs of government space agencies. Both commercial and government customers participate in this market, with growing space-based defense budgets reflecting prioritization of security, resilience, and strategic autonomy by governments globally. For the purpose of sizing our TAM, we exclude the value of satellite communications services, as we include those within our Connectivity segment.
天基方案。根据 Novaspace，天基方案在 2025 年代表 3,700 亿美元的市场，包括航天器制造、发射服务、卫星运营、定位、导航和授时 (PNT) 设备及增值服务，以及政府太空机构未签合同的成本。商业和政府客户均参与该市场，天基国防预算的增长反映了全球政府对安全、韧性和战略自主的优先排序。出于衡量 TAM 的目的，我们排除了卫星通信服务的价值，因为我们已将其归入连接板块。
Lunar Economy. We believe the development of a sustained human and commercial presence on the Moon has the potential to give rise to a new lunar economy encompassing transportation, infrastructure, communications, energy, manufacturing (including the production of satellites and advanced chips), resource extraction, and scientific and commercial activity. Early demand is already emerging from government space agencies and research institutions, and we expect this to expand over time to include commercial enterprises seeking to leverage the Moon as a platform for logistics, industrial activity, and deep-space exploration. Establishing a lunar economy requires first proving reliable extraction of water ice to sustain life and producing hydrogen-oxygen propellant, alongside building power, transport, and storage infrastructure in an extreme, high-cost environment. If achieved, we believe these same resources and the Moon's low gravity unlock the potential for scalable growth through an efficient fuel production and refueling hub, creating a strategic access point that can potentially support deeper space industrialization and serve as a stepping stone to establishing a civilization on Mars. Although we believe the potential size and scope of the lunar economy is extraordinarily large, we are not providing an estimate of the TAM for this opportunity at this time because expectations regarding the timing, pace of adoption, regulatory frameworks, and ultimate scope of commercial activity beyond Earth are rapidly evolving alongside the development and deployment of the technology necessary to establish a lunar presence (such as Starship). As the Moon transitions from a scientific outpost into an industrial frontier, SpaceX is positioned to spearhead this revolutionary expansion, and we believe that continued advancements in our launch capabilities, space infrastructure capabilities, and cost efficiency will allow us to meaningfully accelerate the development of a sustainable lunar economy.
月球经济。我们相信，建立持续的人类与商业月球存在有潜力催生一个全新的月球经济，涵盖运输、基础设施、通信、能源、制造（包括卫星和先进芯片生产）、资源开采，以及科研和商业活动。早期需求已从政府太空机构和研究机构出现，并预计随着时间扩展到寻求将月球作为物流、工业活动和深空探索平台的商业企业。建立月球经济首先需要证明能可靠提取水冰以维持生命并生产氢氧推进剂，同时在极端、高成本环境中构建电力、运输和储存基础设施。如果实现，我们相信这些资源加上月球的低重力，将通过高效的燃料生产和加注枢纽释放可扩展增长潜力，创造一个战略性接入点，可能支撑更深远的太空工业化，并作为建立火星文明的踏脚石。尽管我们相信月球经济的潜在规模和范围极其庞大，但我们目前并未为此机会提供 TAM 估计——因为关于地球以外商业活动的时间点、采用节奏、监管框架和最终范围的预期，正随着建立月球存在所需技术（如 Starship）的研发和部署而快速演变。随着月球从科研前哨过渡为工业前沿，SpaceX 处于引领这一革命性扩张的位置；我们相信发射能力、太空基础设施能力和成本效率的持续进步，将让我们能显著加速可持续月球经济的发展。
Connectivity We believe the global connectivity market represents a substantial and durable opportunity, driven by the increasing reliance of consumers, enterprises, and governments on high-speed, low-latency, reliable connectivity across both terrestrial and remote environments. Across Starlink Broadband and Starlink Mobile, we estimate a total market opportunity of $1.6 trillion reflecting primarily consumer use cases. We believe these traditional use cases, however, do not account for the long-term market opportunity, as connectivity is evolving into a critical infrastructure layer underpinning the global economy, enabling entirely new categories of demand. As high-performance, ubiquitous connectivity becomes embedded across transportation networks, autonomous systems, and smart devices, we expect the scope of the market to extend well beyond the traditional definitions.
连接 我们相信全球连接市场代表了一个庞大而持久的机会——由消费者、企业和政府日益依赖高速、低延迟、可靠连接（无论是地面还是偏远环境）所驱动。在 Starlink Broadband 和 Starlink Mobile 两项加总下，我们估计总市场机会为 1.6 万亿美元，主要反映消费者用例。但我们相信，这些传统用例并未涵盖长期市场机会——连接正在演化为支撑全球经济的关键基础设施层，并促成全新的需求类别。随着高性能、无处不在的连接被嵌入运输网络、自主系统和智能设备中，我们预计市场的范围将远超传统定义。
Starlink Broadband. The global demand for ubiquitous, high-speed broadband internet creates an approximately $870 billion dollar opportunity. Our satellite broadband service, Starlink, is positioned to capture value across multiple massive and rapidly expanding markets:
Starlink Broadband。对无处不在、高速宽带互联网的全球需求形成了约 8,700 亿美元的机会。我们的卫星宽带服务 Starlink 处于在多个庞大且快速扩张的市场中捕获价值的有利位置：
•Consumer Broadband. As the digital economy continues to expand, ubiquitous, high-speed, reliable internet has become a structural necessity for households worldwide--powering opportunity and the next wave of global prosperity. According to Euromonitor, there were approximately 1.8 billion global households in 2025.
• 消费者宽带。随着数字经济持续扩张，无处不在、高速、可靠的互联网已成为全球家庭的结构性必需品——驱动机会和下一波全球繁荣。据 Euromonitor，2025 年全球约有 18 亿家庭。
As Starlink develops, we believe that our broadband network can connect, and improve the existing connection, of every household globally. Given varying economic conditions and consumer purchasing power across different countries, we use a different monthly ARPU for different parts of the world based on country-specific consumer broadband ARPU from Omdia as we seek to make our service affordable and accessible across different economic development contexts. Region-specific ARPU assumptions result in a weighted average of $31 monthly ARPU for residential broadband internet services globally, according to Omdia. This global average consists of a weighted average monthly ARPU of $43 in high-income markets, $16 in upper-middle income markets, and $9 in lower-middle income and low income markets per World Bank classification.
随着 Starlink 的发展，我们相信我们的宽带网络可以连接并改善每个全球家庭的现有连接。鉴于不同国家经济条件和消费者购买力的差异，我们对世界不同部分使用不同月 ARPU，参照 Omdia 的国家特定消费者宽带 ARPU——目的是让我们的服务在不同经济发展背景下都可负担且可及。区域特定 ARPU 假设带来全球住宅宽带互联网服务加权平均月 ARPU 31 美元（据 Omdia）。这一全球平均由按世界银行分类的高收入市场加权平均月 ARPU 43 美元、中高收入市场 16 美元、中低收入和低收入市场 9 美元构成。
Together this represents a total addressable market of $660 billion based on 1.8 billion households.
合计，按 18 亿户家庭计算，这代表 6,600 亿美元的可触达市场。
Approximately 40% of the global population lives in rural areas, remaining structurally underserved by terrestrial broadband infrastructure due to unfavorable deployment economics, limited network density and high last-mile costs.
约 40% 的全球人口居住在乡村地区——由于不利的部署经济性、有限的网络密度和高昂的最后一公里成本，这些人在结构上仍未被地面宽带基础设施充分服务。
This structural imbalance creates a large, durable and relatively uncontested baseline market for satellite-based connectivity solutions. For many of these households, Starlink represents the first viable option for high-speed, low-latency internet access, with limited competition from terrestrial providers. Unlike terrestrial networks, which require significant incremental capital to extend coverage to low-density areas, our space-based architecture enables economically scalable service delivery across these regions with minimal marginal cost per additional user.
这种结构性失衡为基于卫星的连接方案创造了一个庞大、持久且相对没有竞争的基线市场。对许多此类家庭而言，Starlink 代表了首个可行的高速、低延迟互联网接入选项——来自地面提供商的竞争有限。与需要大量增量资本将覆盖扩展到低密度地区的地面网络不同，我们的天基架构能以每位增量用户最小边际成本的方式经济、可扩展地在这些区域提供服务。
Importantly, while rural and underserved geographies provide a compelling initial adoption vector, we believe Starlink's value proposition extends well beyond these markets. As network capacity increases and product performance continues to improve, we expect to compete increasingly in suburban and urban environments.
重要的是，虽然乡村和服务不足的地理区域提供了引人注目的初期采用路径，但我们相信 Starlink 的价值主张远远超出这些市场。随着网络容量增加和产品性能持续改善，我们预计将日益在郊区和城市环境中参与竞争。
Accordingly, while rural households represent a large and durable entry point for our connectivity offering, we view this segment as a foundational layer upon which significantly broader consumer, enterprise and government demand can be built.
因此，尽管乡村家庭代表了我们连接产品庞大且持久的入口，我们将这一细分市场视为可在其上构建显著更广泛的消费者、企业和政府需求的基础层。
•Enterprise Solutions. We offer fixed site broadband solutions tailored for the needs of our enterprise customers across many different industries, including construction, agriculture, retail, telecom, hospitality and others. For the purpose of sizing market opportunity, we include small and medium sized businesses within our Enterprise Solutions market opportunity. Our Starlink enterprise offerings can provide important primary or back-up connectivity for every business in the geographies where we are licensed to operate. According to Grand View Research, the global business broadband market in 2025 across small to medium sized business and enterprise usage is estimated to be $200 billion.
• 企业解决方案 (Enterprise Solutions)。我们针对建筑、农业、零售、电信、酒店等众多不同行业企业客户的需求，提供定制的固定站点宽带方案。出于衡量市场机会的目的，我们将中小企业纳入企业解决方案市场机会中。Starlink 企业产品可在我们获得运营许可的地区为每家企业提供重要的主用或备用连接。据 Grand View Research，2025 年全球商业宽带市场（涵盖中小企业到企业的使用）估计为 2,000 亿美元。
•Government Solutions. Driven by increasing demand for resilient, low-latency, and highly secure communications in contested and remote environments, defense organizations and governments around the world are increasingly turning to commercial satellite providers with connectivity solutions to supplement and enhance traditional military networks. According to Novaspace, the global satellite communications market driven by defense and government demand in 2025 was $5 billion. The estimate of the government communications market includes only publicly disclosed programs and budgets and does not include classified missions or other restricted uses, which we believe represent additional sources of demand.
• 政府解决方案 (Government Solutions)。受争议和偏远环境中对有韧性、低延迟、高度安全通信需求的推动，全球国防组织和政府越来越多地转向商业卫星提供方提供连接方案，以补充和增强传统军用网络。据 Novaspace，2025 年由国防和政府需求驱动的全球卫星通信市场为 50 亿美元。该政府通信市场估计仅包含公开披露的项目和预算，不包括我们认为代表额外需求来源的机密任务或其他受限用途。
Starlink Mobile. According to Omdia, as of December 31, 2025, there were eight billion mobile connected devices globally. We believe our Starlink Mobile offering will be able to provide continuous global coverage and substantially reduce mobile "dead zones," which remain areas that are structurally underserved by the limitations of the networks of current mobile network operators. For example, according to the J.D. Power U.S. Wireless Network Quality Performance Study, U.S. wireless customers experienced service problems in approximately one out of every 11 mobile interactions, even in well-connected areas. In addition, an estimated 40% of the global population resided in rural areas in 2024 according to the World Bank, where terrestrial mobile coverage can be limited or unreliable. While we expect Starlink Mobile service today to be most impactful for customers in remote areas uncovered by terrestrial mobile networks, as our constellation grows and our product performance continues to improve, we will compete to be the preferred connectivity experience to our customers no matter where they are located, whether in rural, suburban, or urban areas. The next-generation of Starlink Mobile satellites, in combination with our recent purchase of wireless spectrum from EchoStar, is designed to provide high bandwidth and low latency connectivity directly to end user devices, enabling a connectivity solution on par with terrestrial mobile networks. Given varying economic conditions and consumer purchasing power across different countries, we assume a different monthly ARPU for different parts of the world as we seek to make our service affordable and accessible across different economic development contexts. Our region-specific ARPU assumptions result in a weighted average monthly mobile ARPU of $8 per user. This global average consists of a weighted average monthly ARPU of $18 in high-income markets, $5 in upper-middle income markets, $2 in lower-middle, and $2 in low income markets. Based on the total number of connected devices globally and the mobile ARPU, we estimate the Starlink Mobile market opportunity to be $740 billion. We expect to continue to partner with mobile network operators globally as we expand coverage and participate in the broader mobile connectivity market.
Starlink Mobile。据 Omdia，截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日，全球有 80 亿台移动联网设备。我们相信 Starlink Mobile 产品将能提供持续的全球覆盖，并大幅减少移动「盲区」——这些区域在结构上仍因当前移动运营商网络的局限而服务不足。例如根据 J.D. Power 美国无线网络质量绩效研究，即便在连接良好的区域，美国无线客户在约每 11 次移动交互中有一次遇到服务问题。此外，据世界银行，2024 年全球约 40% 的人口居住在乡村地区——这些地区的地面移动覆盖可能有限或不可靠。虽然我们预计如今的 Starlink Mobile 服务对地面移动网络未覆盖的偏远地区客户最具影响，但随着星座扩展和产品性能持续改善，我们将致力于无论客户位于乡村、郊区还是城市都成为其首选连接体验。下一代 Starlink Mobile 卫星结合我们近期从 EchoStar 购入的无线频谱，被设计为直接向终端用户设备提供高带宽和低延迟连接，提供与地面移动网络相当的连接方案。鉴于不同国家经济条件和消费者购买力的差异，我们对世界不同区域假定不同的月 ARPU，旨在让我们的服务在不同经济发展背景下都可负担且可及。我们的区域特定 ARPU 假设带来全球移动每用户月加权平均 ARPU 为 8 美元。这一全球平均由高收入市场加权平均月 ARPU 18 美元、中高收入市场 5 美元、中低收入市场 2 美元、低收入市场 2 美元构成。基于全球联网设备总数和移动 ARPU，我们估计 Starlink Mobile 市场机会为 7,400 亿美元。我们预计将继续与全球移动网络运营商合作，扩展覆盖并参与更广泛的移动连接市场。
Additional and Future Starlink Applications. We believe the long-term market opportunity for Starlink extends materially beyond traditional fixed broadband and satellite-to-mobile connectivity. Many of these use cases represent new categories of demand that were not previously addressable with legacy terrestrial or satellite solutions due to limitations in coverage, latency, capacity, or cost. While these additional and future use cases are early stage and not yet captured in conventional industry market definitions, we believe they have the potential to significantly expand the total addressable market for connectivity over time.
Starlink 的额外和未来应用。我们相信 Starlink 的长期市场机会远远超出传统的固定宽带和卫星直连移动连接。其中许多用例代表了过去因覆盖、延迟、容量或成本的局限，传统地面或卫星方案无法触达的新需求类别。虽然这些额外和未来用例尚处早期阶段，未被纳入常规行业市场定义，我们相信它们随时间推移有潜力显著扩大连接的总可触达市场。
•Enterprise Mobility. Because our Starlink solutions are uniquely well-suited for in-motion environments, remote, or hard-to-serve locations, we are able to provide high-performance connectivity across land, air, and sea. We believe we have a differentiated right to win these verticals as existing connectivity solutions are not able to provide sufficient speed, latency and reliability, with frequent service outages driven by weather, orbital mechanics and coverage gaps. Our Starlink constellation directly addresses these deficiencies, creating a compelling path for us to capture a substantial share of opportunities and to unlock previously unattainable levels of service quality and customer willingness to pay.
• 企业移动性。由于 Starlink 方案特别适用于运动中环境、偏远或难以服务的位置，我们能够在陆、空、海上提供高性能连接。我们相信我们具备差异化的胜出权利——因为现有连接方案无法提供足够的速度、延迟和可靠性，并且经常因天气、轨道力学和覆盖缺口造成服务中断。我们的 Starlink 星座直接解决这些缺陷，为我们创造了引人注目的路径，去捕获这些机会中的大量份额，并解锁此前无法达到的服务质量水平和客户付费意愿。
In land mobility, Starlink supports connectivity for vehicle fleets, including trucking, rail, public safety vehicles, and autonomous systems, enabling real-time telematics, route optimization, safety monitoring, and onboard passenger connectivity, as fleets become increasingly connected and data-driven.
在陆地移动方面，Starlink 支持车辆车队的连接，包括卡车、铁路、公共安全车辆和自主系统，支持实时远程信息处理、路线优化、安全监控和车上乘客连接——随着车队越来越联网化和数据驱动。
In aviation, Starlink delivers high-speed, low-latency in-flight connectivity for commercial airlines, business aviation, and government aircraft, supporting passenger broadband, operational communications, and real-time aircraft data transmission, as airlines increasingly prioritize differentiated onboard experiences and operational efficiency. There are approximately 23,900 commercial aircraft, according to Oliver Wyman, and approximately 24,500 privately owned aircraft, according to Corporate Jet Investor, in the world, which can be served by our aviation offering.
在航空方面，Starlink 为商业航空公司、商务航空和政府飞机提供高速、低延迟的机上连接，支持乘客宽带、运营通信和实时飞机数据传输——随着航空公司越来越优先考虑差异化的机上体验和运营效率。据 Oliver Wyman，全球约有 23,900 架商用飞机；据 Corporate Jet Investor，全球约有 24,500 架私有飞机——这些都是我们航空产品可服务的对象。
In maritime, Starlink provides connectivity for commercial shipping, offshore energy platforms, cruise lines, and government vessels, enabling crew welfare, operational optimization, safety systems, and real-time data transfer, as connectivity becomes a standard requirement across global fleets. Our potential customer base as of 2025 consists of approximately 99,000 commercial merchant ships, defined as being 100 gross tons or more, approximately 21,000 fishing vessels, and approximately 4,000 cruise ships and private yachts, according to Marine Traffic Dashboard.
在海运方面，Starlink 为商业航运、海上能源平台、邮轮和政府船舶提供连接，支持船员福祉、运营优化、安全系统和实时数据传输——连接正成为全球船队的标准要求。截至 2025 年，我们的潜在客户基础据 Marine Traffic Dashboard 数据约包括 99,000 艘商用船（定义为 100 总吨以上）、约 21,000 艘渔船以及约 4,000 艘邮轮和私人游艇。
•Expanded Enterprise and Government Applications Enterprise Back-Up and Failover Connectivity. As connectivity becomes a mission critical component of enterprise operations, we believe back-up and failover connectivity is evolving into a foundational layer of enterprise infrastructure. The increasing cost of downtime, combined with the proliferation of cloud-based and latency-sensitive applications, is driving enterprises to prioritize uptime, business continuity, and network resilience and adopt multi-layered connectivity architectures. We believe this shift will result in a meaningful expansion of the connectivity market.
• 扩展的企业与政府应用 — 企业备份与故障切换连接。随着连接成为企业运营的关键任务组件，我们相信备份和故障切换连接正演化为企业基础设施的基础层。停机成本的上升，结合云端和延迟敏感应用的扩散，正推动企业优先关注正常运行时间、业务连续性和网络韧性，并采用多层连接架构。我们相信这一转变将带来连接市场的显著扩张。
Expanded Government Applications. We believe traditional connectivity market estimates do not fully capture the scope of government-related demand, particularly in mission-critical and classified applications. The growing importance of secure communications, real-time intelligence, and resilient network architectures is driving sustained investment in connectivity capabilities across defense and civilian agencies. These use cases tend to command higher value and longer-duration contracts, contributing to a meaningful and durable expansion of the connectivity market.
扩展的政府应用。我们相信传统连接市场估计未能充分捕捉政府相关需求的范围，尤其在关键任务和机密应用方面。安全通信、实时情报和有韧性网络架构日益重要，正推动国防和民用机构在连接能力上的持续投资。这些用例往往伴随更高价值和更长周期的合同，带来连接市场显著且持久的扩展。
Smart Device Connectivity. The proliferation of connected devices across various physical environments-- including sensors, wearables, vehicles, appliances, and infrastructure systems--is driving increasing demand for ubiquitous, reliable, and low-latency connectivity. As of 2025, there were approximately 22 billion IoT connected devices globally, forecasted to reach 47 billion by 2031. As billions of connected devices generate, transmit, and act on data, connectivity becomes an essential enabler of new categories of economic activity. As these devices grow in scale into the tens of billions globally and become more intelligent and data-intensive, we believe the scope of the connectivity market will expand significantly beyond traditional human-centric usage.
智能设备连接。各种物理环境中的联网设备扩散——包括传感器、可穿戴设备、车辆、家电和基础设施系统——正推动对普遍、可靠、低延迟连接的需求增加。截至 2025 年，全球约有 220 亿台物联网联网设备，预计到 2031 年达到 470 亿台。当数十亿台联网设备生成、传输并基于数据采取行动时，连接将成为新经济活动类别的关键支撑。当这些设备规模增长到全球数百亿台并变得更智能、数据更密集时，我们相信连接市场的范围将显著超越传统以人为中心的使用。
In-Orbit Data Transport. We operate a large constellation of over 23,000 inter-satellite lasers that create a dynamic mesh network in space and enable traffic rerouting through orbit. We believe this laser mesh network will help us unlock a new connectivity market by enabling third-party satellites to utilize our in-orbit data transport layer. While most of our laser mesh network capacity is used to power our Starlink services, we selectively monetize excess capacity through our Plaser program. We allow third parties to purchase our space laser hardware and connect their satellites to our Starlink network, allowing them to offload data to a ground station anywhere on Earth while bypassing the need to build their own relay architecture or ground stations. As satellite constellations grow, we expect market demand for high-throughput, low-latency data relay to increase across commercial and government operators. While this market remains nascent, we believe the opportunity represents a meaningful expansion beyond traditional satellite connectivity TAM.
在轨数据传输。我们运营一个由超过 23,000 条星间激光组成的庞大星座，在太空中构建动态网状网络并支持通过轨道的流量重路由。我们相信这种激光网状网络将通过让第三方卫星利用我们的在轨数据传输层，帮助我们开启新的连接市场。虽然我们的激光网状网络容量大部分用于驱动 Starlink 服务，但我们通过 Plaser 计划有选择地对剩余容量进行变现。我们允许第三方购买我们的太空激光硬件并将其卫星接入 Starlink 网络，使其能把数据下传到地球上任何地方的地面站，同时绕开自建中继架构或地面站的需要。随着卫星星座扩展，我们预计商业和政府运营方对高吞吐、低延迟数据中继的市场需求将增加。虽然该市场仍处萌芽期，我们相信该机会代表了对传统卫星连接 TAM 的有意义扩展。
Artificial Intelligence The market for artificial intelligence is currently undergoing explosive structural growth, emerging as a foundational utility for the modern global economy and unlocking a multi-trillion-dollar opportunity. Our frontier models, consumer and enterprise applications, and AI infrastructure solutions are strategically positioned to capture value across four key components of this vast ecosystem, resulting in an estimated total market opportunity of $26.5 trillion.
人工智能 人工智能市场目前正经历爆发式的结构性增长，逐渐成为现代全球经济的基础公用事业，并释放出多万亿美元的机会。我们的前沿模型、消费者和企业应用，以及 AI 基础设施解决方案，被战略性地定位为在这一庞大生态的四大关键组成部分中捕获价值，由此带来约 26.5 万亿美元的预估总市场机会。
AI Infrastructure. According to RAND Corporation, global data center compute demand is estimated to be 235 gigawatts in 2030, of which 70% is estimated to be utilized for AI workloads. Assuming a target Power Usage Effectiveness of 1.2 and an all-in chip power consumption per GPU of 1.3 kilowatts per GPU--that of an H100 SXM--this AI workload demand corresponds to 104 million GPUs required. We apply an 80% utilization rate per the National Electrical Installation Standards and a GPU rental rate of $3.33 per hour, according to Silicon Data, which is based on the median of neocloud GPU rental rates in 2025; we note that the rental rate has historically varied subject to market conditions. As a result, we estimate the AI compute infrastructure market opportunity to be approximately $2.4 trillion.
AI 基础设施。据 RAND Corporation，2030 年全球数据中心算力需求估计为 235 吉瓦，其中 70% 估计用于 AI 工作负载。假设目标 PUE（电源使用效率）为 1.2，每颗 GPU 全功耗为 1.3 千瓦（即 H100 SXM 水平），这一 AI 工作负载需求对应需要 1.04 亿颗 GPU。我们按照 National Electrical Installation Standards 取 80% 利用率，并按 Silicon Data 取 GPU 租赁费率 3.33 美元/小时（基于 2025 年 neocloud GPU 租赁费率中位数）；我们注意到租赁费率历史上随市场情况波动。由此我们估计 AI 算力基础设施市场机会约为 2.4 万亿美元。
Consumer Subscriptions. As demand for AI solutions surges, fueled by widespread adoption of AI tools that enhance productivity, creativity, personalization, and real-time assistance in everyday life, consumers are increasingly turning to subscription-based access to high-performance AI platforms. These platforms, equipped with advanced reasoning, seamless real-time data integration, and multimodal capabilities, are essential in today's ever- more receptive and interconnected world. We believe SpaceX is well positioned to address this opportunity through our X and Grok platforms by delivering a differentiated product centered on truth-seeking and real-time relevance.
消费者订阅。在 AI 解决方案需求激增的驱动下（受 AI 工具广泛采用——这些工具提升日常生活中的生产力、创造力、个性化和实时辅助——的推动），消费者越来越多地转向以订阅方式获取高性能 AI 平台。这些平台配备先进推理、无缝实时数据整合和多模态能力，对当今日益接纳和互联的世界至关重要。我们相信 SpaceX 通过 X 和 Grok 平台处于应对这一机会的有利位置——通过提供以追求真理和实时相关性为中心的差异化产品。
Our roadmap for future models contains multi-trillion parameter models, which could represent a step change in reasoning depth and overall intelligence. Through Grok's integration with X and proprietary access to real-time data inflows, we believe we can better address a broader set of high-frequency, high-value consumer use cases and increase user engagement and willingness to pay, positioning Grok to capture a larger share of the consumer AI subscription market relative to standalone, non-integrated offerings. We estimate our market opportunity based on the global population of individuals aged 10 and over in 2025--approximately five and a half billion according to Euromonitor--multiplied by the weighted average monthly subscription revenue of $12, resulting in an annualized market opportunity of approximately $760 billion. Our weighted average monthly revenue assumes different monthly subscription fees across different geographies around the world. We assume $30 monthly cost of a SuperGrok subscription in high-income countries, $8 monthly cost in upper-middle and lower-middle income countries, and significantly lower monthly cost in low income countries, as defined by the World Bank.
我们对未来模型的路线图包含数万亿参数模型，这可能代表推理深度和整体智能的跃迁。通过 Grok 与 X 的整合及对实时数据流的独家访问，我们相信我们能更好地服务更广泛的高频、高价值消费者用例并提升用户参与度和付费意愿，使 Grok 相对于独立、非整合产品在消费者 AI 订阅市场上能占据更大份额。我们基于 2025 年 10 岁及以上的全球人口约 55 亿（据 Euromonitor）乘以加权平均月订阅收入 12 美元来估算市场机会，年化市场机会约为 7,600 亿美元。我们的加权平均月收入假设在世界不同地区有不同的月订阅费用。我们假设在高收入国家（按世界银行定义）每月 SuperGrok 订阅成本为 30 美元，在中高收入和中低收入国家为 8 美元，在低收入国家显著更低。
Digital Advertising. Digital advertising represents a large and growing global market opportunity as businesses increase marketing budgets towards digital platforms that enable targeted advertising, measurable performance, and direct engagement with consumers. In 2025, global digital advertising spending totaled $600 billion according to S&P Global Market Intelligence. We believe that X's ability to combine large-scale user engagement, real-time content, and advanced AI-driven performance marketing tools positions us well to participate in this significant market opportunity.
数字广告。数字广告代表庞大且不断增长的全球市场机会——企业把营销预算越来越多地投向能提供定向广告、可衡量绩效和与消费者直接互动的数字平台。据 S&P Global Market Intelligence，2025 年全球数字广告支出总计 6,000 亿美元。我们相信 X 把大规模用户互动、实时内容和先进的 AI 驱动效果营销工具组合在一起的能力，让我们能很好地参与这一重大市场机会。
Enterprise Applications. AI is revolutionizing enterprise applications as organizations across industries increasingly adopt AI solutions to automate complex workflows, augment knowledge workers, enhance decision-making, redefine productivity, and improve operational efficiency. Specifically, we believe that our enterprise applications, including Macrohard, agentic AI, will increasingly support knowledge workers across industries by automating routine cognitive tasks, assisting with research and analysis, generating content and code, and refining decision- making processes. Ultimately, we believe this transformation could evolve knowledge workers into empowered managers of autonomous agents, unlocking unprecedented levels of creativity and productivity.
企业应用。AI 正在革新企业应用，因为各行业组织越来越多地采用 AI 方案自动化复杂工作流、增强知识工作者、提升决策、重新定义生产力并改善运营效率。具体而言，我们相信我们的企业应用（包括 Macrohard 和 agentic AI）将通过自动化常规认知任务、协助研究和分析、生成内容和代码以及完善决策流程，越来越多地支持各行业的知识工作者。最终我们相信这一变革可能让知识工作者演变为被赋能的自主 agent 管理者，释放前所未有的创造力和生产力水平。
We believe we are still in the early days of AI transforming enterprises, with AI-powered enterprise applications poised to reshape the digital economy. The Digital Cooperation Organization ("DCO") defines the digital economy as economic activity reliant on, significantly enhanced, or enabled by digital technologies and their applications, including the following products and services: AI and advanced analytics, blockchain and decentralized technologies, cloud services, digital connectivity, digital devices and the IoT, encryption and cybersecurity, immersive technologies, and robotics and autonomous systems. DCO estimates that the digital economy will grow three times faster in 2026 on a year-over-year basis compared to the estimated growth of the global GDP, reaching approximately $22.7 trillion in 2026. In a survey of CTOs, senior technologists, policymakers, and digital economy experts, also conducted by DCO, AI and advanced analytics were identified by 69% of respondents as their top digital technology priority--higher than any other surveyed priority. We believe that our enterprise strategy, which is focused on serving the digital needs of the world's largest industries with AI solutions, positions us competitively to pursue this rapidly growing opportunity.
我们相信我们仍处于 AI 变革企业的早期，AI 驱动的企业应用即将重塑数字经济。Digital Cooperation Organization（DCO）将数字经济定义为依赖数字技术及其应用、并被其显著增强或支撑的经济活动，包括以下产品和服务：AI 和高级分析、区块链和去中心化技术、云服务、数字连接、数字设备和物联网、加密和网络安全、沉浸式技术，以及机器人和自主系统。DCO 估计 2026 年数字经济同比增速将达到估计全球 GDP 增速的三倍，2026 年约达 22.7 万亿美元。在 DCO 同时进行的 CTO、资深技术人员、政策制定者和数字经济专家调查中，69% 的受访者将 AI 和高级分析列为他们的首要数字技术优先项——高于任何其他被调查的优先项。我们相信，我们专注于以 AI 方案服务全球最大行业数字需求的企业战略，让我们处于追求这一快速增长机会的有利竞争位置。
Future Markets Beyond the established markets reflected in our TAM, we envision that ongoing advancements in our technology and infrastructure will unlock entirely new markets over time. As launch costs decline, satellite capabilities advance, and large-scale compute infrastructure expands, innovative applications and new markets may emerge that harness our integrated infrastructure across space, connectivity, and AI. Although these prospects remain nascent, with uncertain timing and scale--and thus are excluded from our quantified total addressable market estimates--we believe they hold trillions of dollars of eventual potential for groundbreaking innovation and value creation, eventually representing multi-trillion-dollar economic opportunities.
未来市场 除了反映在 TAM 中的既有市场之外，我们设想我们的技术和基础设施持续进步将随时间解锁全新市场。随着发射成本下降、卫星能力进步、大规模算力基础设施扩张，可能涌现利用我们跨太空、连接和 AI 一体化基础设施的创新应用和新市场。虽然这些前景仍处萌芽阶段，时间点和规模不确定——因此被排除在我们量化的总可触达市场估计之外——我们相信它们承载着数万亿美元的最终突破性创新和价值创造潜力，最终代表多万亿美元的经济机会。
Long-Haul Point-to-Point Terrestrial Travel. Our Starship vehicle has the potential to revolutionize terrestrial commercial transportation by achieving an unparalleled combination of speed, reliability and cost efficiency. This capability could reduce most international long-haul flights to under 30 minutes, enabling point-to-point travel to the furthest location in an hour or less. While we must surmount technological, economic and regulatory obstacles to fully capitalize on this opportunity--such as restrictions on supersonic flights over land in certain regions due to sonic booms, and the economic feasibility of shorter routes--we believe we are strategically positioned to take share of the terrestrial logistics and transportation market.
长程点对点地面运输。我们的 Starship 载具有潜力通过实现速度、可靠性和成本效率的无与伦比组合，革新地面商业运输。这一能力可以把大多数国际长程航班缩减到 30 分钟以下，使一小时或更短时间内的点对点最远位置旅行成为可能。虽然我们必须克服技术、经济和监管障碍来充分利用这一机会——例如某些区域因音爆对陆地超音速飞行的限制，以及较短航线的经济可行性——我们相信我们处于占据地面物流和运输市场份额的战略有利位置。
Space Tourism. Historically, human spaceflight has been limited to government astronauts, augmented by a limited number of privately funded missions. Yet, with meaningful advances in space technology and the ongoing expansion of orbital flight infrastructure, we anticipate a gradual increase in accessibility of spaceflight over time, potentially enabling a new category of commercial human spaceflight and tourism. Under 30 people out of the global population visited Earth's orbit in 2025, which we believe could be a far greater number in the future.
太空旅游。历史上载人航天仅限于政府宇航员，再加上少数私人资助任务。但随着太空技术的显著进步和轨道飞行基础设施的持续扩张，我们预计随时间推移航天的可及性将逐步提升，可能催生一类新的商业载人航天和旅游。2025 年全球只有不到 30 人访问过地球轨道——我们相信未来这个数字可能远远更大。
Passenger and Cargo Transport to the Moon and Mars. Looking further ahead, advances in reusable launch systems and deep-space transportation infrastructure may enable new forms of interplanetary logistics, including passenger and cargo transportation to the Moon and Mars. Supporting a sustained human presence on another planet would require the regular transport of people, equipment, and materials at a scale not previously possible.
月球和火星的载人与货运运输。展望更远，可重复使用发射系统和深空运输基础设施的进步可能促成新形式的行星际物流，包括月球和火星的载人和货运。支持在另一颗行星上长期人类存在将需要以前所未有的规模定期运送人员、设备和材料。
Energy Production and Manufacturing on the Moon and Mars. Establishing a sustained human and industrial presence on the Moon and Mars would require reliable, large-scale energy generation to support habitats, manufacturing, and scientific operations. Potential solutions could include solar power systems, taking advantage of the thin atmosphere, constant solar exposure, and other advanced energy technologies designed to operate in the unique environmental conditions of the Moon and Mars. Over time, we believe that advances in planetary infrastructure may enable manufacturing on the Moon and Mars using locally available resources.
月球和火星上的能源生产与制造。在月球和火星上建立持续的人类与工业存在将需要可靠、大规模的能源生产，以支持栖息地、制造和科研运营。可能的方案包括利用稀薄大气、恒定太阳曝光的太阳能系统以及为月球和火星独特环境条件设计的其他先进能源技术。随时间推移，我们相信行星基础设施的进步可能让我们在月球和火星上使用本地可用资源进行制造。
In-Orbit Manufacturing. Terrestrial manufacturing is inherently constrained by gravity, which imposes fundamental limitations on processes at the atomic and molecular level. Establishing in-orbit infrastructure unlocks large-scale, high-value production free from those traditional barriers, enabling breakthroughs in precision and efficiency. The microgravity environment of space fosters innovative advancements in key industries, such as pharmaceuticals--where it enhances drug solubility, purity, crystallization, and stability--as well as, advanced materials and semiconductors, allowing for superior crystal formation and material properties unattainable on Earth.
在轨制造。地面制造本质上受重力约束，对原子和分子层面的工艺施加根本限制。建立在轨基础设施可释放摆脱这些传统障碍的大规模、高价值生产，使精度和效率上的突破成为可能。太空的微重力环境推动关键产业的创新——例如制药（提升药物溶解度、纯度、结晶和稳定性），以及先进材料和半导体（地球上无法获得的优良晶体形成和材料性能）。
Beyond these particle-level innovations, in-orbit facilities overcome Earth's energy constraints by harnessing abundant, uninterrupted solar power, facilitating energy-intensive operations with unparalleled sustainability.
除了这些粒子级的创新之外，在轨设施还通过利用丰富、不被打断的太阳能克服地球能源约束，使能源密集型作业以无与伦比的可持续性进行。
Asteroid Mining. Asteroid resources, including platinum-group metals, rare earth elements, nickel, cobalt, iron and water, represent a vast untapped reservoir beyond Earth's gravity well, with some near-Earth objects containing concentrations of elements far exceeding typical terrestrial ore grades. With meaningful advances in reusable launch capabilities, autonomous robotics, and in-situ processing technologies, we believe the accessibility of asteroid resources will expand over time, unlocking a new category of commercial space resource extraction. We believe our experience in launch systems, spacecraft development, and space infrastructure uniquely positions us to pursue asteroid mining operations to extract metals and other critical resources from near-Earth and main-belt asteroids, providing abundant raw materials for space-based infrastructure, reducing the need to launch all mass from Earth.
小行星采矿。小行星资源——包括铂族金属、稀土元素、镍、钴、铁和水——代表地球引力井之外尚未开发的庞大资源储备，一些近地天体所含元素浓度远超典型地面矿石品位。随着可重复使用发射能力、自主机器人和原位加工技术的显著进步，我们相信小行星资源的可及性将随时间扩大，开启商业天基资源开采的新类别。我们相信，我们在发射系统、航天器开发和太空基础设施方面的经验让我们处于独特位置，可以从近地和主带小行星中采集金属和其他关键资源开展小行星采矿运营，为天基基础设施提供丰富原材料，减少把全部质量从地球发射的需要。
Our Solutions & Services Unparalleled Launch Capability Our unmatched launch capability is the foundational competitive advantage that enables our unique solutions and services. We are the market leader in orbital launch, providing low-cost, reliable, and frequent access to space for commercial and government customers. Our launch services are built around a fleet of reusable rockets and spacecraft. SpaceX's family of rocket systems and spacecraft address missions ranging from routine cargo delivery to the International Space Station to deep-space exploration. The Falcon class of rockets delivered over 80% of mass to orbit in the year ending December 31, 2025. Starship, a two-stage super heavy-lift launch vehicle that we have been flight testing since 2023, further enhances our industry-defining launch offerings.
我们的解决方案与服务 无与伦比的发射能力 我们无可匹敌的发射能力是支持独特解决方案和服务的基础竞争优势。我们是轨道发射的市场领导者，为商业和政府客户提供低成本、可靠且高频次的太空进入。我们的发射服务围绕一支可重复使用火箭和航天器舰队构建。SpaceX 的火箭系统和航天器家族涵盖从向国际空间站常规货运到深空探索的各种任务。在截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日的年度内，Falcon 系列火箭交付了全球超过 80% 的入轨质量。Starship 是我们自 2023 年开始飞行测试的两级超重型运载火箭，进一步增强了我们定义行业的发射产品。
Separate from our fleet of reusable rockets, SpaceX's launch advantage is equally underpinned by our fleet of advanced spacecraft. Our International Space Station cargo and human spaceflight missions are launched on Falcon 9 and flown on the Dragon crew and cargo spacecraft. The vehicles autonomously dock to the station, delivering pressurized and unpressurized cargo, and passengers. Both Dragon variants are partially reusable and perform fully autonomous rendezvous, docking, and return operations.
除了可重复使用火箭舰队之外，SpaceX 的发射优势同样由我们先进航天器舰队所支撑。我们的国际空间站货运和载人航天任务由 Falcon 9 发射，并由 Dragon 载人与货运航天器执行。这些载具自主对接到空间站，运送加压和非加压货物及乘客。两种 Dragon 变体都是部分可重复使用，并执行完全自主的交会、对接和返回操作。
Our Fleet of Launch Vehicles and Spacecraft Our Fleet of Launch Vehicles Falcon 9. The Falcon 9 rocket is a reusable, two-stage rocket designed and manufactured by SpaceX for the safe, reliable, and cost-effective transport of satellites, scientific payloads, cargo, and crew to Earth orbit and beyond.
我们的发射载具与航天器舰队 发射载具舰队 Falcon 9。Falcon 9 火箭是由 SpaceX 设计和制造的可重复使用两级火箭，旨在安全、可靠、成本高效地把卫星、科学载荷、货物和机组运送至地球轨道及更远处。
Powered by liquid oxygen and rocket-grade kerosene, the first-stage is equipped with nine Merlin 1D engines producing over 1.7 million pounds of thrust at sea level, while the second stage utilizes a single vacuum-optimized Merlin engine for precise orbital insertion. First launched in 2010, Falcon 9 is the world's first orbital-class rapidly reusable rocket, and has become the most active orbital launch vehicle today, with approximately 620 orbital space launches as of March 31, 2026 and an over 99% mission success rate. Falcon 9 is capable of delivering approximately 23 metric tons to LEO and eight metric tons to geosynchronous transfer orbit. Reusability allows SpaceX to refly the most expensive parts of the rocket, which in turn drives down the cost of space access. Falcon 9's reusable components primarily include its booster, which lands on one of our autonomous drone ships out on the ocean or on one of our landing zones near our launch pads ahead of being refurbished for a future launch, and its payload fairing halves, which are recovered via parachute-assisted splashdowns and are refurbished and reused after retrieval. The second stage is not designed for recovery or reuse and instead safely deorbits after successful payload deployment.
Falcon 9 由液氧和火箭级煤油驱动，一级配备九台 Merlin 1D 引擎，海平面推力超过 170 万磅；二级使用一台为真空环境优化的 Merlin 引擎进行精确入轨。Falcon 9 于 2010 年首飞，是全球首款轨道级快速可重复使用火箭，已成为当今最活跃的轨道发射载具——截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日已完成约 620 次轨道级太空发射，任务成功率超过 99%。Falcon 9 能向 LEO 投送约 23 公吨，向地球同步转移轨道投送 8 公吨。可重复使用让 SpaceX 能复用火箭最昂贵的部分，从而降低太空进入成本。Falcon 9 的可重复使用组件主要包括其助推器（在海上自主无人船或发射台附近的着陆区降落后进行翻新以备未来发射）和载荷整流罩两半（通过降落伞辅助溅落回收，回收后翻新并重复使用）。二级不被设计为回收或重复使用，而是在成功部署有效载荷后安全离轨。
Falcon 9 Overview Falcon 9 introduced a combination of technical innovation, cost reduction, and operational scale that materially altered the economics of orbital launch and established our position as the leading commercial launch provider.
Falcon 9 概览 Falcon 9 引入了技术创新、成本降低与运营规模的组合，实质性地改变了轨道发射的经济性，并确立了我们作为领先商业发射提供方的地位。
•First Orbital-Class Rapidly Reusable Rocket: In December 2015, Falcon 9 achieved the first vertical landing of an orbital-class booster, followed in April 2016 by the first autonomous drone ship landing in the Atlantic Ocean. Reuse of boosters and fairings, a practice pioneered by SpaceX in the launch industry, fundamentally enables our launch rate and capacity and forms the basis for the launch system's inherent reliability. Through recovering, inspecting, and evaluating flown hardware, SpaceX gains insight into system performance that would not be otherwise achievable. Partial reusability for orbital spaceflight has reduced cost per ton to orbit by approximately 85% as compared to the historical average launch cost per kilogram of $18,500.
• 首款轨道级快速可重复使用火箭：2015 年 12 月，Falcon 9 实现了首次轨道级助推器垂直着陆，随后在 2016 年 4 月在大西洋上完成首次自主无人船着陆。SpaceX 在发射行业首创的助推器和整流罩重复使用从根本上支持了我们的发射率和容量，并构成发射系统固有可靠性的基础。通过回收、检查和评估已飞硬件，SpaceX 获得了否则无法获得的系统性能洞察。轨道航天的部分可重复使用相比每公斤 18,500 美元的历史平均发射成本，将每吨入轨成本降低了约 85%。
•Reusability Enabled Cost Structure Advantage: Reuse of the first-stage--representing the majority of vehicle manufacturing cost--has materially reduced marginal launch costs relative to fully expendable systems.
• 可重复使用支撑的成本结构优势：一级的重复使用——占载具制造成本大部分——相比完全一次性系统大幅降低了边际发射成本。
•Highest Operational Tempo in History: With approximately 620 orbital space launches over 15 years of operation, Falcon 9 is the most frequently flown active orbital launch vehicle to date. In 2025, Falcon 9 conducted 165 launches, accounting for over half of all global orbital launches in the year while delivering over 80% of mass to orbit.
• 历史上最高的运营节奏：经过 15 年运营约 620 次轨道级太空发射，Falcon 9 是迄今最频繁飞行的在役轨道发射载具。2025 年 Falcon 9 进行了 165 次发射，占当年全球所有轨道发射的一半以上，并交付了超过 80% 的入轨质量。
•Track Record of Success: As of March 31, 2026, Falcon 9 has achieved an over 99% mission success rate.
• 成功记录：截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，Falcon 9 取得了超过 99% 的任务成功率。
Falcon 9 has achieved over 530 successful booster landings and more than 540 launches completed by a flight- proven Falcon rocket, underscoring the reliability of its reusability architecture.
Falcon 9 已完成超过 530 次成功的助推器着陆，由飞行验证的 Falcon 火箭完成了超过 540 次发射，凸显了其可重复使用架构的可靠性。
•Human Spaceflight Certified: Falcon 9, paired with SpaceX's Dragon crew spacecraft, is the only U.S.-based launch vehicle certified by NASA under the Commercial Crew Program to transport astronauts to and from the International Space Station. As of December 31, 2025, Falcon 9 has successfully launched 19 human spaceflight missions with a 100% mission success rate.
• 载人航天认证：Falcon 9 与 SpaceX 的 Dragon 载人航天器搭配，是 NASA 在 Commercial Crew Program 下认证的唯一一款在美国本土发射、可运送宇航员往返国际空间站的载具。截至 2025 年 12 月 31 日，Falcon 9 已成功执行 19 次载人航天任务，任务成功率为 100%。
•In-House Engine Development and Manufacturing: Falcon 9 is powered by Merlin engines that are designed, developed, and manufactured in‑house, providing vertical integration across propulsion design, production, and testing. The Merlin engine achieves one of the highest thrust‑to‑weight ratios of any rocket engine in operational service, contributing to Falcon 9's performance and payload capacity.
• 内部引擎研发与制造：Falcon 9 由 Merlin 引擎驱动——这些引擎在内部设计、研发和制造，跨推进设计、生产和测试实现垂直整合。Merlin 引擎是在役火箭引擎中推重比最高之一，支撑了 Falcon 9 的性能和载荷能力。
Falcon 9 As we transition primary production and development resources toward the fully and rapidly reusable Starship system, Falcon 9 continues to serve as the backbone of our launch revenue base; generating high-margin recurring cash flows while providing critical operational experience in high-cadence reuse. The proven capabilities of Falcon 9 established us as the leading provider of launch services globally and laid the technological and economic foundation for the next era of space transportation.
Falcon 9 随着我们把主要生产和研发资源向完全且快速可重复使用的 Starship 系统过渡，Falcon 9 继续作为我们发射收入基础的支柱，产生高毛利、经常性现金流，同时为高频重复使用提供关键运营经验。Falcon 9 经过验证的能力让我们成为全球领先的发射服务提供方，并为下一阶段太空运输奠定了技术和经济基础。
Falcon Heavy. Falcon Heavy is a partially reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle, designed to deliver large payloads to orbit. Building on the proven architecture of the Falcon 9 rocket, Falcon Heavy is composed of three reusable Falcon 9 nine-engine boosters whose combined 27 Merlin engines generate more than five million pounds of thrust at liftoff--one of the most powerful operational rockets in the world today. It is capable of carrying approximately 64 metric tons of payload to LEO and 27 metric tons to geosynchronous transfer orbit. Falcon Heavy's reusable components primarily include its three boosters, which are designed to land vertically on drone ships in the ocean and landing zones near our launch sites, and its payload-faring halves, which are recovered via parachute-assisted splashdown and are refurbished and reused after retrieval. The second stage is not designed for recovery or reuse and is designed to safely deorbit after successful payload deployment, similar to Falcon 9.
Falcon Heavy。Falcon Heavy 是一款部分可重复使用的超重型运载火箭，设计用于把大型有效载荷投送入轨。基于 Falcon 9 火箭经过验证的架构，Falcon Heavy 由三枚可重复使用的 Falcon 9 九引擎助推器组成——其 27 台 Merlin 引擎在起飞时合计产生超过 500 万磅推力——是当今世界最强大的在役火箭之一。它能向 LEO 投送约 64 公吨载荷，向地球同步转移轨道投送 27 公吨。Falcon Heavy 的可重复使用组件主要包括三枚助推器（被设计为在海上无人船和发射场附近着陆区垂直着陆）和载荷整流罩两半（通过降落伞辅助溅落回收，回收后翻新并重复使用）。与 Falcon 9 类似，二级不被设计为回收或重复使用，而是在成功部署有效载荷后被设计为安全离轨。
Falcon Heavy Overview •Reusability: Falcon Heavy incorporates a design focused on reusability, which has contributed to lowering the cost of access to space and altering the launch industry's economic model for large or high-value payloads. The vehicle's two side boosters, equipped with hypersonic grid fins and advanced propulsion systems, enable controlled recovery and soft landings. This capability enables reusability, with missions launching on flight- proven boosters generally priced below those of traditional expendable flights. Our Falcon 9 boosters, which are qualified for up to 40 flights, are also used on Falcon Heavy, with an average of 6 flights per booster on Falcon Heavy. Although our Falcon 9 boosters have been engineered and demonstrated to support up to 40 flights, we have established a maximum accounting useful life of 25 flights as an estimate based on forecasted utilization.
Falcon Heavy 概览 • 可重复使用：Falcon Heavy 采用以可重复使用为核心的设计，已助力降低太空进入成本并改变了发射行业对大型或高价值载荷的经济模型。该载具的两枚侧面助推器配备高超音速栅格翼和先进推进系统，使受控回收和软着陆成为可能。这一能力使可重复使用成为现实——使用飞行验证助推器执行的任务通常价格低于传统一次性飞行。我们的 Falcon 9 助推器（认证最多飞行 40 次）也用于 Falcon Heavy，Falcon Heavy 上助推器平均飞行 6 次。虽然我们的 Falcon 9 助推器已经过工程认证并展示出支持最多 40 次飞行，但我们已基于预测使用率，设定了最高 25 次飞行的会计上有效使用寿命估计。
This estimate reflects: (i) our strategic transition to Starship, which is expected to materially reduce future Falcon 9 flight demand; and (ii) restrictions under certain government contracts that prohibit the use of boosters flown more than five times on their missions. These useful life estimates are periodically reassessed based on engineering qualification data, post-flight inspections, recovery success rates, actual fleet performance, cost sensitivity analyses, and the long-range launch manifest.
这一估计反映了：(i) 我们向 Starship 的战略过渡——预计将显著减少未来 Falcon 9 飞行需求；以及 (ii) 某些政府合同禁止在其任务上使用飞行超过 5 次的助推器。这些有效使用寿命估计会基于工程认证数据、飞行后检查、回收成功率、实际舰队表现、成本敏感性分析和长程发射任务清单定期重新评估。
Falcon Heavy •Exploratory Missions Beyond Earth's Orbit: Falcon Heavy first launched in February 2018, when it put a Tesla Roadster and its mannequin passenger, Starman, into orbit around the Sun. This was the first instance of a car sent into deep space and demonstrated the rocket's capability for trans-Mars injection. Since its inaugural flight, Falcon Heavy has completed missions that expanded the scope of space exploration and commercial spaceflight. Falcon Heavy has been selected by NASA to launch critical weather satellites, interplanetary probes including Europa Clipper (Jupiter) and Dragonfly (Saturn), and the upcoming Nancy Grace Roman telescope, designed to study exoplanets and dark energy and matter.
Falcon Heavy • 超越地球轨道的探索任务：Falcon Heavy 于 2018 年 2 月首飞，把一辆 Tesla Roadster 和其人偶乘客 Starman 送入绕太阳轨道。这是首次把汽车送入深空，并展示了火箭的跨火星注入能力。自首飞以来，Falcon Heavy 已完成扩展太空探索和商业航天范围的任务。Falcon Heavy 已被 NASA 选定发射关键气象卫星、行星际探测器（包括 Europa Clipper（木星）和 Dragonfly（土星）），以及即将发射的 Nancy Grace Roman 望远镜——该望远镜用于研究系外行星、暗能量和暗物质。
Starman in Orbit •Perfect Performance Record: As of March 31, 2026, Falcon Heavy had successfully completed 11 launches, all resulting in successful payload delivery. Falcon Heavy flown boosters have also safely completed 18 total recoveries and 16 reflights. It was certified for National Security Space Launch in 2019, authorizing its use for U.S. government operations alongside Falcon 9.
Starman 在轨 • 完美的执行记录：截至 2026 年 3 月 31 日，Falcon Heavy 已成功完成 11 次发射，全部成功交付载荷。Falcon Heavy 已飞行的助推器还安全完成了总共 18 次回收和 16 次复飞。2019 年它获得了国家安全太空发射认证（National Security Space Launch），授权与 Falcon 9 一起用于美国政府作业。
Starship. A fully reusable two-stage super heavy-lift launch vehicle, Starship stands to fundamentally transform spaceflight by making it more accessible, cost-effective, and scalable than ever before. Comprising the Super Heavy booster (powered by 33 Raptor engines) and the Starship upper stage (with three sea-level and three vacuum Raptor engines), Starship V3 is designed to deliver 100 metric tons to space in a fully reusable configuration while enabling rapid turnaround times akin to commercial aviation, and future generations could reach 200 metric tons, potentially as soon as Starship V4. To date, we have executed 11 Starship flight tests. We have also scheduled a 12th flight test, which will debut the next generation Starship vehicle and Super Heavy booster, powered by the next evolution of our Raptor engine and launching from a newly designed pad at Starbase. We expect Starship to commence payload delivery to orbit in the second half of 2026. We have achieved innovative milestones, including multiple successful ascents of the world's most powerful rocket; the launch, return, catch, and reuse of the Super Heavy booster; the return of its upper stage within three meters of its intended landing point; the transfer of approximately five metric tons of cryogenic propellant between tanks while in space, a first of its kind operation that provides key data for future full-scale propellant transfer operations; successful in-space relights of the Raptor engines; and multiple controlled reentries through Earth's atmosphere. The purpose of flight tests is to collect data so no result, even loss of a vehicle, is considered a failure because we learn something.
Starship。一款完全可重复使用的两级超重型运载火箭，Starship 有望通过让航天比以往更可及、更具成本效益和更可扩展，从根本上变革航天。Starship 由 Super Heavy 助推器（由 33 台 Raptor 引擎驱动）和 Starship 上面级（配备三台海平面 Raptor 引擎和三台真空 Raptor 引擎）组成。Starship V3 被设计为在完全可重复使用配置下向太空投送 100 公吨，并实现类似商业航空的快速周转，未来几代——可能最早在 Starship V4 上——可达 200 公吨。截至目前我们已执行 11 次 Starship 飞行测试。我们还安排了第 12 次飞行测试，将首次启用下一代 Starship 载具和 Super Heavy 助推器，由下一代 Raptor 引擎驱动，从 Starbase 全新设计的发射台发射。我们预计 Starship 将在 2026 年下半年开始向轨道投送有效载荷。我们已实现创新里程碑，包括多次成功将全球最强大火箭送上轨道；Super Heavy 助推器的发射、返回、抓回与重复使用；上面级在距其预定着陆点三米范围内返回；在太空中跨贮罐转移约 5 公吨低温推进剂——一种首创操作，为未来全规模推进剂转移提供关键数据；Raptor 引擎在太空成功重新点火；以及多次受控的地球大气重返。飞行测试的目的是收集数据，因此任何结果——即便是失去一艘载具——都不被视为失败，因为我们从中学到东西。
Starship is a key enabler of our growth objectives, including the deployment of next-generation V3 satellites, direct- to-cell constellations, and orbital AI compute at scale. Achieving our targeted launch cadence with Starship will require significant progress on several key milestones and the investment of significant capital resources.
